}

Table member.assignment_rule {
  assignment_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assignment_rule']
  parent_assignment_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on assignment_rule (parent_assignment_rule_id)']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule ceases to be effective (nullable for open-ended).']
  rule_action varchar [note: 'Action to be taken when the rule is satisfied.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification grouping within the rule type.']
  rule_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria or conditions that trigger the rule.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the assignment rule\'s purpose and logic.']
  rule_expression varchar [note: 'Expression or formula that defines the rule logic.']
  rule_immutable_date date [note: 'Date when the rule was marked immutable.']
  rule_immutable_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the rule is immutable.']
  rule_is_custom boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the rule is user-defined.']
  rule_is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the rule is the default for its category.']
  rule_is_immutable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the rule cannot be modified after creation.']
  rule_is_system boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the rule is system-generated.']
  rule_lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the rule (e.g., proposed, approved, retired).']
  rule_logic varchar [note: 'Detailed logic steps or algorithm for the rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the assignment rule.']
  rule_order varchar [note: 'Sequence order for rule evaluation within a group.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts between overlapping rules.']
  rule_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the rule (e.g., member, plan, product).']
  rule_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule\'s scope.']
  rule_source_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule in the source system.']
  rule_source_description varchar [note: 'Description of the rule in the source system.']
  rule_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system where the rule originates.']
  rule_source_version varchar [note: 'Version of the rule in the source system.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the rule (e.g., active, inactive, draft).']
  rule_subtype varchar [note: 'Fine-grained subtype for the rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the rule (e.g., eligibility, benefit, coverage).']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the rule.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the most recent update.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for assignment_rule. Referenced by assignment_rule_id.'
}

Table enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span {
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the enrollment eligibility span record.']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Needed for eligibility reporting to show which benefit package a member\'s coverage span belongs to, essential for benefit design analysis.']
  case_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Eligibility spans are processed by a case‑manager employee; linking enables case‑manager workload and audit reports.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each eligibility span must satisfy particular regulatory obligations (e.g., ACA reporting); linking enables obligation‑by‑span compliance tracking.']
  group_division_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_division. Business justification: Large employers apply division‑specific eligibility rules; linking enables division‑level eligibility analytics.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group for group enrollments.']
  group_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_location. Business justification: Eligibility calculations depend on employer\'s primary location to apply network rules and state‑specific regulations.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health plan associated with this eligibility span.']
  member_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to member.member_eligibility_span. Business justification: Eligibility reconciliation report requires linking each enrollment eligibility span to the member\'s eligibility span to compare periods and resolve discrepancies.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Eligibility engine uses member\'s risk score to determine coverage; the \'Eligibility Determination with Risk Score\' report requires this link.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom this eligibility span applies.']
  primary_care_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Needed to assign a primary care provider to each member’s coverage span for network compliance and care coordination reports.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_program. Business justification: Eligibility Span determines member eligibility for specific Care Programs; required for Care Program Eligibility Reporting and program enrollment tracking.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network associated with the plan.']
  benefit_designation varchar [note: 'Design of the benefit plan (e.g., HMO, PPO).']
  benefit_period_end date [note: 'End date of the benefit period (often same as termination_date).']
  benefit_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the benefit period (often same as effective_date).']
  benefit_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the benefit amounts apply.']
  coverage_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the covered individual (e.g., employee, spouse).']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable amount for a specific benefit within the span.']
  coverage_limit_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the coverage limit (e.g., visits, days).']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of coverage provided in this span (e.g., medical, dental).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the eligibility span record was first created.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual deductible amount applicable for this span.']
  deductible_reset_date date [note: 'Date when the deductible accumulator resets (usually start of benefit year).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the eligibility span becomes active.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Eligibility determination for the member during this span.']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility span.']
  enrollment_event_type varchar [note: 'Category of enrollment event that created or modified this span.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'System or channel through which the enrollment was received.']
  is_primary_coverage boolean [note: 'True if this span represents the member\'s primary coverage.']
  is_waiver_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates if a cost‑sharing waiver (e.g., HSA) is applied to this span.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status field last changed.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line under which the enrollment is processed.']
  moop_reset_date date [note: 'Date when the MOOP threshold resets.']
  moop_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined deductible and OOP limit for this span.']
  oop_maximum decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum out‑of‑pocket expense the member can incur.']
  oop_reset_date date [note: 'Date when the OOP accumulator resets.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External plan identifier used in contracts and communications.']
  prior_coverage_end_date date [note: 'Termination date of the preceding coverage period, if any.']
  prior_coverage_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the member had coverage immediately before this span.']
  qualifying_life_event_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the life event that triggered a special enrollment.']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the span was created or modified retroactively.']
  retroactive_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for a retroactive change to the eligibility span.']
  subscriber_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the covered individual to the subscriber.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the eligibility span ends; null if still active.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the enrollment transaction that generated this span.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  utilization_reset_date date [note: 'Date when utilization counters (e.g., visit counts) reset.']
  waiver_type varchar [note: 'Type of waiver applied, if any.']

  Note: 'Core master record representing a member\'s continuous coverage period under a specific health plan, including benefit year boundaries, deductible/OOP accumulator reset dates, and MOOP thresholds. Captures effective date, termination date, coverage type, line of business (commercial, Medicare, Medicaid), plan code, subscriber relationship, eligibility status, and benefit period attributes. This is the authoritative SSOT for \'is this member covered?\' and \'what benefit period applies?\' at any point in time. One enrollment_transaction may produce one or more eligibility_spans. Sourced from Facets/QNXT enrollment module and CMS EDPS for government programs.'
}

Table enrollment.transaction {
  transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for transaction']
  care_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_plan. Business justification: Each enrollment transaction can generate a Care Plan; linking enables cost, outcome, and compliance reporting per transaction.']
  eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the eligibility span generated from this transaction.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user or process that performed the last audit action.']
  enrollment_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch job that processed this enrollment transaction.']
  enrollment_open_enrollment_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.open_enrollment_period. Business justification: Each enrollment transaction occurs within a defined open enrollment period; linking transaction to period enables period‑based reporting and ensures the period is not a silo.']
  group_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_contact. Business justification: Required for audit of enrollment approvals: employer\'s designated contact must be recorded per ACA compliance reporting.']
  group_renewal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_renewal. Business justification: Financial reconciliation requires associating each enrollment transaction with the employer\'s renewal cycle.']
  member_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group or line of business to which the member belongs.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with this enrollment transaction.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network enrollment reporting for quarterly compliance requires linking each transaction to the provider network.']
  raps_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.raps_submission. Business justification: During enrollment processing, a RAPS risk‑adjustment submission may be generated; linking provides audit trail for regulatory reporting.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.plan_rate. Business justification: Required for premium audit reports that must trace each enrollment transaction to the exact rate applied for regulatory compliance.']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: ACA regulatory filing requires linking each enrollment transaction to the CMS submission record that contains its data for audit and reporting.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment (e.g., discount, surcharge) applied to the gross premium.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code describing why a financial or coverage adjustment was made.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or system that approved the enrollment change.']
  audit_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the audit user (e.g., admin, operator, system).']
  claims_reprocess_flag boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment change requires downstream claims to be re‑processed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the transaction with applicable regulations.']
  coverage_period_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the coverage is continuous, intermittent, or contains a gap.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment transaction record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values in this transaction.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the member\'s coverage under the new enrollment becomes effective.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the coverage period associated with this enrollment (may differ from termination_date).']
  enrollment_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for notes or comments entered by the processor.']
  enrollment_origin varchar [note: 'Channel through which the enrollment was initiated.']
  enrollment_transaction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the enrollment transaction, used for tracking and reference.']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Category of enrollment event: open enrollment, special enrollment period, automatic enrollment, special manual enrollment, or other.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the enrollment event occurred in the source system.']
  financial_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True when the enrollment change has a direct impact on premium billing or financial reporting.']
  grace_period_end_date date [note: 'Date when the applicable grace period expires.']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total premium amount before any adjustments for the enrollment period.']
  health_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health plan (e.g., HMO, PPO, EPO, POS, HDHP).']
  is_grace_period boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment is being processed within a grace period after termination.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final premium amount after adjustments, representing the amount to be billed.']
  original_termination_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior termination transaction that is being reinstated.']
  prior_authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment change triggers a prior authorization requirement.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing stage of the transaction within the enrollment workflow.']
  reactivation_date date [note: 'Effective date of coverage reactivation for reinstatement events.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction must be reported to a regulatory body (e.g., CMS, NAIC).']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction represents a retroactive adjustment to prior coverage dates.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the enrollment transaction record.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which coverage ends for a disenrollment or termination event (nullable if not applicable).']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason why the enrollment was terminated.']
  transaction_source varchar [note: 'Source of the enrollment transaction record, e.g., EDI 834 feed, web portal, batch load, or API.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment transaction record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every enrollment action for a member — initial enrollment, re-enrollment, plan change, disenrollment (voluntary/involuntary termination), reinstatement, and retroactive adjustment. Tracks enrollment source, enrollment period type (open enrollment, SEP, auto-enrollment), effective date, submitted date, processing status, and transaction-type-specific attributes: termination reason/last day of coverage/grace period status for disenrollments; original termination reference/reinstatement effective date/approving authority for reinstatements; adjusted dates/financial impact flag/claims reprocessing trigger for retro adjustments. One enrollment_transaction may produce one or more eligibility_spans. Sourced from Facets/QNXT and 834 EDI transactions via Availity/Change Healthcare.'
}

Table enrollment.event {
  event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each enrollment event record.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Internal audits trace specific enrollment events (e.g., life‑event changes) to audit findings that assess compliance with enrollment rules.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the actor (e.g., member_id, employer_id) that caused the event.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group linked to the enrollment, if applicable.']
  group_plan_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.group_plan_offering. Business justification: Event tracking reports must reference the exact plan offering the change relates to for employer‑level reporting.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the enrollment event.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network change audit log records which network triggered an enrollment event for regulatory audit.']
  risk_underwriting_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_underwriting_case. Business justification: Event‑driven underwriting decisions are recorded; linking supports the \'Underwriting Decision Audit\' report.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the enrollment to which this event belongs.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the party that triggered the event.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the new enrollment status becomes effective.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative providing additional context for the event.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'System or interface that generated the event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the enrollment event occurred in the source system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the state transition (e.g., submitted, approved).']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event was entered manually (True) or generated automatically (False).']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Enrollment status after the event was applied.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Enrollment status before the event was applied.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why the event was generated (e.g., QLE‑DIVORCE, SYSTEM‑ERROR).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the event record.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment was terminated, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Granular audit log of every state transition in the enrollment lifecycle — submitted, pended, approved, rejected, effectuated, terminated, reinstated, retroactively adjusted. Each event captures the triggering action, actor (member, employer, CMS, system), timestamp, reason code, and before/after state. Supports regulatory audit trails required by CMS, state DOI, and HIPAA administrative simplification. Distinct from enrollment_transaction (which is the business action) — this is the immutable event history of that action\'s processing lifecycle.'
}

Table enrollment.qualifying_life_event {
  qualifying_life_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the qualifying life event record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or system that performed the verification.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who reported the qualifying life event.']
  appeal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any appeal filed against a denial.']
  cms_sep_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the CMS SEP eligibility determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualifying life event record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why the SEP request was denied, if applicable.']
  documentation_type varchar [note: 'Type of supporting document submitted for the event.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the qualifying life event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the life event that triggers a special enrollment period.']
  qualifying_life_event_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the qualifying life event record.']
  sep_category_code varchar [note: 'CMS‑defined code that classifies the SEP category for the qualifying life event.']
  sep_window_end date [note: 'Last date the special enrollment period (SEP) is open for this event.']
  sep_window_start date [note: 'First date the special enrollment period (SEP) is open for this event.']
  sep_window_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the SEP window based on dates and actions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the qualifying life event record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date the verification decision was recorded.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the CMS SEP verification process.']

  Note: 'Master record of a qualifying life event (QLE) that triggers a special enrollment period (SEP), including the full SEP verification lifecycle. Captures event type (marriage, birth, loss of coverage, relocation, divorce, adoption), event date, SEP window open/close dates, CMS SEP category codes, submitted documentation type and artifacts (birth certificate, marriage license, loss of coverage letter), verification status, verifier ID, verification date, CMS SEP verification outcome, pend/denial reason, and appeal reference. This is the authoritative SSOT for both QLE registration and SEP verification — no separate verification record exists. Required for ACA compliance, CMS SEP eligibility determinations, and SEP integrity program compliance. Sourced from Facets/QNXT pend workflow and member portal document uploads.'
}

Table enrollment.open_enrollment_period {
  open_enrollment_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for open_enrollment_period']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification outcome for the enrollment period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the enrollment period record was first created in the system.']
  eligibility_segment varchar [note: 'Population segment eligible for this enrollment period (e.g., group, individual, Medicare).']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day for submitting enrollment applications; after this date the window closes.']
  enrollment_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cut‑off time (in local time) after which submissions on the deadline date are rejected.']
  enrollment_deadline_date date [note: 'Final calendar date by which all enrollment submissions must be received.']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the enrollment window based on its recurrence and purpose.']
  exchange_type varchar [note: 'Marketplace or exchange through which the enrollment is offered.']
  is_annual boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment period recurs each year on the same schedule.']
  is_retrospective_allowed boolean [note: 'True if members may submit enrollment changes that become effective prior to the submission date.']
  lob varchar [note: 'Business line to which the enrollment period applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks, exceptions, or operational comments.']
  open_enrollment_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment window.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the enrollment window across systems.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the enrollment window, e.g., "2025 Individual Marketplace OE".']
  regulatory_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment period triggers mandatory regulatory reporting (e.g., CMS filings).']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day on which members may submit enrollment applications.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the enrollment period record.']
  volume_actual varchar [note: 'Actual count of enrollments captured within the window.']
  volume_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of enrollments the organization aims to achieve during the window.']
  volume_target_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual enrollment volume met or exceeded the target.']

  Note: 'Master record defining each open enrollment window — annual employer group OE, ACA marketplace OE, Medicare Annual Enrollment Period (AEP), and Medicaid continuous enrollment periods. Captures period start/end dates, eligible population segment, LOB, exchange type (SHOP, individual marketplace, off-exchange), and enrollment volume targets. Used to govern when enrollment_records are accepted without a QLE. Sourced from CMS plan year calendars and employer group contracts.'
}

Table enrollment.edi_transaction {
  edi_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for edi_transaction']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group associated with the enrollment transaction.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'External identifier supplied by the sender to uniquely identify the transaction (e.g., control number from the sender\'s system).']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trading partner that sent the 834 file (e.g., employer or broker ID).']
  average_record_size_bytes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average size of each member record within the file, calculated as file_size_bytes divided by member_count.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment coverage becomes effective for the member(s).']
  enrollment_action_code varchar [note: 'Standard X12 834 code indicating Add (A), Delete (D), Modify (M), or Correct (C) action.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code returned when processing fails (e.g., X12 validation error).']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the processing error.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Original filename of the inbound 834 transaction as received from the EDI gateway.']
  file_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the 834 file was first ingested into the lakehouse.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Total size of the inbound 834 file in bytes.']
  group_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number identifying the GS functional group for the transaction.']
  interchange_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number identifying the ISA interchange envelope for the transaction.']
  last_attempt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent processing attempt.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the transaction from ingestion to final disposition.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual member enrollment records contained in the 834 file.']
  processing_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of times the transaction has been attempted for processing.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the transaction within the ingestion pipeline.']
  receiver_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trading partner that receives the 834 file (typically the health insurer).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Result of matching the transaction to internal enrollment records.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the external EDI gateway or clearinghouse that delivered the file.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment coverage ends, if applicable.']
  transaction_set_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number that uniquely identifies the 834 transaction set within the interchange.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the business event time encoded in the 834 transaction (e.g., effective date of the enrollment action).']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the 834 file adds new enrollments, changes existing ones, terminates coverage, or corrects prior data.']

  Note: 'Raw and processed 834 EDI transaction records received from employer groups, exchanges, and CMS — the primary electronic mechanism for enrollment adds, changes, and terminations. Captures ISA/GS envelope metadata, transaction set control number, sender/receiver IDs, transaction type (full file vs. change only), processing status, error codes, and reconciliation status. Sourced from Availity/Change Healthcare EDI gateway. Distinct from enrollment_record (the business outcome) — this is the inbound EDI artifact.'
}

Table enrollment.eligibility_verification {
  eligibility_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for eligibility_verification']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the member is enrolled.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique member identifier used in the eligibility request (e.g., member number, SSN, MRN).']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Required for automated provider credential checks during eligibility verification; the process matches providers to member eligibility inquiries.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Eligibility verification must confirm provider credential status for CMS compliance; linking to credential_record enables automated credential checks during eligibility processing.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the source system for the eligibility transaction.']
  authorization_number varchar [note: 'Authorization number returned when prior authorization is required.']
  authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization is required for the service.']
  benefit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of benefits being queried (e.g., medical, dental).']
  benefit_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount allowed for the queried benefit.']
  benefit_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining dollar amount of the benefit available.']
  benefit_used decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the benefit already utilized in the current period.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Level of coverage returned (full, partial, or none).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the eligibility record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  deductible_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Member\'s remaining deductible balance for the benefit period.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD diagnosis code associated with the request, if applicable.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Overall result of the eligibility check (eligible, ineligible, error, or pending).']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Technical or business error code returned in a failed eligibility response.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the error condition.']
  inquiry_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the source system (e.g., Availity) for the eligibility request.']
  inquiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the eligibility request was sent to the payer.']
  member_identifier_type varchar [note: 'Type of identifier supplied for the member (SSN, MRN, or internal member ID).']
  oop_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining out‑of‑pocket amount before maximum OOP is reached.']
  response_message varchar [note: 'Free‑form message returned by the payer providing additional context.']
  response_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elapsed time between request and response, measured in seconds.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the eligibility response was received.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'CPT code of the service/procedure for which eligibility is being checked.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'EDI gateway or clearinghouse that supplied the eligibility response.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the eligibility record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of real-time 270/271 eligibility verification requests and responses. Captures requesting provider NPI, member identifier used, inquiry date/time, response status, coverage details returned, deductible/OOP balances returned, and response time SLA. Supports provider-facing eligibility verification at point of care. Sourced from Availity/Change Healthcare 270/271 gateway and Facets real-time eligibility API.'
}

Table enrollment.cobra_election {
  cobra_election_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the COBRA election record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group from which the member lost group coverage.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health benefit plan associated with the COBRA election.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who is electing COBRA continuation coverage.']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'External reference number or code used by the business to identify the election.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement; identifies it as a COBRA continuation election.']
  cobra_premium_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monthly premium amount for COBRA coverage (typically 102% of the full premium).']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when COBRA coverage terminates.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Effective date when COBRA coverage begins.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of COBRA coverage (individual, family, or dependent only).']
  dependent_count varchar [note: 'Count of dependents included in the COBRA election.']
  early_termination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the COBRA coverage was terminated before the scheduled end date.']
  early_termination_notice_sent_flag boolean [note: 'True when an early termination notice has been sent to the member.']
  election_decision varchar [note: 'Member\'s decision regarding COBRA continuation coverage.']
  election_notice_sent_flag boolean [note: 'True when the election notice has been dispatched to the member.']
  election_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the COBRA election process.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent premium payment received for the COBRA coverage.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the COBRA election record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional comments or special instructions.']
  notice_date_election date [note: 'Date the election-specific notice (reminder) was sent to the member.']
  notice_date_initial date [note: 'Date the initial COBRA continuation coverage notice was sent to the member.']
  notice_deadline date [note: 'Last date by which the member must elect COBRA coverage.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the member to pay the COBRA premium.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of premium payment for the COBRA election.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the COBRA premium.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the COBRA premium is billed.']
  qualifying_event_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualifying event occurred.']
  qualifying_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualifying event that triggered COBRA eligibility.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the COBRA election record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the COBRA election record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the COBRA election data.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the subsidy applied to the premium.']
  subsidy_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member qualifies for an ARP COBRA subsidy.']
  subsidy_type varchar [note: 'Type of subsidy applied to the COBRA premium.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking COBRA continuation coverage elections for members who have lost group health coverage due to a qualifying event (termination, reduction in hours, divorce, death, aging off dependent). Captures qualifying event type, election notice date, election deadline, election status, COBRA premium amount (102% of full premium), coverage start/end dates, subsidy eligibility (ARP COBRA subsidy), and COBRA-specific notice tracking (initial notice, election notice, early termination notice). Governed by ERISA Section 601-608 and DOL COBRA regulations. Sourced from Facets/QNXT COBRA module.'
}

Table enrollment.enrollment_cms_submission {
  enrollment_cms_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cms_submission']
  benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific benefit package within the plan.']
  cms_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by CMS for the submission.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan associated with the enrollment.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the member whose enrollment is being submitted.']
  submission_batch_id varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier for the batch of enrollment records submitted together.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any premium adjustments (e.g., discounts, fees) applied to the submission.']
  audit_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created or last updated the record.']
  audit_user_role varchar [note: 'Role or job function of the audit user (e.g., Data Steward, Operations Analyst).']
  compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance validation was performed.']
  compliance_error_code varchar [note: 'Code representing a specific compliance validation failure.']
  compliance_error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the compliance error.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission passed all regulatory compliance validations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the submission record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting the overall quality of the submission data.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the member\'s coverage becomes effective.']
  enrollment_cms_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the submission within the processing pipeline.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Technical error code returned by the CMS response file.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message associated with the error code.']
  is_legacy_submission boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the submission originates from a legacy system.']
  is_test_submission boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the submission is a test (non‑production) record.']
  member_number varchar [note: 'External member identifier used in member communications and EDI files.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final premium amount after adjustments, to be billed.']
  processing_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when internal processing of the submission completed.']
  processing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when internal processing of the submission began.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of internal processing of the submission file.']
  raf_score_impact decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric impact on the member\'s RAF score resulting from the enrollment.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'CMS‑provided code indicating why a submission was rejected.']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the rejection reason.']
  risk_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission impacts the RAF (Risk Adjustment Factor) score.']
  submission_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EDI file transmitted to CMS.']
  submission_file_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the EDI file was created or received by the gateway.']
  submission_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating core administration system that generated the submission.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the enrollment batch was transmitted to CMS.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the submission is an initial enrollment, a correction, a deletion, or a termination request.']
  submission_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the submission schema or format.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the member\'s coverage ends, if applicable.']
  total_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross premium amount for the enrollment before adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the submission record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of enrollment data submissions to CMS for Medicare Advantage, Part D, and Medicaid managed care programs via RAPS and EDPS. Captures submission batch ID, submission type (initial, correction, delete), CMS transaction ID, acceptance/rejection status, error detail codes, risk adjustment impact flag, and reconciliation status. Critical for government program compliance and RAF score accuracy. Sourced from CMS EDPS/RAPS response files.'
}

Table enrollment.plan_election {
  plan_election_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the plan election record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan that was elected.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subscriber who made the election.']
  primary_plan_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the election record.']
  prior_plan_election_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous election record when this election is a change or reinstatement.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Plan election pricing is derived from the assigned risk pool; the \'Plan Election Pricing\' report needs this association.']
  cobra_eligibility_end_date date [note: 'Date when COBRA eligibility expires.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Level of coverage elected (e.g., employee only, employee + spouse, employee + children, family).']
  dental_rider_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a dental coverage rider was elected.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the elected coverage becomes effective.']
  election_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the election by the enrollment system.']
  election_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the election event (new enrollment, change, termination, reinstatement).']
  enrollment_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of enrollment event that triggered the election.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the election was submitted.']
  fsa_election_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a Flexible Spending Account was elected.']
  hra_election_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a Health Reimbursement Arrangement was elected.']
  hsa_election_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a Health Savings Account was elected.']
  is_cobra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the election is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions related to the election.']
  plan_election_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the election record.']
  premium_contribution_employee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employee.']
  premium_contribution_employer decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employer.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'Billing frequency for the premium (e.g., monthly).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the election record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the election record.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the elected coverage ends, if applicable.']
  total_premium decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total premium amount for the elected coverage (employer + employee).']
  vision_rider_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a vision coverage rider was elected.']

  Note: 'Master record of a subscriber\'s specific plan selection, coverage tier, and covered member roster during an enrollment or plan change event. Captures elected plan ID, coverage tier (employee-only, employee+spouse, employee+children, family), effective date, prior plan reference, premium contribution split (employer vs. employee), HSA/HRA/FSA election flag, dental/vision rider elections, and the roster of covered individuals with their relationship codes, age-out dates, and COBRA continuation eligibility. References member domain for person identity — this product owns the enrollment-specific election and roster, not the person demographics. Represents the member\'s choice artifact — distinct from eligibility_span (the resulting coverage). Sourced from Facets/QNXT benefits election module.'
}

Table enrollment.pend_queue {
  pend_queue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pend_queue']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or team responsible for resolving the pend.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group associated with the enrollment, if applicable.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose enrollment is pending.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pend raises a compliance concern under HIPAA, ACA, or other regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pend record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment would become effective if the pend is resolved successfully.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pend has been escalated to a higher tier.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Severity level of the escalation.']
  is_aptc_csr_conflict_flag boolean [note: 'True when there is a conflict between Advanced Premium Tax Credit (APTC) eligibility and Cost Sharing Reduction (CSR) calculations.']
  is_auto_resolved boolean [note: 'True when the system automatically resolves the pend without human intervention.']
  is_cms_match_failure_flag boolean [note: 'True when the enrollment data fails validation against CMS EDPS/RAPS rules.']
  is_documentation_missing_flag boolean [note: 'True when required enrollment documentation has not been received.']
  is_duplicate_flag boolean [note: 'True when the pending enrollment is identified as a potential duplicate of an existing member record.']
  is_eligibility_discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'True when there is a mismatch between submitted eligibility data and system records.']
  is_employer_verification_hold_flag boolean [note: 'True when the employer group verification is pending, causing the enrollment to be held.']
  is_manual_review_required boolean [note: 'True when the pend must be reviewed manually rather than automatically.']
  last_escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent escalation event for the pend.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by staff documenting additional context or actions.']
  pend_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the enrollment was placed in a pending state.']
  pend_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing detail about the pend reason.']
  pend_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the pend record for tracking and communication.']
  pend_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pending enrollment.']
  pend_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the enrollment transaction entered the pending queue.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the pend for handling order.']
  regulatory_rule_code varchar [note: 'Code of the specific regulatory rule (e.g., CMS_14DAY) that governs the pend\'s timeliness.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the pend (e.g., document upload, eligibility verification, manual override).']
  resolution_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pend was resolved.']
  resolution_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the pending issue must be resolved to meet SLA or regulatory timeliness.']
  resolver_team varchar [note: 'Name of the team or workgroup handling the pend.']
  sla_actual_days varchar [note: 'Actual number of days taken to resolve the pend.']
  sla_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pend is meeting the Service Level Agreement targets.']
  sla_target_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days defined by SLA for resolving the pend.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment is terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pend record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for enrollment transactions placed in a pending/suspended state awaiting resolution — missing documentation, eligibility discrepancy, duplicate member detection, CMS data match failure, employer group verification hold, or APTC/CSR eligibility conflict. Captures pend reason code, pend date, resolution deadline, assigned resolver (individual or team queue), resolution action taken, resolution date, escalation history, and SLA compliance status. Tracks enrollment processing turnaround SLA compliance per CMS timeliness standards (e.g., 14-day effectuation requirement) and state regulatory requirements. High-volume operational product — typically 5-15% of all enrollment transactions pend.'
}

Table enrollment.medicaid_eligibility {
  medicaid_eligibility_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the Medicaid eligibility record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Medicaid eligibility determinations are tied to a specific Medicaid health plan for state reporting and compliance.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom this eligibility applies.']
  prior_eligibility_medicaid_eligibility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous eligibility record for continuity tracking.']
  assets_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total countable assets reported for eligibility assessment.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when Medicaid coverage under this eligibility ends, if known.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date when Medicaid coverage under this eligibility begins.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the eligibility record was first created in the system.']
  dual_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is also eligible for Medicare.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the Medicaid coverage becomes effective.']
  eligibility_category varchar [note: 'Classification of Medicaid eligibility based on income and other criteria.']
  eligibility_determination_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the eligibility determination was made.']
  eligibility_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured by eligibility staff during determination.']
  eligibility_number varchar [note: 'Business reference number for the eligibility determination, used in reporting and external communications.']
  eligibility_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating the reason for eligibility determination (e.g., income, disability).']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility record.']
  eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which eligibility information was verified.']
  eligibility_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify eligibility information.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the eligibility record (e.g., EDI 834 file, Facets system, manual entry).']
  federal_program_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the eligibility is linked to a federal program such as Medicare.']
  fpl_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Member\'s income expressed as a percentage of the Federal Poverty Level.']
  household_size varchar [note: 'Number of individuals in the member\'s household used for eligibility calculations.']
  income_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Reported annual income used for eligibility calculation.']
  income_verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the income verification process for eligibility.']
  last_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the eligibility record.']
  medicaid_program_type varchar [note: 'Type of Medicaid program the member is enrolled in.']
  medical_need_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member qualifies based on medical need criteria.']
  redetermination_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the member must be re-evaluated for continued eligibility.']
  special_program_indicator varchar [note: 'Identifies enrollment in special Medicaid programs (e.g., pregnant, disability).']
  state_agency varchar [note: 'Two-letter code of the state Medicaid agency governing the eligibility.']
  state_medicaid_number varchar [note: 'State-assigned Medicaid identification number for the member.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the Medicaid coverage ends or is scheduled to end.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking Medicaid and CHIP eligibility determinations for members enrolled in managed Medicaid plans. Captures Medicaid eligibility category (MAGI, SSI, CHIP, dual-eligible), state Medicaid ID, eligibility determination date, redetermination due date, income verification status, household size, FPL percentage, and dual-eligible Medicare coordination flag. Interfaces with state Medicaid agency eligibility systems and CMS EDPS. Sourced from state 834 files and Facets Medicaid module.'
}

Table enrollment.medicare_entitlement {
  medicare_entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the Medicare entitlement record.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member to whom this entitlement belongs.']
  cms_contract_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CMS contract under which the member\'s Medicare coverage is administered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the entitlement record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  eligibility_span_end date [note: 'End date of the member\'s overall Medicare eligibility period (null if ongoing).']
  eligibility_span_start date [note: 'Start date of the member\'s overall Medicare eligibility period.']
  entitlement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number used by business partners to identify this entitlement.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the Medicare entitlement record.']
  entitlement_type varchar [note: 'Category of Medicare coverage (e.g., Medicare Advantage, Part D, Dual Eligible).']
  extra_help_effective_date date [note: 'Date the Extra Help status became effective.']
  extra_help_status varchar [note: 'Eligibility status for the Extra Help program (assists with Part D costs).']
  irmaa_effective_date date [note: 'Date the IRMAA status became effective.']
  irmaa_income_bracket varchar [note: 'Income bracket used to calculate the member\'s IRMAA surcharge.']
  irmaa_status varchar [note: 'Current IRMAA status indicating whether the member pays an income‑based surcharge.']
  is_dual_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is eligible for both Medicare and Medicaid.']
  is_retired boolean [note: 'True if the entitlement has been retired (e.g., member deceased or moved to non‑Medicare coverage).']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the entitlement information was last verified against CMS.']
  lis_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the LIS status became effective.']
  lis_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the member\'s Low Income Subsidy eligibility.']
  ma_enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'Date the member\'s Medicare Advantage (MA) plan enrollment became effective.']
  mbi varchar [note: 'Unique 11‑character identifier assigned to each Medicare beneficiary.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional remarks or audit comments.']
  part_a_entitlement_date date [note: 'Effective date on which the member became entitled to Medicare Part A benefits.']
  part_a_termination_date date [note: 'Date on which Part A entitlement ended, if applicable.']
  part_b_entitlement_date date [note: 'Effective date on which the member became entitled to Medicare Part B benefits.']
  part_b_termination_date date [note: 'Date on which Part B entitlement ended, if applicable.']
  part_d_enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'Date the member\'s Medicare Part D prescription drug coverage became effective.']
  pbp_assignment varchar [note: 'Code representing the primary business partner (e.g., health plan) assigned to the member.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the source record in the originating system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that supplied the entitlement data (e.g., MARx, EDPS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the entitlement record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the entitlement data.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing Medicare entitlement and enrollment details for members in Medicare Advantage (MA) and Part D plans. Captures Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI), Part A/B entitlement dates, MA enrollment effective date, Part D enrollment effective date, Low Income Subsidy (LIS) status, Extra Help level, IRMAA status, and CMS contract/PBP assignment. Sourced from CMS MARx system data and EDPS enrollment confirmations.'
}

Table enrollment.reconciliation {
  reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for reconciliation']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service account that executed the reconciliation.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group whose roster is being reconciled.']
  auto_resolution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the system automatically resolved the discrepancy.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the analyst about the reconciliation outcome.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for financial impact values.']
  discrepancy_add_count varchar [note: 'Count of enrollment records present in the source but missing in the internal system.']
  discrepancy_change_count varchar [note: 'Count of enrollment attribute changes (e.g., plan, coverage) that differ between systems.']
  discrepancy_demographic_mismatch_count varchar [note: 'Number of records where member demographic data (name, DOB, SSN) does not match.']
  discrepancy_detail_file_path varchar [note: 'File system or object storage path to the detailed discrepancy report generated by the run.']
  discrepancy_termination_count varchar [note: 'Count of enrollment records terminated in the source but still active internally.']
  discrepancy_total_count varchar [note: 'Aggregate number of mismatches identified in the reconciliation run.']
  financial_impact_adjustment decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of adjustments (credits, penalties) applied to the gross impact.']
  financial_impact_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary impact of all unresolved discrepancies before adjustments.']
  financial_impact_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net monetary impact after adjustments; the amount that may affect premium billing.']
  manual_resolution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether human intervention is required to resolve the discrepancy.']
  period_end date [note: 'End date of the enrollment coverage period being reconciled.']
  period_start date [note: 'Start date of the enrollment coverage period being reconciled.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reconciliation process.']
  resolution_deadline date [note: 'Date by which outstanding discrepancies must be resolved to avoid financial impact.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to each reconciliation execution for tracking and audit purposes.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reconciliation run was initiated.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the run was a full reconciliation or an incremental update.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the external enrollment data used in the reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing periodic reconciliation between the health plan\'s enrollment records and external authoritative sources — employer group rosters, CMS enrollment files (TRR/MARx), state Medicaid agency 834 files, and ACA exchange enrollment data. Captures reconciliation run date, source system, discrepancy count by type (adds, terms, changes, demographic mismatches), auto-resolution vs. manual resolution status, resolution deadline, and financial impact of unresolved discrepancies on premium billing and risk adjustment. Critical for CMS compliance (monthly TRR reconciliation), premium billing accuracy, and employer group roster integrity.'
}

Table enrollment.exchange_enrollment {
  exchange_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the exchange enrollment record.']
  enrollment_last_updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Exchange enrollment updates are performed by staff; FK supports regulatory audit of who last modified enrollment data.']
  enrollment_original_exchange_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original enrollment record that this renewal references.']
  exchange_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original transaction received from the ACA exchange gateway.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group (if group coverage) linked to the enrollment.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Health Insurance Oversight System (HIOS) plan identifier as provided by the marketplace.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with this enrollment.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: ACA marketplace reporting requires associating each exchange enrollment with the provider network for network‑level statistics.']
  qualifying_life_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualifying life event that triggered a special enrollment, if any.']
  aptc_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the advance premium tax credit applied to the member\'s premium.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment is for an individual, family, or group coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exchange enrollment record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  csr_variant varchar [note: 'Indicates the level of cost‑sharing reduction applied (none, full, or partial).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment coverage becomes effective.']
  effectuation_deadline date [note: 'Latest date by which the enrollment must be effectuated to avoid loss of coverage.']
  effectuation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the enrollment effectuation process with the marketplace.']
  enrollment_effectuation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the enrollment was effectuated with the marketplace.']
  enrollment_renewal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this enrollment record represents a renewal of a prior enrollment.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment.']
  enrollment_termination_initiator varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the termination of the enrollment.']
  enrollment_termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for enrollment termination.']
  enrollment_termination_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the termination as voluntary or involuntary.']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the enrollment event (e.g., new enrollment, renewal, reinstatement, termination).']
  exchange_eligibility_determination_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking to the eligibility determination used for this enrollment.']
  marketplace_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the enrollment: Federally Facilitated Marketplace (FFM), State-Based Marketplace (SBM), or Small Business Health Options Program (SHOP).']
  marketplace_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the marketplace enrollment originated (if applicable).']
  marketplace_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the marketplace enrollment period.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the premium payment must be received.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to collect the premium payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of premium payment for the enrollment.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'The plan year to which this enrollment belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly (or applicable frequency) premium amount for the enrollment.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'Billing frequency for the premium (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annually).']
  reporting_1095a_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment must be reported on Form 1095‑A.']
  reporting_1095a_generated_date date [note: 'Date on which the 1095‑A form was generated for this enrollment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'EDI gateway or clearinghouse that supplied the enrollment data.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total subsidy amount (APTC + CSR) applied to the premium.']
  subsidy_type varchar [note: 'Type of subsidy applied (APTC, Cost Sharing Reduction, or none).']
  tax_credit_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the reconciliation of tax credits (APTC/CSR) with the IRS.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment coverage ends, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exchange enrollment record.']

  Note: 'Master record for enrollments originating from ACA health insurance exchanges — Federally Facilitated Marketplace (FFM), State-Based Marketplaces (SBM), and SHOP exchange. Captures exchange transaction ID, marketplace plan ID (HIOS ID), APTC amount and reconciliation status, CSR variant, eligibility determination reference, effectuation status, 1095-A reporting flag, and premium payment effectuation deadline. Interfaces with CMS FFM enrollment data feeds. Critical for ACA compliance, APTC reconciliation with IRS, and annual 1095-A generation. Sourced from Availity/Change Healthcare exchange gateway and CMS 834 files.'
}

Table enrollment.submission_batch {
  submission_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for submission_batch']
  parent_submission_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on submission_batch (parent_submission_batch_id)']
  batch_approval_by varchar [note: 'Email address of the user who approved the batch.']

  batch_archival_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was archived.']
  batch_deletion_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was deleted.']
  batch_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the batch purpose.']
  batch_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors encountered during batch processing.']
  batch_error_details varchar [note: 'Detailed error messages for failed submissions.']
  batch_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of failed submissions in the batch.']
  batch_failure_details varchar [note: 'Details of failed submissions.']
  batch_finalization_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was finalized.']
  batch_is_approved boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the batch has been approved for processing.']
  batch_is_archived boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the batch has been archived.']
  batch_is_automatic boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the batch was generated automatically.']
  batch_is_deleted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the batch has been deleted.']
  batch_is_finalized boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the batch has been finalized.']
  batch_is_manual boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the batch was created manually.']
  batch_is_retroactive boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the batch contains retroactive submissions.']
  batch_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the batch.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to the batch for internal tracking.']
  batch_processing_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when processing of the batch completed.']
  batch_processing_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when processing of the batch began.']
  batch_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the batch, e.g., CMS reference.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the batch.']
  batch_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the batch was submitted to the system.']
  batch_submission_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of batch submission.']
  batch_success_count varchar [note: 'Number of successful submissions in the batch.']
  batch_success_details varchar [note: 'Details of successful submissions.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Category of the batch indicating the enrollment context.']
  batch_warning_count varchar [note: 'Number of warnings encountered during batch processing.']
  batch_warning_details varchar [note: 'Detailed warning messages for submissions.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when the batch becomes effective.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date when the batch expires or is no longer effective.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the batch.']
  source_system_version varchar [note: 'Version or release of the source system.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all submissions in the batch.']
  total_submissions varchar [note: 'Number of individual submissions included in the batch.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for submission_batch. Referenced by submission_batch_id.'
}

Table enrollment.enrollment_batch {
  enrollment_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for enrollment_batch']
  parent_enrollment_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on enrollment_batch (parent_enrollment_batch_id)']
  batch_approval_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved or rejected the batch.']
  batch_approval_comments varchar [note: 'Comments provided during the approval or rejection of the batch.']
  batch_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the batch in the workflow.']
  batch_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was approved or rejected.']
  batch_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing context about the batch.']
  batch_load_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was loaded into the lakehouse.']
  batch_load_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the load.']
  batch_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to the batch.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable identifier for the enrollment batch, often used in reporting and communication.']
  batch_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the batch for processing order.']
  batch_priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric representation of the batch priority for sorting.']
  batch_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number associated with the batch, used for cross-system traceability.']
  batch_reference_type varchar [note: 'Type of reference used for the batch (e.g., CMS_EDPS, internal).']
  batch_source varchar [note: 'Source of the batch data, such as CMS EDPS or internal system.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch indicating the enrollment process type.']
  batch_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the batch record for change tracking.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the batch becomes effective for enrollment processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the batch ceases to be effective. Nullable for open-ended batches.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors encountered during batch processing.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch has been archived for long-term retention.']
  is_auto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was generated automatically by the system.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch has been logically deleted.']
  is_live boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch is a live production batch.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was created manually by an operator.']
  is_retroactive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch contains retroactive adjustments.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch is a test batch used for validation purposes.']
  processing_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of batch processing in seconds.']
  processing_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when batch processing completed.']
  processing_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when batch processing began.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the batch data.']
  source_system_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch in the originating system.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the batch.']
  success_count varchar [note: 'Number of successful records processed in the batch.']
  total_adjustments varchar [note: 'Number of retroactive adjustment events processed in this batch.']
  total_enrollments varchar [note: 'Number of enrollment events processed in this batch.']
  total_reinstatements varchar [note: 'Number of reinstatement events processed in this batch.']
  total_terminations varchar [note: 'Number of termination events processed in this batch.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the batch record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for enrollment_batch. Referenced by enrollment_batch_id.'
}

Table plan.health_plan {
  health_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the health plan record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for budgeting and expense tracking of each health plan; finance cost center reports aggregate plan costs.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Maps each health plan to its primary general ledger account for recording premiums, claims, and administrative expenses.']
  pbm_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pbm_contract. Business justification: Required for PBM contract management linking each health plan to its pharmacy benefit manager contract for formulary, pricing, and regulatory reporting.']
  pharmacy_formulary_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formulary that defines drug tiering and coverage for the plan.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network associated with the plan.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Required for underwriting and risk‑adjustment calculations linking each health plan to its associated risk pool, used in actuarial models and regulatory reporting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Required for PBM vendor assignment per health plan to manage formulary and meet ACA reporting.']
  benefit_design_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the benefit design configuration.']
  cms_plan_code varchar [note: 'CMS plan identifier used for regulatory reporting.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of allowed amount the member pays after deductible is met.']
  copay_primary_care decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed copayment amount for a primary care office visit.']
  copay_specialist decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed copayment amount for a specialist office visit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_family decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual amount a family must pay before cost‑sharing begins.']
  deductible_individual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual amount an individual member must pay before cost‑sharing begins.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the plan becomes binding for members.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last date members may enroll in the plan for the plan year.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First date members may enroll in the plan for the plan year.']
  hcc_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated HCC score for the plan population used in risk adjustment.']
  hios_plan_code varchar [note: 'CMS‑assigned HIOS identifier for the plan.']
  is_exempt_from_mlr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is exempt from Medical Loss Ratio reporting requirements.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date the plan design was last reviewed for compliance or pricing updates.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Broad business segment the plan belongs to, such as commercial or government.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market for the plan: individual, small group, large group, Medicare, or Medicaid.']
