Records outreach type, outreach date, outreach purpose (directory verification, re-credentialing reminder, contract renewal, network recruitment, data quality correction), outreach method (phone, email, mail, portal, fax, in-person), outreach outcome (reached, confirmed, updated, voicemail, no response, unable to reach, removed from directory), fields verified (address, phone, accepting patients, specialty, hours), staff member conducting outreach, next scheduled verification date, follow-up required flag, and attempt count. Supports CMS quarterly directory accuracy reporting, no-surprise billing compliance, state DOI directory accuracy mandates, provider relations workflows, and regulatory audit trails. This is the authoritative record for demonstrating directory verification compliance during CMS and state audits.'
}

Table provider.participation_status {
  participation_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the provider participation status record.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider contract governing this participation relationship. Links to the provider contract entity.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan associated with this participation status. Links to the plan master entity.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the healthcare provider whose participation status is being tracked. Links to the provider master entity.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network in which the provider participates. Links to the network master entity.']
  accepting_new_patients_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is currently accepting new patients within this network and plan. Critical for provider directory accuracy and member access to care.']
  claims_adjudication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether claims from this provider should be adjudicated as in-network for this plan and network. This is the authoritative flag for claims processing network determination.']
  continuity_of_care_end_date date [note: 'The date through which continuity of care provisions apply for members in active treatment with this provider. Allows in-network benefits to continue for a transition period after provider termination.']
  credentialing_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the provider was approved through the credentialing process for this participation arrangement.']
  credentialing_status varchar [note: 'The current credentialing status of the provider for this participation arrangement. Credentialing approval is a prerequisite for active participation status.']
  current_record_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the current active version of the participation status record. True for the most recent version, false for historical versions.']
  directory_display_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider should be displayed in the member-facing provider directory for this network and plan. May be false during pending credentialing or for providers with restricted panel status.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this participation status becomes effective. Claims with service dates on or after this date are subject to this participation status for network determination.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'The line of business for which this participation status applies. Determines the regulatory framework and benefit structure applicable to this participation relationship.']
  member_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which affected members were notified of the provider participation status change. Required for regulatory compliance with provider termination notification requirements.']
  member_notification_method varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify members of the provider participation status change.']
  network_tier_code varchar [note: 'The network tier assignment for this provider participation. Determines member cost-sharing levels in tiered network products.']
  next_recredentialing_date date [note: 'The date by which the provider must complete recredentialing to maintain active participation status. Typically occurs every 24-36 months per NCQA standards.']
  panel_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the provider panel is open to new member assignments. Closed or restricted panels may still serve existing members but do not accept new PCP assignments.']
  participation_category varchar [note: 'The category of participation indicating the scope of services the provider is authorized to deliver under this arrangement.']
  participation_status_code varchar [note: 'Current participation status of the provider within the network and plan. Active status indicates the provider is eligible for in-network claims adjudication. This is the authoritative field for network participation determination.']
  participation_status_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the participation status code.']
  pcp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is participating as a primary care provider in this network and plan. Determines PCP assignment eligibility for HMO and POS products.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this participation status record was first created in the data platform.']
  record_effective_date date [note: 'The business effective date of this participation status record version. Used for slowly changing dimension type 2 historization.']
  record_end_date date [note: 'The business end date of this participation status record version. Null for the current active version. Used for slowly changing dimension type 2 historization.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this participation status record was last updated in the data platform.']
  regulatory_sanction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is currently under regulatory sanction that affects their participation status. Sanctions may be imposed by state medical boards, CMS, or other regulatory bodies.']
  risk_arrangement_code varchar [note: 'The financial risk arrangement under which the provider participates. Indicates whether the provider is paid fee-for-service, capitation, or under a value-based care model.']
  sanction_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which a regulatory sanction affecting participation status became effective.']
  sanction_end_date date [note: 'The date on which a regulatory sanction affecting participation status is scheduled to end or was lifted.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this participation status record originated. Typically the provider management system such as Cactus or ProviderSource.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this participation status record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  specialist_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is participating as a specialist in this network and plan. Used for network adequacy reporting and referral management.']
  status_change_trigger varchar [note: 'The business event or process that triggered this participation status change. Used for audit trail and root cause analysis of network changes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREDENTIALING_DECISION|CONTRACT_EXECUTION|CONTRACT_TERMINATION|SANCTION_IMPOSED|VOLUNTARY_REQUEST|NETWORK_CHANGE|RECREDENTIALING_OUTCOME — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  telehealth_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider offers telehealth services under this participation arrangement. Required for provider directory display and member access to virtual care.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which this participation status ends. Null for active, ongoing participation. Claims with service dates after this date are subject to out-of-network adjudication unless continuity of care provisions apply.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for participation termination. Required for regulatory reporting and member notification requirements.']
  termination_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the termination reason. Provides additional context beyond the standardized termination reason code.']

  Note: 'Tracks the participation status history of a provider within a specific health plan, network, and line of business (LOB). Records participation status (active, inactive, terminated, suspended, pending credentialing), effective date, termination date, termination reason code, LOB (commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, CHIP, marketplace/QHP), network ID, and status change trigger (credentialing decision, contract termination, sanction, voluntary withdrawal). This is the authoritative record for claims adjudication in-network/out-of-network determination. Supports member notification requirements for provider terminations, continuity of care provisions, and regulatory reporting of network changes.'
}

Table provider.dea_registration {
  dea_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for dea_registration']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: dea_registration must be linked to the core provider entity to avoid silo; provider_npi is redundant once provider_id is present.']
  controlled_substance_authorized_flag boolean [note: 'True if the provider is authorized to prescribe controlled substances.']
  current_record_flag boolean [note: 'True if this row represents the current active registration for the provider.']
  dea_number varchar [note: 'Unique DEA registration number assigned to the provider for controlled substance prescribing.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the DEA registration expires unless renewed.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the DEA registration became effective.']
  issuing_agency varchar [note: 'Agency that issued the DEA registration (typically DEA).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the DEA registration record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any additional comments or remarks about the registration.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DEA registration record was first created in the system.']
  record_end_date date [note: 'Date the record became inactive (e.g., when registration was revoked).']
  registration_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the DEA registration was issued. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AL|AK|AZ|AR|CA|CO|CT|DE|FL|GA|HI|ID|IL|IN|IA|KS|KY|LA|ME|MD|MA|MI|MN|MS|MO|MT|NE|NV|NH|NJ|NM|NY|NC|ND|OH|OK|OR|PA|RI|SC|SD|TN|TX|UT|VT|VA|WA|WV|WI|WY — promote to reference product]']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DEA registration.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the registration is for an individual provider or an organization.']
  schedule_authorized varchar [note: 'DEA schedule(s) the provider is authorized to prescribe (II, III, IV, V).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the DEA registration data (e.g., ProviderSource, Cactus).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the DEA registration record in the source system.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date the DEA registration information was last verified against the primary source.']

  Note: 'Tracks Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) registration records for providers authorized to prescribe controlled substances. Stores DEA registration number, DEA schedules authorized (II, III, IV, V), registration state, issue date, expiration date, registration status (active, expired, revoked, surrendered), and primary source verification date. Required for NCQA credentialing standards for prescribing providers and supports pharmacy benefit management and prior authorization workflows.'
}

Table provider.provider_network_participation {
  provider_network_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the NetworkParticipation association']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the facility']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the provider network']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the facility\'s participation became effective']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification of the facility within the network (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2)']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current in‑network or out‑of‑network status for the facility in this network']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the facility\'s participation ends or is scheduled to end']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual participation of a healthcare facility in a provider network. Each record links one facility to one network and stores attributes that describe the nature of that participation.. Existence Justification: A healthcare facility can belong to multiple provider networks simultaneously, and each provider network includes many facilities. The relationship is actively managed (contracts/agreements) and carries attributes such as participation status and effective/termination dates.'
}

Table provider.obligation_compliance {
  obligation_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the provider_obligation_compliance association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation master record']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the provider master record']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the provider for this obligation']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment for this provider‑obligation pair']

  Note: 'This association captures the compliance relationship between a provider and a regulatory obligation. Each record links one provider to one regulatory obligation and stores compliance_status and last_assessment_date, which are specific to that link.. Existence Justification: Providers must meet many regulatory obligations, and each regulatory obligation applies to many providers. The compliance team actively tracks the compliance status and assessment dates for each provider‑obligation pair, creating a distinct record for every association. This operational process requires a many‑to‑many association with its own attributes.'
}

Table provider.audit_assignment {
  audit_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the provider_audit_assignment association']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audit_engagement']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider_provider']
  audit_period_end date [note: 'End date of the audit period for this provider']
  audit_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the audit period for this provider']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit as it applies to the specific provider']

  Note: 'Association representing the assignment of an audit engagement to a provider, capturing the scope and period specific to that provider.. Existence Justification: A provider can be subject to multiple audit engagements, and each audit engagement can cover multiple providers. The audit‑provider link is actively managed by compliance teams, who assign audits to providers and record scope and period for each association.'
}

Table provider.outreach_campaign {
  outreach_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for outreach_campaign']
  parent_outreach_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on outreach_campaign (parent_outreach_campaign_id)']
  approval_by varchar [note: 'Email address of the user who approved or rejected the campaign.']
  approval_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided during the approval process.']
  approval_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign was approved or rejected.']
  campaign_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the campaign.']
  campaign_budget decimal(15,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for the campaign in the campaign currency.']
  campaign_channel varchar [note: 'Specific channel used for the campaign such as web, mobile app, email, SMS, phone, mail, or social media. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|email|sms|phone|mail|social_media — promote to reference product]']
  campaign_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created.']
  campaign_created_by varchar [note: 'Email address of the user who created the campaign record.']
  campaign_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for budget and spend amounts.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the outreach campaign objectives and scope.']
  campaign_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the campaign is executed or repeated.']
  campaign_goal varchar [note: 'Primary objective or KPI the campaign aims to achieve.']
  campaign_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure campaign performance (e.g., response rate, conversion rate).']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the outreach campaign.']
  campaign_owner varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual responsible for the campaign.']
  campaign_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual spend incurred for the campaign to date.']
  campaign_target_audience varchar [note: 'Broad description of the audience the campaign is intended to reach.']
  campaign_target_audience_age_range varchar [note: 'Age range of the target audience (e.g., 18-25).']
  campaign_target_audience_demographics varchar [note: 'Demographic attributes of the target audience (e.g., age, gender, income).']
  campaign_target_audience_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the target audience characteristics.']
  campaign_target_audience_gender varchar [note: 'Gender distribution of the target audience.']
  campaign_target_audience_geo varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code(s) representing the geographic focus of the campaign.']
  campaign_target_audience_income_range varchar [note: 'Income bracket of the target audience.']
  campaign_target_audience_insurance_type varchar [note: 'Type of insurance coverage held by the target audience.']
  campaign_target_audience_member_since_date date [note: 'Date when the target audience members became part of the insurer\'s system.']
  campaign_target_audience_member_status varchar [note: 'Current membership status of the target audience within the insurer\'s system.']
  campaign_target_audience_plan_type varchar [note: 'Plan tier of the target audience\'s insurance coverage.']
  campaign_target_audience_size varchar [note: 'Estimated number of individuals in the target audience.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Mode of outreach such as email, SMS, phone, mail, in-person, digital, or social media. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|phone|mail|in_person|digital|social_media — promote to reference product]']
  campaign_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  campaign_updated_by varchar [note: 'Email address of the user who last updated the campaign record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the campaign becomes effective and starts reaching the target audience.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the campaign ends or is no longer effective.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign (e.g., active, paused, completed, archived).']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit where the campaign owner is based.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the campaign owner.']
  owner_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the campaign owner.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Role or title of the campaign owner within the organization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for outreach_campaign. Referenced by campaign_id.'
}

Table claim.header {
  header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for header']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Needed for account‑level statements that combine premium billing and claim charges, a core reporting requirement for member cost‑sharing and financial reconciliation.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Audit engagements review specific claim sets; linking claim to audit engagement enables audit scope reports and compliance tracking.']
  care_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_enrollment. Business justification: Supports enrollment‑specific claim tracking required by regulatory reporting (e.g., CMS program‑level cost reporting) linking claim to the exact enrollment record.']
  care_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_plan. Business justification: Required for Care Plan Cost Attribution report, linking each claim to the member\'s active care plan to evaluate plan effectiveness and compliance.']
  claim_adjustment_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking related claim adjustments.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory reporting process requires each claim to be linked to the specific regulatory obligation it satisfies for CMS/State filings.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to reference the specific provider contract governing fees; essential for contract performance reporting and compliance audits.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest status change.']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Needed to tie each claim to the eligibility span governing coverage, enabling eligibility checks and regulatory reporting of claim eligibility periods.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Required for employer‑sponsored plan cost reporting and ACA employer reporting; each claim must be attributed to the employer group that provided the coverage.']
  header_claiming_provider_provider_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Required for network participation and quality reporting; each claim must be linked to the provider entity for regulatory and performance analytics.']
  header_rendering_provider_id varchar [note: 'FK to provider.provider.provider_id — Every claim must resolve to a rendering provider for network status determination, fee schedule lookup, and provider payment. This is the second most critical operational join in health insurance.']
  inpatient_admission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.inpatient_admission. Business justification: Required for Admission‑to‑Claim reconciliation report; claims are generated from inpatient admissions, so linking each claim to its admission record is standard practice.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member (patient) associated with the claim.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Actuarial claim cost analysis and risk‑adjusted reporting require each claim to reference the member\'s risk score at service time.']
  plan_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.plan_service_area. Business justification: Network adequacy and service‑area compliance reports need to map each claim to the plan’s service area where the service was rendered.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_invoice. Business justification: Links each claim to the premium invoice covering the member at service time, essential for ACA reporting and premium‑vs‑claim reconciliation processes.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_program. Business justification: Needed for Program ROI analysis; associates each claim with the care program enrollment at service time for financial and outcome reporting.']
  provider_credential_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Needed for Claim Intake Eligibility Check: validates rendering provider\'s credential status before claim acceptance.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to apply network‑specific contracts and cost‑share rules; claim processors need to know the provider network for each claim.']
  provider_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.party. Business justification: Needed to associate each claim with the contracting party (provider organization) for regulatory reporting and payment reconciliation.']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Regulatory submissions aggregate claims; associating claim with its submission supports submission tracking and audit of filing completeness.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Regulatory and solvency reporting aggregates claims by risk pool; linking claim to its pool enables accurate financial and compliance analysis.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'FK to member.subscriber.subscriber_id — The claim-to-member join is the single most critical operational query in health insurance. Every claim must resolve to a member for eligibility verification, accumulator updates, and EOB generation. ']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to reference the exact enrollment transaction that established coverage at service date, used in CMS audit and eligibility verification reports.']
  um_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_case. Business justification: Needed for Case Management Dashboard; each claim must be tied to the utilization case that governs its clinical and financial review.']
  year_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.plan_year. Business justification: Regulatory reporting and rate filing require associating each claim with the specific plan year under which coverage was provided.']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the claim includes any adjustments.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date of patient admission for inpatient claims.']
  allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the insurer permits for the services.']
  billed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount billed to the insurer before adjustments.']
  billing_provider_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the provider responsible for billing.']
  billing_type varchar [note: 'Method of billing for the claim.']
  claim_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the primary service rendered for the claim.']
  claim_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of line detail records associated with this claim.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Unique claim identifier assigned by the insurer.']
  claim_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the claim submission.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the claim.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Category of claim based on service type.']
  claim_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the claim for tracking revisions.']
  cob_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether coordination of benefits processing is required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim header record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the reason for claim denial, if applicable.']
  diagnosis_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of diagnosis codes associated with the claim.']
  discharge_date date [note: 'Date of patient discharge for inpatient claims.']
  drg_code varchar [note: 'DRG assignment for inpatient claims.']
  is_hipaa_compliant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the claim data complies with HIPAA regulations.']
  is_mlr_excluded boolean [note: 'Indicates if the claim is excluded from MLR calculations.']
  is_suspended boolean [note: 'Indicates if the claim is currently suspended pending additional information.']
  length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of days between admission and discharge.']
  lob varchar [note: 'Business line to which the claim belongs.']
  paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount paid to the provider after adjustments.']
  place_of_service_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating where the service was provided.']
  prior_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for prior authorization, if required.']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'Previous status before the most recent transition.']
  procedure_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of procedure codes billed on the claim.']
  referral_provider_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the referring provider, if applicable.']
  sla_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the claim must be processed to meet service level agreement.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the claim met the SLA deadline.']
  status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status transition.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the claim was suspended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim header record.']

  Note: 'Core master record and lifecycle hub for every submitted health insurance claim — professional (CMS-1500/837P), institutional (UB-04/837I), dental (ADA/837D), and behavioral health. Captures claim-level identifiers (ICN), claim type, bill type, place of service, financial totals (billed/allowed/paid), adjudication status with full status transition history (prior status, new status, timestamp, reason, changed-by), service dates, COB indicator, LOB, plan and member identifiers, provider NPIs (rendering, billing, facility, attending, referring), DRG assignment, admission details, claim source (EDI/paper/portal), adjustment linkage, pend/suspend reason tracking, SLA monitoring dates, and HIPAA compliance flags. Serves as the central hub linking all claim-related detail records.'
}

Table claim.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for line']
  auth_service_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.auth_service_line. Business justification: Audit of Prior Authorization compliance; claim line references the authorized service line to verify authorized quantity, price, and status.']
  benefit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.benefit. Business justification: Adjudication uses benefit definitions to determine coverage and cost sharing for each claim line; linking claim line to plan.benefit is required for accurate payment calculation.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent claim to which this line belongs.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Each claim line maps to a specific ledger account for revenue recognition; required for daily financial reporting and cost allocation.']
  provider_credential_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Enables Line‑Level Credential Verification Process: ensures each line\'s service provider is credentialed for reimbursement.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Line‑level provider attribution enables detailed utilization, cost, and service‑type analysis required by utilization review and case‑mix reporting.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_tier. Business justification: Needed for patient billing statements; tier determines deductible, copay, and coinsurance for each claim line in network‑based cost‑share calculations.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Vendor cost analysis report requires linking each claim line to the service vendor for expense tracking and compliance auditing.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any adjustment applied to the line after initial adjudication.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for the line adjustment.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date the patient was admitted (applicable to institutional claims).']
  allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the payer agrees to pay for the line.']
  anesthesia_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes of anesthesia administered for the line (if applicable).']
  billed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Charged amount submitted to the payer for the line.']
  cob_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount paid by secondary/tertiary payers under COB processing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why the line was denied.']
  discharge_date date [note: 'Date the patient was discharged (applicable to institutional claims).']
  emergency_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the service was rendered as an emergency.']
  epsdt_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the service qualifies for EPSDT coverage (for Medicaid).']
  family_planning_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the service is related to family planning.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the service rendered.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the claim.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the claim line.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Category of service rendered on the line.']
  modifier_1 varchar [note: 'First two‑character modifier associated with the procedure code.']
  modifier_2 varchar [note: 'Second two‑character modifier associated with the procedure code.']
  modifier_3 varchar [note: 'Third two‑character modifier associated with the procedure code.']
  modifier_4 varchar [note: 'Fourth two‑character modifier associated with the procedure code.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for drug products when the line represents a pharmacy claim.']
  paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount actually paid by the payer for the line.']
  patient_responsibility_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of copay, coinsurance, and deductible amounts owed by the member.']
  place_of_service_code varchar [note: 'Two‑digit code indicating where the service was provided.']
  prior_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the prior authorization associated with the line.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Standard procedure code representing the service rendered.']
  remark_code varchar [note: 'Additional informational code attached to the line.']
  revenue_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code used for institutional billing to indicate type of service.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the service was provided.']
  units_of_service decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quantity of the service rendered (e.g., number of visits, dosage units).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each service rendered within a claim. Captures line number, CPT/HCPCS procedure code, procedure modifier codes (up to 4), revenue code (institutional), service date, units of service, billed amount per line, allowed amount, paid amount, copay amount, coinsurance amount, deductible amount, COB paid amount, line status, denial reason code, remark codes, place of service code, rendering provider NPI at line level, NDC code (for drug lines), quantity qualifier, anesthesia minutes, emergency indicator, EPSDT indicator, family planning indicator, and prior authorization number.'
}

Table claim.diagnosis {
  diagnosis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for diagnosis']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim to which this diagnosis belongs.']
  risk_hcc_mapping_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.hcc_mapping. Business justification: Clinical quality and risk‑adjustment reports need each diagnosis linked to its HCC mapping for proper risk score calculations.']
  chronic_condition_flag boolean [note: 'True if the diagnosis represents a chronic condition relevant for care management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the diagnosis line was initially loaded into the data lake.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Official diagnosis code assigned to the claim line.']
  diagnosis_date date [note: 'Calendar date the diagnosis was documented on the claim.']
  diagnosis_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the diagnosis code.']
  diagnosis_status varchar [note: 'Operational status indicating if the diagnosis line is active, pending review, or logically deleted.']
  diagnosis_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the role of the diagnosis (e.g., principal, admitting, external cause, other).']
  drg_code varchar [note: 'DRG (Diagnosis Related Group) code assigned for inpatient claims.']
  drg_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the DRG code.']
  hcc_category varchar [note: 'HCC category code used for risk‑adjustment and RAF calculations.']
  icd_version varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the diagnosis code follows ICD‑9‑CM or ICD‑10‑CM standards.']
  line_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of times the same diagnosis appears on the claim; typically 1.']
  poa_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates if the condition was present at the time of admission (Y=Yes, N=No, U=Unknown).']
  qualifier varchar [note: 'Free‑text qualifier providing extra context (e.g., laterality, severity).']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric factor used in risk‑adjustment calculations for the diagnosis.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the diagnosis on the claim (1‑n).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the diagnosis information.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the diagnosis line.']

  Note: 'Diagnosis code assignments at the claim level supporting ICD-10-CM coding. Captures claim ICN, diagnosis sequence number, ICD version (ICD-10/ICD-9), diagnosis code, diagnosis description, diagnosis type (principal, admitting, external cause, other), POA (Present on Admission) indicator, HCC category mapping, chronic condition flag, and diagnosis qualifier. Supports DRG grouping, risk adjustment (HCC/RAF), HEDIS measure attribution, and clinical analytics.'
}

Table claim.procedure {
  procedure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for procedure']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent claim to which this procedure belongs.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.service_area. Business justification: Supports network adequacy compliance reporting by linking procedure location to the defined service area of the provider network.']
  service_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the procedure was performed (e.g., hospital NPI or internal location code).']
  surgeon_credential_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Required for Provider Credential Verification Report: ensures surgeon\'s credentials are valid for each procedure billed.']
  surgeon_provider_provider_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Links surgeon NPI to provider entity for surgeon performance, outcome, and credentialing dashboards mandated by quality‑measure reporting.']
  anesthesia_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes of anesthesia administered for the procedure.']
  billing_category varchar [note: 'Category used for billing and reimbursement rules.']
  charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Billed amount for the procedure before adjustments.']
  claim_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the adjustment applied to the procedure charge.']
  claim_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for any post‑adjudication adjustment applied to this procedure.']
  claim_line_number varchar [note: 'Line number of this procedure within the claim file.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the claim line containing this procedure.']
  code_system varchar [note: 'The coding system used for the procedure_code field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  diagnosis_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑delimited list of primary diagnosis codes (ICD‑10‑CM) associated with the procedure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: multiple codes — promote to reference product]']
  documentation_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates completeness of clinical documentation for the procedure.']
  drg_code varchar [note: 'DRG assignment for the inpatient case derived from the procedure and diagnoses.']
  drg_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Relative weight of the DRG used for reimbursement calculations.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'True if the procedure was performed under emergency conditions.']
  is_outpatient boolean [note: 'True if the procedure was performed in an outpatient setting.']
  is_primary_procedure boolean [note: 'True if this is the principal procedure for the claim.']
  laterality varchar [note: 'Anatomical side on which the procedure was performed.']
  modifier varchar [note: 'Additional qualifier or modifier associated with the procedure code (e.g., bilateral, emergency).']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final reimbursable amount after adjustments, discounts, and contractual allowances.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Clinician‑entered free‑text comments about the procedure.']
  prior_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization request associated with the procedure, if any.']
  prior_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the prior authorization request.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the medical procedure performed.']
  procedure_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the procedure was performed.']
  procedure_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the procedure record.']
  procedure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the procedure was performed, captured to the second.']
  quality_measure_flag boolean [note: 'True if the procedure contributes to a quality measure (e.g., HEDIS).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the procedure record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the procedure record.']
  revenue_code varchar [note: 'Hospital revenue code associated with the procedure for institutional claims.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor used for risk‑adjusted payment calculations.']
  secondary_procedure_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑delimited list of additional procedure codes related to the primary procedure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: multiple codes — promote to reference product]']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the procedure within the claim (1‑based).']
  service_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the place where the procedure occurred.']
  surgeon_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the surgeon who performed the procedure.']
  units_of_service varchar [note: 'Number of units billed for the procedure (e.g., time‑based units).']

  Note: 'Procedure code assignments at the claim header level (distinct from line-level CPT). Captures principal procedure code (ICD-10-PCS for inpatient), procedure date, procedure sequence, surgeon NPI, procedure qualifier, and secondary procedure codes. Supports DRG assignment, surgical case management, and inpatient utilization analytics. Primarily applicable to 837I institutional claims.'
}

Table claim.adjudication {
  adjudication_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the adjudication record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the claim that this adjudication decision applies to.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that performed the adjudication.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the benefit plan under which the claim was submitted.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: GL posting of adjudication results requires a journal entry; finance team uses journal_entry to record each adjudication for audit and reporting.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the insured member associated with the claim.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the treating provider.']
  accumulator_deductible_impact decimal(12,2) [note: 'Impact on the member\'s deductible accumulator from this adjudication.']
  accumulator_oop_impact decimal(12,2) [note: 'Impact on the member\'s out‑of‑pocket accumulator.']
  adjudication_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the adjudication for audit and communication purposes.']
  adjudication_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the adjudication decision.']
  adjudication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjudication decision was generated.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date the patient was admitted (for institutional claims).']
  allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the plan permits for the service after contracts and fee schedules.']
  allowed_amount_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the allowed amount.']
  auto_adjudication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim was processed automatically without manual review.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Category of the claim based on service setting.']
  cob_adjusted_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount adjusted after coordination of benefits.']
  cob_processing_result varchar [note: 'Result of COB processing for the claim.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the member\'s deductible applied by this adjudication.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Primary diagnosis code associated with the claim.']
  discharge_date date [note: 'Date the patient was discharged (for institutional claims).']
  duplicate_claim_flag boolean [note: 'True if the claim is identified as a potential duplicate.']
  edit_bypass_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when an edit was bypassed.']
  edit_code varchar [note: 'Code of the clinical or administrative edit applied (e.g., NCCI, OCE).']
  edit_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the edit.']
  edit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the edit evaluation.']
  edit_override_authority varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that overrode the edit.']
  edit_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the edit result was manually overridden.']
  edit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the edit was evaluated.']
  medical_necessity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service met medical necessity criteria.']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Network relationship of the provider for this claim.']
  oop_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Member out‑of‑pocket responsibility after this adjudication.']
  prior_authorization_required boolean [note: 'True if the service required prior authorization.']
  prior_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the prior authorization request.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjudication record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the adjudication record.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the service was rendered.']
  timeliness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim was filed within the required time window.']
  total_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all adjustments (deductibles, coinsurance, contractual) applied.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate charge amount submitted on the claim before any adjustments.']
  total_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount payable to the provider after all adjustments.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of the adjudication decision for each claim and claim line, capturing the full pre-payment processing lifecycle including all clinical and administrative edit results. Includes adjudication status (paid/denied/pended/suspended/voided), auto-adjudication flag, adjudicator ID, benefit plan applied, accumulator impacts (deductible/OOP), COB processing results, allowed amount calculation method (fee schedule/MAC/RBP/capitation), network status, medical necessity determination, and final disposition. Captures granular edit-level results (NCCI/OCE/LCD/NCD/plan-specific/timely filing/duplicate) with edit code, edit description, pass/fail/bypass/override outcomes per line, bypass reason, and override authority. Supports coding compliance, payment accuracy, and CMS edit mandate adherence.'
}

Table claim.eob {
  eob_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the EOB record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claim.header. Business justification: EOB is generated for a specific claim; linking to claim header provides the parent relationship']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the covered member associated with the claim.']
  provider_credential_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Required for EOB Generation Rule: includes credential status of provider on explanation of benefits for compliance.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary subscriber (often the employee or policyholder).']
  allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the plan permits for the service.']
  appeal_rights_text varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the member\'s right to appeal the decision.']
  appeal_rights_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the appeal rights language used.']
  billed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount billed by the provider before any adjustments.']
  claim_icn varchar [note: 'Unique claim identifier used throughout the adjudication process.']
  cob_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount adjusted due to coordination of benefits with other payers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the EOB record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date the EOB was actually delivered to the recipient.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the EOB to the member or provider.']
  denial_reason_summary varchar [note: 'Brief text explaining why a claim or line was denied.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External document identifier assigned to the EOB for tracking and reference.']
  eob_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EOB record.']
  eob_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the EOB is intended for the member or the provider.']
  generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the EOB was generated by the claims engine.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑2 language code indicating the language of the EOB content.']
  member_responsibility_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the claim the member must pay (deductible, copay, coinsurance, or non‑covered).']
  member_responsibility_type varchar [note: 'Category of the member\'s financial responsibility for the claim.']
  plan_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount paid by the health plan to the provider.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the provider who rendered the service.']
  provider_npi varchar [note: 'Unique national identifier for the provider.']
  remark_codes varchar [note: 'Standardized codes providing additional claim processing information.']
  sbc_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the applicable Summary of Benefits and Coverage document.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Last date of service covered by the claim.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'First date of service covered by the claim.']
  suppression_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EOB should be suppressed from member delivery (e.g., for zero‑payment claims).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the EOB record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for revisions of the EOB.']

  Note: 'Explanation of Benefits document record generated for members and providers following claim adjudication. Captures EOB document ID, EOB type (member/provider), generation date, delivery method (mail/portal/EDI 835), member ID, subscriber ID, claim ICN, service dates, provider name, billed amount, allowed amount, plan paid amount, member responsibility (deductible, copay, coinsurance, non-covered), COB adjustment, denial reason summary, remark codes, appeal rights language version, SBC reference, and EOB suppression flag. Supports member transparency and ACA SBC compliance.'
}

Table claim.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for adjustment']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Adjustment records are created after an appeal decision; linking to the appeal case provides audit trail and regulatory reporting of appeal‑driven adjustments.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original claim to which this adjustment applies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that initiated the adjustment.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Financial adjustments to claims are posted as journal entries; linking enables accurate GL impact tracking and regulatory audit.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claim.line. Business justification: Adjustment may apply to a specific claim line; replace claim_line_number with a proper FK to claim.line']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the claim adjustment.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider linked to the adjusted claim.']
  adjusted_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount after the adjustment is applied.']
  adjustment_code varchar [note: 'Internal code categorizing the adjustment for reporting.']
  adjustment_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment was recorded or became effective.']
  adjustment_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why and how the adjustment was made.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the adjustment.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the adjustment indicating its nature.']
  audit_finding_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit finding or case that triggered the adjustment.']
  audit_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the audit finding (e.g., internal, RAC, OIG, provider self‑disclosure).']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Type of audit that triggered the adjustment.']
  compliance_60_day_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment complies with the CMS 60‑day overpayment recoupment rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  demand_lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of the demand/collection lifecycle for the adjustment.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD diagnosis code(s) associated with the claim adjustment, if relevant.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the adjustment takes effect.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the adjustment ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  identifier varchar [note: 'External or business-facing identifier for the adjustment (e.g., RAC audit number).']
  initiator_role varchar [note: 'Business role of the initiator.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Flag identifying the adjustment as a duplicate payment correction.']
  is_fraud boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment is related to suspected fraud.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment reverses a prior adjustment.']
  is_void boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment voids the original claim payment.']
  net_adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between original and adjusted amounts (positive for overpayment recovery, negative for additional payment).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by the adjuster.']
  original_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount originally paid on the claim before any adjustment.']
  overpayment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the adjustment relates to an overpayment.']
  overpayment_type varchar [note: 'Specific category of overpayment being corrected.']
  prior_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the prior authorization linked to the claim, if applicable.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Procedure code(s) related to the adjusted claim line.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the business reason for the adjustment.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recover the overpayment.']
  recovery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recovery process.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True when the adjustment must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., CMS RAC).']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Overall resolution state of the adjustment case.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of adjustments applied to the same claim.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the adjustment record.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all post-adjudication corrections, recoveries, and audit findings — including claim adjustments, voids, reprocessing events, overpayment identification and recovery, and post-payment audit results for payment integrity and FWA detection. Captures adjustment type (void/replacement/corrected/retroactive), overpayment type (duplicate payment/incorrect rate/ineligible member/billing error/fraud), audit type (post-payment/prepayment/clinical/coding/billing), audit source (internal/RAC/OIG/provider self-disclosure), financial impact (original vs adjusted amounts, net adjustment, overpayment amount, recovery amounts, demand lifecycle), initiator, CMS 60-day overpayment rule compliance, recovery method (offset/check/installment), recovery status, and resolution status. Supports payment integrity programs, CMS RAC audit compliance, and regulatory overpayment reporting.'
}

Table claim.denial {
  denial_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the denial record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the claim associated with this denial.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the resolution action.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claim.line. Business justification: Denial may apply to a specific claim line; replace claim_line_number with a proper FK to claim.line']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose claim was denied.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  appeal_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the denial is eligible for an appeal.']
  carc_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the reason for the claim adjustment/denial.']
  clinical_criteria varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical guideline or utilization management criteria applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the denial record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  denial_date date [note: 'Date the denial decision was rendered.']
  denial_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the denial, used in communications and reporting.']
  denial_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the denial.']
  denial_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the denial reason.']
  denied_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Adjustments (e.g., contractual allowances, discounts) applied to the denied amount.']
  denied_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original amount of the claim that was denied before any adjustments.']
  denied_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final net amount after adjustments that the member is responsible for due to the denial.']
  letter_generated_flag boolean [note: 'True if a formal denial letter was generated and sent to the member/provider.']
  medical_necessity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim failed medical necessity criteria (true = not medically necessary).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by reviewers or staff during denial handling.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'True if the denial was manually overridden by a reviewer.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the denial was overridden.']
  rac_code varchar [note: 'Standardized remark code that provides additional context for the denial.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why the claim or line was denied.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the denial was resolved or closed.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the denial resolution process.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the denial record (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  subtype varchar [note: 'More granular category within the denial type, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the denial record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of claim and claim line denials, capturing denial reason codes, clinical rationale, and resolution tracking. Includes denial ID, claim ICN, line number (if line-level denial), denial date, denial type (clinical, administrative, technical, COB, timely filing), CARC code, RARC code, denial reason description, clinical criteria applied (InterQual/MCG), medical necessity determination, denial letter generated flag, appeal eligibility flag, appeal deadline date, denial override flag, override reason, and resolution status. Supports appeals workflow, regulatory reporting, and denial management analytics.'
}

Table claim.cob {
  cob_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cob']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claim.header. Business justification: COB processing is performed per claim; linking to claim header enables traceability']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the user who performed the COB processing.']
  primary_payer_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the primary insurance payer.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the adjustment applied to the net liability.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any monetary adjustment applied during COB processing.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ETL batch that loaded this COB record.']
  claim_icn varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the underlying claim that this COB record relates to.']
  claim_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items on the underlying claim.']
  cob_method varchar [note: 'Method used for coordination of benefits: standard, non‑duplication, or maintenance of benefits.']
  cob_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the COB record.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the processor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the COB record was first created in the system.']
  crossover_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim crosses over between primary and secondary payer responsibilities.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for monetary values (e.g., USD).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the COB processing became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the COB record expires or is no longer valid (nullable).']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this COB record was generated for a duplicate claim.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'True if a user manually overrode automated COB decisions.']
  medicaid_crossover_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag showing if Medicaid is involved in a crossover scenario.']
  msp_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether Medicare is acting as the secondary payer for this claim.']
  msp_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of Medicare secondary payer relationship (full or partial).']
  net_liability_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Remaining liability amount after primary and secondary payments and adjustments.']
  other_insurance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the secondary insurance when it is not a standard payer (e.g., employer group, Medicare, Medicaid, auto, workers\' compensation).']
  primary_payer_allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the primary payer allowed for the claim before COB.']
  primary_payer_denial_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description why the primary payer denied all or part of the claim.']
  primary_payer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the primary insurance payer.']
  primary_payer_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount actually paid by the primary payer after adjudication.']
  process_order varchar [note: 'Sequential order in which multiple payer COB steps are applied for the claim.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the COB processing was completed.']
  processing_user_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the user who performed the COB processing.']
  secondary_payer_allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the secondary payer is allowed to pay for the claim.']
  secondary_payer_code varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the secondary insurance payer.']
  secondary_payer_denial_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for any denial by the secondary payer.']
  secondary_payer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the secondary insurance payer.']
  secondary_payer_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount paid by the secondary payer after COB processing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating operational system (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  total_allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of allowed amounts for all claim lines as determined by the primary payer.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate charge amount from all claim lines before any adjustments.']
  total_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by the primary payer before secondary adjudication.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the COB record.']

  Note: 'Coordination of Benefits processing record for claims involving multiple payers. Captures COB record ID, claim ICN, primary payer ID, primary payer name, primary payer paid amount, primary payer allowed amount, primary payer denial reason, secondary payer ID, COB processing order, COB method (standard/non-duplication/maintenance of benefits), other insurance type (employer group/Medicare/Medicaid/auto/workers comp), Medicare as secondary payer (MSP) indicator, MSP type, crossover claim flag, Medicaid crossover indicator, and net liability after COB. Supports accurate secondary adjudication and CMS MSP compliance.'
}

Table claim.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Claim payments are disbursed through designated bank accounts; linking supports cash‑management reconciliation and audit trails.']
  outcome_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_outcome. Business justification: Payments may be adjusted based on appeal outcomes; linking to the appeal outcome enables accurate reconciliation and compliance reporting of appeal‑related payment changes.']
  payment_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'FK to claim.header']
  payment_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim to which this payment applies.']
  payment_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the entity receiving the payment (provider NPI or member ID).']
  payment_payer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity funding the payment (usually the health plan).']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all adjustments (CARC/RARC) applied to the gross amount.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch in which this payment was processed.']
  batch_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence order of the payment within its batch.']
  carc_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of CARC codes applied to the claim payment.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Delivery channel for the payment transaction.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Check number when payment method is a paper check.']
  clearinghouse_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the EDI clearinghouse that processed the 835 transaction.']
  clearinghouse_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the clearinghouse (e.g., Availity, Change Healthcare).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment.']
  cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency with which the payment is issued.']
  gl_account_number varchar [note: 'General ledger account to which the payment is posted.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date the payment entry was posted to the general ledger.']
  gl_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking the payment to the GL transaction.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments or taxes.']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment has been fully reconciled.']
  is_returned boolean [note: 'True if the payment was returned by the bank or clearinghouse.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'True if the payment was voided after issuance.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the payee after adjustments.']
  note varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes related to the payment.']
  payee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payee: provider, member, or other.']
  payment_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was issued to the payee.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the payment purpose.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to disburse the payment.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'External payment reference number used in remittance advice and provider communications.']
  payment_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the payment originated from internal systems or an external entity.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment (e.g., claim settlement, capitation offset).']
  racr_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of RARC codes providing additional adjustment details.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why the payment was made (e.g., claim settlement, rebate).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment\'s financial reconciliation.']
  returned_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the payment was returned.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax withheld or applied to the payment, if applicable.']
  trace_number varchar [note: 'Bank‑provided trace number for tracking the payment.']
  transaction_control_number varchar [note: 'Unique control number for the 835 remittance advice transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the payment was voided.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all claim payment disbursements and electronic remittance advice (835 ERA) transmitted to providers and members. Captures payee information (NPI/TIN/member ID), payment amount, payment date, payment method (EFT/check/virtual card/capitation offset), check/EFT trace number, payment status lifecycle (issued/cleared/voided/returned/stopped), 835 transaction control number, clearinghouse ID (Availity, Change Healthcare), CARC/RARC adjustment codes at claim level, claim-level payment detail, reconciliation status, and GL posting reference. Consolidates provider remittance advice and claim payment into a single authoritative record for payment reconciliation, cash posting, and provider inquiry resolution.'
}

Table claim.ibnr {
  ibnr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ibnr']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the actuary responsible for the reserve estimate.']
  actuarial_method varchar [note: 'Actuarial technique used to calculate the IBNR reserve.']
  completion_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor representing the proportion of ultimate loss expected to be reported.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reserve record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  development_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor applied to historical development to project IBNR.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the reserve becomes effective for reporting purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the reserve ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended reserves).']
  estimated_ibnr_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actuarial estimate of incurred‑but‑not‑reported liability.']
  ibnr_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or notes for the reserve.']
  ibnr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IBNR reserve record.']
  incurred_month date [note: 'Month in which the underlying claims were incurred.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the reserve applies (e.g., Medical, Dental).']
  mlr_numerator_allocation decimal(10,4) [note: 'Portion of the IBNR reserve allocated to the Medical Loss Ratio numerator.']
  paid_to_date_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount paid on the claim cohort to date.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of health plan associated with the reserve.']
  prior_period_reserve_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'IBNR reserve amount from the previous reporting period.']
  reserve_change_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between current and prior period reserve amounts.']
  reserve_period_month varchar [note: 'Month (1‑12) of the reserve reporting period.']
  reserve_period_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the reserve reporting period.']
  reserve_run_date date [note: 'Date on which the IBNR reserve calculation was performed.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Category of services covered by the reserve.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reserve record.']

  Note: 'Incurred But Not Reported (IBNR) reserve estimates at the claim cohort level, supporting actuarial reserving and MLR reporting. Captures reserve ID, reserve period (month/year), LOB, plan type, service category, incurred month, paid-to-date amount, estimated IBNR amount, development factor applied, completion factor, actuarial method (chain-ladder/Bornhuetter-Ferguson), reserve run date, actuary ID, prior period reserve amount, reserve change amount, and MLR numerator allocation. Interfaces with Milliman MG-ALFA and Oracle Financials for statutory and GAAP reporting.'
}

Table claim.subrogation {
  subrogation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subrogation record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with this subrogation.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insured member whose claim is being subrogated.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the attorney representing the insurer in the subrogation.']
  accident_city varchar [note: 'City of the accident location.']
  accident_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the accident location.']
  accident_date date [note: 'Date on which the loss event occurred.']
  accident_state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the accident occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the record was first inserted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  demand_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount requested in the initial demand to the third‑party insurer.']
  demand_sent_date date [note: 'Date the demand letter was sent to the third‑party insurer.']
  gross_recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount claimed from the third‑party before deductions.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subrogation case was created in the system.']
  is_lien_applied boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a lien has been applied to the recovery.']
  is_recovery_closed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the subrogation case is closed and no further action is expected.']
  is_settlement_agreed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the parties have reached a settlement agreement.']
  legal_fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Attorney and legal costs associated with the subrogation.']
  liability_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the liability relationship with the third party.']
  lien_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of any lien placed on the recovery.']
  lien_holder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity holding a lien on the recovery.']
  lien_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the lien.']
  negotiation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal negotiations began.']
  net_recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount recovered after legal fees and other adjustments.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain the recovery (e.g., direct payment, offset against claim, court judgment).']
  recovery_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing negotiations, special conditions, or observations.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subrogation must be reported to state regulators.']
  reporting_state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter code of the state for regulatory reporting purposes.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount agreed upon in the settlement of the subrogation.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date the subrogation settlement was finalized.']
  subrogation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the subrogation case.']
  subrogation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subrogation case.']
  subrogation_type varchar [note: 'Category of third‑party liability (e.g., auto accident, workers\' compensation).']
  third_party_insurer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third‑party insurer or liable party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Third-party liability (TPL) and subrogation recovery tracking for claims where another party bears financial responsibility — auto accidents, workers compensation, premises liability, and other TPL scenarios.
