Captures subrogation type, third-party insurer details, accident information, demand and recovery lifecycle (identified → demand sent → negotiating → settled → closed), attorney/lien details, recovery amounts, and net recovery after legal fees. Supports recovery revenue optimization, LAE offset, and state-mandated TPL reporting.'
}

Table claim.drg {
  drg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for drg']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim to which this DRG assignment belongs.']
  arithmetic_mean_los decimal(5,2) [note: 'Arithmetic mean length of stay for the DRG across the reference population.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the DRG assignment.']
  base_rate_applied decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base payment rate applied to the DRG before adjustments.']
  case_mix_index_contribution decimal(10,5) [note: 'Contribution of this DRG to the provider\'s overall case mix index.']
  cc_mcc_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the claim has no CC/MCC, a CC, or an MCC.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DRG assignment record was first created.']
  drg_code varchar [note: 'Three- or four‑digit Diagnosis Related Group code assigned to the claim.']
  drg_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the DRG code.']
  drg_type varchar [note: 'Category of the DRG based on care setting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the DRG assignment becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the DRG assignment expires, if applicable.']
  geometric_mean_los decimal(5,2) [note: 'Geometric mean of length of stay for the DRG across the reference population.']
  grouper_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to generate the DRG (e.g., MS‑DRG, APR‑DRG).']
  grouper_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the DRG grouper was executed for this claim.']
  grouper_software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the software that performed the DRG grouping.']
  grouper_status varchar [note: 'Processing status of the grouper run for this assignment.']
  line_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of DRG units represented by this line (typically 1).']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequence order of the DRG assignment within the claim (normally 1).']
  major_diagnostic_category varchar [note: 'Broad diagnostic category (MDC) associated with the DRG.']
  outlier_payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional payment made when the claim exceeds the outlier threshold.']
  outlier_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost threshold above which an outlier payment is triggered.']
  payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Reimbursable amount calculated for the DRG before any claim‑level adjustments.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the DRG payment amount.']
  principal_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD‑10‑CM code of the principal diagnosis used for DRG assignment.']
  principal_procedure_code varchar [note: 'CPT or HCPCS code of the principal procedure influencing DRG assignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the DRG assignment record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version of the DRG grouper used (e.g., MS-DRG v38, APR-DRG v30).']
  weight decimal(10,5) [note: 'Relative weight used to calculate reimbursement for the DRG.']

  Note: 'Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) assignment record for inpatient institutional claims, capturing the DRG grouper output. Includes DRG assignment ID, claim ICN, DRG version (MS-DRG/APR-DRG/IR-DRG), DRG code, DRG description, MDC (Major Diagnostic Category), DRG weight, geometric mean LOS, arithmetic mean LOS, base rate applied, outlier threshold, outlier payment amount, case mix index contribution, grouper software version, grouper run date, principal diagnosis used, principal procedure used, and complication/comorbidity (CC/MCC) flag. Supports inpatient reimbursement, case mix management, and utilization benchmarking.'
}

Table claim.accumulator {
  accumulator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for accumulator']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Benefit accumulators are allocated to cost centers for budgeting and MLR calculations; linking provides traceability of utilization costs.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan governing the benefit limits.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom the accumulator applies.']
  accumulated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount that has been applied to the accumulator to date.']
  accumulator_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the accumulator, used in reporting and member communications.']
  accumulator_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form description or notes about the accumulator record.']
  accumulator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accumulator record.']
  accumulator_type varchar [note: 'Type of benefit being tracked (e.g., deductible, out‑of‑pocket maximum).']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text or coded reason describing why an adjustment was applied.']
  benefit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the benefit tracked by the accumulator.']
  benefit_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the benefit period for which the accumulator is calculated.']
  benefit_period_start date [note: 'First day of the benefit period for which the accumulator is calculated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accumulator record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent claim event that modified the accumulator.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the latest change to the accumulator was an adjustment (e.g., post‑payment correction).']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'True if the most recent transaction reversed a prior accumulator entry.']
  last_reset_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent reset of the accumulator (e.g., annual renewal).']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed for the accumulator in the benefit period.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for the member\'s coverage.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Network tier defining provider cost-sharing rules for the accumulator.']
  remaining_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount remaining before the member reaches the benefit limit.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for a reversal operation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the accumulator record (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the accumulator record.']

  Note: 'Member-level benefit accumulator ledger tracking deductibles, out-of-pocket maximums (MOOP), visit limits, and other benefit thresholds applied during claims adjudication. Captures current balances (accumulated amount, remaining balance, benefit limit) by member, benefit period, accumulator type, LOB, plan, and network tier, plus the full transaction-level audit trail of credits, debits, reversals, and resets applied by each claim event. Supports real-time adjudication decisions, ACA MOOP compliance, and member cost-sharing transparency.'
}

Table claim.adjudication_rate {
  adjudication_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each adjudication rate record.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract under which this rate was negotiated.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'FK to contract.fee_schedule']
  adjudication_rate_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description or notes about the rate.']
  adjudication_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate.']
  allowed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum reimbursable amount for the procedure under this rate.']
  awp_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of AWP used for drug pricing calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this rate becomes effective.']
  geographic_cbsa varchar [note: 'CBSA code representing the metropolitan area of service.']
  geographic_county varchar [note: 'County name or FIPS code for the service location.']
  geographic_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state abbreviation where service was rendered.']
  mac_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable cost for a drug or service as defined by MAC.']
  medicare_fee_schedule_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the Medicare fee schedule applied to calculate the allowed amount.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification of the provider within the plan network.']
  place_of_service_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the location where service was provided. See POS code list; values beyond six are managed via reference table.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Standard procedure code used for billing (CPT or HCPCS).']
  procedure_modifier varchar [note: 'Optional modifier that further specifies the procedure.']
  provider_specialty_code varchar [note: 'Taxonomy code representing the provider\'s specialty.']
  provider_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the provider entity.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rate definition (e.g., CMS, plan‑specific, contracted).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rate (procedure, drug, bundle, or service).']
  rvu_based_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allowed amount is derived from Relative Value Units.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this rate is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rate record.']

  Note: 'Adjudication-time reimbursement rate lookup defining allowed amounts for procedure codes by provider type, network tier, geographic area, and effective date. Captures rate ID, fee schedule name and version, CPT/HCPCS code, modifier, place of service, provider specialty, geographic area (state/county/CBSA), network tier, allowed amount, RVU-based calculation flag, Medicare fee schedule percentage, MAC rate, AWP percentage (for drug codes), effective date, termination date, and rate source (CMS/plan-specific/contracted). Owned by the claim domain as the adjudication-time rate snapshot — the contract domain owns the negotiated rate master; this product captures the rate as-applied at the moment of claim pricing.'
}

Table claim.attachment {
  attachment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the attachment record.']
  claim_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific claim line (service) the attachment supports, if applicable.']
  parent_attachment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on attachment (parent_attachment_id)']
  related_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim to which this attachment belongs.']
  access_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the attachment has been accessed by users or systems.']
  attachment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes supplied by the submitter about the document.']
  attachment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the attachment.']
  checksum_sha256 varchar [note: 'SHA-256 hash of the file used for integrity verification.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification indicating the sensitivity of the attachment per corporate policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the attachment record was first created in the data lake.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the attachment content.']
  document_subtype varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the document category (e.g., radiology_report, discharge_summary).']
  encryption_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to encrypt the attachment, if applicable.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time after which the attachment may be archived or purged.']
  external_document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the originating source system (e.g., provider, clearinghouse) to the document.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Original name of the attached file as supplied by the submitter.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the attachment in bytes.']
  file_type varchar [note: 'Standard MIME type describing the file format (e.g., application/pdf, image/jpeg).']
  is_encrypted boolean [note: 'True if the attachment is stored in encrypted form.']
  is_original boolean [note: 'True if the attachment is the original source document; false if it is a copy or derived file.']
  is_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the attachment contains PHI or other restricted information.']
  last_access_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent access.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or internal notes about the attachment.']
  received_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the attachment was submitted.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the attachment must be retained to satisfy regulatory requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the attachment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: facets|cactus|provider_source|rxclaim|argus|interqual|mcg|casenet|tru_care|salesforce|healthedge|oracle_financials|availity|change_healthcare — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of the document in the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the attachment record was last modified.']
  upload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the attachment was received into the lakehouse.']
  virus_scan_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the malware scan.']
  virus_scanned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the attachment has been scanned for malware.']

  Note: 'Clinical and supporting document attachments submitted with or requested for a claim — operative reports, medical records, itemized bills, and other documentation required for adjudication. Captures attachment type, submission method (275 EDI, portal, fax), receipt date, and association to claim header or line.'
}

Table claim.status_event {
  status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each immutable claim status event record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim whose status transition is recorded.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or automated process that triggered the status change.']
  parent_status_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on status_event (parent_status_event_id)']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this event record (e.g., after correction or annotation).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text notes added by the actor for context or clarification.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the status event (e.g., EDI gateway, core claims engine, pharmacy benefit manager).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the status transition occurred.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status change was performed manually (True) or automatically (False).']
  previous_status_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the claim\'s status before this transition.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the status changed (e.g., payment received, denial rule, manual override).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the reason for the status change.']
  status_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the claim\'s new lifecycle status after the transition.']
  user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the actor initiating the transition (system process, internal operator, provider, or member).']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of claim lifecycle status transitions — received, pended, adjudicated, paid, denied, appealed, adjusted. Captures status code, transition timestamp, triggering user or system, and reason code. Supports 277 claim status inquiry responses.'
}

Table claim.payer {
  payer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payer']
  payer_account_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current balance of the payer\'s account.']

  payer_state varchar [note: 'State or province abbreviation of the payer\'s address.']
  payer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payer.']
  payer_tax_number varchar [note: 'Employer Identification Number (EIN) for the payer.']
  payer_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payer entity.']
  payer_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payer record.']
  payer_website varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the payer.']
  payer_zip varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the payer\'s address.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payer. Referenced by primary_payer_id.'
}

Table pharmacy.drug_master {
  drug_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each drug master record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins across formulary, claims, DUR, rebate contracting, and PBM adjudication.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Data governance requires tracking which employee entered or updated drug master records.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Required for drug rebate and pricing management: linking each drug to its manufacturer/vendor enables accurate rebate calculations, pricing negotiations, and CMS reporting.']
  atc_code varchar [note: 'The WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code at the 5th level, identifying the drug\'s anatomical main group, therapeutic subgroup, pharmacological subgroup, chemical subgroup, and chemical substance. Used for therapeutic class reporting, HEDIS pharmacy measures, population health analytics, and international drug classification alignment.']
  awp_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the current AWP unit price became effective. Used to apply the correct AWP for historical claims repricing, audit reconciliation, and retroactive rebate calculations.']
  awp_unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The Average Wholesale Price (AWP) per unit of the drug as published by First Databank or Medi-Span. AWP is the benchmark pricing reference used in pharmacy reimbursement calculations, MAC pricing comparisons, and rebate contract negotiations. Expressed in USD per unit (tablet, mL, gram, etc.).']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The proprietary trade name under which the drug is marketed by the manufacturer (e.g., Lipitor, Humira). Used in formulary tier assignment, rebate contracting, member communications, and brand vs. generic substitution logic in PBM adjudication.']
  controlled_substance_schedule varchar [note: 'The DEA controlled substance schedule classification (I through V) indicating the drug\'s potential for abuse and accepted medical use. Schedule II-V drugs require enhanced DUR edits, quantity limits, early refill restrictions, and special handling in PBM adjudication. Not_scheduled indicates no DEA restriction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drug master record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and change data capture tracking per HIPAA Security Rule requirements.']
  dea_number_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid DEA registration number is required on the prescription for this drug due to its controlled substance classification. Drives prescription validation edits in PBM adjudication and pharmacy dispensing workflows.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The physical form in which the drug is dispensed (e.g., tablet, capsule, solution, injection, patch, inhaler). Critical for formulary configuration, quantity limit rules, step therapy protocols, and DUR screening in RxClaim/Argus. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|solution|injection|patch|inhaler|cream|ointment|suspension|powder — promote to reference product]']
  drug_class_code varchar [note: 'The classification code for the drug class within the GPI or ETC hierarchy, representing a more granular grouping below therapeutic subclass (e.g., specific statin subtype). Used in formulary step therapy rules, DUR clinical edits, and rebate tier segmentation in PBM systems.']
  drug_class_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable name of the drug class corresponding to the drug_class_code. Provides the most granular named classification level in the therapeutic hierarchy for formulary configuration and clinical reporting.']
  drug_name varchar [note: 'The full proprietary or established name of the drug as it appears on the FDA-approved label and in the First Databank or Medi-Span drug database feed. Used as the primary human-readable identity label across formulary displays, EOB communications, and member-facing materials.']
  drug_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drug record in the master file. Active drugs are eligible for formulary placement and PBM adjudication. Discontinued or recalled drugs trigger claim edits and member/provider notifications. Drives formulary maintenance workflows in RxClaim/Argus.']
  drug_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drug as brand-name, generic, biosimilar, over-the-counter (OTC), compounded, or repackaged. Drives formulary tier assignment, cost-sharing rules, generic substitution logic, and rebate eligibility in PBM adjudication and formulary management.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this drug master record became effective and eligible for use in formulary configuration, PBM adjudication, and claims processing. Used for temporal validity enforcement in adjudication date-of-service lookups and formulary effective date management.']
  fda_approval_status varchar [note: 'The current FDA regulatory approval status of the drug product. Approved drugs are eligible for formulary coverage. Tentatively approved generics may be covered under specific plan designs. Withdrawn or not-approved drugs are excluded from formulary and trigger claim denials. Required for CMS Part D formulary compliance.']
  generic_name varchar [note: 'The International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or United States Adopted Name (USAN) for the active ingredient(s) of the drug (e.g., atorvastatin, adalimumab). Used for generic substitution logic, DUR screening, therapeutic equivalence grouping, and HEDIS pharmacy measure reporting.']
  gpi_code varchar [note: 'The 14-digit Medi-Span Generic Product Identifier (GPI) code providing a hierarchical drug classification from drug group (2 digits) through drug class, subclass, name, name extension, dosage form, and strength. Used as the primary therapeutic classification key in RxClaim/Argus PBM systems for formulary management, DUR screening, and rebate contracting.']
  hcc_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug is clinically associated with one or more CMS Hierarchical Condition Categories (HCCs) for risk adjustment purposes. HCC-relevant drugs are used in RAF score modeling, RAPS/EDPS encounter data submissions, and population health stratification for Medicare Advantage plans.']
  is_biosimilar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug is an FDA-approved biosimilar to a reference biological product. Biosimilars have distinct formulary placement rules, interchangeability designations, and rebate contracting considerations under CMS Part D and commercial plan designs.']
  is_generic_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an FDA-approved generic equivalent is available in the market for this brand-name drug. Used in formulary tier assignment, member cost-sharing communications, and generic substitution prompts during PBM adjudication and pharmacy dispensing.']
  is_maintenance_drug boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug is classified as a maintenance medication intended for long-term chronic condition management (e.g., antihypertensives, statins, diabetes medications). Maintenance drugs are eligible for 90-day supply dispensing at mail-order pharmacies and are used in HEDIS medication adherence measures (PDC).']
  is_preventive_drug boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug qualifies as a preventive medication under ACA Section 2713 or HDHP/HSA preventive care rules, making it eligible for $0 cost-sharing before the deductible is met. Used in benefit configuration and claims adjudication cost-sharing logic.']
  is_specialty_drug boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug is classified as a specialty pharmaceutical requiring special handling, storage, administration, or monitoring (e.g., biologics, oncology agents, hepatitis C treatments). Specialty drugs are subject to mandatory specialty pharmacy channel requirements, prior authorization, and enhanced DUR in PBM adjudication. Critical for CMS Part D specialty tier placement.']
  labeler_code varchar [note: 'The 5-digit FDA-assigned labeler code (first segment of the NDC) identifying the firm that manufactures, repackages, relabels, or distributes the drug. Used for manufacturer-level rebate aggregation and PBM contract management.']
  multi_source_code varchar [note: 'The FDA Orange Book multi-source code indicating the competitive market status of the drug: O=originator brand with no generic, M=multi-source (generic available), N=no current market (discontinued), Y=single-source generic. Used in generic substitution logic, formulary tier assignment, and MAC pricing eligibility determination.']
  ndc varchar [note: 'The 11-digit National Drug Code assigned by the FDA uniquely identifying the labeler, product, and package size of a drug. This is the primary external business identifier for the drug and is used in pharmacy claims (NCPDP/837P), formulary management, PBM adjudication (RxClaim/Argus), and CMS Part D reporting. Stored in 5-4-2 format without hyphens.']
  ndc_11 varchar [note: 'The NDC formatted in the standard 5-4-2 hyphenated display format (e.g., 12345-6789-01) as required for human-readable labeling, SBC documentation, and regulatory submissions to CMS and state DOIs.']
  package_size decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of drug units contained in the dispensed package as defined by the NDC (e.g., 30 tablets, 100 mL, 1 vial). Used in unit-of-use pricing calculations, days supply verification, quantity limit enforcement, and MAC pricing per-unit derivation.']
  package_size_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the package size quantity (e.g., EA for each/tablet/capsule, ML for milliliter, GM for gram). Used in conjunction with package_size for accurate dispensing quantity calculations, days supply verification, and unit-cost pricing in PBM adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH|KIT — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pharmacological_category varchar [note: 'The pharmacological mechanism-of-action category of the drug (e.g., ACE Inhibitor, Beta Blocker, SSRI, Statin, Monoclonal Antibody). Used for DUR drug-drug interaction screening, therapeutic duplication edits, clinical program targeting in care management, and HEDIS measure attribution.']
  reference_drug_ndc varchar [note: 'The NDC of the FDA Reference Listed Drug (RLD) or reference biological product to which this generic or biosimilar is therapeutically equivalent. Enables generic substitution relationship mapping, step therapy configuration, and biosimilar interchangeability tracking in the PBM system.']
  rems_program varchar [note: 'The name of the FDA-mandated Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program associated with this drug, if applicable (e.g., iPLEDGE for isotretinoin, TIRF REMS for fentanyl products). REMS drugs require special dispensing controls, patient enrollment, and prescriber certification that must be validated during PBM adjudication and prior authorization.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The path by which the drug is administered to the patient (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical, inhalation). Drives formulary benefit category assignment (medical vs. pharmacy benefit), prior authorization requirements, and specialty pharmacy routing in PBM adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|ophthalmic|otic|rectal — promote to reference product]']
  source_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (First Databank, Medi-Span, RxClaim, or Argus) for this drug master record. Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and upstream change tracking in the lakehouse ingestion pipeline.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which this drug master record was ingested (e.g., FDB=First Databank, MEDISPAN=Medi-Span, RXCLAIM=RxClaim PBM, ARGUS=Argus PBM, INTERNAL=internally maintained). Used for data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during ETL, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  strength varchar [note: 'The concentration or potency of the active ingredient(s) expressed as a numeric value with unit of measure (e.g., 10 mg, 500 mg/5 mL, 0.25%). Used in DUR duplicate therapy screening, quantity limit enforcement, and clinical appropriateness review in UM/PA workflows.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which this drug master record was terminated or superseded, indicating the drug is no longer active for formulary placement or new claim adjudication. Null indicates the record is currently active. Used for historical claims repricing and formulary audit trails.']
  therapeutic_class_code varchar [note: 'The standardized code identifying the broad therapeutic class of the drug (e.g., cardiovascular agents, anti-infectives, antineoplastics). Used for formulary tier grouping, rebate contract aggregation, utilization reporting, and disease management program enrollment triggers.']
  therapeutic_class_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable name of the therapeutic class corresponding to the therapeutic_class_code. Used in formulary displays, member-facing SBC documents, provider communications, and regulatory reporting to CMS and state DOIs.']
  therapeutic_equivalence_code varchar [note: 'The FDA Orange Book therapeutic equivalence evaluation code (e.g., AB, AA, BC) indicating whether a generic drug is therapeutically equivalent to the reference listed drug. AB-rated generics are substitutable at the pharmacy. Used in generic substitution logic, formulary step therapy, and DAW (Dispense As Written) override processing in PBM adjudication.']
  therapeutic_subclass_code varchar [note: 'The standardized code identifying the therapeutic subclass within the broader therapeutic class (e.g., HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibitors within cardiovascular agents). Enables granular formulary tier differentiation, step therapy protocol design, and rebate contract segmentation.']
  therapeutic_subclass_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable name of the therapeutic subclass corresponding to the therapeutic_subclass_code. Used in formulary management workflows, clinical program configuration, and detailed utilization analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drug master record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for incremental data refresh, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline.']
  wac_unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per unit as reported by the manufacturer, representing the list price to wholesalers before discounts and rebates. Used as a pricing benchmark for specialty drug reimbursement, rebate baseline calculations, and CMS Part D coverage gap discount program calculations.']

  Note: 'Authoritative NDC-level drug master record for all covered pharmaceutical products. Captures National Drug Code (NDC), drug name, generic name, brand name, manufacturer, dosage form, strength, route of administration, drug class, controlled substance schedule, FDA approval status, and therapeutic equivalence codes (AB-rated/biosimilar). Includes generic substitution relationships and the full therapeutic classification hierarchy: ATC (Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical) code, GPI (Generic Product Identifier) prefix, pharmacological category, therapeutic class, therapeutic subclass, class name, and subclass name. This is the SSOT for all drug identity, classification, equivalence, and therapeutic hierarchy data used across formulary, claims, DUR, rebate contracting, HEDIS pharmacy measures, and PBM adjudication. Sourced from First Databank/Medi-Span drug database feeds.'
}

Table pharmacy.formulary {
  formulary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the formulary record. Primary key for the formulary data product in the pharmacy domain.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Formulary design must adhere to specific regulatory obligations (ACA, CMS); linking enables reporting of compliance status per formulary.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Formulary management assigns a responsible pharmacist/employee; needed for formulary change approval and governance reporting.']
  prior_version_formulary_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding version of this formulary. Enables formulary change tracking, member notification workflows, and historical adjudication lookups for appeals and grievances.']
  change_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which member notification was sent for formulary changes. CMS Part D requires 60-day advance notice for non-preferred formulary changes and immediate notification for safety-related removals.']
  cms_formulary_code varchar [note: 'The CMS-assigned formulary identifier used in Part D formulary file submissions to CMS. Required for Medicare Part D plans to identify the formulary in RAPS and EDPS submissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this formulary record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance documentation purposes.']
  drug_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique drug entries (NDC or drug-tier combinations) included in this formulary at the time of the last update. Used for formulary adequacy assessments and CMS Part D formulary file validation.']
  drug_utilization_review_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Drug Utilization Review (DUR) edits are applied during adjudication under this formulary. DUR programs screen for drug interactions, duplicate therapy, and inappropriate dosing per OBRA \'90 requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this formulary becomes active and applicable to member benefit adjudication. Used by PBM systems (RxClaim/Argus) to determine which formulary version applies at point of dispensing.']
  formulary_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the formulary indicating its benefit richness level relative to other formularies offered by the plan. Used in benefit design analytics, member communications, and plan comparison tools.']
  formulary_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the formulary across systems, used in EDI transactions, PBM interfaces, and CMS Part D submissions. Corresponds to the formulary ID field in RxClaim/Argus PBM system.']
  formulary_description varchar [note: 'Free-text business description of the formulary, including its intended population, key design features, and any notable coverage inclusions or exclusions. Used in internal documentation and plan benefit configuration.']
  formulary_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable business name of the formulary (e.g., \'Standard Commercial Formulary 2025\', \'Medicare Part D Enhanced Formulary\'). Used in member-facing materials, SBC documents, and plan benefit configurations.']
  formulary_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the formulary record. Active formularies are used in live adjudication; pending formularies are approved but not yet effective; retired formularies are historical records no longer in use.']
  formulary_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formulary structure indicating coverage breadth and tier design. Open formularies cover most drugs; closed formularies restrict to listed drugs only; tiered formularies apply differential cost-sharing by tier.']
  generic_substitution_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing generic drug substitution at point of dispensing. Mandatory substitution requires dispensing of generic equivalents; permissive allows brand if requested; DAW (Dispense As Written) honored respects prescriber instructions.']
  is_aca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary meets ACA Qualified Health Plan (QHP) essential health benefit (EHB) drug coverage requirements, including the required drug categories and classes under 45 CFR Part 156.']
  is_cms_part_d boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary is subject to CMS Medicare Part D regulatory requirements, including annual bid submission, formulary file submission to CMS, and coverage determination obligations.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee last reviewed and approved this formulary. P&T Committee review is required for CMS Part D compliance and NCQA accreditation.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Line of Business (LOB) associated with this formulary, indicating the regulatory and market segment under which the formulary operates. Drives CMS Part D, Medicaid, or commercial compliance requirements.']
  low_income_subsidy_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary is designated for use by Low Income Subsidy (LIS) eligible members under Medicare Part D. LIS formularies must meet CMS benchmark plan requirements for cost-sharing and drug coverage.']
  mail_order_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary supports mail-order pharmacy dispensing. Mail-order programs typically offer 90-day supplies at reduced cost-sharing and are a key component of pharmacy benefit design.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee review of this formulary. Supports compliance tracking for CMS Part D requirements mandating regular formulary review cycles.']
  pbm_formulary_code varchar [note: 'The formulary identifier as assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the health plan\'s internal formulary record and the PBM\'s formulary configuration.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'The benefit plan year (e.g., 2025) for which this formulary is effective. Used for annual formulary change management, CMS Part D annual bid submissions, and benefit year reporting.']
  prior_auth_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary requires prior authorization (PA) for any covered drugs. PA requirements are subject to CMS Part D coverage determination timelines and member appeal rights.']
  pt_committee_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee formally approved this formulary or the most recent formulary update. Required documentation for CMS Part D compliance and NCQA accreditation audits.']
  quantity_limit_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary applies quantity limits (QL) on covered drugs, restricting the amount dispensed per fill or per defined time period. Quantity limits are a utilization management tool subject to CMS Part D exception requirements.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which CMS or the applicable State Department of Insurance approved this formulary for use. Required for Part D compliance documentation and audit trails.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the formulary was submitted to CMS or the applicable State Department of Insurance for regulatory review and approval.']
  regulatory_filing_status varchar [note: 'Status of the formulary\'s regulatory filing with CMS or applicable State Department of Insurance (DOI). Tracks whether the formulary has been submitted, approved, or rejected for compliance with Part D or state market requirements.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which this formulary record originated (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM system, Facets). Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  specialty_pharmacy_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary designates a specialty pharmacy network for dispensing high-cost specialty drugs. Specialty pharmacy programs may include limited distribution networks and enhanced clinical support services.']
  specialty_tier_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary includes a specialty drug tier for high-cost specialty medications. Specialty tiers typically apply to drugs with monthly costs exceeding CMS specialty tier threshold (currently $670/month for Part D).']
  step_therapy_ind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this formulary employs step therapy (also known as fail-first) protocols requiring members to try lower-cost or preferred drugs before coverage of higher-cost alternatives is authorized.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which this formulary is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended formulary with no defined end date. Used for formulary version management and historical adjudication lookups.']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'The total number of cost-sharing tiers defined in this formulary (e.g., 3-tier, 4-tier, 5-tier, 6-tier). Determines the tier structure used in drug tier assignments and member cost-sharing calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this formulary record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit trail requirements.']
  url varchar [note: 'Public-facing URL where members can access the formulary drug list. CMS Part D and ACA QHP regulations require health plans to publish formularies online and provide the URL in plan materials and SBC documents.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the formulary, incremented with each approved update or mid-year formulary change. Used to track formulary change history and ensure members receive required 60-day advance notice of non-preferred changes.']

  Note: 'Master record for each formulary (drug benefit list) offered by the health plan. Defines the formulary name, effective date range, line of business (LOB), plan year, formulary type (open/closed/tiered), PBM contract reference, and regulatory filing status. A formulary is the SSOT for which drugs are covered under a given benefit design and is required for CMS Part D compliance.'
}

Table pharmacy.formulary_drug_tier {
  formulary_drug_tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formulary drug tier assignment record. Primary key for this association entity linking a drug (NDC) to a formulary with its tier, cost-sharing, and utilization management (UM) controls.']
  drug_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal drug master record corresponding to this NDC, enabling joins to the drug master for drug name, therapeutic class, and other drug attributes.']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formulary to which this drug tier assignment belongs. A formulary is the approved list of covered drugs for a given plan and benefit year.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this formulary drug tier assignment applies. Links the tier assignment to the specific plan for cost-sharing and benefit configuration.']
  benefit_year varchar [note: 'The plan benefit year (e.g., 2024, 2025) to which this formulary drug tier assignment applies. CMS Part D formulary files are submitted annually by benefit year.']
  coinsurance_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Member coinsurance percentage applied to the drug cost under this formulary tier assignment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.20 = 20%). Used when cost-sharing is percentage-based rather than fixed copay. Applies to specialty and high-cost tiers.']
  copay_mail_order decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed member copay amount for a 90-day supply dispensed through a mail-order pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Mail-order copays are typically lower than retail to incentivize mail-order utilization.']
  copay_retail_30 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed member copay amount for a 30-day supply dispensed at a retail pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Drives member cost-sharing at point of sale. Used in SBC and EOB generation.']
  copay_retail_90 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed member copay amount for a 90-day supply dispensed at a retail pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Applicable for maintenance medications at extended-supply retail pharmacies.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the drug is covered under this formulary assignment. \'covered\' = fully covered per tier rules; \'covered_with_restrictions\' = covered subject to UM controls (PA, ST, QL); \'not_covered\' = not on formulary; \'excluded\' = explicitly excluded per CMS or plan rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this formulary drug tier assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  deductible_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan deductible must be met before cost-sharing for this drug tier applies. Relevant for High Deductible Health Plans (HDHP) and plans with drug-specific deductibles.']
  dispensing_channel varchar [note: 'Pharmacy dispensing channel(s) through which this drug may be dispensed under this formulary tier assignment. \'specialty\' indicates dispensing is restricted to a specialty pharmacy network. Drives pharmacy routing at point of sale.']
  drug_tier_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for a change to the drug\'s tier assignment (e.g., \'generic available\', \'contract renegotiation\', \'clinical review\', \'CMS requirement\', \'formulary refresh\'). Supports audit trail and CMS formulary change notification requirements.']
  dur_alert_type varchar [note: 'Type of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert associated with this formulary drug tier assignment (e.g., \'drug-drug interaction\', \'drug-disease contraindication\', \'duplicate therapy\', \'dose range check\'). Drives DUR edits at point of sale in the PBM adjudication system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drug_drug_interaction|drug_disease|duplicate_therapy|dose_range|drug_allergy|drug_age — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this formulary drug tier assignment becomes effective. Used to determine the applicable tier and cost-sharing rules at the time of dispensing. Required for CMS formulary file submissions.']
  formulary_status_code varchar [note: 'CMS-defined formulary status code used in the Part D formulary file submission. Values: 1=Covered, 2=Covered with Restrictions, 3=Not Covered, 4=Covered (Grandfathered), 5=Covered (Transition), 6=Covered (LIS). Required field in CMS formulary extract file.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Line of business to which this formulary drug tier assignment applies (e.g., \'commercial\', \'medicare_part_d\', \'medicaid\', \'exchange\', \'fehb\'). Determines applicable regulatory requirements and cost-sharing rules.']
  mac_pricing_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC) pricing applies to this drug under this formulary tier assignment. MAC pricing caps reimbursement for generic drugs and is a key PBM contract term.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: '11-digit National Drug Code uniquely identifying the specific drug product, dosage form, and package size assigned to this formulary tier. NDC is the primary drug identifier used in pharmacy claim adjudication and CMS formulary file submissions.']
  pa_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the clinical criteria that must be met for prior authorization approval of this drug. Supports PA adjudication, member appeals, and CMS/NCQA compliance documentation.']
  pa_type varchar [note: 'Type of prior authorization process required for this drug (e.g., \'standard\' = standard review timeline; \'expedited\' = urgent/expedited review; \'retrospective\' = post-service review). Null or \'not_applicable\' if prior_auth_required is false.']
  pbm_formulary_code varchar [note: 'PBM system-specific formulary identifier used in RxClaim or Argus to reference this formulary configuration. Enables reconciliation between the health plan\'s internal formulary records and the PBM\'s adjudication system.']
  prior_auth_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization (PA) is required before this drug will be covered under this formulary tier assignment. When true, a PA request must be approved before the claim will adjudicate. Reported in the CMS PA extract file.']
  ql_clinical_basis varchar [note: 'Clinical rationale or basis for the quantity limit rule (e.g., \'FDA-approved maximum dose\', \'clinical guidelines\', \'safety limit\'). Documents the evidence basis for the QL control as required for CMS and NCQA compliance.']
  ql_days_supply varchar [note: 'The number of days supply over which the maximum quantity limit applies (e.g., 30 days, 90 days). Defines the time window for the quantity limit rule. Null if quantity_limit_required is false.']
  ql_dosage_form_restriction varchar [note: 'Specifies any dosage form restrictions associated with the quantity limit rule (e.g., \'tablet only\', \'extended-release excluded\', \'oral solution only\'). Captures clinical basis for dosage form-specific QL controls.']
  ql_max_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the drug allowed per fill or per defined time period under the quantity limit rule. Expressed in the drug\'s dispensing unit (e.g., tablets, mL, units). Null if quantity_limit_required is false.']
  quantity_limit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a quantity limit (QL) applies to this drug under this formulary assignment. When true, the dispensed quantity per fill or per time period is restricted. Reported in the CMS QL extract file.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this formulary drug tier assignment record. \'active\' = currently in effect; \'inactive\' = terminated or expired; \'pending\' = approved but not yet effective; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer version of the same assignment.']
  specialty_drug_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drug is designated as a specialty drug under this formulary. Specialty drugs typically require dispensing through a specialty pharmacy, have higher cost-sharing, and may require PA. Used in specialty pharmacy channel routing and PBM contract management.']
  st_clinical_rationale varchar [note: 'Clinical rationale or evidence basis for the step therapy protocol (e.g., \'preferred generic available\', \'clinical guidelines recommend first-line therapy\', \'cost-effectiveness\'). Required for CMS and NCQA UM documentation.']
  st_override_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria under which the step therapy requirement may be overridden without completing the required step sequence (e.g., \'contraindication to step drug\', \'prior treatment failure documented\', \'clinical exception\'). Supports PA override adjudication.']
  st_required_step_ndc varchar [note: 'National Drug Code (NDC) of the required step drug that must be tried and failed before coverage of this drug is approved under the step therapy protocol. Null if step_therapy_required is false.']
  st_step_number varchar [note: 'The sequence number of this drug in the step therapy protocol (e.g., 1 = first-line, 2 = second-line). Defines the order in which step drugs must be tried and failed before coverage of the target drug is approved. Null if step_therapy_required is false.']
  step_therapy_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step therapy (ST) applies to this drug under this formulary assignment. When true, the member must first try and fail one or more preferred (step) drugs before coverage of this drug is approved. Reported in the CMS ST extract file.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this formulary drug tier assignment expires or is terminated. Null indicates the assignment is currently active with no defined end date. Supports historical tracking and CMS formulary file change submissions.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the drug tier (e.g., \'Generic\', \'Preferred Brand\', \'Non-Preferred Brand\', \'Specialty\', \'Specialty Preferred\'). Displayed on member-facing materials including the Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) and Explanation of Benefits (EOB).']
  tier_number varchar [note: 'Numeric tier level assigned to the drug within the formulary (e.g., 1=Generic, 2=Preferred Brand, 3=Non-Preferred Brand, 4=Specialty, 5=Specialty Preferred, 6=Select Care). Drives member cost-sharing at point of sale. CMS requires tier number in formulary file submissions.']
  transition_fill_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a transition fill is permitted for this drug when a member is new to the plan or the drug\'s formulary status has changed. CMS Part D requires transition fills for certain drugs during the transition period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this formulary drug tier assignment record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and CMS formulary change notification compliance.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a specific drug (NDC) to a formulary with its assigned tier, cost-sharing rules, coverage status, and all utilization management (UM) controls. UM controls owned by this product include: quantity limits (max quantity per fill/time period, dosage form restrictions, clinical basis), step therapy protocols (fail-first drug sequences, required step drugs, step sequence, clinical rationale, override criteria), prior authorization flags, and specialty drug designation. Captures effective date range for each tier assignment and each UM control configuration. This is the single operational record that drives member cost-sharing and UM edits at point of sale, and is the SSOT for CMS formulary file submissions including formulary, QL, ST, and PA extract files.'
}

Table pharmacy.drug_pricing {
  drug_pricing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each NDC-level drug pricing record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the drug_pricing data product.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Drug pricing tables are derived from PBM contract agreements; linking ensures price source traceability for regulatory filings and rebate calculations.']
  drug_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug_master. Business justification: drug_pricing records are defined per NDC; linking to drug_master provides the authoritative drug attributes and eliminates duplicated columns.']
  mac_list_id varchar [note: 'FK to pharmacy.mac_list']
  awp_discount_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to AWP to derive the reimbursement rate under AWP-based contracts (e.g., AWP minus 15%). Expressed as a positive decimal percentage (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Used for PBM contract compliance validation and reimbursement audits.']
  awp_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Average Wholesale Price per unit as published by First Databank or Medi-Span. Commonly used as the benchmark for pharmacy reimbursement calculations and discount negotiations. Expressed in USD.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drug pricing record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring of pricing file processing.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary price fields in this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for Puerto Rico and other US territories. Defaults to USD for domestic plans.']
  days_supply varchar [note: 'Number of days of drug supply for which this pricing record applies (e.g., 30-day retail, 90-day mail order). Used to differentiate pricing tiers by supply duration per PBM contract terms.']
  dea_schedule varchar [note: 'DEA controlled substance schedule classification (CI through CV) or NON-CONTROLLED. Used for DUR edits, quantity limit enforcement, prior authorization requirements, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  dispensing_channel varchar [note: 'Pharmacy dispensing channel for which this pricing record applies: retail (community pharmacy), mail_order (90-day supply mail service), specialty (specialty pharmacy), or long_term_care (LTC facility pharmacy). Pricing may differ by channel per PBM contract terms.']
  dispensing_fee decimal(10,4) [note: 'Fixed dispensing fee in USD paid to the pharmacy per prescription fill, separate from the ingredient cost. Defined in PBM network contracts and applied during claim adjudication to calculate total pharmacy reimbursement.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this pricing record becomes effective and applicable for claim adjudication. Used to select the correct price for a claim based on the date of service. Aligns with PBM pricing file effective dates.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this pricing record expires and is no longer applicable for new claim adjudication. Null indicates an open-ended pricing record with no defined end date. Used with effective_date to determine the active pricing window.']
  formulary_tier varchar [note: 'Formulary tier assignment for this drug under the applicable plan benefit design (e.g., tier_1=preferred generic, tier_2=generic, tier_3=preferred brand, tier_4=non-preferred brand, tier_5=specialty). Drives member cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) during claim adjudication.']
  mac_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the MAC list, used to track updates and revisions to the MAC pricing schedule. Supports audit trails for MAC list changes and pharmacy reimbursement disputes. Null when price_type is not MAC.']
  mac_methodology varchar [note: 'Describes the methodology used to derive the MAC price (e.g., MARKET_BASKET=based on market survey of acquisition costs, NADAC_BASED=derived from CMS NADAC data, AWP_DISCOUNT=percentage discount from AWP, WAC_DISCOUNT=percentage discount from WAC, CUSTOM=plan-specific methodology). Required for PBM contract compliance and regulatory transparency. Null when price_type is not MAC.']
  mac_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum Allowable Cost per unit set by the PBM or health plan for generic and multi-source drugs. Represents the upper reimbursement limit for a drug under the applicable MAC list. Expressed in USD. Null when price_type is not MAC.']
  multi_source_code varchar [note: 'FDA Orange Book multi-source code indicating the competitive market status of the drug: O=Originator brand (no generic), M=Multi-source (generic available), N=Non-multi-source, Y=Generic product. Used to determine MAC eligibility and generic substitution applicability.']
  package_size decimal(10,4) [note: 'Quantity of units contained in the standard package as defined by the NDC. Used to convert between package-level and per-unit pricing and to validate dispensed quantity against package size during adjudication.']
  package_size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the package size quantity (e.g., TAB for tablets, ML for milliliters). Distinct from unit_of_measure which applies to the pricing unit; this field describes the package-level quantity unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|UN|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH — promote to reference product]']
  price_change_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage change in unit price from the prior pricing record to the current record, calculated as ((unit_price - prior_unit_price) / prior_unit_price) * 100. Used for drug price inflation monitoring, CMS price increase reporting, and PBM contract compliance.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the pricing methodology represented by this record: AWP (Average Wholesale Price), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost), MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), RBP (Reference Based Pricing), NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or Usual and Customary. Determines how the price is applied during claim adjudication and reimbursement calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AWP|WAC|MAC|RBP|NADAC|USUAL_AND_CUSTOMARY — promote to reference product]']
  pricing_file_date date [note: 'Publication or release date of the source pricing file from which this record was extracted. Used to track pricing file currency, identify stale pricing data, and support audit and reconciliation processes.']
  pricing_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the source pricing file from which this record was loaded (e.g., \'FDB_PRICE_20240101.txt\', \'RXCLAIM_MAC_Q1_2024.csv\'). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and troubleshooting pricing discrepancies.']
  pricing_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating source of the pricing data (e.g., First Databank, Medi-Span, RxClaim PBM feed, Argus PBM feed, CMS published rates, internal custom pricing). Critical for audit trails and PBM contract compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDB|MEDISPAN|RXCLAIM|ARGUS|CMS|INTERNAL|CUSTOM — promote to reference product]']
  pricing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing record: active (currently in use for adjudication), inactive (no longer applicable), pending (approved but not yet effective), superseded (replaced by a newer pricing record for the same NDC and price type).']
  prior_unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Previous per-unit price for this NDC and price type before the most recent pricing update. Used for price change analysis, trend reporting, and PBM contract compliance audits.']
  rbp_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Reference Based Pricing per unit used as the benchmark reimbursement amount under RBP pharmacy contracts. Expressed in USD. Null when price_type is not RBP.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Dispensing unit of measure for which the price applies (e.g., EA=Each, ML=Milliliter, GM=Gram, MG=Milligram, UN=Unit, TAB=Tablet, CAP=Capsule, VIAL=Vial, PATCH=Patch). Required for accurate per-unit cost calculation during adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|UN|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH — promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Per-unit drug price expressed in USD for the applicable price type (AWP, WAC, MAC, or RBP). Used as the basis for claim adjudication, cost-sharing calculation, and pharmacy reimbursement. Precision to 6 decimal places to accommodate high-precision specialty drug pricing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drug pricing record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  wac_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Wholesale Acquisition Cost per unit as reported by the manufacturer to the pricing compendia. Represents the list price from manufacturer to wholesaler before discounts. Expressed in USD. Used in WAC-based reimbursement contracts.']

  Note: 'NDC-level drug pricing record capturing AWP (Average Wholesale Price), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost), MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), and RBP (Reference Based Pricing) values sourced from PBM pricing files. Includes effective date, pricing source, unit of measure, package size, price type, MAC list name, and MAC list methodology reference. Used for claim adjudication, cost-sharing calculation, and pharmacy reimbursement. MAC list grouping attributes are included directly on pricing records for governance and PBM contract compliance. Sourced from RxClaim or Argus PBM pricing feeds and First Databank/Medi-Span pricing updates.'
}

Table pharmacy.pharmacy_claim {
  pharmacy_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pharmacy claim record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the pharmacy_claim data product. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: AP Invoice Generation: insurer creates an accounts‑payable invoice to reimburse the dispensing pharmacy for each approved claim.']
  ap_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_payment. Business justification: AP Payment Recording: payment of the AP invoice for a claim is tracked to close the reimbursement cycle.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Required for the \'Pharmacy Claim Appeal\' process where each claim can generate an appeal case; enables claim‑level appeal reporting and tracking.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Claim adjudication uses PBM contract rates, step‑therapy and rebate rules; linking claim to provider_contract enables accurate pricing and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑Center Allocation: claim expense is allocated to the appropriate cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  dispensing_pharmacy_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate reference to the pharmacy that dispensed the drug. Links to the provider/pharmacy master record. Distinguishes retail, mail-order, and specialty pharmacy channels. Used for network adequacy reporting and pharmacy audit trail requirements.']
  drug_master_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate reference to the NDC-level drug master record in the pharmacy domain. Links to the formulary drug master product which carries NDC, drug name, tier, and pricing (AWP/WAC/MAC). Central to formulary compliance and drug utilization review (DUR).']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to determine member\'s eligibility span at service date, supporting CMS reporting and accurate cost‑share calculations.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claim.header. Business justification: Required for integrated care reporting: links pharmacy claims to the corresponding medical claim header for episode‑of‑care analysis and coordinated benefits calculations.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this pharmacy claim was adjudicated. Links to the plan and benefit product. Determines formulary, cost-sharing rules, and CMS Part D contract identification.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Required for Claim Adjudication Posting process: each pharmacy claim expense must be posted to a GL ledger for financial reporting and regulatory audit.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate reference to the health plan member who is the beneficiary of this pharmacy claim. Links to the member master record in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT). Required for eligibility verification, coordination of benefits (COB), and CMS Part D risk adjustment (RAF/HCC).']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Risk‑adjusted claim adjudication report requires each claim to reference the member\'s risk score to apply CMS risk adjustment factors.']
  pbm_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Source system transaction identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) at the time of adjudication. Used for claim traceability, PBM reconciliation, EDI 835 remittance matching, and pharmacy audit trail. Enables lineage back to the operational system of record.']
  prescriber_credential_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Claim adjudication requires verifying prescriber credentials per CMS regulations; linking claim to credential_record enables automated credential status checks.']
  prior_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate reference to the prior authorization record that approved dispensing of this drug, if applicable. Null when no PA was required. Links to the utilization management PA product. Required for specialty drug claims and step therapy override tracking.']
  processor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Claim Processing Audit Report to track which employee adjudicated each pharmacy claim.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate reference to the prescribing provider who authored the prescription. Links to the provider master record in the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource). Used for prescriber profiling, fraud/waste/abuse (FWA) detection, and HEDIS measure attribution.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Provides audit link between each pharmacy claim and the enrollment transaction that established coverage, needed for reconciliation and regulatory audit.']
  um_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_case. Business justification: Claims are associated with utilization‑management cases for case‑level cost analysis and outcome tracking, required by case management dashboards.']
  adjudication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the pharmacy claim was adjudicated (approved or rejected) by the PBM system. Distinct from fill_date (dispensing event). Used for claims processing SLA measurement, 835 remittance matching, and audit trail compliance.']
  basis_of_cost_determination varchar [note: 'Code indicating the pricing benchmark used to determine the ingredient cost for this claim. AWP = Average Wholesale Price; WAC = Wholesale Acquisition Cost; MAC = Maximum Allowable Cost; usual_and_customary = pharmacy\'s standard retail price; negotiated = contracted rate; 340B = 340B program pricing; other = alternative basis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: awp|wac|mac|usual_and_customary|negotiated|340b|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable claim reference number assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) at adjudication. Used for member communications, Explanation of Benefits (EOB), and 835 remittance reconciliation. Maps to NCPDP D.0 transaction reference number.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current adjudication lifecycle status of the pharmacy claim as determined by the PBM system. \'paid\' = claim adjudicated and payment issued; \'rejected\' = claim denied with reject code; \'reversed\' = previously paid claim reversed; \'adjusted\' = claim reprocessed with changed amounts; \'pending\' = awaiting adjudication; \'void\' = cancelled before processing.']
  cob_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether coordination of benefits (COB) was applied on this claim (True = COB applied, another payer was involved). Triggers COB segment processing and other_payer_amount population. Used for COB reconciliation and CMS Part D TrOOP calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacy claim record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and HIPAA record retention requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  daw_code varchar [note: 'NCPDP Dispense As Written (DAW) code indicating whether a brand drug was dispensed when a generic was available, and the reason. 0 = no product selection indicated; 1 = substitution not allowed by prescriber; 2 = substitution allowed, patient requested brand; 3 = substitution allowed, pharmacist selected brand; 4 = substitution allowed, generic not in stock; 5 = substitution allowed, brand dispensed as generic. Used for generic substitution rate analytics and formulary compliance.']
  days_supply varchar [note: 'Number of days of medication supply dispensed on this claim as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for refill-too-soon edits, medication adherence (MPR/PDC) calculations, HEDIS pharmacy measures, and CMS Part D days supply validation.']
  deductible_applied decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of this pharmacy claim applied toward the member\'s pharmacy or combined medical/pharmacy deductible. Relevant for HDHP/HSA plans and CMS Part D deductible phase tracking. Contributes to OOP/MOOP accumulation.']
  dispensing_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the dispensing pharmacy for the act of dispensing the prescription, separate from ingredient cost. Negotiated as part of the PBM network contract. Used for PBM contract reconciliation and total claim cost analysis.']
  dispensing_pharmacy_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the dispensing pharmacy as submitted on the pharmacy claim. Denormalized for claim-level auditability and NCPDP D.0 compliance. Used for pharmacy network validation, audit trail, and CMS Part D encounter data.']
  dur_outcome_code varchar [note: 'NCPDP standard code indicating the outcome of the prospective drug utilization review (DUR) edit performed during adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, duplicate therapy, dose check). Null if no DUR alert was triggered. Critical for pharmacy safety and regulatory compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — NCPDP DUR outcome codes exceed 6 values]']
  fill_date date [note: 'The date the prescription was dispensed by the pharmacy to the member. This is the principal real-world business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). Used for days supply calculations, refill-too-soon edits, and CMS Part D encounter data date of service reporting.']
  formulary_tier varchar [note: 'Formulary tier assigned to the dispensed drug at the time of adjudication. Determines member cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) and plan benefit design. Tier 1 = preferred generic; Tier 2 = generic; Tier 3 = preferred brand; Tier 4 = non-preferred brand; Tier 5 = specialty; non_formulary = not on formulary.']
  ingredient_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'The cost of the drug ingredient(s) as billed by the dispensing pharmacy, before dispensing fee and other adjustments. Basis may be AWP, WAC, MAC, or negotiated rate. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for pharmacy claim financials. Used for PBM contract reconciliation and MLR calculation.']
  is_340b_claim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacy claim was dispensed under the 340B Drug Pricing Program (True = 340B claim). Used for 340B duplicate discount prevention, HRSA compliance reporting, and separation of 340B and non-340B claims in financial reconciliation.']
  is_compound_claim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this claim is for a compounded drug product containing multiple ingredients (True = compound claim). Compound claims require line-level detail in the pharmacy_claim_line product. Used for compound drug utilization review and cost management.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Line of business under which this pharmacy claim was adjudicated. Drives regulatory reporting requirements, formulary rules, and cost-sharing structures. \'commercial\' = employer-sponsored or individual; \'medicare_part_d\' = CMS Medicare Part D; \'medicaid\' = state Medicaid; \'exchange\' = ACA marketplace QHP; \'cobra\' = COBRA continuation coverage.']
  member_coinsurance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Percentage-based cost-sharing amount paid by the member for this pharmacy claim, calculated as a percentage of the allowed cost. Applies when the benefit design uses coinsurance rather than fixed copay. Contributes to OOP/MOOP accumulation.']
  member_copay decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount paid by the member at point of sale as their cost-sharing obligation for this pharmacy claim. Determined by formulary tier and benefit design. Contributes to member out-of-pocket (OOP) and maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) accumulation.']
  ndc varchar [note: '11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product dispensed, including labeler, product, and package segment. Denormalized from the drug master for claim-level auditability and CMS Part D encounter data submission (EDPS). Required for 340B duplicate discount prevention and drug utilization review (DUR).']
  original_claim_number varchar [note: 'Claim number of the original pharmacy claim that this record adjusts or reverses. Populated only for adjusted or reversed claims; null for original claims. Enables claim adjustment chain tracing for financial reconciliation, audit, and CMS Part D encounter data correction submissions.']
  other_payer_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount paid by another payer (secondary insurer, Medicare, Medicaid, manufacturer coupon) under coordination of benefits (COB). Used for COB reconciliation, 340B duplicate discount prevention, and CMS Part D TrOOP (True Out-of-Pocket) calculations.']
  pharmacy_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the drug was dispensed. Drives cost-sharing differentials, formulary tier rules, and network adequacy reporting. \'retail\' = community pharmacy; \'mail_order\' = 90-day mail service; \'specialty\' = specialty pharmacy for high-cost/complex drugs; \'long_term_care\' = SNF/LTC pharmacy; \'home_infusion\' = infusion therapy at home.']
  plan_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by the health plan for this pharmacy claim after applying member cost-sharing (copay, coinsurance, deductible) and COB adjustments. Net total component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for 835 remittance, MLR reporting, and CMS Part D encounter data.']
  prescriber_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing provider as submitted on the pharmacy claim. Denormalized for claim-level auditability, NCPDP D.0 compliance, and CMS Part D encounter data submission. Used for prescriber fraud/waste/abuse (FWA) analytics.']
  prescription_written_date date [note: 'Date the prescription was written by the prescriber. Used to validate prescription age, enforce days-supply-from-written-date rules, and support medication adherence analytics. Distinct from fill_date (dispensing date).']
  quantity_dispensed decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of the drug dispensed (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters, grams) as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for quantity limit (QL) edits, drug utilization review (DUR), and CMS Part D encounter data submission.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'NCPDP standard unit of measure code for the quantity dispensed. EA = each (tablets/capsules); ML = milliliters; GM = grams; UN = units; F2 = international units; ME = milliequivalents. Required for accurate quantity limit (QL) edit enforcement and CMS Part D encounter data.']
