  rems_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specialty drugs in this program are subject to an FDA-mandated Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS). When true, dispensing requires REMS-certified pharmacy and prescriber enrollment.']
  specialty_drug_list_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specialty drug list (SDL) associated with this program, identifying the set of National Drug Codes (NDCs) covered under the program. Managed in the PBM formulary system.']
  specialty_pharmacy_network_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specialty pharmacy network designated to dispense drugs under this program. Defines the restricted network of pharmacies eligible to fill specialty prescriptions.']
  specialty_tier_designation varchar [note: 'Formulary tier designation assigned to specialty drugs in this program. Determines member cost-sharing obligations and CMS Part D specialty tier reporting requirements.']
  step_therapy_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step therapy protocols are enforced for drugs in this program, requiring members to try and fail on preferred therapies before accessing specialty drugs. Governed by state step therapy laws and CMS guidance.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the specialty drug program is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended program with no scheduled end. Used for plan year transitions and CMS Part D reporting.']
  therapeutic_category varchar [note: 'Primary therapeutic or disease category associated with the specialty drugs in this program (e.g., Oncology, Rheumatology, Multiple Sclerosis, HIV/AIDS). Used for clinical reporting, HEDIS measures, and population health analytics.']
  wac_discount_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Contracted discount percentage applied to the Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) for specialty drugs in this program. Used as an alternative pricing basis to AWP in PBM contract negotiations.']

  Note: 'Master record for specialty pharmacy programs managing high-cost, complex specialty drugs and associated patient affordability programs. Captures program name, specialty drug list (NDCs), specialty pharmacy network, clinical management requirements, REMS flag, cold chain handling, hub services enrollment, and program effective dates. Owns all copay assistance and affordability program tracking: manufacturer copay assistance programs (copay cards), patient assistance programs (PAP), foundation grants, copay accumulator adjustment program (CAAP) interactions, benefit amounts applied, accumulator adjustment flags, and coordination with member out-of-pocket (OOP) accumulators. This is the SSOT for specialty drug management, affordability program administration, accumulator adjustment compliance, and CMS Part D specialty tier reporting.'
}

Table pharmacy.mtm_service {
  mtm_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mtm_service']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Part D prescription drug plan (PDP) or Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug (MA-PD) plan under which the member is enrolled and MTM eligibility is determined.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan member who received the MTM service. Links to the member master record in the core administration platform (Facets/QNXT).']
  mtm_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific MTM program offering under which this service was delivered. A plan sponsor may operate multiple MTM programs targeting different chronic condition cohorts.']
  chronic_condition_count varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying chronic conditions the member has at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of chronic conditions (typically 2-3 depending on plan criteria). Used for eligibility validation and population stratification.']
  cmr_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the annual Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) was completed for the member. CMS requires at least one CMR per calendar year for all enrolled MTM beneficiaries. Directly drives the CMS STAR Ratings MTM CMR Completion Rate measure.']
  cms_reporting_period varchar [note: 'CMS Part D reporting period to which this MTM service record is attributed (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-ANNUAL). Used for CMS Part D MTM program reporting submissions and STAR ratings measurement year alignment.']
  contact_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Total number of outreach attempts made to the member to schedule or deliver the MTM service. CMS requires documentation of good-faith efforts to reach enrolled members. Used for compliance audits and outreach effectiveness analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MTM service record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  disenrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the member was disenrolled or opted out of the MTM program. Null if the member remains enrolled. Used to track program retention and calculate MTM completion rates.']
  drug_therapy_problem_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of drug therapy problem identified during the MTM service. Common types include: unnecessary drug therapy, needs additional drug therapy, ineffective drug, dosage too low, dosage too high, adverse drug reaction, non-adherence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UNNECESSARY_DRUG|NEEDS_ADDITIONAL_DRUG|INEFFECTIVE_DRUG|DOSE_TOO_LOW|DOSE_TOO_HIGH|ADVERSE_REACTION|NON_ADHERENCE|NONE — promote to reference product]']
  dur_outcome_code varchar [note: 'NCPDP-standard Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code documenting the result of the pharmacist\'s clinical intervention during the MTM service (e.g., 1A = filled as is, 1B = filled with prescriber approval, 1C = not filled). Sourced from RxClaim or Argus PBM system DUR transaction records.']
  eligibility_determination_date date [note: 'Date on which the member was determined to be eligible for the MTM program based on CMS Part D criteria (multiple chronic conditions, multiple Part D drugs, likely to incur annual costs above the CMS threshold).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the eligible member was enrolled into the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment of all eligible beneficiaries with opt-out capability.']
  estimated_annual_drug_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated annual cost of Part D covered drugs for the member at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS requires members likely to incur annual drug costs above the established threshold to be enrolled. Expressed in USD.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the follow-up contact or service following the MTM encounter. Null if no follow-up is required or scheduled. Used to track closure of open MAP items and clinical recommendations.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up contact or service is required after the current MTM encounter based on clinical findings or unresolved MAP items. True = follow-up required; False = no follow-up needed.']
  follow_up_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the follow-up action required after the MTM service. PENDING = follow-up scheduled but not yet completed; COMPLETED = follow-up successfully conducted; DECLINED = member declined follow-up; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted for follow-up; NOT_REQUIRED = no follow-up needed.']
  intervention_count varchar [note: 'Total number of clinical interventions or recommendations made during the MTM service encounter (e.g., drug therapy problem resolutions, adherence recommendations, prescriber referrals). Used for quality reporting and pharmacist productivity analytics.']
  last_contact_attempt_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent outreach attempt made to the member for MTM scheduling or delivery. Used alongside contact_attempt_count to document good-faith outreach efforts for CMS compliance.']
  map_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of action items documented in the Medication Action Plan (MAP) provided to the member. Each MAP item represents a specific recommendation or goal agreed upon between the pharmacist and the member.']
  map_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a written Medication Action Plan (MAP) was provided to the member following the CMR. CMS requires a standardized MAP to be delivered to the member and their prescriber(s) after each CMR. True = MAP provided; False = MAP not yet provided.']
  member_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member provided consent to participate in the MTM service and for sharing of clinical findings with their prescriber(s). True = consent obtained; False = consent not obtained or pending. Required for HIPAA compliance when sharing PHI with prescribers.']
  opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted out of the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment with opt-out capability. True = member has opted out; False = member remains enrolled. When True, disenrollment_date should be populated.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the member\'s opt-out or disenrollment from the MTM program. Null if the member has not opted out. Used for program retention analysis and CMS reporting.']
  part_d_drug_count varchar [note: 'Number of Part D covered drugs the member is taking at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of Part D drugs (typically 8 or more). Used for eligibility validation.']
  pbm_member_code varchar [note: 'Member identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) used to cross-reference the MTM record with pharmacy claims and formulary data.']
  personal_medication_list_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Personal Medication List (PML) was provided to the member as part of the CMR standardized format. CMS requires both a MAP and a PML to be delivered following each CMR. True = PML provided; False = PML not provided.']
  pharmacist_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the pharmacist or clinical pharmacist who delivered the MTM service, including Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) or Targeted Medication Review (TMR). Required for CMS Part D MTM reporting.']
  pharmacist_recommendation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical notes documenting the pharmacist\'s recommendations, findings, and interventions during the MTM service encounter. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI) and must be handled in accordance with HIPAA Privacy Rule requirements.']
  prescriber_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings and MAP items. Null if notification has not yet been sent. Used for compliance tracking against CMS timelines.']
  prescriber_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member\'s prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings, recommendations, or MAP items. CMS requires that the MAP and relevant clinical findings be shared with the prescriber. True = prescriber notified; False = notification pending or not completed.']
  service_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the MTM service (CMR, TMR, or follow-up) was delivered to the member. This is the principal real-world event date used for CMS Part D MTM reporting and STAR ratings quality measure calculations.']
  service_delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Mode through which the MTM service was delivered to the member. CMS requires CMRs to be offered as interactive, person-to-person consultations (in-person or telehealth). Supports channel-level analytics and compliance verification.']
  service_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the MTM service encounter in minutes. Used for pharmacist productivity reporting, billing reconciliation with PBM, and quality analytics. CMR encounters typically range from 30 to 60 minutes.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MTM service record. SCHEDULED = appointment set; IN_PROGRESS = service initiated; COMPLETED = service fully delivered and documented; DECLINED = member declined participation; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted; CANCELLED = service cancelled prior to delivery.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MTM service delivered. CMR = Comprehensive Medication Review (annual face-to-face or telehealth); TMR = Targeted Medication Review (quarterly or as needed); FOLLOW_UP = follow-up contact on prior CMR/TMR; REFERRAL = referral to prescriber or specialist; OTHER = other MTM-related service.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this MTM service record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across PBM (RxClaim/Argus) and Care Management (Casenet TruCare/Altruista) platforms.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating source system (RxClaim, Argus, Casenet TruCare, or Altruista) for this MTM service record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  star_measure_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MTM service record is eligible to be counted toward the CMS STAR Ratings MTM Completion Rate for Comprehensive Medication Reviews (CMR) quality measure. True = eligible for STAR measure numerator/denominator; False = excluded from STAR measure calculation.']
  tmr_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Targeted Medication Review (TMR) was conducted. TMRs are performed at least quarterly for enrolled members who have not yet received a CMR or as clinically indicated. Null if no TMR has been performed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MTM service record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, incremental processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Record for Medication Therapy Management (MTM) services delivered to eligible members, as required under CMS Part D. Captures member eligibility criteria, MTM program enrollment date, comprehensive medication review (CMR) completion date, targeted medication review (TMR) date, pharmacist NPI, interventions recommended, medication action plan (MAP) items, follow-up status, and outcomes. Supports CMS Part D MTM reporting and STAR ratings quality measures.'
}

Table pharmacy.formulary_exception {
  formulary_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for formulary_exception']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated appeal record if the member or prescriber has filed an appeal against a denied formulary exception. Links to the appeals management entity. Null if no appeal has been filed.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Formulary exception requests are reviewed under regulatory obligations; linking captures which obligation governs each exception.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical pharmacist, pharmacy technician, or medical director who reviewed and rendered the decision on the formulary exception request. Links to the workforce/staff entity.']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific formulary against which the exception is being evaluated. A plan may maintain multiple formularies (e.g., commercial, Medicare Part D, Medicaid). Links to the formulary master in the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus).']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health benefit plan under which the formulary exception is being requested. Determines the applicable formulary, tier structure, and exception adjudication rules.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan member on whose behalf the formulary exception request is submitted. Links to the member master record in the core administration platform (Facets/QNXT).']
  pbm_transaction_id varchar [note: 'External transaction identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) to this formulary exception request. Used for reconciliation between the health plan\'s internal records and the PBM system of record.']
  prior_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an associated Prior Authorization (PA) request in the UM system (InterQual/MCG) when the formulary exception is linked to or triggered by a PA requirement. Null if no PA is associated.']
  appeal_rights_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the appeal rights notification was sent to the member and prescriber following a denial. Required for CMS Part D compliance tracking and audit purposes.']
  appeal_rights_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required appeal rights notification was sent to the member and prescriber following a denial decision, as mandated by CMS Part D regulations. True = notification sent; False = notification not yet sent.']
  clinical_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical rationale provided by the prescriber or member supporting the formulary exception request. May include diagnosis, treatment history, contraindications to formulary alternatives, or medical necessity documentation. Treated as confidential clinical information.']
  cms_coverage_determination_type varchar [note: 'CMS-defined classification of the coverage determination process type for Part D reporting. Standard: routine 72-hour review; Expedited: 24-hour urgent review; Reopen: plan-initiated reconsideration; Revised: updated determination on a prior decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the formulary exception record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  current_drug_tier varchar [note: 'The formulary tier at which the requested drug is currently placed (or non_formulary if not on the formulary) at the time the exception request is submitted. Used to assess the tier differential and cost-sharing impact of the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|tier_4|tier_5|specialty|non_formulary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  days_supply_requested varchar [note: 'Number of days of drug supply requested in the formulary exception, applicable for quantity limit or day supply override exceptions. Standard values are 30, 60, or 90 days.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the final determination (approval or denial) was made on the formulary exception request. Used to calculate turnaround time and assess CMS timeliness compliance.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the exception determination was rendered. Enables exact measurement of response time against CMS 72-hour standard and 24-hour expedited decision deadlines.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for denial of the formulary exception request (e.g., formulary alternative available, insufficient clinical documentation, drug not medically necessary). Null for approved or pending requests. Used in EOB and appeal rights notifications.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable explanation of the reason for denial of the formulary exception request. Included in the written notice of denial sent to the member and prescriber as required by CMS Part D regulations.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'International Classification of Diseases (ICD-10-CM) diagnosis code supporting the medical necessity of the requested drug. Provided by the prescriber as part of the clinical justification for the exception. Treated as confidential health information.']
  drug_name varchar [note: 'Brand or generic name of the drug for which the formulary exception is requested, as submitted by the requestor. Stored for human-readable reference alongside the NDC code.']
  drug_ndc varchar [note: '11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) of the specific drug product for which the formulary exception is requested. Identifies the labeler, product, and package configuration at the NDC level.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date through which an approved formulary exception remains valid. After this date, the member must reapply or the standard formulary restriction resumes. Null for open-ended approvals or non-approved requests.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which an approved formulary exception is valid and the member may obtain the drug at the excepted tier or without the formulary restriction. Null for denied or pending requests.']
  exception_request_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the formulary exception request at intake. Used by members, prescribers, and pharmacies to track the status of the request. Generated by the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus) or UM system.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the formulary exception request. Pending: under review; Approved: exception granted; Denied: exception not granted; Withdrawn: requestor cancelled; Expired: approved exception past effective period; Appealed: denial under appeal review.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formulary exception request indicating the specific restriction being challenged. Values: non_formulary (drug not on formulary), tier_exception (request lower cost-sharing tier), pa_exception (override Prior Authorization requirement), step_therapy_exception (bypass step therapy protocol), quantity_limit_exception (override quantity/day supply limit), coverage_determination (standard CMS Part D coverage determination). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_formulary|tier_exception|pa_exception|step_therapy_exception|quantity_limit_exception|coverage_determination — promote to reference product]']
  formulary_alternative_ndc varchar [note: '11-digit NDC of the formulary-covered alternative drug suggested by the plan when denying a non-formulary or tier exception request. Required by CMS to be communicated to the member and prescriber in the denial notice.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the formulary exception request was submitted and processed under the expedited (24-hour) review pathway, as opposed to the standard (72-hour) pathway. Expedited requests require prescriber attestation that the standard timeframe would seriously jeopardize the member\'s life, health, or ability to regain maximum function.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Line of Business (LOB) under which the formulary exception is processed. Determines applicable regulatory framework, formulary rules, and exception adjudication standards (e.g., CMS Part D rules apply only to Medicare Part D LOB).']
  prescriber_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or authorized prescriber who submitted or supports the formulary exception request. Required for CMS Part D coverage determination documentation.']
  quantity_requested decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of the drug (in dispensing units) for which the exception is requested, applicable for quantity limit exception requests. Expressed in the drug\'s standard dispensing unit (e.g., tablets, milliliters).']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the formulary exception request was submitted. Values: phone (member/prescriber call), fax (fax submission), portal (member or prescriber web portal), edi (electronic data interchange transaction), mail (paper/postal), prescriber_portal (dedicated prescriber e-prescribing or PBM portal).']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the formulary exception request was formally submitted by the member, prescriber, or authorized representative. This is the principal business event date used for CMS timeliness compliance tracking (72-hour standard, 24-hour expedited).']
  request_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the formulary exception request was received and logged in the PBM or UM system. Used for exact timeliness measurement against CMS regulatory response deadlines.']
  requested_drug_tier varchar [note: 'The formulary cost-sharing tier to which the requestor is asking the drug to be assigned for this member (e.g., requesting Tier 2 generic pricing for a Tier 3 preferred brand). Applicable for tier exception requests.']
  requestor_type varchar [note: 'Identifies who initiated the formulary exception request. Member: the enrollee themselves; Prescriber: the treating physician or authorized prescriber; Authorized Representative: a legally designated representative acting on behalf of the member; Pharmacy: the dispensing pharmacy acting on behalf of the member.']
  review_level varchar [note: 'Level of clinical review applied to the formulary exception request. Pharmacist: initial review by clinical pharmacist; Medical Director: escalated review by physician medical director; Peer to Peer: prescriber-to-medical-director discussion; Committee: pharmacy and therapeutics committee review.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this formulary exception record originated. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer. Values: rxclaim, argus (PBM systems), interqual, mcg (UM systems), facets, qnxt (core admin), manual (manually entered). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rxclaim|argus|interqual|mcg|facets|qnxt|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  step_therapy_drugs_tried varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of NDCs or drug names of step therapy prerequisite drugs that the member has already tried and failed, as documented in the step therapy exception request. Supports clinical review and CMS step therapy override requirements.']
  supporting_doc_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required clinical supporting documentation (e.g., prescriber letter of medical necessity, lab results, treatment history) has been received from the prescriber or member. True = documentation received; False = documentation pending or not received.']
  supporting_doc_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the required clinical supporting documentation was received from the prescriber or member. Used to calculate the effective review start date and CMS timeliness compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the formulary exception record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks status changes, decision updates, and documentation additions throughout the exception lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for member or prescriber requests to cover a non-formulary drug or override a formulary restriction (tier exception, PA exception, step therapy exception). Captures request date, member ID, prescriber NPI, requested drug NDC, exception type, clinical justification, decision status (approved/denied), effective date range, and appeal rights notification. Supports CMS Part D coverage determination and exception request requirements.'
}

Table pharmacy.part_d_submission {
  part_d_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CMS Part D regulatory data submission record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  plan_benefit_package_id varchar [note: 'The CMS-assigned Plan Benefit Package identifier associated with this submission. Distinguishes specific benefit packages within a Part D contract for formulary, cost-sharing, and coverage gap reporting.']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Part D submissions are a type of regulatory filing; linking to regulatory_submission records submission details and compliance tracking.']
  accepted_record_count varchar [note: 'The number of records within the submission batch that CMS accepted and processed successfully. Derived from the CMS acknowledgment response file. Used to calculate acceptance rate and identify partial rejections.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned batch identifier assigned by the CMS submission system (RAPS, EDPS, or CGDP portal) for the submission file. Used to correlate records across the submission lifecycle and match CMS acknowledgment responses.']
  benefit_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit calendar year of the Medicare Part D benefit period to which this submission applies. Drives payment year reconciliation, risk adjustment settlement, and DIR reporting cycles with CMS.']
  cgdp_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total dollar amount invoiced under the Coverage Gap Discount Program for this submission, applicable to CGDP submission types. Represents manufacturer discount obligations for brand-name drugs dispensed to Part D beneficiaries in the coverage gap.']
  clearinghouse_tracking_number varchar [note: 'The tracking or reference identifier assigned by the EDI clearinghouse (Availity or Change Healthcare) upon receipt of the submission file. Used to trace submission status through the clearinghouse to CMS.']
  cms_contract_number varchar [note: 'The CMS-assigned Part D plan contract identifier (e.g., H1234 or S5678) under which this submission is filed. Identifies the health plan\'s Medicare Part D contract for regulatory reporting and payment purposes.']
  cms_region_code varchar [note: 'The CMS regional office code associated with the Part D contract under which this submission is filed. Used for regulatory routing and regional compliance reporting.']
  cms_response_code varchar [note: 'The acknowledgment or disposition code returned by CMS upon processing the submission batch. Maps to CMS-defined response code sets (e.g., RAPS response codes, EDPS acknowledgment codes). Used for automated error triage and compliance tracking.']
  cms_response_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the CMS acknowledgment or response file was received by the health plan. Used to calculate CMS processing turnaround time and to trigger downstream reconciliation workflows.']
  cms_response_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the CMS response code returned for this submission. Provides context for the acceptance, rejection, or error condition reported by CMS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this Part D submission record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the record audit created timestamp for data lineage and ETL traceability.']
  dir_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total Direct and Indirect Remuneration dollar amount reported in this submission, applicable to DIR submission types. Represents post-point-of-sale price concessions, rebates, and other remuneration from manufacturers and pharmacies that reduce net drug costs. Required for CMS Part D DIR reconciliation.']
  edi_interchange_control_number varchar [note: 'The ISA13 interchange control number from the EDI transmission envelope, applicable to EDI-formatted submissions. Used to correlate submission files with EDI gateway acknowledgments (997/999) from the clearinghouse or CMS.']
  error_detail varchar [note: 'Structured or free-text description of errors returned by CMS for rejected or partially accepted records. May include CMS error codes, field-level rejection reasons, and record-level identifiers. Supports error remediation and resubmission workflows.']
  hcc_record_count varchar [note: 'The number of Hierarchical Condition Category diagnosis records included in this submission batch, applicable to RAPS and EDPS submission types. Used to monitor HCC capture completeness and risk score optimization.']
  is_resubmission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is a resubmission of a previously rejected or corrected batch (True) or an original submission (False). Used to track correction cycles and measure first-pass acceptance rates.']
  is_timely_submission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission was transmitted to CMS on or before the regulatory deadline date (True) or after the deadline (False). Supports CMS compliance reporting and internal SLA monitoring.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'The Medicare Part D line of business associated with this submission. MAPD = Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug plan; PDP = standalone Prescription Drug Plan; EGWP = Employer Group Waiver Plan; LIS = Low Income Subsidy. Drives regulatory reporting segmentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, manual override justifications, or compliance annotations associated with this submission record. Used by compliance and operations teams to document exception handling, CMS communications, or remediation actions.']
  original_batch_number varchar [note: 'For resubmissions, the batch_id of the original submission that this record corrects or replaces. Null for original submissions. Enables lineage tracing across submission correction cycles.']
  pbm_system_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the source PBM system from which the submission data was extracted. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements. Values align to operational PBM platforms (RxClaim, Argus).']
  pde_record_count varchar [note: 'The number of Prescription Drug Event records included in this submission batch, applicable to PDE and EDPS submission types. PDE records are the foundational data element for CMS Part D payment and reconciliation.']
  pending_record_count varchar [note: 'The number of records within the submission batch that CMS has received but not yet finalized (e.g., held for additional review or in a processing queue). Supports real-time submission status monitoring.']
  raf_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated revenue impact in dollars attributable to the risk adjustment data submitted in this batch, applicable to RAPS and EDPS submission types. Supports risk adjustment revenue integrity analysis and HCC-based payment reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the submission was fully reconciled against the CMS response. Null if reconciliation is not yet complete. Used for SLA tracking and audit trail.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the reconciliation state of the submission against CMS response data. UNRECONCILED = CMS response not yet matched; IN_PROGRESS = reconciliation underway; RECONCILED = fully matched and closed; EXCEPTION = discrepancy identified requiring manual review.']
  rejected_record_count varchar [note: 'The number of records within the submission batch that CMS rejected. Derived from the CMS acknowledgment response file. Drives error remediation workflows and resubmission prioritization.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the reporting period covered by this Part D submission. Together with reporting_period_start_date, defines the data window for the submission batch.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the reporting period covered by this Part D submission. Used to align submission data with the applicable benefit year, payment year, or reconciliation cycle as defined by CMS.']
  resubmission_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of resubmission attempts for a given original batch. Value is 0 for original submissions, 1 for first resubmission, 2 for second, etc. Supports escalation workflows and CMS deadline compliance monitoring.']
  submission_date timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the submission batch was transmitted to CMS. Distinct from the record audit created timestamp. Used for timeliness compliance tracking against CMS filing deadlines.']
  submission_deadline_date date [note: 'The regulatory deadline date by which this submission must be received by CMS. Used to assess timeliness compliance and trigger escalation alerts for at-risk submissions.']
  submission_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the file transmitted to CMS for this submission batch. Used for audit trail, resubmission tracking, and reconciliation against CMS acknowledgment files.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'The electronic data interchange or file format used for the CMS submission. EDI_837 = ANSI X12 837 pharmacy claim transaction; FLAT_FILE = CMS-specified fixed-width flat file; XML = extensible markup language; CSV = comma-separated values.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the Part D submission. PENDING = prepared but not yet transmitted; SUBMITTED = transmitted to CMS; ACCEPTED = CMS confirmed full acceptance; REJECTED = CMS rejected the batch; PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED = CMS accepted a subset of records; RECONCILED = submission reconciled against CMS response and closed.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of CMS Part D regulatory submission. RAPS = Risk Adjustment Processing System pharmacy encounter; EDPS = Encounter Data Processing System; DIR = Direct and Indirect Remuneration report; CGDP = Coverage Gap Discount Program invoice; PDE = Prescription Drug Event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RAPS|EDPS|DIR|CGDP|PDE — promote to reference product if additional types are added]']
  submitter_name varchar [note: 'The name of the organization or entity submitting the Part D data to CMS. May be the health plan, TPA, or PBM acting on behalf of the plan. Supports audit trail and accountability tracking.']
  total_record_count varchar [note: 'The total number of records (encounters, claims, or line items) included in the submission batch as transmitted to CMS. Used as the control total for reconciliation against CMS acknowledgment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this Part D submission record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Used to detect record changes, support incremental processing, and maintain audit trail integrity.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking CMS Part D regulatory data submissions including RAPS (Risk Adjustment Processing System) pharmacy encounter submissions, EDPS (Encounter Data Processing System) submissions, Direct and Indirect Remuneration (DIR) reporting, and Coverage Gap Discount Program (CGDP) invoicing. Captures submission batch ID, submission type, submission date, reporting period, record count, accepted count, rejected count, CMS response code, error detail, reconciliation status, and resubmission flag. Supports CMS Part D compliance, risk adjustment revenue integrity, and regulatory filing audit trail.'
}

Table pharmacy.benefit_accumulator {
  benefit_accumulator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for benefit_accumulator']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the employer group or sponsor associated with the member\'s pharmacy benefit. Relevant for group-level accumulator reporting and ASO/TPA arrangements.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the health benefit plan under which the member is enrolled, determining the applicable deductible, OOP maximum, and Part D benefit phase thresholds.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the health plan member whose pharmacy benefit accumulators are being tracked. Links to the member master record in the enrollment system (Facets/QNXT).']
  pharmacy_claim_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the most recent pharmacy claim that triggered an update to this accumulator record. Supports audit trail and real-time adjudication reconciliation.']
  accumulator_reset_date date [note: 'Date on which the accumulator balances were last reset to zero, typically at the start of a new benefit year. Supports annual reconciliation and audit of accumulator lifecycle.']
  accumulator_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated or last updated this accumulator record (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM, Facets, QNXT). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.']
  accumulator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the accumulator record. \'active\' indicates in-use for real-time adjudication; \'suspended\' indicates member eligibility gap; \'closed\' indicates benefit period ended; \'pending_reset\' indicates awaiting annual reset; \'reconciled\' indicates CMS or plan reconciliation completed.']
  accumulator_version varchar [note: 'Optimistic locking version counter incremented with each update to the accumulator record. Supports concurrent adjudication event handling and prevents race conditions in real-time point-of-sale updates.']
  benefit_period_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the benefit year or coverage period to which this accumulator record applies. Defines the boundary for annual accumulator reset.']
  benefit_period_start_date date [note: 'First date of the benefit year or coverage period to which this accumulator record applies. Accumulators reset at the start of each new benefit period.']
  catastrophic_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative drug cost amounts applied toward the Medicare Part D catastrophic coverage threshold. Tracks member spend after exiting the coverage gap and entering catastrophic coverage.']
  catastrophic_limit_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Medicare Part D catastrophic coverage threshold amount for the current benefit year. Once TrOOP reaches this level, the member qualifies for catastrophic cost-sharing rates. Published annually by CMS.']
  cms_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the CMS annual Part D reconciliation process for this accumulator record. Tracks whether the accumulator data has been submitted to and accepted by CMS via RAPS/EDPS for risk adjustment and subsidy reconciliation.']
  coordination_of_benefits_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the coordination of benefits (COB) role of this plan for the member\'s pharmacy benefit. Determines how accumulator amounts are applied when multiple payers are involved.']
  coverage_gap_discount_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative manufacturer discount amounts applied to brand-name drugs while the member is in the Medicare Part D coverage gap (donut hole). These discounts count toward TrOOP under the ACA Coverage Gap Discount Program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accumulator record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  deductible_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount the member has applied toward their pharmacy deductible in the current benefit period. Updated with each pharmacy claim adjudication event. Used at point of sale to determine member cost-sharing responsibility.']
  deductible_limit_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total pharmacy deductible amount the member must satisfy before plan cost-sharing begins, as defined in the benefit plan design for the current benefit period.']
  eob_generated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Explanation of Benefits (EOB) has been generated for the member reflecting the current accumulator balances. Supports member communication and regulatory EOB delivery requirements.']
  family_deductible_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount applied toward the family-level pharmacy deductible for plans with embedded or aggregate family deductible structures. Applicable for commercial group plans with family coverage tiers.']
  family_oop_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative out-of-pocket spend applied toward the family-level MOOP for plans with family OOP limits. Tracks combined member cost-sharing across all family members enrolled under the same plan.']
  formulary_tier_applied varchar [note: 'The formulary drug tier level most recently applied during adjudication for this member\'s accumulator. Indicates the cost-sharing tier structure in effect, which affects deductible and OOP accumulation rates.']
  hsa_eligible_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of pharmacy cost-sharing that qualifies as HSA-eligible expenses under IRS rules for members enrolled in a High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). Supports member HSA contribution and tax reporting.']
  icl_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative total drug costs (plan paid plus member cost-sharing) applied toward the Medicare Part D Initial Coverage Limit (ICL). When this amount reaches the ICL threshold, the member enters the coverage gap (donut hole).']
  icl_limit_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Medicare Part D Initial Coverage Limit (ICL) threshold amount for the current benefit year, as published annually by CMS. Defines the boundary between the initial coverage phase and the coverage gap (donut hole).']
  is_deductible_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has fully satisfied their pharmacy deductible for the current benefit period. When true, plan cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) applies rather than full deductible responsibility.']
  is_moop_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has reached their maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) limit for the current benefit period. When true, the plan covers 100% of covered pharmacy costs for the remainder of the benefit period.']
  last_adjudication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent pharmacy claim adjudication event that updated this accumulator record. Critical for real-time point-of-sale cost-sharing calculation and concurrency control.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'The line of business (LOB) under which the member\'s pharmacy benefit is administered. Determines applicable regulatory framework, benefit phase rules, and accumulator logic (e.g., Part D coverage gap applies only to Medicare Part D).']
  lis_copay_tier varchar [note: 'The applicable LIS copay tier for the member, determining the fixed copay amounts for generic, preferred brand, non-preferred, and specialty drugs under the Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) program.']
  lis_level varchar [note: 'Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) eligibility level for Medicare Part D members, determining reduced cost-sharing amounts. \'full\' indicates 100% subsidy; \'partial_1/2/3\' indicate graduated subsidy tiers; \'none\' indicates no LIS. Affects TrOOP accumulation rules.']
  mail_order_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative cost-sharing amount applied for drugs dispensed through mail-order pharmacy channels in the current benefit period. Supports mail-order utilization reporting and member EOB generation.']
  moop_limit_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) dollar threshold the member must reach before the plan covers 100% of covered pharmacy costs for the remainder of the benefit period. Set per plan design and ACA/CMS requirements.']
  oop_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative out-of-pocket (OOP) spend by the member for pharmacy benefits in the current benefit period, including deductible, copays, and coinsurance. Used to track progress toward the maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) limit.']
  part_d_benefit_phase varchar [note: 'Current Medicare Part D benefit phase for the member based on accumulated TrOOP and ICL amounts. Drives cost-sharing calculation at point of sale. \'not_applicable\' for non-Part D lines of business.']
  pbm_member_code varchar [note: 'Member identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus). Used to cross-reference accumulator balances between the health plan and PBM for reconciliation.']
  rx_claim_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pharmacy claims adjudicated and applied to this accumulator record in the current benefit period. Used for utilization reporting and EOB generation.']
  specialty_drug_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative cost-sharing amount applied specifically for specialty tier drugs in the current benefit period. Tracked separately to support specialty pharmacy reporting, prior authorization analytics, and plan design evaluation.']
  third_party_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount paid by third parties (e.g., manufacturer copay assistance programs, other insurance) toward the member\'s pharmacy cost-sharing. Tracked separately as these amounts may not count toward TrOOP under CMS rules.']
  troop_applied_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative true out-of-pocket (TrOOP) expenditure for Medicare Part D members, counting only qualifying cost-sharing amounts toward the catastrophic coverage threshold. Excludes amounts paid by third parties not qualifying under CMS TrOOP rules. Required for CMS Part D benefit phase determination.']
  troop_limit_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual TrOOP threshold amount that, when reached, transitions a Medicare Part D member into catastrophic coverage. Published annually by CMS as part of the Part D benefit parameters.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accumulator record was most recently modified, reflecting the latest claim adjudication event or manual adjustment. Supports concurrency control and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Tracks member-level pharmacy benefit accumulator balances including deductible applied, out-of-pocket (OOP) spend, maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) progress, true out-of-pocket (TrOOP) for Medicare Part D, initial coverage limit (ICL) progress, and catastrophic coverage threshold. Updated in real-time with each pharmacy claim adjudication event. Supports real-time cost-sharing calculation at point of sale, coverage gap (donut hole) determination for Part D members, low-income subsidy (LIS) cost-sharing adjustments, and member Explanation of Benefits (EOB) generation. Critical for CMS Part D benefit phase tracking and annual reconciliation.'
}

Table pharmacy.pharmacy_contract {
  pharmacy_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacy contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  dispensing_pharmacy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dispensing pharmacy or pharmacy chain entity in the pharmacy master. Links this contract to the specific pharmacy party.']
  mac_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MAC pricing list applied under this contract for generic and multi-source drug reimbursement. References the specific MAC schedule version used by the PBM or health plan. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is mac.']
  ncpdp_provider_id varchar [note: '7-digit NCPDP pharmacy identifier used in pharmacy claim transactions (NCPDP Telecom D.0) and PBM adjudication. Complements the NPI for pharmacy-specific identification.']
  pbm_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PBM contract under which this pharmacy dispensing agreement is administered, when the contract is PBM-administered rather than direct. Null for direct health-plan contracts.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per network; linking contracts to provider_network enables contract‑level reporting, tiering, and compliance tracking.']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Type of accreditation required or held by the pharmacy under this contract. URAC and ACHC are common for specialty pharmacies. PCAB (Pharmacy Compounding Accreditation Board) for compounding pharmacies. Relevant for specialty and mail-order contracts.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Contractually stipulated frequency at which the health plan or PBM may conduct pharmacy audits. Applicable when audit_rights_indicator is true.']
  audit_rights_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health plan or PBM retains the right to conduct on-site or desk audits of the pharmacy\'s dispensing records under this contract. Required for CMS Part D compliance.']
  auto_renewal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless either party provides notice of termination within the required notice period.']
  awp_discount_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contracted discount percentage applied to the drug\'s Average Wholesale Price (AWP) to determine ingredient cost reimbursement for brand and generic drugs. Expressed as a positive percentage (e.g., 16.50 = AWP minus 16.50%). Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is awp_discount.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the health plan or PBM. Used in correspondence, audits, and regulatory filings. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this agreement.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pharmacy contract. Active contracts are eligible for claim adjudication. Suspended contracts are temporarily ineligible. Terminated/expired contracts are closed. Drives network eligibility checks in RxClaim/Argus.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractual relationship: direct (health plan contracts directly with pharmacy), pbm_administered (PBM manages on plan\'s behalf), chain (multi-location chain agreement), independent (single-location independent pharmacy), mail_order, or specialty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|pbm_administered|chain|independent|mail_order|specialty — promote to reference product]']
  credentialing_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy must complete and maintain credentialing requirements (state licensure, DEA registration, accreditation) as a condition of this contract.']
  days_supply_mail_max varchar [note: 'Maximum days supply per mail-order prescription fill authorized under this contract (typically 90 days). Drives adjudication edits in RxClaim/Argus.']
  days_supply_retail_max varchar [note: 'Maximum days supply per retail prescription fill authorized under this contract (typically 30 or 34 days). Claims exceeding this limit may be rejected during adjudication.']
  dispensing_channel varchar [note: 'The dispensing channel(s) authorized under this contract. Retail covers walk-in pharmacies; mail_order covers 90-day supply dispensing; specialty covers high-cost/complex drugs; long_term_care covers SNF/LTC facilities; home_infusion covers IV therapy.']
  dispensing_fee_mail_order decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid per mail-order prescription dispensed. Typically lower than retail due to higher volume and automation. Applicable for mail-order and specialty pharmacy contracts.']
  dispensing_fee_retail decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid to the pharmacy per retail prescription dispensed, in addition to ingredient cost reimbursement. A core financial term of the pharmacy contract.']
  dispensing_fee_specialty decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid per specialty drug prescription dispensed. Specialty drugs require enhanced clinical services, justifying a separate fee schedule.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy contract becomes binding and the pharmacy is eligible to dispense claims under this agreement. Used for network eligibility validation during adjudication.']
  executed_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy contract was fully executed (signed by all parties). May precede the effective_date if there is a future start date.']
  generic_awp_discount_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contracted AWP discount percentage specifically for generic drugs, which typically carries a deeper discount than brand drugs. Expressed as a positive percentage. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is awp_discount and a separate generic rate is negotiated.']
  generic_dispensing_rate_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contracted minimum generic dispensing rate (GDR) the pharmacy must achieve as a performance requirement. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.00 = 85%). Supports CMS Part D Star Ratings and cost management objectives.']
  ingredient_cost_basis varchar [note: 'The pricing methodology used to calculate ingredient cost reimbursement to the pharmacy. AWP (Average Wholesale Price) discount, MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost) discount, NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or Usual and Customary (U&C). Core reimbursement term in the contract.']
  last_amended_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or addendum to this pharmacy contract. Used to track contract evolution and ensure the current fee schedule and terms are applied during adjudication.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'The line of business to which this pharmacy contract applies. Determines applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., CMS Part D rules for Medicare, state Medicaid rules). A contract may be scoped to one or multiple LOBs.']
  mail_order_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacy is authorized to dispense mail-order (90-day supply) prescriptions under this contract.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Network participation tier assigned to the pharmacy under this contract. Preferred pharmacies typically offer lower member cost-sharing. Drives member cost-sharing calculations and formulary benefit design.']
  part_d_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacy contract is applicable to Medicare Part D prescription drug benefit administration. When true, CMS Part D network adequacy, pricing, and reporting requirements apply.']
  performance_guarantee_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes performance guarantees (e.g., generic dispensing rate, medication adherence, fill accuracy). When true, performance_guarantee_details apply.']
  pharmacy_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned to the dispensing pharmacy by CMS. Used for claims adjudication, EDI transactions (NCPDP 5.1/D.0), and network directory publication.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacy contract record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pharmacy contract record was most recently updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  recoupment_provision_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes provisions allowing the health plan or PBM to recoup overpayments identified through audits or claims reviews.']
  source_system_contract_code varchar [note: 'Native contract identifier from the originating PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) or provider management system (Cactus/ProviderSource). Used for lineage tracing and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.']
  specialty_drug_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pharmacy is contracted and credentialed to dispense specialty drugs under this agreement. Specialty pharmacies must meet enhanced accreditation requirements (URAC, ACHC).']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code identifying the primary state jurisdiction governing this pharmacy contract. Determines applicable state DOI regulations, MAC transparency laws, and pharmacy contracting statutes.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy contract ends or was terminated. Null for open-ended contracts. Claims with service dates after this date are not eligible for reimbursement under this contract.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the contract without cause. Regulatory minimums vary by state DOI requirements.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the pharmacy contract document (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, Amendment 3). Tracks contract amendments and renegotiations over time for audit and compliance purposes.']
  wac_discount_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contracted discount percentage applied to the drug\'s Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) for ingredient cost reimbursement. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is wac_discount.']

  Note: 'Contractual record governing the reimbursement terms between the health plan (or PBM on its behalf) and individual dispensing pharmacies or pharmacy chains. Captures pharmacy NPI, network tier (preferred/standard/specialty), dispensing fee schedule, ingredient cost basis (AWP discount/MAC/WAC), effective date range, contract type (direct/PBM-administered), performance requirements, and audit provisions. Distinct from pbm_contract which governs the PBM relationship.'
}

Table pharmacy.mac_list {
  mac_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mac_list']
  parent_mac_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mac_list (parent_mac_list_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturer.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Brand name under which the drug is marketed.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Physical form of the drug (e.g., tablet, capsule).']
  dosage_strength_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for dosage strength.']
  dosage_strength_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric strength of the dosage.']
  drug_category varchar [note: 'Category of the drug (e.g., pain management, cardiovascular).']
  drug_class varchar [note: 'Class of the drug (e.g., analgesic, antibiotic).']
  drug_code varchar [note: 'Standard code identifying the drug (e.g., RxNorm code).']
  drug_code_description varchar [note: 'Description of the drug code.']
  drug_code_type varchar [note: 'Type of drug code used (RxNorm, NDC, or other).']
  drug_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the drug.']
  drug_form_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the dosage form of the drug.']
  drug_form_description varchar [note: 'Description of the dosage form.']
  drug_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the drug.']
  drug_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drug.']
  drug_strength_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the dosage strength.']
  drug_strength_description varchar [note: 'Description of the dosage strength.']
  drug_therapeutic_class_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the therapeutic class of the drug.']
  drug_therapeutic_class_description varchar [note: 'Description of the therapeutic class.']
  drug_type varchar [note: 'Type of drug (prescription, over-the-counter, specialty).']
  generic_name varchar [note: 'Generic name of the drug.']
  mac_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MAC record was created in the system.']
  mac_created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the MAC record.']
  mac_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the MAC value is expressed.']
  mac_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the MAC value becomes effective.']
  mac_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the MAC value expires.']
  mac_source varchar [note: 'Source of the MAC value (PBM, manufacturer, CMS).']
  mac_source_date date [note: 'Date when the MAC value was sourced.']
  mac_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MAC record was last updated.']
  mac_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the MAC record.']
  mac_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable cost for the drug as per PBM contract.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the drug manufacturer.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'Standard 10-digit NDC code identifying the drug.']
  package_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for package size.']
  package_size_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the package size.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the audit trail.']
  record_audit_created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the record in the audit trail.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was last updated in the audit trail.']
  record_audit_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who updated the record in the audit trail.']
  therapeutic_class varchar [note: 'Therapeutic classification of the drug.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for mac_list. Referenced by mac_list_id.'
}

Table billing.premium_invoice {
  premium_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the premium invoice record.']
  billing_clerk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Invoice creation is performed by a billing clerk; required for internal audit and workload tracking.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory filing of premium invoices required by state insurance premium reporting obligations.']
  credentialing_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.application. Business justification: Required for the Credentialing Application Fee Invoicing process, linking each premium invoice to the specific credentialing application it charges, enabling audit and regulatory reconciliation.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group billed for group coverage.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan or benefit design underlying the premium.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: General Ledger posting of premium invoices for revenue recognition required for financial statements.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual member associated with the invoice, if applicable.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Required for CMS risk‑adjusted premium reporting linking each invoice to the member\'s risk score at billing time.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.service_area. Business justification: Invoices are generated per service area to satisfy network adequacy reporting and state filing requirements.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Required for premium invoicing: each invoice must reference the enrollment transaction that generated the premium, enabling audit, reporting, and reconciliation.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the coverage period for which the premium is billed.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'First day of the coverage period for which the premium is billed.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the collection effort for the invoice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the invoice to the recipient.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any discount (e.g., early‑pay, promotional) applied to the invoice.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received to avoid delinquency.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after due date before the invoice is considered delinquent.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number assigned by the billing system, used for member and regulator reference.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the invoice.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice based on the recipient and billing arrangement.']
  is_eligible_for_subsidy boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the member/group qualifies for a premium subsidy.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the invoice was generated and issued to the recipient.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the invoice belongs.']
  net_amount_due decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount the recipient must pay after adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional comments or remarks added by billing staff.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Interface through which the payment was made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary instrument used to settle the invoice.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the health plan.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount returned to the payer for over‑payment or claim reversal.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the invoice must be reported to CMS or state DOI for compliance.']
  retroactive_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Adjustment for prior‑period premium changes applied to the current invoice.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating billing system that generated the invoice.']
  source_system_invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Original invoice identifier from the source billing system.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for an individual member statement when invoice_type is individual_statement.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of premium assistance applied (e.g., APTC, state subsidy).']
  subsidy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subsidy applied to the premium.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the premium, if applicable.']
  total_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross premium before any subsidies, taxes, or discounts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Core billing document representing a premium invoice or statement issued to an employer group, individual member (IFP/ACA marketplace direct-pay), COBRA qualified beneficiary, or government sponsor for a specific billing period. Captures invoice number, billing period start/end, total premium due, subsidy/APTC amounts applied, net amount due, invoice status (draft, issued, paid, partially paid, void, delinquent), due date, document type (group list-bill invoice, group self-bill invoice, individual member statement, COBRA invoice, government remittance request), delivery method (mail, email, member portal), line-of-business (LOB), statement-specific fields for IFP direct-pay members (statement number, member reference, plan name, statement delivery preference), and source system reference from HealthEdge or custom billing platform. SSOT for ALL premium billing documents — both group invoices and individual member statements — in the billing domain.'
}

Table billing.invoice_line {
  invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the invoice line record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan or benefit design associated with the charge.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Each invoice line is posted to the appropriate revenue ledger account for detailed revenue accounting.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the covered member or subscriber for this premium charge.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: Required for Prior Authorization Billing Reconciliation Report linking each billed line to its originating PA request, enabling audit of authorized services vs. charges.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent invoice header to which this line belongs.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Needed for line‑level reconciliation: each invoice line reflects a specific enrollment transaction (plan tier, coverage), supporting detailed financial reporting.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for a premium adjustment.']
  adjustment_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the adjustment reason.']
  aptc_subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Advanced Premium Tax Credit amount applied to reduce the member\'s premium.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period associated with this line.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period associated with this line.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Last day of coverage for which the premium is billed (nullable for open‑ended coverage).']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'First day of coverage for which the premium is billed.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the coverage level for the member (e.g., employee only, employee + spouse, employee + child, family).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the invoice line record was first created in the system.']
  csr_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of cost-sharing reduction (CSR) applied to the premium.']
  employee_contribution decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employee (or subscriber).']
  employer_contribution decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the premium paid by the employer on behalf of the member.']
  is_refund boolean [note: 'True if this line represents a refund rather than a charge.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the invoice.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for this premium line is due.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status for the premium line.']
  premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base premium amount before any contributions, subsidies, or adjustments.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the premium amounts.']
  premium_line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the premium line (e.g., "Monthly medical premium for employee only").']
  premium_reconciliation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line has been reconciled to the general ledger.']
  premium_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the premium line (e.g., HealthEdge, CustomBilling).']
  premium_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the premium line.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Type of premium rate applied to the line (standard, discount, subsidy, retroactive, or other adjustment).']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the member or employer for this line (if applicable).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this line must be included in regulatory reports (e.g., CMS, state DOI).']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line reflects a retroactive premium adjustment.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the premium line, if applicable.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after employer/employee contributions, subsidies, adjustments, and taxes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the invoice line record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a premium invoice, representing a single member or subscriber coverage charge for a billing period. Captures member/subscriber reference, plan code, coverage tier (EE, EE+SP, EE+CH, FAM), rate type, gross premium, employer contribution, employee contribution, APTC subsidy amount, CSR adjustment, retroactive adjustment indicator, and effective coverage dates. Supports PMPM-level billing reconciliation.'
}

Table billing.premium_payment {
  premium_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the premium payment record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Premium payment receipts must be reported to regulators for premium receipt compliance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost center allocation of premium payments enables budgeting and expense tracking per business unit.']
  credentialing_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.application. Business justification: Needed to record payments made for credentialing application fees, supporting financial tracking and compliance reporting.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group responsible for the payment, when applicable.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Cash receipt accounting posts each premium payment to the cash ledger for accurate cash balance reporting.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member associated with the premium payment.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insurance policy to which the premium payment applies.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the premium invoice that this payment is applied to.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Payment processing is performed by finance staff; linking enables reconciliation and performance metrics.']
  risk_adjustment_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.adjustment_payment. Business justification: Supports risk‑adjustment payment reconciliation linking each premium payment to its corresponding risk adjustment transaction.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Payments are applied to invoices tied to an enrollment transaction; linking allows tracking payment against the originating enrollment for compliance and financial analysis.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Required for payment‑processor reconciliation reports; health insurers track which external vendor processed each premium payment for PCI compliance and audit.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total of fees, discounts, or other adjustments applied to the gross amount.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that included this payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the payment.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any processing or service fees associated with the payment.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments, representing the amount applied to the invoice.']
  nsf_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the payment was returned due to non‑sufficient funds.']
  payer_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number or identifier used by the payer for the transaction.']
  payer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity making the payment.']
  payer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payer (e.g., employer, individual member, CMS, state Medicaid agency).']
  payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount received before any adjustments.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Interface through which the payment was submitted.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the payment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the payment.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Unique payment reference number assigned by the billing system.']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was received.']
  premium_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the payment in the accounting reconciliation process.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the suspense item was resolved.']
  resolution_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the suspense item should be resolved.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the suspense investigation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the payment record.']
  suspense_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the payment is in suspense (e.g., unidentified payer, amount mismatch).']
  suspense_resolver varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or system responsible for investigating the suspense item.']
  suspense_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment within the suspense workflow.']
