  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component of the payment, if applicable.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment transaction.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment that could not be matched to an invoice and is placed in suspense.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']

  Note: 'Records an actual payment received against a premium invoice, capturing payment date, payment amount, payment method (ACH, check, wire, credit card, EFT), payment reference number, payer type (employer group, individual member, CMS, state Medicaid agency), applied invoice reference, unapplied/suspense balance, NSF/return indicator, reconciliation status, and full suspense management lifecycle (suspense status, suspense reason code — unidentified payer, amount mismatch, duplicate payment, missing invoice reference — received amount, receipt date, payer name, payer account reference, assigned resolver, resolution due date, resolution outcome — applied to invoice, refunded, written off — resolution date). Payments in suspense status are tracked inline with complete resolution workflow rather than in a separate suspense entity. SSOT for all inbound premium cash receipts including suspense items, their investigation, and resolution lifecycle.'
}

Table billing.payment_allocation {
  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each payment allocation record.']
  credentialing_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.application. Business justification: Allows allocation of payments to the corresponding credentialing application fee, essential for accurate reconciliation of provider credentialing charges.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the reconciliation.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice line to which the payment amount is allocated.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Payment allocation entries are posted to the ledger to reconcile allocated amounts with cash receipts.']
  premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the premium payment that is being allocated.']
  allocated_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the premium that is allocated to the invoice line.']
  allocated_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of premium units (e.g., member‑months) allocated to the invoice line.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the allocation was recorded.']
  allocation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of allocation lines within the same payment.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the allocation record.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation purpose (e.g., standard payment, partial payment, over‑payment, suspense resolution, adjustment, refund).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the allocated amount.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Discount or credit applied to the allocated premium.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the allocation becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the allocation ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  is_overpayment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation represents an overpayment scenario.']
  is_suspense_resolution boolean [note: 'True when the allocation resolves a suspense balance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the allocation.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was submitted.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the premium payment.']
  reconciliation_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier (e.g., 2023-04) for the premium reconciliation cycle.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation record.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the reconciliation (e.g., monthly, quarterly).']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the allocated premium.']
  total_adjustments decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all adjustments (e.g., refunds, credits) applied during the period.']
  total_billed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all invoice line amounts billed for the reconciliation period.']
  total_collected decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all payments collected for the reconciliation period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between total billed and total collected after adjustments.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any variance observed in the reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Association and reconciliation entity linking premium payments to invoice lines, tracking line-level allocation, and managing periodic premium reconciliation outcomes. For allocation: captures allocated amount, allocation date, allocation type (standard, partial, overpayment, suspense resolution), and line-level reconciliation status. Supports many-to-many payment-to-invoice matching and enables accurate accounts receivable aging. For periodic reconciliation: captures reconciliation period, total billed, total collected, total adjustments, variance amount, variance reason, reconciliation status (open, in review, approved, closed), approver reference, and reconciliation type (monthly, quarterly, annual). Supports MLR calculation inputs, financial close processes, CMS premium reconciliation, and end-to-end premium reconciliation from individual line-level allocation through period-level summary and variance analysis. SSOT for all payment allocation and premium reconciliation in the billing domain.'
}

Table billing.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the billing account.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assign billing accounts to cost centers for consolidated financial reporting of account balances.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the internal representative responsible for the account.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Required for Employer Billing Account Management report linking each billing account to its employer group for contribution tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  group_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: Group practices receive consolidated reimbursements; the link enables the Group Practice Billing Summary and CMS reporting of provider‑level payments.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Needed for Provider Payment Reconciliation report linking each provider to its billing account for electronic fund transfers and regulatory payment tracking.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network contract billing – each billing account is tied to a provider network contract for negotiated rates and regulatory reporting.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'External account number assigned to the billing entity for invoicing and payment processing.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing account.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing entity (e.g., employer group, individual subscriber, government program).']
  apr_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to calculate the annual premium for the account.']
  aptc_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of ACA premium tax credit applied to the account.']
  auto_pay_authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the auto‑pay authorization was granted.']
  auto_pay_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has authorized automatic payments.']
  auto_pay_enrollment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is enrolled in automatic payment processing.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account automatically renews at the end of its term.']
  billing_calendar_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the calendar configuration governing billing cycles.']
  billing_cycle_type varchar [note: 'Configuration of the recurring billing cycle for the account.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often invoices are generated for this account.']
  billing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver bills to the account holder.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the collection effort for overdue balances.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing account record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount authorized for the billing entity.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts on the account.']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding monetary balance on the account as of the latest reconciliation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the billing account became effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the billing account is scheduled to terminate (null if open‑ended).']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the due date before the account is considered delinquent.']
  invoice_generation_day varchar [note: 'Day of month when the invoice is generated (1‑31).']
  last_invoice_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent invoice was generated.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the most recent payment.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received for the account.']
  next_invoice_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next invoice issuance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for internal comments or special instructions related to the account.']
  payment_due_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount currently due on the account, including any pending charges.']
  payment_due_day varchar [note: 'Day of month by which payment must be received.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms defining when payment is due after invoice issuance.']
  pmpm_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate used to calculate monthly charges on a per‑member basis.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is subject to special regulatory reporting (e.g., CMS, state DOI).']
  subsidy_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total subsidy applied to the account (e.g., employer or government subsidies).']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is exempt from sales or other applicable taxes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the billing account record.']

  Note: 'Master billing account representing the financial relationship between the health plan and a billing entity — employer group, individual subscriber, or government program (Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP). Captures account number, account type, billing frequency (monthly, quarterly, annual), billing method (self-bill, list-bill, direct-bill), payment terms, auto-pay enrollment, current balance, credit limit, account status, assigned billing representative, billing cycle configuration (cycle type — monthly/quarterly/semi-annual/annual, invoice generation date, payment due date, grace period days, next/last invoice dates, auto-renewal flag, billing calendar reference), and registered payment methods (payment method type — ACH/EFT, check, wire transfer, credit card, debit card, HSA/FSA — with masked/tokenized bank routing and account numbers, card last four digits, card expiration date, auto-pay authorization flag, authorization date and reference, active/inactive status per method, PCI-DSS compliant storage). SSOT for billing account identity, billing cycle configuration, and authorized payment methods.'
}

Table billing.premium_rate {
  premium_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each premium rate record.']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.rate_schedule']
  age_band varchar [note: 'Age band classification for age‑rated plans (e.g., 0‑20, 21‑30).']
  aptc_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of APTC applied to the premium for ACA‑eligible members.']
  cobra_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate applied to COBRA participants (active employee rate plus 2 % administrative fee).']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of coverage (e.g., individual, employee + spouse, family) that the rate applies to.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the premium rate record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the premium rate becomes effective.']
  employee_contribution_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Portion of the premium (as a percentage) that the employee contributes.']
  employer_contribution_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Portion of the premium (as a percentage) that the employer contributes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the premium rate expires; null if open‑ended.']
  family_tier_structure varchar [note: 'Definition of how family members are tiered for premium calculation (e.g., adult, child, infant).']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the rate applies (individual, group, small group, large group).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for the health insurance plan to which the rate belongs.']
  premium_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the premium rate record.']
  premium_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rate is an Annual Premium Rate (APR) or Per Member Per Month (PMPM) rate.']
  rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base monetary amount (per member per month) before adjustments.']
  rating_area varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area code used for regional premium variations.']
  rating_area_reference varchar [note: 'External reference linking the rate to a specific rating‑area master record.']
  rating_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate premiums (e.g., community rated, age‑rated, composite rated, experience rated).']
  regulatory_filing_metadata varchar [note: 'Free‑text field capturing additional regulatory filing details (e.g., submission dates, approval status).']
  state_doi_filing_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the rate filing submitted to the State Department of Insurance.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of any subsidy applied to the premium (e.g., employer or government subsidy).']
  tobacco_surcharge_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Additional percentage surcharge applied to members who use tobacco.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the premium rate record.']
  wellness_discount_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied for members meeting wellness program criteria.']

  Note: 'Defines the APR (Annual Premium Rate) and PMPM rate structures for a plan, coverage tier, rating area, and effective date range, including the parent rate schedule configuration that governs how rates are structured and applied. Captures rate schedule name, rating method (community rated, age-rated, composite rated, experience rated), market segment, plan code, coverage tier, rating area, age band (for ACA age-rated plans), tobacco surcharge rate and rules, wellness discount rate and rules, employer contribution rate, employee contribution rate, COBRA rate (active employee rate + 2% admin fee), family tier structure, rate effective/expiration dates, state DOI rate filing reference number, rating area reference, and regulatory filing metadata. SSOT for all premium calculation inputs, rate schedule configurations, rate filing metadata, and rate structure definitions used in invoice generation.'
}

Table billing.grace_period {
  grace_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the grace period record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account to which this grace period applies.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member associated with the account for this grace period.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that triggered the grace period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the grace period record was first created in the system.']
  day_count varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed since the start of the grace period.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period is scheduled to end.']
  extension_end_date date [note: 'New end date if the grace period was extended.']
  extension_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the original grace period was extended.']
  grace_period_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the grace period.']
  grace_period_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the grace period, used in reporting and member communications.']
  grace_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grace period.']
  grace_period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the grace period based on product or regulatory rules.']
  is_eligible_for_aptc boolean [note: 'Indicates if the member is eligible for Advanced Premium Tax Credit during this grace period.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the grace period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes captured by the billing team.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the grace period after it ends.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the premium payment was originally due before the grace period started.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the overdue payment was received, if applicable.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the grace period was initiated.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grace period must be reported to CMS or state DOI.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period began.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state abbreviation for jurisdictional reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AL|AK|AZ|AR|CA|CO|CT|DE|FL|GA|HI|ID|IL|IN|IA|KS|KY|LA|ME|MD|MA|MI|MN|MS|MO|MT|NE|NV|NH|NJ|NM|NY|NC|ND|OH|OK|OR|PA|RI|SC|SD|TN|TX|UT|VT|VA|WA|WV|WI|WY — promote to reference product]']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any subsidy applied during the grace period.']
  termination_warning_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a termination warning has been sent to the member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the grace period record.']

  Note: 'Tracks the grace period status for a billing account or member when premium payment is overdue. Captures grace period start date, grace period end date (typically 30 days for individual/ACA, 31 days for group), grace period type (ACA APTC grace period is 90 days), triggering invoice reference, current grace period day count, termination warning issued flag, and resolution outcome (paid, terminated, reinstated). Required for ACA compliance and state DOI reporting.'
}

Table billing.retro_adjustment {
  retro_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the retroactive billing adjustment record.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom the adjustment applies.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the adjustment; positive for additional charge, negative for credit.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the adjustment as used in communications and audit.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the adjustment.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was recorded in the system.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the retroactive adjustment indicating the nature of the change.']
  authorization_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any prior authorization supporting the adjustment.']
  credit_memo_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credit memo generated as a result of the adjustment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the adjustment amount.']
  dispute_open_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was initially opened.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dispute lifecycle.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispute prompting the adjustment.']
  disputed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount that is under dispute.']
  invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice impacted by the adjustment.']
  original_billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period to which the original charge applied.']
  original_billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period to which the original charge applied.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the business reason for the adjustment.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the adjustment record.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was resolved.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the resolution details.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Outcome of the dispute resolution process.']

  Note: 'Captures retroactive premium adjustments and billing disputes resulting from enrollment changes, eligibility corrections, rate corrections, COB updates, or contested billing amounts applied to prior billing periods. Records adjustment type (add, term, rate change, tier change, dispute credit, dispute correction), original billing period, adjustment amount (positive or negative), reason code, dispute tracking fields (dispute type — invoice amount, payment misapplication, rate error, retro adjustment, APTC discrepancy — disputed amount, dispute open date, dispute status — open, under review, resolved, escalated — resolution type — credit issued, invoice corrected, upheld — resolution date, resolution notes), authorization reference, and resulting invoice or credit memo reference. SSOT for all retroactive billing adjustments and billing dispute resolution in the billing domain. Critical for accurate premium reconciliation, dispute lifecycle management, and financial reporting.'
}

Table billing.premium_refund {
  premium_refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the premium refund record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Refunds must be reported to regulators for premium refund compliance.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group associated with the refund, if applicable.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member receiving the refund.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insurance policy linked to the refund.']
  premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original premium payment that is being refunded.']
  primary_premium_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who created the refund record.']
  related_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a claim associated with the refund, if applicable.']
  related_invoice_premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an invoice linked to the refund transaction.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval outcome for the refund request.']
  cleared_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund cleared the financial system.']
  is_partial_refund boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the refund covers only part of the original payment.']
  is_tax_refund boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the refund includes a tax component.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was issued to the payee.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the refund transaction.']
  original_payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of the original premium payment before refund.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the refund record.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount refunded to the member, employer, or government program.']
  refund_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for grouping multiple refunds processed together.']
  refund_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the refund amount (e.g., USD).']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method used to return the refund to the payee.']
  refund_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments related to the refund.']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the refund transaction.']
  refund_processing_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any fee charged for processing the refund.']
  refund_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the business reason for the refund.']
  refund_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why the refund was issued.']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the refund based on business cause.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the refund must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., CMS, DOI).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the refund record.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of tax returned as part of the refund.']

  Note: 'Records premium refunds issued to employer groups, individual members, or government programs due to overpayment, retroactive termination, rate correction, or plan cancellation. Captures refund amount, refund method (ACH, check, credit to account), original payment reference, refund reason code, approval status, issued date, and cleared date. Supports accounts payable integration with Oracle Financials.'
}

Table billing.aptc_subsidy {
  aptc_subsidy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the APTC subsidy record.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member receiving the subsidy.']
  annual_aptc_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total APTC amount the member may receive in a calendar year.']
  aptc_monthly_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly amount of APTC applied to the member\'s premium.']
  aptc_subsidy_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the subsidy record.']
  cms_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the subsidy record in CMS premium remittance reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subsidy record was first created in the system.']
  csr_variant varchar [note: 'CSR variant percentage (73%, 87%, or 94%) applicable to the member.']
  exchange_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace exchange where the member enrolled.']
  qhp_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code of the Qualified Health Plan associated with the subsidy.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the subsidy data.']
  subsidy_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the subsidy becomes effective.']
  subsidy_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the subsidy agreement.']
  subsidy_termination_date date [note: 'Date when the subsidy ends or is terminated; null if still active.']
  subsidy_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is an Advance Premium Tax Credit (APTC) or Cost‑Sharing Reduction (CSR) subsidy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subsidy record.']
  ytd_aptc_applied decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative APTC amount applied to the member\'s premiums to date.']

  Note: 'Tracks Advance Premium Tax Credit (APTC) and Cost-Sharing Reduction (CSR) subsidy amounts for ACA marketplace members. Captures member reference, QHP plan code, marketplace exchange ID, APTC monthly amount, CSR variant level (73%, 87%, 94%), annual APTC cap, year-to-date APTC applied, subsidy effective date, subsidy termination date, and CMS reconciliation status. Required for ACA Section 1401/1402 compliance and CMS premium remittance reporting.'
}

Table billing.cms_remittance {
  cms_remittance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each CMS remittance record.']
  cms_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the transaction as assigned by CMS.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: CMS remittance submissions are tied to specific CMS regulatory obligations.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the remittance.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan or benefit package for which the remittance applies.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider (if applicable) linked to the remittance.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining any manual adjustments applied to the remittance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the remittance record was first loaded into the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the remittance amounts (e.g., USD).']
  edi_820_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated EDI 820 payment file.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by CMS before any adjustments or offsets.']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member was eligible for the payment period.']
  mlr_rebate_offset_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Medical Loss Ratio rebate offset applied to the gross payment.']
  net_remittance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount after all adjustments, representing the amount to be credited to the insurer.']
  payment_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the payment period for which the remittance applies.']
  payment_period_start date [note: 'First day of the payment period for which the remittance applies.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Category of CMS payment such as capitation, risk corridor, reinsurance, or risk adjustment.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remittance reconciliation process.']
  remittance_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by CMS for the remittance transaction.']
  remittance_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the remittance record within the insurer\'s system.']
  remittance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the remittance was received from CMS.']
  risk_adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount related to risk adjustment transfers included in the remittance.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the remittance data was submitted to the insurer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the remittance record.']

  Note: 'Records CMS premium remittance transactions for Medicare Advantage (MA), Part D, and ACA QHP plans. Captures CMS payment type (capitation, risk corridor, reinsurance, risk adjustment transfer), payment period, gross payment amount, risk adjustment amount, MLR rebate offset, net remittance amount, CMS transaction ID, 820 EDI transaction reference, and reconciliation status. SSOT for government premium remittance from CMS.'
}

Table billing.cycle {
  cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the billing cycle configuration.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member or group account to which this cycle applies.']
  billing_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the calendar that defines holidays and non‑billing days.']
  renewal_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the next billing cycle that follows this one upon renewal.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing cycle automatically renews at the end of its term.']
  billing_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months that define one billing period (e.g., 1 for monthly, 3 for quarterly).']
  billing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the invoice to the member or group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing cycle record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code that uniquely identifies the billing cycle configuration.']
  cycle_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing special rules or notes for the cycle.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing cycle.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing cycle frequency.']
  default_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard premium amount before any adjustments.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the default premium.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the billing cycle ends; null for open‑ended cycles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the billing cycle becomes effective.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the due date before the account is considered delinquent.']
  invoice_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Calendar day of the month when the invoice is generated (1‑28).']
  is_pro_rata boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cycle amount is prorated based on start/stop dates.']
  last_invoice_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent invoice was generated.']
  net_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Premium amount after discounts but before taxes.']
  next_invoice_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next invoice generation.']
  payment_due_day_offset varchar [note: 'Number of days after the invoice date that payment is due.']
  prorated_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated amount when the cycle is prorated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the record (e.g., HealthEdge).']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component added to the net premium.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of net premium, tax, and any other charges.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the billing cycle record.']

  Note: 'Defines the billing cycle configuration for a billing account or group, specifying billing frequency, invoice generation date, payment due date, grace period days, billing method, and cycle status. Captures cycle type (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual), next invoice date, last invoice date, auto-renewal flag, and billing calendar reference. Drives the automated invoice generation schedule in HealthEdge.'
}

Table billing.collection_case {
  collection_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the collection case.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Collection cases identified as audit findings require linkage for regulatory monitoring.']
  case_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Collections team assigns a case owner; linking supports case management dashboards and compliance.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose premium is in delinquency.']
  um_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_case. Business justification: Needed for Integrated Collections & Utilization Management Dashboard that tracks collection actions against specific utilization cases for compliance and risk analysis.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external collection agency assigned to the case.']
  action_count varchar [note: 'Total number of collection actions recorded for the case.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any adjustments (fees, penalties, interest) applied to the delinquent amount.']
  case_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the collection case reached a terminal state (resolved, written off, or closed).']
  case_number varchar [note: 'External case number used in communications and reporting.']
  case_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the collection case was initially opened.']
  collection_case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the collection case (e.g., pre-collection, first notice, second notice, active collection, agency referral, legal, written off, resolved).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the collection case record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  delinquency_age_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the premium has been delinquent.']
  delinquent_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total premium amount that is past due at the time the case is created.']
  final_resolution varchar [note: 'Outcome of the collection case after closure.']
  last_action_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent collection action occurred.']
  last_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of the most recent collection action taken.']
  net_amount_due decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount owed after adjustments, before any payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by collection agents or supervisors.']
  payment_plan_arrangement_details varchar [note: 'Details of any payment plan agreed with the member (installments, amounts, schedule).']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date the case was referred to a collection agency.']
  reinstatement_conditions varchar [note: 'Textual description of conditions required for reinstatement (e.g., payment of past due plus fees).']
  reinstatement_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is eligible for policy reinstatement after collection.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the collection case record.']
  termination_notice_issued_date date [note: 'Date a termination notice was sent to the member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the collection case record.']
  write_off_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of the delinquent balance that has been written off as bad debt.']
  write_off_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or note indicating approval authority for the write‑off.']

  Note: 'Tracks premium collection activities for delinquent billing accounts from initial delinquency through final resolution. Captures collection status (pre-collection, first notice, second notice, active collection, agency referral, legal, written off, resolved), delinquency amount, delinquency age in days, collection agency assignment and referral date, collection action history with dated entries and action types, termination notice issued date, reinstatement eligibility flag and conditions, write-off amount and approval reference, payment plan arrangement details, and final resolution (paid in full, partial payment accepted, written off, bankruptcy, plan termination). Supports collections workflow, bad debt management, state DOI delinquency reporting, and financial write-off reporting to Oracle Financials.'
}

Table billing.premium_reconciliation {
  premium_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each premium reconciliation record.']
  cms_remittance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the CMS remittance record associated with this reconciliation.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Reconciliation reports are filed to satisfy premium accounting regulatory obligations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the reconciliation.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group associated with the member.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the premium is billed.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Premium reconciliation generates journal entries to reflect net premium earned and adjustments in the accounting system.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose premium is being reconciled.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Needed for premium reconciliation reports that compare billed amounts against risk‑adjusted expectations per member.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the enrollment record that drives expected premium calculations.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reconciliation was formally approved.']
  apr_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate used to calculate monthly premium based on member coverage.']
  aptc_subsidy_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Subsidy amount applied to the member\'s premium under ACA marketplace rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  is_finalized boolean [note: 'True when the reconciliation has been closed and no further changes are allowed.']
  mlr_input_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation record is included in MLR calculations.']
  net_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net premium after subtracting adjustments from the total billed amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by the reconciler.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the premium reconciliation period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the premium reconciliation period.']
  pm_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standardized premium amount calculated on a per‑member‑per‑month basis.']
  premium_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation record.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the reconciliation process for tracking and audit.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the reconciliation was executed.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the reconciliation (monthly, quarterly, or annual).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation is included in statutory reporting (e.g., CMS, DOI).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system (e.g., HealthEdge) that generated the reconciliation data.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter US state code for state‑level reporting and regulatory filing.']
  total_adjustments_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all adjustments (credits, refunds, write‑offs) applied during reconciliation.']
  total_billed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all premium amounts billed for the period before any payments or adjustments.']
  total_collected_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all premium payments received for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between expected premium (based on enrollment) and net premium collected.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for any premium variance identified.']

  Note: 'Captures the monthly or periodic premium reconciliation process between billed premium, received payments, enrollment-based expected premium, and CMS/state remittances. Records reconciliation period, total billed, total collected, total adjustments, variance amount, variance reason, reconciliation status (open, in review, approved, closed), and approver reference. Supports MLR calculation inputs and financial close processes.'
}

Table billing.cobra_billing {
  cobra_billing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the COBRA billing record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: COBRA billing must comply with ERISA/DOL regulatory obligations and be reported.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group from which the original coverage originated.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan associated with the COBRA coverage.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the qualified beneficiary who elected COBRA coverage.']
  admin_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional 2% administrative fee applied to the base premium for COBRA.']
  billing_cycle_month varchar [note: 'Year‑month (YYYYMM) representing the billing cycle for the premium.']
  billing_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the COBRA invoice was generated.']
  billing_number varchar [note: 'External billing reference number assigned to the COBRA invoice.']
  cobra_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the COBRA coverage for the beneficiary.']
  compliance_flag_dol boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing record meets Department of Labor regulatory requirements.']
  compliance_flag_erisa boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing record complies with ERISA regulations.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Last day of COBRA coverage eligibility (or expected termination).']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'First day of COBRA coverage eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the COBRA billing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the premium amounts (e.g., USD).']
  election_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking to the member\'s COBRA election election record.']
  initial_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days (typically 45) after the due date during which payment is still accepted without penalty.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Logical delete flag indicating the record has been soft‑deleted.']
  last_payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent payment received for this COBRA billing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special handling instructions.']
  ongoing_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days (typically 30) after each subsequent due date during which payment is accepted.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the first COBRA premium payment must be received.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the premium payment.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the payment transaction (e.g., check number, transaction ID).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the COBRA premium payment.']
  premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly premium amount based on the active employee rate before administrative fees.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is currently active in the system.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the beneficiary for over‑payment or termination.']
  retroactive_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any retroactive premium adjustment.']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a retroactive premium adjustment has been applied.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the COBRA billing record (e.g., HealthEdge).']
  total_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of base premium and administrative fee for the billing period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the COBRA billing record.']

  Note: 'Manages COBRA premium billing for qualified beneficiaries who have lost employer-sponsored coverage. Captures COBRA election reference, qualified beneficiary identity, COBRA coverage period, applicable premium (active employee rate + 2% administrative fee), payment due dates, 45-day initial payment grace period, 30-day ongoing grace period, COBRA status (elected, active, lapsed, exhausted), and DOL/ERISA compliance flags. Distinct from standard group billing due to COBRA-specific regulatory requirements.'
}

Table billing.edi_820 {
  edi_820_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the EDI 820 transaction record.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Member who is the recipient of the premium payment.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Provider (or other payee) associated with the payment.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier grouping multiple EDI 820 transactions processed together.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment event occurred in the business process.']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique business reference number for the EDI 820 transaction (e.g., remittance advice number).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the EDI 820 transaction was received from a payer (inbound) or sent to a payer (outbound).']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error or rejection code returned by the clearinghouse if processing failed.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the error code.']
  invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the invoice or premium bill associated with the payment.']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the payment has been reconciled to the corresponding invoice.']
  isa_receiver_qualifier_code varchar [note: 'Interchange Receiver ID (ISA08) identifying the receiving trading partner.']
  isa_sender_qualifier_code varchar [note: 'Interchange Sender ID (ISA06) identifying the sending trading partner.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the transaction.']
  payee_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the payee (member or provider) receiving the payment.']
  payer_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the payer entity.']
  payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the payment.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was made or is scheduled.']
  payment_method_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment instrument used.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the nature of the payment.']
  processing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured during processing.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when reconciliation was completed.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first captured in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']
  remittance_advice_number varchar [note: 'Remittance advice number assigned by the payer.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment is settled in the financial system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that generated or received the EDI 820 transaction (e.g., Availity, Change Healthcare).']
  transaction_set_control_number varchar [note: 'EDI transaction set identifier for payment order/remittance advice (always 820).']

  Note: 'Stores inbound and outbound EDI 820 (Premium Payment Order/Remittance Advice) transaction records processed through the Availity or Change Healthcare clearinghouse. Captures transaction set ID, sender/receiver ISA identifiers, transaction direction (inbound/outbound), payer/payee NPI or TIN, payment amount, payment date, payment method code, associated invoice references, processing status (received, validated, applied, rejected), and error codes. SSOT for EDI premium payment transactions.'
}

Table billing.suspense_account {
  suspense_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the suspense account record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or team member assigned to resolve the suspense item.']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the entity that made the payment (member, employer, or other).']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount received for the unidentified payment.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch or file in which the payment was received.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the suspense account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the payment amount.']
  is_amount_mismatch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment amount does not match any outstanding invoice.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment appears to be a duplicate of another received payment.']
  is_overpayment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received amount exceeds the expected invoice amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes documenting details, comments, or actions related to the suspense item.']
  payer_account_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the payer\'s account when known.']
  payer_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the payer associated with the unidentified payment.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was submitted.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the payment.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the unidentified payment was received.']
  resolution_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the suspense item should be resolved.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the resolution process for the suspense payment.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resolution action was performed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system that supplied the payment record (e.g., HealthEdge, Custom Billing).']
  suspense_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the suspense account record.']
  suspense_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the suspense account for tracking and reconciliation.']
  suspense_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the payment was placed in suspense.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from the original payment transaction (e.g., check number, EFT reference).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the suspense account record.']

  Note: 'Tracks unidentified or unallocated premium payments held in suspense pending resolution. Captures received amount, receipt date, payer name, payer account reference (if known), payment method, suspense reason (unidentified payer, amount mismatch, duplicate payment, missing invoice reference), assigned resolver, resolution due date, and resolution outcome (applied, refunded, written off). Supports cash management and AR reconciliation.'
}

Table billing.mlr_rebate {
  mlr_rebate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the MLR rebate record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: MLR rebate calculations are reported under specific MLR regulatory obligations.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which the rebate applies.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network entity associated with the rebate, if applicable.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to compute the rebate (standard CMS formula or custom adjustment).']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MLR rebate amount was calculated.']
  cms_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CMS filing that includes this rebate information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rebate record was first created in the system.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Date on which the rebate was paid or applied.']
  disbursement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the rebate (check, premium credit, or enrollment reduction).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the period for which the rebate calculation is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the period for which the rebate calculation is valid (null if open‑ended).']
  eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rebate meets all eligibility criteria for payment.']
  hhs_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the annual MLR reporting submission to HHS.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., individual, group, Medicare, Medicaid) associated with the rebate.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market the rebate applies to (individual, small group, or large group).']
  mlr_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated MLR ratio (claims + QI expenses) divided by earned premium, expressed as a percent.']
  mlr_rebate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rebate record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional comments or explanations about the rebate.']
  quality_improvement_expenses decimal(15,2) [note: 'Qualified expenses that improve quality of care, counted toward the MLR numerator.']
  rebate_amount_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of rebate owed to the member or group after MLR calculation.']
  rebate_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the rebate calculation.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the MLR rebate is calculated.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter US state abbreviation where the rebate is reported.']
  total_incurred_claims decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of claim payments incurred during the reporting period.']
  total_premium_earned decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate premium dollars earned for the reporting period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rebate record.']

  Note: 'Captures Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) rebate calculations and disbursements required under ACA Section 2718. Records market segment (individual, small group, large group), state, LOB, reporting year, total premium earned, total incurred claims, quality improvement expenses, MLR percentage, rebate amount due, rebate disbursement method (check, premium credit, enrollment reduction), disbursement date, CMS filing reference, and HHS annual MLR reporting submission status. Owned by billing as the premium revenue source system that feeds MLR numerator/denominator calculations.'
}

Table billing.payment_method {
  payment_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment method record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account to which this payment method is attached.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member (policyholder) who owns the payment method.']
  authorization_date date [note: 'Date the payment method was authorized for use.']
  authorization_reference varchar [note: 'External reference or token returned by the payment processor confirming authorization.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution that holds the account.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address associated with the payment method.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (optional).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the billing address.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the billing address.']
  billing_state varchar [note: 'State/Province component of the billing address.']
  card_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the credit/debit card.']
  card_last_four varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the credit/debit card for display purposes.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Network brand of the credit/debit card.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment method record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the payment method becomes effective for billing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the payment method expires or is deactivated (null if open‑ended).']
  holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual to whom the card is issued.']
  is_auto_pay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has authorized automatic recurring payments using this method.']
  masked_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number with middle digits masked for PCI‑DSS compliance.']
  masked_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number with middle digits masked to meet PCI‑DSS requirements.']
  method_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment instrument (e.g., ACH, Check, Wire, Credit Card, Debit Card, Health Savings Account).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for operational comments or special handling instructions.']
  payment_method_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment method.']
  pci_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stored payment method meets PCI‑DSS compliance.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned by the payment processor (0.00‑99.99).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the payment method record (e.g., HealthEdge).']
  tokenized_account_number varchar [note: 'PCI‑token representing the full bank account number for secure storage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment method record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the payment method verification process.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when verification was performed.']

  Note: 'Master record of authorized payment methods registered for a billing account, capturing payment method type (ACH/EFT, check, wire transfer, credit card, debit card, HSA/FSA), bank routing number (masked), bank account number (masked/tokenized), card last four digits, card expiration date, auto-pay authorization flag, authorization date, authorization reference, and active/inactive status. Supports PCI-DSS compliant payment processing.'
}

Table billing.premium_statement {
  premium_statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the premium statement record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the premium statement record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health insurance plan associated with the statement.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom the premium statement is issued.']
  premium_rate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the premium rate schedule used to calculate the gross premium.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the premium statement record.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any manual adjustment to the statement.']
  aptc_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of APTC subsidy applied to reduce the member\'s premium.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Identifier for the billing cycle (e.g., 2024-01, 2024-Q1).']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the coverage period for which the premium is billed.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'First day of the coverage period for which the premium is billed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the premium statement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the premium statement to the member.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Discount applied to the premium before tax calculation.']
  gross_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total premium before any subsidies, discounts, or adjustments.']
  is_eligible_for_subsidy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member qualifies for APTC or other subsidies.']
  is_late_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the payment is past the due date.']
  is_refund boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the statement represents a refund rather than a charge.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional fee assessed for late payment, if applicable.']
  net_premium_due decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final premium amount the member must pay after subsidies and adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for internal comments or member communications.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was received.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the member must remit payment to avoid late fees.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used by the member to remit payment.']
  rebate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Rebate or discount applied to the premium after statement issuance.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the member if the statement is a refund.']
  statement_date date [note: 'Date the premium statement was generated and issued to the member.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the premium statement, used for member communication and accounting.']
  statement_pdf_url varchar [note: 'Link to the generated PDF version of the premium statement.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the premium statement.']
  statement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the statement based on enrollment type.']
  statement_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the statement for revisions or re‑issuances.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total subsidy amount applied to this statement (including APTC).']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the premium, if applicable.']
  total_payments_applied decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all payments that have been allocated to this statement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the premium statement record.']

  Note: 'Individual member premium statement (distinct from group invoice) generated for direct-pay individual and family plan (IFP) members. Captures statement number, statement date, member reference, plan name, coverage period, gross premium, APTC applied, net premium due, payment due date, statement delivery method (mail, email, portal), and statement status. Supports member self-service portal and ACA marketplace member communications.'
}

Table billing.rate_schedule {
  rate_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the rate schedule configuration.']
  applicable_year varchar [note: 'Plan year to which the rate schedule applies.']
  aptc_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Advance Premium Tax Credit amount applied to the premium.']
  cobra_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Premium rate applied to COBRA continuation coverage participants.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of coverage the schedule governs (medical, dental, vision, pharmacy).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate schedule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the rate schedule becomes effective.']
  employee_contribution_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which the employee contributes toward the premium.']
  employer_contribution_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which an employer contributes toward the member\'s premium.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the rate schedule expires or is superseded.']
  family_tier_structure varchar [note: 'Definition of family tier tiers (e.g., employee only, employee+spouse, employee+children) and associated premium multipliers.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the filing was submitted to the state regulator.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory filing with the state DOI.']
  is_default_schedule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule is the default for its plan and market segment.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the schedule was last reviewed for compliance or rate updates.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market the schedule applies to (individual, group, etc.).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan to which the rate schedule is attached.']
  premium_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the premium component (base premium, supplemental coverage, discount).']
  rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base monetary amount used as the starting point for premium calculations.']
  rate_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context or notes about the schedule.']
  rate_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate schedule.']
  rating_area varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area code used for premium calculations.']
  rating_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate premiums (e.g., community‑rated, age‑rated, composite‑rated, experience‑rated).']
  rating_methodology_details varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the rating methodology and any special rules.']
  regulatory_county varchar [note: 'County name or code associated with the state filing, if required.']
  regulatory_filing_metadata varchar [note: 'Additional metadata about the state filing (e.g., filing version, submission channel).']
  regulatory_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the schedule is filed.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent compliance or actuarial review.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name given to the rate schedule for reporting and UI display.']
  state_doi_filing_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory filing identifier assigned by the State Department of Insurance.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any subsidy applied to the premium under this schedule.']
  tobacco_surcharge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a tobacco surcharge is applicable for this schedule.']
  tobacco_surcharge_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to members who use tobacco, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15%).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rate schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for changes to the rate schedule.']
  wellness_discount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a wellness discount is offered under this schedule.']
  wellness_discount_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Discount percentage applied for members meeting wellness criteria.']

  Note: 'Defines the rate schedule configuration governing how premium rates are structured and applied for a plan year, market segment, and rating methodology. Captures rate schedule name, rating method (community rated, age-rated, composite rated, experience rated), effective date, expiration date, rating area reference, tobacco surcharge rules, wellness discount rules, family tier structure, and regulatory filing reference (state DOI rate filing number). Parent configuration for premium_rate records.'
}

Table billing.billing_dispute {
  billing_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the billing dispute record.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Audit findings often trigger billing disputes; linking enables compliance tracking of dispute resolution.']
  related_invoice_premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that is the subject of the dispute.']
  related_payment_premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment that is associated with the disputed invoice, if applicable.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party that initiated the dispute.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Needed to associate disputes with the responsible vendor (e.g., third‑party claim processor) for dispute resolution workflow and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount.']
  dispute_category varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the billing dispute.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispute for tracking and communication.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the dispute.']
  disputed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount being contested in the dispute.']
  initiator_type varchar [note: 'Type of party that raised the dispute (member, employer group, or provider).']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was first opened.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., CMS, state DOI).']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the outcome and any actions taken.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was resolved.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Method used to resolve the dispute.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the dispute record (e.g., HealthEdge, Custom Billing).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dispute record.']

  Note: 'Tracks billing disputes raised by employer groups or individual members contesting invoice amounts, payment applications, rate calculations, or retroactive adjustments. Captures dispute type (invoice amount, payment misapplication, rate error, retro adjustment, APTC discrepancy), disputed amount, dispute open date, dispute status (open, under review, resolved, escalated), resolution type (credit issued, invoice corrected, upheld), resolution date, and resolution notes. Distinct from clinical appeals managed in the appeal domain.'
}

  fiscal_day varchar [note: 'Fiscal day within the fiscal year.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_calendar. Referenced by billing_calendar_id.'
}

Table billing.payer {
  payer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payer']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the payer organization.']
  parent_payer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payer (parent_payer_id)']
  payer_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the payer\'s mailing address.']
  payer_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the payer\'s mailing address (optional).']
  payer_balance_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding balance owed by the payer.']
  payer_balance_outstanding decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance after payments.']
  payer_balance_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by the payer to date.']
  payer_city varchar [note: 'City component of the payer\'s address.']
  payer_communication_contact_preference varchar [note: 'Indicates if the payer prefers to be contacted by the designated contact person.']
  payer_communication_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for electronic communications.']
  payer_communication_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method of communication for the payer.']

  payer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the payer\'s contact person.']
  payer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the payer.']
  payer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the payer\'s contact person.']
  payer_contact_role varchar [note: 'Role of the contact person (e.g., billing, legal, executive).']
  payer_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the payer\'s address.']
  payer_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payer record was first created.']
  payer_credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount extended to the payer.']
  payer_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for payer transactions.']
  payer_ein varchar [note: 'Employer Identification Number for the payer.']
  payer_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the payer.']
  payer_fax varchar [note: 'Fax number for the payer.']
  payer_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communication with the payer.']
  payer_last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment.']
  payer_last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received from the payer.']
  payer_last_payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the most recent payment.']
  payer_last_payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the most recent payment transaction.']
  payer_legal_name varchar [note: 'Legally registered name of the payer.']
  payer_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the payer entity.']
  payer_payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the payer.']
  payer_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the payer.']
  payer_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the payer\'s address.']
  payer_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code of the payer\'s address.']
  payer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payer.']
  payer_tax_number varchar [note: '9‑digit tax identification number assigned to the payer.']
  payer_time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone of the payer\'s primary location.']
  payer_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payer (e.g., insurer, employer, government, third‑party administrator).']
  payer_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payer record.']
  payer_website varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the payer.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payer. Referenced by payer_id.'
}

Table utilization.pa_request {
  pa_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the prior authorization request.']
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific clinical rule applied.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory reporting of prior authorizations requires linking each PA request to the governing regulatory obligation.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: PA requests must be evaluated against the specific provider contract to determine coverage, prior‑auth requirements, and payment methodology; required for PA decision and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PA Cost Allocation Report requires each prior‑auth request to be charged to the processing cost center.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who made the authorization decision.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer cost reporting requires associating each prior‑auth request with the sponsoring employer group.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Prior Authorization Request Volume Report groups requests by health plan to monitor utilization and cost.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Utilization Management uses member\'s risk score to prioritize prior auth decisions; the \'PA Risk Score Review\' report requires linking each PA request to the member_risk_score record.']
