  pa_member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member requesting the service.']
  pa_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Required for Prior Authorization request processing to link each request to the provider entity for reporting, compliance, and performance metrics.']
  pa_requesting_provider_id varchar [note: 'FK to provider.provider.provider_id — PA requests must resolve to the requesting provider for network status verification, gold card eligibility, and provider communication.']
  pa_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'FK to member.subscriber.subscriber_id — PA requests must resolve to a member for eligibility verification, benefit determination, and UM turnaround time compliance tracking.']
  plan_election_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the member is covered.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network relevant to the request.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Prior Authorization requests must verify provider\'s credential status (NCQA, state license) before approval; linking enables automated credential checks for compliance.']
  related_request_pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related prior authorization request, if any.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Prior Authorization requests must verify the member\'s active enrollment transaction to determine coverage and cost‑share, required for CMS PA reporting and claim adjudication.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Prior Authorization Request Report requires linking each request to the contracted vendor (lab, imaging) that will perform the service.']
  additional_information_requests_count varchar [note: 'Total number of additional information requests issued during review.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Final date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical criteria set applied to the request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the estimated amounts.']
  decision_maker_role varchar [note: 'Professional role of the decision maker.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the reason for denial, if applicable.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Diagnosis code supporting the medical necessity of the request.']
  estimated_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated adjustments (e.g., discounts, co-pays) applied to the gross amount.']
  estimated_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Initial estimated gross cost of the requested service before adjustments.']
  estimated_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final estimated net cost after adjustments.']
  is_appealable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member may appeal a denial.']
  is_duplicate_request boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this request is a duplicate of an existing request.']
  last_air_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the most recent additional information request.']
  last_air_received_date date [note: 'Date the last additional information request was received.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes related to the request.']
  pa_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prior authorization request.']
  patient_age_at_request varchar [note: 'Age of the member at the time of request submission.']
  patient_gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the member at the time of request.']
  prior_auth_decision_date date [note: 'Date the prior authorization decision was made.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units or sessions requested.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the prior authorization request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prior authorization request was submitted.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Urgency classification of the prior authorization request.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Procedure, service, or drug code associated with the request.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of the service being requested for prior authorization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the request record.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the prior authorization request was submitted.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from submission to final decision.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the request record.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a prior authorization (PA) request submitted by a provider or member for a proposed service, procedure, drug, or admission. Captures requesting provider NPI, member ID, requested service codes (CPT/HCPCS/ICD), urgency type (standard, expedited, urgent), submission channel (portal, fax, phone, EDI 278), submission timestamp, current PA status, and full status transition history. Also tracks additional information requests (AIRs) issued during review — information type requested, requested-from party, due date, received date, and TAT clock extension impact. Authoritative SSOT for all PA requests, their status lifecycle, and associated documentation requests.'
}

Table utilization.pa_decision {
  pa_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each prior authorization decision record.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Audits evaluate prior‑auth decisions; linking decisions to specific audit findings enables audit‑driven compliance analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PA Decision Audit Report requires recording which employee authorized each prior‑auth decision for compliance and traceability.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer‑level analytics on prior‑auth decisions need a link to the employer group for regulatory reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: PA Decision Summary Dashboard needs the plan context to calculate approval rates per plan.']
  medical_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.medical_policy. Business justification: pa_decision references the medical policy that governs the decision; child (decision) gets FK to parent (medical_policy).']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member/patient associated with the request.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior‑authorization request to which this decision belongs.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Links PA decision to the originating provider for audit trails, regulatory reporting, and provider‑specific denial analysis.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Prior Authorization decisions require confirming the provider’s participation in a specific network; network_id enables compliance reporting and eligibility checks.']
  amendment_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates if this record is the original decision or an amendment/reversal.']
  appeal_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is eligible to appeal the decision.']
  authorization_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the approved authorization period.']
  authorization_period_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the authorization period is a fixed date range or rolling.']
  authorization_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (e.g., days, visits, doses) authorized.']
  authorization_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the approved authorization period.']
  authorization_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the authorized quantity.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Name of the clinical criteria library applied (e.g., InterQual, MCG).']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the criteria set used for the decision.']
  clinical_rationale varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the clinical reasoning behind the decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decision record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  criteria_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the applied clinical criteria version became effective.']
  criteria_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request met all required clinical criteria (true) or not (false).']
  decision_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prior authorization decision was rendered.']
  decision_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the decision, often used in communications and audit trails.']
  decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the decision record.']
  decision_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the decision outcome.']
  denial_reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the denial reason.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for denial.']
  denial_regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory or policy citation supporting the denial.']
  diagnosis_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of ICD‑10 diagnosis codes supporting the medical necessity determination.']
  is_telehealth boolean [note: 'True if the service requested is delivered via telehealth.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'True if the request was marked urgent.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  prior_auth_number varchar [note: 'Business number assigned to the prior‑authorization request.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with NCQA UM accreditation or state PA regulations.']
  reviewer_credentials varchar [note: 'Professional credentials of the reviewer (e.g., MD, RN, PharmD, NP).']
  reviewer_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the clinician or reviewer who rendered the decision.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the decision record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the authorization decision and underlying medical necessity determination rendered on a PA request. Stores decision date, decision type (approved, denied, partially approved, pended, withdrawn), clinical criteria applied (InterQual/MCG criteria set, version, effective date), criteria met/not met flag, clinical rationale narrative, ICD diagnosis codes supporting the determination, deciding reviewer NPI and credentials (MD, RN, PharmD), denial reason code and category (medical necessity, administrative, benefit exclusion, out-of-network, experimental/investigational), denial regulatory citation, appeal eligibility flag, and effective authorization period (start/end dates). Each PA request may have one or more decision records reflecting amendments, P2P reversals, or appeal overrides. Serves as the single evidentiary record for NCQA audits, state DOI market conduct exams, and denial defense.'
}

Table utilization.auth_service_line {
  auth_service_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each line item within a prior authorization request.']
  care_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_plan. Business justification: Required for Care Plan Progress Report: authorized service lines must be linked to the member\'s active care plan to track service fulfillment and plan adherence.']
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific clinical rule set (InterQual/MCG) applied to this line.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Authorized Service Line Entry Log tracks the employee who entered or approved the service line, supporting internal audit and error correction.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.fee_schedule. Business justification: Each authorized service line uses a fee schedule to calculate allowed amount; linking enables accurate payment calculation and regulatory fee‑schedule reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Authorization Service Line Cost Tracking per plan supports financial analysis and rate setting.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member for whom the service is authorized.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent prior authorization request to which this service line belongs.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Associates each authorized service line with the ordering provider, enabling utilization analytics and provider reimbursement validation.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Service line authorizations require confirming the provider\'s credential and sanction status for each CPT/HCPCS, supporting claim adjudication and audit.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Authorization Service Line Detail report tracks which vendor performs each authorized CPT/HCPCS service for performance and compliance.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the service line within the prior authorization workflow.']
  authorized_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date after which the authorization is no longer valid.']
  authorized_facility_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the facility where the service is to be performed.']
  authorized_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum reimbursable amount per unit for the authorized service.']
  authorized_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units of the service or product approved for the member.']
  authorized_start_date date [note: 'Effective date when the authorized service may begin.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical criteria used for medical necessity determination.']
  cpt_code varchar [note: 'Standard CPT code representing the clinical service or procedure authorized.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the service line record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the authorized price.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the authorization decision for this line was made.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation code or text why the service line was denied or partially approved.']
  diagnosis_icd_code varchar [note: 'ICD code linking the authorized service to the clinical diagnosis supporting medical necessity.']
  hcpcs_code varchar [note: 'HCPCS code (Level II) for services, supplies, or drugs not covered by CPT.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'True if the authorized service is classified as an emergency.']
  is_experimental boolean [note: 'True if the service is considered experimental or investigational.']
  is_partial_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether only a portion of the requested service quantity was approved.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the prior authorization request.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions from the reviewer.']
  place_of_service varchar [note: 'Location type where the service is to be rendered.']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the prior authorization request containing this line was submitted.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the authorized service (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, DME, pharmacy).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the authorized quantity (e.g., tablets, sessions, days).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the service line record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each service, procedure, or drug authorized within a PA request. Captures CPT/HCPCS code, ICD diagnosis code, authorized quantity, authorized units of service, service category (inpatient, outpatient, DME, pharmacy, behavioral health), place of service, authorized facility NPI, and approved date range. Supports partial approvals where only a subset of requested services are authorized.'
}

Table utilization.concurrent_review {
  concurrent_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the concurrent review record.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Concurrent utilization reviews are often scoped within an audit engagement; the link tracks which engagement oversees each review.']
  coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the care coordinator assigned to the member for this episode.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Concurrent Review Cost Tracking assigns each review to the cost center responsible for concurrent review operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Concurrent Review Assignment process assigns a case‑manager employee to each review; the assignment is reported in the Review Assignment Dashboard.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employers monitor concurrent reviews of their members; linking enables employer‑specific review metrics.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Concurrent Review Utilization Management reports require linking each review to the member\'s health plan.']
  inpatient_admission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inpatient admission linked to this review.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose stay is under review.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating prior‑authorization request.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Connects concurrent review records to the admitting provider for LOS benchmarking and clinical oversight reporting.']
  um_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the overarching UM case that this review belongs to.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Concurrent Review Dashboard aggregates metrics by vendor for home‑health and post‑acute services under review.']
  actual_discharge_date date [note: 'Date the member actually left the hospital.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date the member was admitted to the hospital.']
  approved_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days approved for continued stay based on the review.']
  authorized_post_acute_service varchar [note: 'Post‑acute care service(s) approved during the review (e.g., physical therapy, home health).']
  benchmark_source varchar [note: 'Source of the LOS benchmark values.']
  benchmark_source_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the benchmark source (e.g., specific CMS report or Milliman model).']
  clinical_reviewer_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the clinician performing the review.']
  concurrent_review_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the review workflow.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of InterQual or MCG criteria applied.']
  current_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of days the member has been hospitalized to date.']
  discharge_barriers varchar [note: 'Identified obstacles preventing timely discharge (e.g., equipment, placement).']
  discharge_destination varchar [note: 'Intended location after hospital discharge.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the review is flagged as critical due to high acuity or risk.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why continued stay is medically necessary.']
  los_benchmark_mean decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average LOS for the DRG or service line used as a benchmark.']
  los_benchmark_outlier_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Threshold beyond which the stay is considered an outlier.']
  los_benchmark_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Plan‑specific target LOS for the case.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the subsequent concurrent review.']
  planned_discharge_date date [note: 'Projected date for member discharge as determined by the reviewer.']
  readmission_risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the readmission risk score.']
  readmission_risk_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Predictive score indicating likelihood of readmission within 30 days.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']
  review_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the review was completed or closed.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments entered by the reviewer.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code assigned to the review for tracking and communication.']
  review_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the concurrent review was initiated.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the review is concurrent (ongoing) or retrospective.']
  social_work_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a social worker was consulted for discharge planning.']

  Note: 'Manages inpatient concurrent review records for admitted members — the ongoing UM review process evaluating continued medical necessity during a hospital stay. Captures admission date, current and approved length of stay (LOS), LOS benchmarks by DRG (geometric/arithmetic mean LOS, plan-specific target, outlier threshold, benchmark source — CMS, Milliman, plan actuarial), next review date, clinical reviewer NPI, InterQual/MCG criteria version applied, continued stay justification. Includes integrated discharge planning: planned discharge date, discharge destination (home, SNF, LTAC, home health, hospice), post-acute services authorized, barriers to discharge, social work involvement, care coordinator assigned, actual discharge date, and readmission risk score. Links to the originating PA request, UM case, and inpatient admission.'
}

Table utilization.retrospective_review {
  retrospective_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each retrospective utilization review record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with the service being retrospectively reviewed.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Retrospective reviews assess claims against contract terms to identify over‑payments and ensure contractual compliance; required for audit and reimbursement adjustments.']
  gap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_gap. Business justification: Retrospective Review Closure Report uses review findings to close identified care gaps; linking each review to its care_gap provides auditability.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Regulatory audit of retrospective reviews is performed per employer; FK provides required grouping.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Retrospective Review Audit needs plan context to assess compliance and cost impact.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose service is being reviewed.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider who delivered the service under review.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Retrospective claim reviews assess compliance with network contracts; linking to network provides contract‑specific audit data.']
  um_reviewer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical reviewer who made the final decision.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Retrospective Review Compliance report evaluates claim adjustments and denials by the vendor that supplied the service.']
  adjusted_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment resulting from the review decision (e.g., denied amount, approved reimbursement).']
  clinical_criteria_applied varchar [note: 'Clinical decision support criteria set used for the review.']
  compliance_state varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the review with regulatory timelines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the retrospective review record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the adjusted amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|CAD|JPY|AUD|CHF|CNY|MXN|INR — promote to reference product]']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a service was denied after retrospective review.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the denial reason.']
  documentation_completeness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required documentation was complete and sufficient.']
  documentation_received_date date [note: 'Date the required clinical documentation was received for the review.']
  medical_necessity_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the medical necessity determination for the reviewed service.']
  retro_review_deadline_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review was completed within the state‑mandated retro‑review deadline.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date the review decision was finalized.']
  review_initiation_date date [note: 'Date the retrospective review process was started.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the reviewer regarding the decision.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the review for tracking and reporting.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the retrospective review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review; typically retrospective when prior authorization was not obtained.']
  service_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the medical service was provided.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']

  Note: 'Records retrospective (post-service) UM reviews conducted when prior authorization was not obtained before service delivery. Captures service date, review initiation date, clinical documentation received date, medical necessity outcome, denial or approval result, and applicable state retro-review deadline compliance flag. Supports NCQA UM accreditation standards for retrospective review turnaround time (TAT) compliance.'
}

Table utilization.peer_to_peer_review {
  peer_to_peer_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for peer_to_peer_review']
  associated_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with this peer-to-peer review.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization request linked to this review.']
  requesting_provider_provider_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Captures the requesting provider in peer‑to‑peer reviews for peer performance tracking and compliance with clinical peer‑review regulations.']
  review_request_id varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the peer-to-peer review request.']
  completed_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the peer-to-peer review was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review record was first captured in the system.']
  deadline_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review was completed within the state-mandated deadline.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or observations related to the review.']
  outcome_description varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the discussion and rationale for the review outcome.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with NCQA Utilization Management accreditation requirements.']
  request_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the peer-to-peer review was requested by the provider.']
  review_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between request_date and completed_date.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the peer-to-peer review indicating whether the original denial was upheld, partially approved, or fully reversed.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the peer-to-peer review.']
  reviewer_provider_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the plan physician reviewer who conducted the peer-to-peer review.']
  scheduled_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the peer-to-peer review was scheduled to occur.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code governing the applicable peer-to-peer review deadline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AL|AK|AZ|AR|CA|CO|CT|DE|FL|GA|HI|ID|IL|IN|IA|KS|KY|LA|ME|MD|MA|MI|MN|MS|MO|MT|NE|NV|NH|NJ|NM|NY|NC|ND|OH|OK|OR|PA|RI|SC|SD|TN|TX|UT|VT|VA|WA|WV|WI|WY — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this review record.']

  Note: 'Tracks peer-to-peer (P2P) review interactions between the health plan\'s medical director or physician reviewer and the requesting provider following an initial denial. Captures P2P request date, scheduled date, completed date, requesting provider NPI, plan physician reviewer NPI, discussion summary, outcome (upheld denial, partial approval, full reversal), and state P2P deadline compliance flag. Critical for NCQA UM accreditation and state PA reform mandates requiring P2P availability within specified timeframes.'
}

Table utilization.tat_compliance_event {
  tat_compliance_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the TAT compliance event record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Turn‑around‑time events are measured against specific regulatory standards; linking ties each event to its governing obligation.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer‑level TAT compliance reports need to associate each event with the employer group.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the member (patient) involved in the request.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization or utilization review request associated with this compliance event.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the provider who submitted or is linked to the request.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: TAT compliance events are reported per network to satisfy regulator‑mandated network performance metrics.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: TAT compliance events track timeliness of credential verification for providers, needed for regulatory reporting.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the decision was made within the configured turnaround time.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the event after evaluation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance event record was created in the data warehouse.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final decision (approval/denial) was recorded.']
  due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Calculated deadline by which a decision must be made to meet the configured TAT.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the TAT compliance event was logged.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Descriptor of the event type (e.g., tat_compliance).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State, federal, or other jurisdiction governing the TAT requirement.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the request belongs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the compliance event.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the request was first received by the utilization management system.']
  regulatory_reporting_period varchar [note: 'Period (e.g., 2023Q1) for which the compliance event is reported to regulators.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Category of the utilization management review.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level reason why a TAT breach occurred.']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the root cause.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the prior authorization or utilization review request.']
  tat_standard_days varchar [note: 'Target turnaround time in days when the standard is expressed in days.']
  tat_standard_hours varchar [note: 'Target turnaround time in hours for the specific review type, urgency, and jurisdiction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compliance event record.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Priority level of the request as defined by clinical or regulatory policy.']
  variance_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference in days between actual decision time and the TAT target (positive = late).']
  variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference in hours between actual decision time and the TAT target (positive = late).']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking turnaround time (TAT) compliance for each PA request or UM review event. Captures applicable TAT standard configuration (review type, urgency level, line of business, state/federal/NCQA/CMS TAT limits in hours/days, plan-specific targets), request receipt timestamp, decision due timestamp, actual decision timestamp, compliant/non-compliant flag, variance in hours/days, root cause category for non-compliance (staffing, documentation pending, escalation delay), and regulatory reporting period. Supports NCQA UM accreditation TAT reporting, state DOI compliance filings, and CMS MA organization determination timeliness.'
}

Table utilization.um_case {
  um_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the UM case.']
  care_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_plan. Business justification: Case Management Dashboard needs to reference the member\'s care plan to align interventions and report case outcomes against the plan.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Utilization management cases are frequently initiated to satisfy specific regulatory obligations (e.g., CMS guidelines).']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Utilization Management cases are governed by the provider\'s contract terms (coverage criteria, cost‑share); linking supports case review, compliance, and audit.']
  coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_coordinator. Business justification: Case Management Process assigns a care coordinator to each utilization case; linking enables case‑to‑coordinator reporting and workload tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Utilization Management Cost Tracking links each case to the cost center responsible for case management expenses.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the utilization management nurse or case manager assigned to the case.']
  episode_of_care_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the case to the broader episode of care.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Case management dashboards for employers require tying each utilization case to the employer sponsoring the member.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: UM Case Management Dashboard aggregates cases by health plan to monitor utilization trends.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the case.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: um_case may be initiated by a PA request; child (um_case) gets FK to parent (pa_request).']
  primary_provider_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider who rendered the service under review.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Case management reports attribute each case to the primary provider’s network for network adequacy analysis and cost allocation.']
  um_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_program. Business justification: um_case belongs to a UM program configuration; child (case) gets FK to parent (um_program).']
  appeal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the case has been appealed.']
  assigned_nurse_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the UM nurse or case manager handling the case.']
  case_close_date date [note: 'Date the UM case was closed or completed; null if still open.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible case number used for tracking and communication.']
  case_open_date date [note: 'Date the UM case was opened and became active.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the case for workload management.']
  case_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating reflecting clinical urgency and complexity.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the UM case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of the case based on care setting or service type.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Source set of clinical criteria applied (InterQual or MCG).']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria set used for the case.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case meets NCQA UM accreditation requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the UM case record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a prior authorization or claim was denied.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Textual explanation of the denial reason.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the final outcome of the case.']
  disposition_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the case outcome.']
  length_of_stay_actual varchar [note: 'Observed length of stay for the episode, expressed in days.']
  length_of_stay_target varchar [note: 'Planned maximum length of stay for the episode, expressed in days.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for clinical or administrative comments.']
  primary_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD code representing the principal diagnosis driving the case.']
  primary_diagnosis_description varchar [note: 'Text description of the principal diagnosis.']
  primary_provider_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the primary provider.']
  prior_authorization_decision_date date [note: 'Date the prior authorization decision was rendered.']
  prior_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the prior authorization request linked to the case.']
  prior_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the prior authorization request.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from case open to final disposition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the UM case record.']
  urgency_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case requires expedited review per clinical criteria.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a utilization management (UM) case — the overarching workflow container grouping all UM activities (PA requests, concurrent reviews, retrospective reviews, P2P reviews, appeals) for a single member episode of care. Captures case type (inpatient, outpatient, behavioral health, DME, pharmacy), case open/close dates, assigned UM nurse or case manager, episode of care identifier, primary diagnosis, and case disposition. Enables end-to-end UM workflow tracking, workload management, and NCQA UM accreditation reporting.'
}

Table utilization.um_reviewer {
  um_reviewer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the utilization management reviewer.']
  assigned_review_queue varchar [note: 'Primary review queue to which the reviewer is assigned.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of reviewer’s address.']
  compliance_state varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the reviewer with internal and external UM regulations.']
  country varchar [note: 'Country of reviewer’s residence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reviewer record was first created in the system.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Professional credential held by the reviewer.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Reviewer’s birth date.']
  delegation_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of authority the reviewer has to delegate or approve utilization decisions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email for reviewer communications.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s given name.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Concatenated full legal name of the reviewer.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the reviewer was hired by the health plan.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s family name.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent required UM/PA training completed by the reviewer.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the reviewer’s professional license expires.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued license number for the reviewer.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'State that issued the professional license.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s middle name, if any.']
  ncqa_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewer meets NCQA accreditation qualifications.']
  next_training_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the reviewer’s next required training.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations about the reviewer.']
  npi varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the reviewer by the National Provider Identifier system.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number.']
  reviewer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reviewer within UM operations.']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Medical specialty area of the reviewer (e.g., cardiology, oncology).']
  state varchar [note: 'State of reviewer’s address.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Reviewer’s mailing street address.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the reviewer’s employment ended, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reviewer record.']
  urac_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewer meets URAC accreditation qualifications.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of reviewer’s address.']

  Note: 'Master entity for UM clinical reviewers — nurses, medical directors, and physician advisors who conduct UM and PA reviews. Captures reviewer NPI (if applicable), reviewer name, credential type (RN, MD, PharmD, LCSW), specialty, active status, state licensure details, URAC/NCQA reviewer qualification status, assigned review queues, and delegation authority level. Distinct from the provider domain — this entity represents internal plan staff performing UM functions.'
}

Table utilization.pa_notification {
  pa_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each prior authorization notification record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with the prior authorization, if the notification relates to a claim.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the facility (e.g., hospital, clinic) receiving the notification, when applicable.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: PA Notification Compliance tracking needs the originating health plan for audit trails.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom the notification is addressed.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: pa_notification must reference the PA request it notifies about; child (notification) gets FK to parent (pa_request).']
  primary_pa_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the notification record.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health care provider receiving the notification, when applicable.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Notification Audit Log records which vendor received the prior‑auth decision notification for traceability.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recipient acknowledged receipt of the notification, if applicable.']
  approval_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which an approved prior authorization expires, if applicable.']
  attachment_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the notification includes an attached document (e.g., detailed denial letter).']
  compliance_state varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the notification with applicable accreditation or regulatory rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  decision_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization request or decision that triggered this notification.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The medium used to deliver the notification to the recipient.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the notification delivery process.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delivery channel confirmed successful transmission or receipt.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for a denial, when notification_type is denial.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the denial reason.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the notification requires expedited handling.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 code of the language used for the notification content.']
  message_body varchar [note: 'Full text content of the notification sent to the recipient.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the notification, used for tracking and reference in communications.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the notification record within the system.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the notification indicating the decision outcome of the prior authorization request.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the notification for processing and escalation.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the notification is addressed to a member, a health care provider, or a facility.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or mandate that requires this notification to be sent.']
  sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the notification was transmitted to the delivery channel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the notification record.']

  Note: 'Records all required notifications sent to members and providers regarding PA decisions, pend status, and denial notices. Captures notification type (approval notice, denial notice, pend notice, expedited acknowledgment), recipient type (member, provider, facility), delivery channel (mail, fax, portal, phone), sent timestamp, delivery confirmation, and regulatory notice requirement reference (ACA, state mandate, CMS MA). Supports NCQA UM accreditation notification standards and state PA reform mandates.'
}

Table utilization.inpatient_admission {
  inpatient_admission_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inpatient admission record.']
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific clinical rule used for concurrent review.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Hospital Admission Cost Allocation report assigns each inpatient admission to the inpatient services cost center.']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Admission eligibility checks need the member\'s enrollment eligibility span to confirm benefit coverage dates, essential for clinical validation and regulatory audit of inpatient services.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Inpatient Admission Utilization reports are generated per health plan for network adequacy analysis.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the insured member associated with the admission.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost incurred for the admission after discharge.']
  actual_los_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the member actually stayed in the facility.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date the member was admitted to the facility.']
  admission_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the admission by the health plan.']
  admission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the admission.']
  admission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the admission based on urgency.']
  authorization_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the payer\'s authorization for this admission.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical criteria set (e.g., InterQual v2023) applied to the admission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the admission record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing why the admission was denied.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the denial reason.']
  discharge_date date [note: 'Date the member was discharged from the facility.']
  discharge_disposition varchar [note: 'Post‑discharge destination of the member.']
  drg_code varchar [note: 'DRG assignment for the admission used for payment and benchmarking.']
  expected_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected cost of the admission before services are rendered.']
  expected_los_days varchar [note: 'Projected number of days the member is expected to stay, based on clinical criteria.']
  facility_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the admitting facility where the inpatient stay began.']
  is_critical_care boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the admission involved critical care services.']
  is_readmission boolean [note: 'True if the admission is a readmission within the defined look‑back period.']
  los_benchmark_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether actual LOS met the benchmark target.']
  los_target_days varchar [note: 'Benchmark target days for LOS based on plan and diagnosis.']
  payer_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payer\'s prior‑authorization for the admission.']
  physician_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the physician responsible for the admission.']
  primary_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD code representing the principal diagnosis for the admission.']
  readmission_within_30_days boolean [note: 'True if the admission occurs within 30 days of a prior discharge.']
  review_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the utilization management review.']
  review_decision_date date [note: 'Date the UM reviewer rendered a decision.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the utilization management review.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the admission data (e.g., InterQual, MCG).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the admission record.']

  Note: 'UM-managed inpatient admission record for admissions subject to concurrent review. Captures admitting facility NPI, admitting physician NPI, admission date, admission type (elective, urgent, emergent), primary admitting diagnosis (ICD), DRG assignment, expected LOS, actual discharge date, discharge disposition (home, SNF, rehab, expired), and payer authorization status. Serves as the anchor for concurrent review workflows and LOS benchmark comparisons. Distinct from the claim domain\'s institutional claim — this is the clinical/UM view of the admission, not the billing view.'
}

Table utilization.um_program {
  um_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each UM program definition.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Programs are designed to meet regulatory requirements; the FK supports program‑to‑obligation reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: UM Program Enrollment reports require associating programs with the health plans they serve.']
  program_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Program Ownership Management requires linking each utilization program to the responsible HR employee for accountability and budgeting.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Required for underwriting and pricing reports that allocate program costs by risk pool; program eligibility depends on the assigned risk pool.']
  accreditation_category varchar [note: 'Specific NCQA/URAC accreditation domain the program addresses (Turn‑Around‑Time, Criteria, Notification, Delegation, Peer‑to‑Peer).']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status with NCQA/URAC accreditation standards.']
  accreditation_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent accreditation survey for this program.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Name of the InterQual or MCG criteria set assigned to the program.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional compliance observations or exceptions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the UM program record was first created in the data lake.']
  criteria_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the assigned clinical criteria become effective for the program.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria set (e.g., v2024.1).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the UM program becomes operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the UM program is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  gap_analysis_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent gap analysis was completed.']
  gap_analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the gap analysis performed against accreditation requirements.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment(s) to which the UM program is applicable.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next formal program review or update.']
  pa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization is mandatory for services under this program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code used to reference the UM program in contracts and reports.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the utilization management program.']
  program_notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal comments or documentation about the UM program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Category of services the UM program applies to.']
  program_version varchar [note: 'Version label for the program definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.3).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the specific NCQA, URAC, or state DOI regulation governing the program.']
  review_type_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of review types covered by the program.']
  services_covered varchar [note: 'Free‑text list or coded reference of clinical services that require UM review under this program.']
  um_program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and key features of the UM program.']
  um_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the UM program.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the UM program record.']

  Note: 'Master reference for the health plan\'s UM program configurations — the formal UM program definitions required by NCQA, URAC, and state DOI. Captures program name, program type (inpatient UM, outpatient UM, behavioral health UM, pharmacy UM, specialty drug UM), applicable lines of business, clinical criteria set assigned (InterQual/MCG version and effective date), review type scope, NCQA/URAC accreditation compliance status by standard category (TAT, criteria, notification, delegation, P2P), accreditation survey dates, and current gap analysis. Governs which services require PA and which clinical criteria apply.'
}

Table utilization.pa_required_service {
  pa_required_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each PA requirement record.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan to which this PA rule applies.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: pa_required_service defines services required for a specific PA request; child (required_service) gets FK to parent (pa_request).']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Name of the clinical criteria set (e.g., InterQual, MCG) used for evaluation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the PA requirement becomes effective.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'Reason why PA may be exempted for this service.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the PA requirement ends; null if indefinite.']
  gold_card_approval_rate_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the provider met the approval rate threshold.']
  gold_card_approval_rate_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum approval rate percentage required for gold card eligibility.']
  gold_card_effective_date date [note: 'Start date of gold card exemption.']
  gold_card_expiration_date date [note: 'End date of gold card exemption.']
  gold_card_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a gold card exemption applies.']
  gold_card_provider_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the provider granted gold card status.']
  gold_card_revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for revoking gold card status.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment for the plan (e.g., commercial, Medicare Advantage).']
  pa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization is required for the service.']
  pa_required_service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PA requirement record.']
  pa_type varchar [note: 'Type of PA process applicable.']
  provider_specialty_exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the provider\'s specialty grants an exemption.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory rule or statute governing the PA requirement.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'The standardized code representing the medical service or procedure.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the service.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the record.']

  Note: 'Unified reference master defining PA requirements, exemptions, and gold card overrides by service, provider, plan, and line of business. Captures CPT/HCPCS code or service category, plan ID, line of business (commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid), effective date, PA required/exempt flag, exemption type (state mandate, CMS requirement, gold card, plan policy), PA type (standard, expedited, concurrent), applicable clinical criteria set, regulatory citation, and provider specialty exemption criteria. For gold card entries: provider NPI, gold card effective/expiration dates, approval rate threshold met, qualifying performance period, and revocation reason. Drives PA intake routing, provider-facing PA requirement lookups, state PA reform compliance, and gold card mandate tracking.'
}

Table utilization.um_delegation {
  um_delegation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each UM delegation arrangement.']
  um_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_program. Business justification: um_delegation is associated with a UM program; child (delegation) gets FK to parent (um_program).']
  audit_result_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent audit.']
  audit_schedule_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next oversight audit of the delegation.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'NCQA UM delegation compliance status.']
  contract_terms_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of key contractual obligations and limits.']
  corrective_action_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for any corrective action plan triggered by audit findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delegation record was first created in the system.']
  delegated_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the external organization to which UM functions are delegated.']
  delegated_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delegated organization. Values: tpa, health_system, aco, ipa, specialty_vendor.']
  delegated_functions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of UM functions the external entity performs under the delegation.']
  delegation_number varchar [note: 'External contract number or code assigned to the delegation agreement.']
  delegation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delegation agreement.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the delegation agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the delegation agreement ends or is scheduled to terminate (null for open‑ended).']
  performance_metric_actual_days varchar [note: 'Actual average number of days achieved for UM turnaround time.']
  performance_metric_target_days varchar [note: 'Target number of days for UM turnaround time as stipulated in the contract.']
  sub_delegation_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delegated entity may further sub‑delegate UM functions.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why the delegation was terminated.']
  termination_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation of the termination reason.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the delegation record.']

  Note: 'Master entity tracking UM delegation arrangements where the health plan delegates UM functions to an external entity — TPA, health system, ACO, IPA, or specialty vendor. Captures delegated entity name and type, delegated UM functions (PA, concurrent review, retrospective review), delegation effective/termination dates, performance metrics, oversight audit schedule and results, NCQA delegation compliance status, sub-delegation restrictions, and corrective action plan references. Required for NCQA UM accreditation delegation oversight standards and state DOI delegation reporting.'
}

Table utilization.medical_policy {
  medical_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the medical policy record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Medical policies must align with regulatory obligations; linking provides traceability for compliance audits.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Medical Policy Management Expense Tracking needs each policy linked to the cost center that maintains it.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Certain medical policies are employer‑specific; linking enables policy applicability checks per employer.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Medical Policy Applicability Matrix links each policy to the plans it governs for compliance reporting.']
  parent_medical_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on medical_policy (parent_medical_policy_id)']
  applicable_cpt_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of CPT procedure codes covered under the policy.']
  applicable_hcpcs_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of HCPCS codes (including drugs) covered under the policy.']
  clinical_category varchar [note: 'High‑level clinical grouping (e.g., cardiology, orthopedics) to which the policy applies.']
  clinical_criteria_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the clinical criteria used in the policy.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria set applied.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the policy with respect to external regulations.']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cap applied to the total covered amount for the policy period.']
  coverage_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the coverage limit amount.']
  coverage_limit_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the coverage limit (e.g., visits, days).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes binding for members.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the policy ceases to be binding; null for open‑ended policies.']
  is_emergency_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether emergency services are covered without prior authorization.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the policy includes exemptions for specific services or member groups.']
  is_experimental_covered boolean [note: 'True if experimental or investigational services are permitted under the policy.']
  is_prior_auth_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prior authorization must be obtained for services covered by this policy.']
  is_telehealth_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether telehealth services are covered under the policy.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent policy review.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy was last reviewed for clinical or regulatory updates.']
  medical_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments, special considerations, or audit remarks.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the policy for reporting and UI display.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the policy, used in member communications and claim adjudication.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of the policy indicating the primary clinical domain it governs.']
  prior_auth_approval_timeframe_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days allowed to approve a prior authorization request.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the regulatory rule or guideline that mandates this policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the policy record.']
  version_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the policy became effective.']
  version_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the policy was superseded or retired.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for policy revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the policy record.']

  Note: 'Master reference for medical policies and clinical criteria used in utilization management decisions — including medical necessity criteria, clinical guidelines, evidence-based medicine references, and policy effective dates. Captures policy ID, policy name, clinical category, applicable CPT/HCPCS codes, review criteria source (InterQual, MCG, internal), and version history.'
}

Table utilization.bed_day_review {
  bed_day_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bed day review record.']
  concurrent_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.concurrent_review. Business justification: bed_day_review records daily reviews for a concurrent review; child (bed_day_review) gets FK to parent (concurrent_review).']
  coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the care coordinator assigned to the case.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Bed Day Review Cost Allocation links each review to the cost center budgeting bed‑day expenses.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer cost analysis for extended bed‑day approvals requires linking reviews to the employer group.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Bed Day Review Metrics are reported per health plan to evaluate length‑of‑stay performance.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose stay is under review.']
  parent_bed_day_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bed_day_review (parent_bed_day_review_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Links bed‑day review to the responsible provider to support length‑of‑stay variance analysis and readmission risk reporting.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Bed‑day reviews compare LOS against network‑specific benchmarks; network_id is required for accurate network adequacy reporting.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Bed‑day reviews verify attending provider’s credential status before approving extended stay, satisfying payer and accreditation rules.']
  approval_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the review regarding continuation of stay.']
  bed_day_review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bed day review.']
  clinical_criteria_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient met the required clinical criteria for continued stay.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Name of the clinical criteria set applied during the review (e.g., InterQual, MCG).']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical criteria set used for the assessment.']
  clinical_status varchar [note: 'Clinician-assessed health status of the patient on the review date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for denial, if applicable.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the denial reason.']
  discharge_planning_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative notes regarding discharge planning and anticipated needs.']
  extension_days_approved varchar [note: 'Number of additional bed days approved beyond the original length of stay.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the review is flagged as critical due to clinical urgency.']
  length_of_stay_benchmark_met_flag boolean [note: 'True if the current LOS is within the acceptable benchmark range.']
  length_of_stay_current varchar [note: 'Number of days the patient has been admitted up to the review date.']
  length_of_stay_target varchar [note: 'Targeted length of stay based on clinical criteria and benchmarks.']
  readmission_risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk category derived from the readmission risk score.']
  readmission_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score indicating probability of readmission within 30 days.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review complies with applicable regulations (e.g., NCQA, CMS).']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the reviewer during the assessment.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to each bed day review.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bed day review was performed.']
  reviewer_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the clinical reviewer who performed the bed day assessment.']
  social_work_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a social worker is engaged in the patient\'s care plan.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the review data (e.g., InterQual, MCG).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the review record.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual bed day reviews for inpatient concurrent review cases — daily clinical assessments of continued stay medical necessity. Captures review date, reviewer, clinical status, Milliman/InterQual criteria met, extension days approved, and discharge planning notes.'
}

Table utilization.um_program_enrollment {
  um_program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the um_program_enrollment association']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employer group']
  um_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the UM program']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the employer group\'s contribution to the UM program costs']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the employer group enrollment in the UM program terminates']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the employer group enrollment in the UM program becomes effective']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment contract between a utilization management program and an employer group. It captures the effective period of the enrollment and the contribution strategy specific to that employer group.. Existence Justification: An employer group can enroll in multiple utilization management (UM) programs, and a single UM program can be applied to many employer groups. The enrollment is actively managed, with start/end dates and contribution strategy captured for each enrollment. This relationship is therefore a true many‑to‑many operational entity.'
}

Table utilization.clinical_criteria {
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for clinical_criteria']
  parent_clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on clinical_criteria (parent_clinical_criteria_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  criteria_category varchar [note: 'Broad category indicating the clinical setting or context of the criteria.']
  criteria_code varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned to the clinical criteria, used for lookup and integration.']
  criteria_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the clinical criteria, including its purpose and application.']
  criteria_detail varchar [note: 'Textual or structured detail of the rule or logic that defines the criteria.']
  criteria_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the clinical criteria becomes effective and applicable.']
  criteria_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the clinical criteria is no longer valid or applicable.']
  criteria_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the limit value (e.g., days, dollars).']
  criteria_limit_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric limit value used in the criteria rule (e.g., maximum allowed).']
  criteria_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to external standard or guideline that the criteria aligns with.']
  criteria_rule_expression varchar [note: 'Formal expression or logic that defines how the criteria is evaluated.']
  criteria_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or authority that defines the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clinical criteria.']
  criteria_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the category to refine the criteria context.']
  criteria_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the threshold value (e.g., days, dollars).']
  criteria_threshold_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value used in the criteria rule (e.g., maximum days, minimum cost).']
  criteria_type varchar [note: 'Category of the clinical criteria indicating the type of clinical element it applies to.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria to track updates and revisions.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the record for data lifecycle management.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was last updated in the system.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for clinical_criteria. Referenced by clinical_criteria_id.'
}

Table utilization.episode_of_care {
  episode_of_care_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for episode_of_care']
  identity_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the patient associated with this episode of care.']
  parent_episode_of_care_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on episode_of_care (parent_episode_of_care_id)']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the insurance plan covering the episode of care.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the healthcare provider delivering services in this episode.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date the patient was admitted to the facility for this episode.']
  claim_approval_date date [note: 'Date the claim was approved by the insurer.']
  claim_denial_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for claim denial, if applicable.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the claim associated with this episode.']
  claim_submission_date date [note: 'Date the claim was submitted to the insurer.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'The type of care episode, indicating the setting and nature of services.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the episode record was first created in the system.']
  discharge_date date [note: 'Date the patient was discharged from the facility.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the episode of care becomes effective.']
