  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the assessment originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Social Determinants of Health (SDOH) assessment records capturing non-clinical factors affecting member health outcomes — including assessment date, SDOH domain (food insecurity, housing instability, transportation, social isolation, financial strain, education), screening tool used (AHC HRSN, PRAPARE, Hunger Vital Sign), risk level, referral to community resource made flag, and follow-up status. Supports CMS health equity requirements, NCQA SDOH standards, and targeted care management interventions.'
}

Table care.team {
  team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the care team.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Care teams operate under specific provider contracts that dictate service scope and payment; linking supports team‑level cost and quality reporting.']
  lead_coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary care coordinator leading the team.']
  parent_team_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on team (parent_team_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the care program to which the team is linked.']
  audit_created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that created the record.']
  audit_updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  behavioral_health_provider_count varchar [note: 'Count of behavioral health clinicians (psychologists, counselors) on the team.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred method for the team to receive communications.']
  community_health_worker_count varchar [note: 'Count of community health workers on the team.']
  consent_obtained_date date [note: 'Date on which privacy consent was obtained.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address used for team communications.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Primary way the team is contacted for operational matters.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number used for team communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the care team record was first created.']
  data_quality_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of the data quality for this record.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the care team was deactivated or retired.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the care team became operational.']
  hcc_included boolean [note: 'True if HCCs are included in the team’s risk profile.']
  hcc_version varchar [note: 'Version of the HCC model applied to the team’s risk calculations.']
  is_multidisciplinary boolean [note: 'True if the team includes multiple professional disciplines.']
  is_primary_care_team boolean [note: 'True if this team is the member\'s primary care team.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the record’s data quality was last reviewed.']
  lead_coordinator_end_date date [note: 'Date the lead coordinator\'s assignment ended, if applicable.']
  lead_coordinator_start_date date [note: 'Date the lead coordinator began their assignment.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Count of members currently assigned to this care team.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes about the care team.']
  pharmacist_count varchar [note: 'Count of pharmacists participating in the care team.']
  privacy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team has documented privacy consent for handling PHI.']
  provider_count varchar [note: 'Total number of provider‑type members (PCP, specialists, etc.) on the team.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factor used in risk adjustment calculations for the team.']
  social_worker_count varchar [note: 'Count of social workers on the team.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., Casenet TruCare).']
  specialist_count varchar [note: 'Count of specialist clinicians on the team.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the current status of the team.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the care team in external systems.']
  team_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the care team.']
  team_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the care team.']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the team based on its functional focus.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the care team record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Multi-disciplinary care team composition for members enrolled in care management programs — documenting team members by role (PCP, specialist, behavioral health provider, pharmacist, social worker, community health worker), their NPI or staff ID, team role start/end dates, lead coordinator designation, and communication preferences. Supports NCQA care management standards requiring documented care team composition, CMS chronic care management (CCM) billing requirements, and care coordination across clinical and non-clinical team members. Enables care team roster reporting, role gap identification, and team-based care analytics.'
}

Table care.coordinator_assignment {
  coordinator_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for coordinator_assignment']
  coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Auto-generated FK linking siloed coordinator_assignment to coordinator']

  Note: 'Represents the operational assignment of a member identity to a care coordinator. Captures when the assignment started, its type (primary, backup, specialist), current status, reason for the assignment, and the weighted caseload factor.. Existence Justification: Members can be assigned to multiple care coordinators (primary, backup, specialist) and each care coordinator manages many members. The assignment is an operational entity that is created, updated, and deleted by care management staff and carries its own attributes such as assignment date, type, status, reason, and caseload weight.'
}

Table care.program_obligation_mapping {
  program_obligation_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ProgramObligationMapping association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the care program']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the program for this obligation']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory obligation becomes effective for the program']

  Note: 'This association product represents the mapping between care programs and regulatory obligations. It captures the effective date of the obligation for the program and the current compliance status, enabling the business to manage and report compliance per program.. Existence Justification: A care program may be subject to multiple regulatory obligations, and a single regulatory obligation can apply to many care programs. The business actively tracks which obligations each program must meet, including effective dates and compliance status, as a distinct operational activity.'
}

Table care.program_accreditation {
  program_accreditation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ProgramAccreditation association']
  accreditation_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the accreditation program']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the care program']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the accreditation (e.g., accredited, provisional, denied) for this specific care program']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of accreditation (e.g., health plan, provider) for this program pairing']

  Note: 'This association captures the accreditation assignments between care programs and accreditation programs. Each record links one care_program to one accreditation_program and stores attributes that are specific to that pairing, such as the accreditation type and status.. Existence Justification: A care program can be accredited by multiple accreditation programs (e.g., NCQA, URAC) and each accreditation program can apply to many care programs. The accreditation assignment is actively managed by the health plan, with status and type tracked for each program‑accreditation pairing.'
}

Table care.measure_obligation_mapping {
  measure_obligation_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the measure_obligation_mapping association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation']
  hedis_measure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the HEDIS measure']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the measure for the given obligation']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Year the measure is reported for the specific obligation']

  Note: 'This association captures the operational link between HEDIS measures and regulatory obligations. Each record represents one measure required to satisfy one obligation, including the reporting year and compliance status specific to that pairing.. Existence Justification: A HEDIS quality measure can satisfy multiple regulatory obligations, and a single regulatory obligation often requires several HEDIS measures. The compliance team actively maintains a mapping between measures and obligations, tracking the reporting year and compliance status for each link.'
}

Table care.gap_obligation {
  gap_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the gap_obligation association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory_obligation record']
  gap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the care_gap record']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the care gap\'s compliance with the regulatory obligation for the reporting period']
  reporting_period date [note: 'The period for which the care gap is reported to the regulatory obligation']

  Note: 'This association captures the linkage between a care gap and a regulatory obligation. Each record represents a specific reporting instance, storing the reporting period and compliance status that are unique to the gap‑obligation pair.. Existence Justification: A care gap can be reported under multiple regulatory obligations (e.g., HEDIS, state reporting) and each regulatory obligation can apply to many care gaps across members. The compliance team creates and maintains records linking specific gaps to the obligations they satisfy, tracking reporting periods and compliance status for each link.'
}

Table care.program_enrollment {
  program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the program_enrollment association']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employer group']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the care program']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the program enrollment ends for the employer group']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the program enrollment becomes effective for the employer group']
  enrollment_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of members the employer group may enroll in the program']
  participation_rate decimal [note: 'Target participation rate (percentage) for the employer group in the program']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual enrollment of an employer group in a specific care program. Each record captures the period the program is offered, enrollment capacity, and participation rate for that employer‑program pair.. Existence Justification: Employer groups can sponsor multiple care programs and each care program can be offered to many employer groups. The insurer manages a contract for each employer‑program pairing that includes effective dates, enrollment caps, and participation rates. This relationship is actively created, updated, and deleted by business users as part of contract administration.'
}

Table care.questionnaire {
  questionnaire_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for questionnaire']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who owns or manages the questionnaire.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  parent_questionnaire_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on questionnaire (parent_questionnaire_id)']
  question_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the question set that groups related questions.']
  survey_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the survey that the questionnaire belongs to.']
  approval_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the questionnaire.']
  approval_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided during the approval process.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the questionnaire was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the questionnaire.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the questionnaire record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the questionnaire becomes active and usable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the questionnaire is no longer valid or is retired.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is currently active.']
  is_assessment boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is an assessment type.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is the most recent version.']
  is_customizable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire can be customized by users.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this questionnaire is the default for its type.']
  is_form boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is a form type.']
  is_legacy boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is from a legacy system.']
  is_obsolete boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is obsolete and should not be used.']
  is_predefined boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire was predefined by the system.']
  is_public boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is publicly available.']
  is_questionnaire boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is a general questionnaire type.']
  is_retired boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire has been retired.']
  is_survey boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is a survey type.']
  is_template boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the questionnaire is a reusable template.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language code of the questionnaire content.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Locale code indicating regional formatting for the questionnaire.']
  question_count varchar [note: 'Total number of questions contained in the questionnaire.']
  question_set_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the question set.']
  questionnaire_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the questionnaire type and version.']
  questionnaire_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the questionnaire’s purpose and content.']
  questionnaire_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the questionnaire.']
  questionnaire_type varchar [note: 'Category of the questionnaire (e.g., Health Risk Assessment, Chronic Condition Registry).']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system record that maps to this questionnaire.']
  source_record_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the source system record creation or update.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that originally created or last updated the questionnaire.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system instance or module.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the questionnaire.']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the survey.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the questionnaire record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version of the questionnaire (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for questionnaire. Referenced by questionnaire_id.'
}

Table care.question_set {
  question_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for question_set']
  parent_question_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on question_set (parent_question_set_id)']
  administration_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode by which the question set is administered to members, such as telephonic outreach, in-person visit, digital/online portal, mail, or a combination of methods.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the current version of the question set was formally approved for use in care management programs by the clinical governance committee.']
  branching_logic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the question set uses conditional branching logic where subsequent questions depend on prior answers, affecting the actual number of questions presented.']
  care_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the care management program that primarily owns or utilizes this question set (e.g., disease management, case management, wellness program).']
  category varchar [note: 'Clinical or programmatic category that groups the question set by subject area, such as behavioral health, chronic condition management, wellness, functional status, social determinants of health (SDOH), or member satisfaction.']
  clinical_domain varchar [note: 'Specific clinical domain or condition area addressed by the question set, such as diabetes management, cardiovascular risk, mental health, substance use, or fall risk.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique business code or mnemonic used to reference the question set in operational systems and reporting (e.g., \'HRA-2024\', \'PHQ9\', \'CAHPS-ADULT\').']
  consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit member consent must be obtained before administering this question set, based on regulatory or organizational policy requirements.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the question set\'s purpose, target population, and intended use within care management or population health programs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the question set is no longer valid for new administrations. Null indicates the set is open-ended and currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the question set becomes valid and available for administration to members in care programs.']
  estimated_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes required for a member to complete the full question set, used for scheduling and member communication purposes.']
  external_reference_id varchar [note: 'External identifier or catalog number from the source system or industry standard library used to cross-reference this question set with external systems.']
  frequency_requirement varchar [note: 'Required or recommended frequency at which this question set should be administered to eligible members for ongoing monitoring and compliance.']
  hcc_raf_relevance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether responses to this question set may contribute to HCC coding or RAF score adjustments for risk-adjusted payment models.']
  hedis_measure_alignment varchar [note: 'Specific HEDIS quality measures that this question set supports or aligns with for performance measurement and reporting purposes.']
  hipaa_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the sensitivity of information collected by this question set under HIPAA regulations, particularly for behavioral health, substance use, or HIV-related content.']
  instrument_source varchar [note: 'Originating organization or publication source of the validated instrument (e.g., \'Pfizer - PHQ-9\', \'SAMHSA\', \'CMS\'), or \'Internal\' for proprietary question sets.']
  is_required_for_enrollment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this question set is mandatory for member enrollment or continued participation in a specific care management program.']
  is_validated_instrument boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the question set is a clinically validated and peer-reviewed assessment instrument (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7, AUDIT-C) versus an internally developed questionnaire.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the question set is authored, using ISO 639-1 language codes with optional country qualifier (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'zh-CN\').']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the question set was last reviewed for clinical accuracy, regulatory compliance, and continued relevance to care programs.']
  maximum_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Highest possible aggregate score achievable on this question set, establishing the upper bound for scoring interpretation and risk stratification.']
  minimum_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lowest possible aggregate score achievable on this question set, establishing the baseline for scoring interpretation.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the question set, used to identify the assessment instrument in care management workflows (e.g., \'Annual HRA 2024\', \'PHQ-9 Depression Screening\').']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the question set to ensure ongoing alignment with clinical guidelines and regulatory requirements.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department or clinical team responsible for maintaining, reviewing, and approving updates to this question set.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this question set record was first created in the data platform.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this question set record was last modified in the data platform.']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality program that mandates or recommends use of this question set, such as HEDIS, CMS Star Ratings, CAHPS, or state Medicaid requirements.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the question set was retired from active use, after which no new administrations should be initiated.']
  risk_stratification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the question set results are used for population health risk stratification to identify high-risk members for targeted interventions.']
  scoring_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the scoring algorithm or methodology used to calculate results from member responses, including any validated clinical scoring scales (e.g., Likert, weighted sum, threshold-based).']
  set_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the question set by its functional purpose within care management programs, such as Health Risk Assessment (HRA), clinical screening, member survey, functional assessment, or intake questionnaire.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system where this question set is maintained (e.g., Casenet TruCare, Altruista, custom care management platform).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the question set indicating whether it is available for use in care management workflows.']
  superseded_by_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the newer question set that replaces this one upon retirement, enabling migration tracking and historical continuity.']
  target_population varchar [note: 'Description of the intended member population for this question set, such as Medicare beneficiaries, high-risk chronic condition members, pediatric members, or dual-eligible populations.']
  total_question_count varchar [note: 'Total number of questions contained within this question set, used for administration planning and completion tracking.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the question set, enabling tracking of revisions over time as clinical guidelines or regulatory requirements change.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for question_set. Referenced by question_set_id.'
}

Table credential.application {
  application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for application']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation for credentialing application processing is required for monthly cost‑center expense reporting.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external entity that delegated the credentialing submission.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credentialing specialist responsible for managing this application.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Required for Provider Application for Plan Network Enrollment; the application must specify which health plan the provider seeks to join.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider (e.g., NPI) to whom the credentialing application belongs.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date the credentialing application was originally submitted.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the credentialing application, used for tracking and communication with providers.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the credentialing application.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the submission is an initial credentialing, a re‑credentialing, or a reinstatement request.']
  committee_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the credentialing committee review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the application record was first created in the system.']
  credentialing_cycle_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the credentialing cycle for reporting purposes.']
  dea_license_number varchar [note: 'Drug Enforcement Administration license number for controlled substance prescribing.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date the committee rendered its decision.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the application after committee decision.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the credentialing approval expires and a new application is required.']
  hospital_privileges_verified boolean [note: 'True when hospital privileges have been confirmed as part of the credentialing.']
  is_delegated boolean [note: 'True if the application was submitted by a delegated entity on behalf of the provider.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the application has been marked as urgent.']
  last_psv_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the primary source verification data.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the provider\'s state medical license.']
  malpractice_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider has a disclosed malpractice history.']
  ncqa_cycle varchar [note: 'NCQA cycle identifier applicable to this credentialing application.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the specialist during processing.']
  npdb_query_date date [note: 'Date the National Practitioner Data Bank was queried for this provider.']
  primary_psv_status varchar [note: 'Status of the primary source verification process.']
  requires_additional_documents boolean [note: 'True if the provider must submit supplemental documentation.']
  sanction_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of OIG/SAM sanctions screening for the provider.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which the application originated.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'State medical license number for the provider.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Method by which the application was received (paper, web portal, CVO, or delegated entity).']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the credentialing process should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the application record.']

  Note: 'Core master record for a provider credentialing or re-credentialing application submitted to the health plan. Captures the full lifecycle of the credentialing request — application date, application type (initial, re-credential, reinstatement), submission channel (paper, portal, CVO, delegated entity), current workflow status (received, in-review, PSV-in-progress, committee-ready, decided, closed), assigned credentialing specialist, NCQA credentialing cycle, target completion date, expiration date, and overall disposition. This is the central orchestrating entity — all downstream verification activities (PSV), sanctions screenings, NPDB queries, committee reviews, attestations, and outreach communications link back to this application. Sourced from CAQH ProView, Cactus, ProviderSource, or direct submission.'
}

Table credential.record {
  record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the credential record.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Credentialing budget allocations are tracked per credential record for budget‑vs‑actual variance reports.']
  credentialing_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for audit trail of which credentialing staff created/maintained each credential record; essential for compliance reporting (NCQA, state licensing).']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider to whom this credential applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credential record was first created in the system.']
  credential_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the health plan or delegated organization that issued the credential.']
  credential_expiration_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the credential was terminated or expired.']
  credential_revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the credential was revoked, if applicable.']
  credential_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the credential data.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credential.']
  credential_suspension_end date [note: 'End date of the suspension period for the credential.']
  credential_suspension_start date [note: 'Start date of any suspension period for the credential.']
  credential_tier varchar [note: 'Tier level assigned to the credential based on provider qualifications.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credential (e.g., primary, secondary, delegated, direct).']
  credential_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the credential record for change tracking.']
  credentialing_committee_decision_date date [note: 'Date the credentialing committee rendered its decision.']
  credentialing_committee_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the credentialing committee review.']
  credentialing_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes from the credentialing review process.']
  dea_license_number varchar [note: 'Drug Enforcement Administration registration number for controlled substance prescribing.']
  delegated_credential_flag boolean [note: 'True if credentialing was performed by a delegated entity rather than the health plan directly.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the credential becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the credential expires.']
  hospital_privileges_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider holds active hospital privileges.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the provider\'s state medical license.']
  malpractice_claims_count varchar [note: 'Number of malpractice claims filed against the provider.']
  malpractice_history_flag boolean [note: 'True if the provider has any recorded malpractice claims.']
  ncqa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential meets NCQA credentialing requirements.']
  next_recredentialing_due date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the next re‑credentialing must be completed.']
  recredentialing_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful re‑credentialing cycle.']
  sanctions_screened_flag boolean [note: 'True if the provider has been screened against OIG and SAM sanctions lists.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'State medical license identifier for the provider.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credential record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record of a provider\'s current credentialing status with the health plan. Represents the approved, active credential that gates network participation — distinct from the application workflow that produced it. Stores credentialing effective date, expiration date, credential tier (if applicable), delegated vs. direct credentialing flag, NCQA compliance status, credentialing entity (health plan or delegated entity), last re-credentialing completion date, next re-credentialing due date, and overall standing (active, expired, suspended, revoked, voluntarily terminated). One record per provider per credentialing entity. This is the SSOT for \'is this provider credentialed and eligible for network participation?\' — consumed by the provider and network domains to determine participation status.'
}

Table credential.psv_verification {
  psv_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each PSV verification record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the person or system that performed the verification.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the provider to whom this credential element belongs.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Primary source verifications support a credential record; provider_id redundant.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: PSV verification is often performed by third‑party vendors; linking enables audit of vendor‑performed verifications.']
  board_certification varchar [note: 'Identifier for the board certification (e.g., ABMS certificate number).']
  credential_element_identifier varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned by the issuing authority for the credential element.']
  credential_element_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the kind of credential element being verified.']
  dea_schedule varchar [note: 'Schedule (I‑V) associated with a DEA registration.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the credential element became effective.']
  element_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the credential element for reporting and analytics.']
  element_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credential element.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the credential element expires or is no longer valid.']
  hospital_privilege_scope varchar [note: 'Description of clinical privileges granted at a hospital (e.g., admitting, surgical).']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that issued or granted the credential element.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'License number assigned by the state licensing board.']
  malpractice_insurance_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum coverage amount per occurrence or aggregate, as applicable.']
  malpractice_insurance_type varchar [note: 'Policy type: occurrence or claims‑made.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PSV verification record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PSV verification record.']
  training_program varchar [note: 'Name of residency, fellowship, or other graduate medical education program.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the verification was completed.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the primary source verification.']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments captured during verification (e.g., discrepancies, follow‑up actions).']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the verification attempt.']
  work_history_details varchar [note: 'Narrative description of recent work positions, gaps, and explanations.']
  work_history_years varchar [note: 'Number of years of professional work experience in the last five years.']

  Note: 'Unified master record for each credential element held by a provider and its primary source verification (PSV). This is the SOLE authoritative product (SSOT) for ALL verifiable credential element types — no other product in this domain stores credential element data. Supported element types include: state professional licenses (MD, DO, NP, PA, RN, LCSW), DEA controlled substance registrations (with schedules II-V), specialty board certifications (ABMS, AOA, ABPS with MOC status), professional liability (malpractice) insurance (occurrence/claims-made, per-occurrence and aggregate limits, tail coverage), hospital clinical privileges (admitting, surgical, procedural, courtesy), graduate medical education and training (medical school, residency, fellowship, ECFMG for IMGs), and professional work history (5-year history with gap explanations per NCQA). Each record stores: element type discriminator, issuing/granting authority, element-specific attributes (number, schedules, limits, scope, restrictions), effective and expiration dates, current status, PSV source contacted (FSMB, AMA, ABMS, NPDB, OIG, DEA Diversion Control, state boards, facilities, carriers, ECFMG), verification method, verification date, verification result (verified, unable to verify, discrepancy), and verifier identity. One record per credential element per provider per application cycle. Core to NCQA CR 1-4 compliance.'
}

Table credential.sanction_screening {
  sanction_screening_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sanction_screening']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent review.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the provider entity being screened.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the credential record to which this screening pertains.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Sanction screening services are outsourced; vendor linkage required for compliance reporting and cost allocation.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory rule that mandates the screening (e.g., CMS Conditions of Participation).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the screening record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  databases_queried varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of external sanction/exclusion databases queried during the screening.']
  impact_on_credential_status varchar [note: 'Effect of the sanction on the provider\'s credentialing status within the network.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the screening result was last reviewed by a credentialing committee.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the screening record.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the screening data (e.g., Provider Management System).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the specific regulation or guidance (e.g., 42 CFR Part 482).']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the screening resolution workflow.']
  result_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the match or reason for pending review, including identifiers of the finding.']
  sanction_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the sanction or exclusion became effective.']
  sanction_end_date date [note: 'Date when the sanction or exclusion expires or is lifted, if known.']
  sanction_type varchar [note: 'Category of confirmed adverse action identified by the screening.']
  sanctioning_authority varchar [note: 'Entity that issued the sanction or exclusion (e.g., OIG, CMS, State Health Department).']
  screening_event_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the screening as an initial credentialing check, a scheduled routine check, or a triggered ad‑hoc check.']
  screening_initiated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system name that triggered the screening.']
  screening_notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations captured by the reviewer.']
  screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the screening: no issues, a potential match, or pending manual review.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the screening was performed.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned to the sanction based on impact and regulatory guidance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the screening record.']

  Note: 'Consolidated record for the full sanctions and exclusions lifecycle — both screening events and confirmed adverse findings. Captures each screening event (date, type — initial/monthly routine/triggered, databases queried including OIG LEIE, SAM.gov, state Medicaid exclusion lists, NPDB, screening result — clear/match found/pending review, resolution status) and each confirmed sanction, exclusion, or disciplinary action (sanction type, sanctioning authority, effective/end dates, severity, impact on credentialing status). Record type discriminator distinguishes screening events from confirmed sanctions. Monthly OIG/SAM screening is a CMS Conditions of Participation requirement. Confirmed sanctions drive automatic credentialing holds and committee escalation. Links to application for initial/re-credentialing screenings; routine monthly screenings may exist independently.'
}

Table credential.npdb_query {
  npdb_query_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each NPDB query record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who initiated the NPDB query.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Internal system identifier of the provider being queried.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: NPDB queries are tied to a credential record; provider_id duplicated.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: NPDB queries may be executed by external data‑vendor platforms; linking supports audit trails and spend tracking.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification for the record as defined by enterprise policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NPDB query record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  hcqia_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the query satisfies HCQIA mandatory credentialing requirements.']
  internal_review_disposition varchar [note: 'Health plan\'s internal decision after reviewing NPDB reports.']
  is_continuous_enrollment boolean [note: 'True if the provider is enrolled in continuous NPDB monitoring.']
  last_report_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent adverse action report returned.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments or observations about the query.']
  npdb_assigned_query_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by NPDB for the submitted query.']
  npdb_query_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the NPDB query.']
  number_of_reports varchar [note: 'Total count of individual adverse action reports returned by NPDB for this query.']
  provider_npi varchar [note: '10‑digit NPI of the provider, used for external identification.']
  query_type varchar [note: 'Type of credentialing query: initial, re‑credentialing, or continuous monitoring.']
  query_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the NPDB query schema used for this submission.']
  report_processing_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds between query submission and receipt of the final report.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the NPDB returned the query response.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the NPDB query was submitted to the NPDB system.']
  total_malpractice_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount of malpractice payments reported across all returned reports.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this NPDB query record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) query and the individual adverse action or malpractice payment reports returned. Header-detail structure: header captures query submission (query type — initial/re-credentialing/continuous, submission date, NPDB-assigned query ID, queried provider, response date, number of reports returned); detail captures each report returned (NPDB report ID, report type — malpractice payment/clinical privileges action/DEA action/exclusion-debarment/judgment-conviction, reporting entity, subject of report, incident date, payment amount for malpractice, action taken, action date, and the health plan\'s internal review disposition — reviewed/flagged for committee/no action required). NPDB querying is mandatory under HCQIA for initial credentialing and re-credentialing. Continuous Query enrollment status tracked here. Each query with its nested reports is a complete transactional record.'
}

Table credential.committee_review {
  committee_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for committee_review']
  committee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master record of the credentialing committee.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Committee review pertains to a specific credential record; linking enables traceability and removes need for duplicate provider info.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks the employee who records the credentialing committee decision; needed for committee minutes and regulatory audit.']
  agenda_items varchar [note: 'List of agenda items discussed during the committee meeting.']
  appeal_rights_notification_date date [note: 'Date the provider was notified of appeal rights following a denial.']
  chair_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the committee chair.']
  chair_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the committee chair.']
  committee_review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the committee review.']
  compliance_flag_dea_valid boolean [note: 'True if the provider\'s DEA registration is valid.']
  compliance_flag_malpractice_history boolean [note: 'True if the provider has a documented malpractice history.']
  compliance_flag_oig_sanction boolean [note: 'True if the provider is flagged on the OIG sanctions list.']
  compliance_flag_state_license_valid boolean [note: 'True if the provider holds a current state medical license.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the committee review record was first created in the system.']
  decision_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or restrictions attached to an approved decision.']
  decision_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the committee decision becomes effective.']
  decision_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the decision expires or must be re-evaluated (nullable).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation supporting the committee\'s decision.']
  decision_type varchar [note: 'Outcome of the committee decision for a provider.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a provider\'s credentialing was denied.']
  meeting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the committee meeting took place.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any supplemental information about the committee review.']
  quorum_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the meeting met quorum requirements; otherwise False.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the committee review session (e.g., CR-2023-0001).']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Category of the committee session: credentialing, quality, or peer review.']
  total_providers_reviewed varchar [note: 'Count of distinct providers whose credentials were reviewed in this session.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the committee review record.']
  voting_record varchar [note: 'Summary of how the committee voted (e.g., unanimous, majority, split).']

  Note: 'Master record of a credentialing committee or peer review committee session and the formal credentialing decisions rendered. Captures committee meeting details (date, committee type — credentialing/quality/peer review, quorum indicator, agenda items, committee chair attestation) and the per-provider decisions produced (decision type — approved/approved with conditions/deferred/denied/revoked/suspended, decision effective date, decision expiration date, conditions or restrictions imposed, denial reason codes, appeal rights notification date, voting record, decision rationale). This is the authoritative record that triggers network participation status changes and is the SSOT for credentialing decisions — no separate decision product exists. NCQA requires documented committee review for all credentialing decisions. One committee session record with embedded per-provider decision outcomes.'
}

Table credential.recredential_cycle {
  recredential_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for recredential_cycle']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal staff member responsible for managing the cycle.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider whose credential is being re‑credentialed.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credential record associated with the provider.']
  application_received_date date [note: 'Date the provider submitted the recredentialing application.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of specific compliance items (e.g., DEA license, state license) required for this cycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recredentialing cycle record was first created.']
  cycle_completion_date date [note: 'Date the recredentialing cycle was marked completed.']
  cycle_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the recredentialing must be completed (typically 36 months from start).']
  cycle_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the cycle for scheduling and escalation.']
  cycle_start_date date [note: 'Date the recredentialing cycle officially begins.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recredentialing cycle.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cycle (full, partial, or renewal).']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date the escalation was triggered.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cycle has been escalated due to approaching due date or non‑response.']
  last_outreach_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent outreach attempt.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by staff during the cycle.']
  outreach_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of outreach attempts made to the provider during the cycle.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing regulation or standard that mandates the recredentialing (e.g., NCQA 2023).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the record (e.g., Cactus, ProviderSource).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recredentialing cycle record.']

  Note: 'Master record managing the re-credentialing cycle schedule for each credentialed provider. Captures cycle start date, cycle due date (NCQA mandates re-credentialing every 36 months), cycle status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, overdue, waived), outreach attempts and dates, application received date, and cycle completion date. Drives automated workflow triggers for re-credentialing initiation, provider outreach, and escalation when providers approach expiration. Distinct from the credentialing application — this is the scheduling and tracking wrapper.'
}

Table credential.delegated_entity {
  delegated_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delegated_entity']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Regulatory requirement to track which external vendor provides delegated credentialing services for each entity.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form observations, findings, and recommendations from the latest audit.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent audit (e.g., compliant, non‑compliant, conditional).']
  audit_schedule varchar [note: 'Planned frequency for oversight audits of the delegated credentialing entity.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory and accreditation requirements that the delegate must satisfy (e.g., OIG, SAM, HIPAA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delegated entity record was first created in the system.']
  delegated_entity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delegation relationship.']
  delegation_agreement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned to the delegation agreement between the health plan and the delegate organization.']
  delegation_scope varchar [note: 'Specific scope of authority granted to the delegate organization.']
  delegation_type varchar [note: 'Category of delegation: full credentialing, re‑credentialing only, or primary source verification only.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the delegation agreement becomes legally binding.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit performed on the delegate.']
  ncqa_accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the delegate organization according to NCQA standards.']
  npi varchar [note: 'Unique 10‑digit identifier for the delegated organization as assigned by the National Plan and Provider Enumeration System.']
  organization_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the organization to which credentialing authority is delegated.']
  oversight_audit_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled oversight audits of the delegate.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the delegated entity data (e.g., ProviderSource, Cactus).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking the delegation to a specific regulatory mandate or guidance.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the delegation agreement ends or is terminated (null if open‑ended).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the delegated entity record.']

  Note: 'Master record for an organization (hospital system, medical group, IPA, CVO) to which the health plan has delegated credentialing authority under a formal delegation agreement. Captures delegate organization name, NPI, delegation agreement effective date, delegation scope (full credentialing, re-credentialing, primary source verification), NCQA accreditation status of the delegate, oversight audit schedule, last audit date, audit result, and delegation status (active, suspended, terminated). NCQA requires health plans to oversee and audit delegated credentialing entities.'
}

Table credential.delegation_audit {
  delegation_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the delegation audit record.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delegated credentialing entity being audited.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual or entity that performed the audit.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit was performed (principal business event).']
  audit_disposition varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the audit: passed, passed with conditions, or failed.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Additional observations, comments, or contextual information captured by the auditor.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the audit, often used in reports and communications.']
  audit_period_end date [note: 'End date of the audit coverage period.']
  audit_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the audit coverage period.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the functional and geographic scope covered by the audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit record.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on purpose: initial, annual, or for‑cause.']
  audit_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the audit took place.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the auditor.']
  compliance_findings varchar [note: 'Free‑text summary of findings mapped to NCQA standard elements.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the delegated entity must complete required corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any corrective actions were mandated as a result of the audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  delegation_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the delegated credentialing organization.']
  files_reviewed_count varchar [note: 'Total count of credentialing files examined during the audit.']
  overall_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0‑100) representing overall compliance across all NCQA elements.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing standard or regulation (e.g., NCQA CR 5).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the audit data (e.g., Provider Management System – Cactus).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an oversight audit conducted on a delegated credentialing entity. Captures audit date, audit type (initial, annual, for-cause), audit scope, auditor identity, number of files reviewed, compliance findings by NCQA standard element, overall compliance rate, corrective action required indicator, corrective action plan due date, and audit disposition (passed, passed with conditions, failed). Required by NCQA CR 5 to ensure delegated entities meet health plan credentialing standards. Each audit is a distinct transactional record.'
}

Table credential.credential_attestation {
  credential_attestation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for attestation']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who reviewed and acted on the attestation.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider who submitted the attestation.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credentialing record to which this attestation is attached.']
  application_accuracy boolean [note: 'Provider attests that all information provided in the credentialing application is true and accurate.']
  attestation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the attestation submission.']
  attestation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the attestation within the credentialing workflow.']
  attestation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the provider signed and submitted the attestation.']
  attestation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the attestation is for an initial application, a re‑credentialing cycle, or an annual renewal.']
  competence_current boolean [note: 'Provider confirms they are currently competent to perform the services covered by the credential.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or accreditation rule that mandates the attestation (e.g., NCQA, CMS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the attestation record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  disclosure_text varchar [note: 'Any additional disclosures or comments supplied by the provider with the attestation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the attestation is no longer valid and must be renewed.']
  felony_conviction boolean [note: 'Provider attests they have no felony convictions that would affect credential eligibility.']
  impairment_absence boolean [note: 'Provider attests they have no physical or mental impairment that would affect care delivery.']
  malpractice_history_accuracy boolean [note: 'Provider confirms that all disclosed malpractice history is complete and accurate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by staff during processing of the attestation.']
  privileges_loss boolean [note: 'Provider confirms they have not lost any hospital or health‑system privileges.']
  provider_signature_indicator boolean [note: 'True when the provider has electronically signed the attestation; false otherwise.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the attestation record (e.g., ProviderSource).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation, guideline, or standard governing the attestation.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the attestation review: approved, rejected, or requires additional information.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the attestation review decision was recorded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the attestation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a provider\'s signed attestation submitted as part of the credentialing or re-credentialing application. Captures attestation date, attestation type (initial application, re-credentialing, annual attestation), attestation questions answered (current competence, absence of impairment, no loss of privileges, no felony convictions, malpractice history accuracy, application accuracy), provider signature indicator, and any disclosures made. NCQA requires provider attestation within 180 days of credentialing decision. Each attestation submission is a distinct record.'
}

Table credential.credential_outreach {
  credential_outreach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for outreach']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system process that originated the outreach.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider to whom the outreach was sent.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Outreach communications relate to a credential record; provider_id duplicated.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if the outreach included attached files or forms; otherwise false.']
  contact_detail varchar [note: 'Specific address, email, or phone number used to deliver the outreach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the outreach record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  credential_outreach_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the outreach (e.g., pending, sent, acknowledged, completed, closed, cancelled).']
  document_type_requested varchar [note: 'Category of documentation the provider was asked to supply.']
  items_requested varchar [note: 'Free‑text list of documents, data elements, or actions requested from the provider.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or instructions related to the outreach.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the outreach after the response window closed.']
  outreach_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the outreach (mail, email, phone call, provider portal, or fax).']
  outreach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time the outreach communication was transmitted to the provider.']
  outreach_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outreach purpose, such as initial request, follow‑up, final notice, expiration warning, or reminder.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority level for the outreach, used for routing and escalation.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference code assigned to the outreach event for tracking and reporting.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Calendar date by which the provider is expected to respond to the outreach.']
  response_method varchar [note: 'Channel the provider used to submit their response.']
  response_notes varchar [note: 'Additional information supplied by the provider in their response.']
  response_received_date date [note: 'Date the provider\'s response was recorded in the system.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider responded within the SLA target (true) or not (false).']
  sla_target_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days defined as the service‑level agreement for provider response.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the outreach record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the outreach record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each outreach communication sent to a provider during the credentialing or re-credentialing process to request missing information, incomplete documents, or application updates. Captures outreach date, outreach method (mail, email, phone, portal), outreach type (initial request, follow-up, final notice, expiration warning), items requested, response due date, response received date, and outreach outcome. Tracks the full communication trail required for NCQA file completeness standards and supports SLA monitoring for credentialing turnaround time.'
}

Table credential.credential_lifecycle_event {
  credential_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lifecycle_event']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Audit findings often mandate credential status changes; linking events to the originating audit finding supports audit‑driven remediation reporting.']
  primary_credential_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the actor (staff or system) that performed the action.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider associated with the credential.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credential record this event pertains to.']
  related_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of any supporting document attached to the event.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed audit trail for this event.']
  audit_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the audit user (e.g., auditor, manager, admin).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the event satisfies compliance requirements (e.g., NCQA).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data confidentiality classification for this event record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was created in the data warehouse.']
  decision_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the committee decision outcome, if applicable.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date when the committee decision was rendered.']
  document_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the attached document creation.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of supporting document (e.g., PDF, image, text, XML).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the new status becomes effective.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the escalation occurred.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the event (e.g., application, verification, decision, activation, suspension, termination, recredentialing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application|verification|decision|activation|suspension|termination|recredentialing — promote to reference product]']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the event details.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lifecycle event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of lifecycle event (e.g., application_received, psv_initiated, psv_completed, committee_scheduled, decision_rendered, credential_activated, credential_suspended, credential_terminated, recredentialing_triggered). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application_received|psv_initiated|psv_completed|committee_scheduled|decision_rendered|credential_activated|credential_suspended|credential_terminated|recredentialing_triggered — promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the credential status is set to expire, if applicable.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the event is considered critical for audit or operational purposes.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the event was escalated to higher authority.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event was entered manually (True) or generated automatically (False).']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Credential status after this event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|approved|rejected|suspended|terminated|active — promote to reference product]']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'Credential status before this event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|approved|rejected|suspended|terminated|active — promote to reference product]']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the record (e.g., system feed, manual entry, import).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory rule or guideline applicable to the event.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Final resolution status of the event.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event was resolved or closed.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned to the event based on compliance and sanction considerations.']
  risk_score_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the assigned risk score.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the event was recorded.']
  triggering_actor varchar [note: 'Entity that triggered the event (e.g., system, staff, committee, external).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional event log capturing all significant status changes and lifecycle events in the credentialing workflow for a provider application or credential record. Captures event type (application received, PSV initiated, PSV completed, committee scheduled, decision rendered, credential activated, credential suspended, credential terminated, re-credentialing triggered), event timestamp, prior status, new status, triggering actor (system, staff, committee), and event notes. Provides the full audit trail of the credentialing lifecycle required for NCQA and regulatory audit readiness.'
}

Table credential.cvo_relationship {
  cvo_relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CVO relationship record.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Credentials Verification Organization (CVO) engaged.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan that contracts with the CVO.']
  primary_cvo_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the record.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the CVO relationship meets current regulatory compliance requirements.']
  contact_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address for the CVO contact.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary CVO contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary contact person name for the CVO relationship.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary CVO contact.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract reference number assigned by the health plan.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Scope of services provided by the CVO under the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CVO relationship record was first created.']
  cvo_relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CVO relationship.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the CVO relationship becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the CVO relationship ends or is scheduled to terminate (null if open-ended).']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount payable to the CVO for contracted services.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the contract fee (e.g., USD).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the CVO is the exclusive provider of credentialing services for the health plan.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or performance review.']
  ncqa_certification_expiration date [note: 'Expiration date of the CVO\'s NCQA certification.']
  ncqa_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CVO holds a current NCQA certification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or special conditions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms for the contract.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing regulatory body or standard (e.g., CMS, NCQA) applicable to the contract.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CVO relationship (e.g., primary vendor, secondary backup).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the CVO relationship record (e.g., Facets, Cactus).']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for contract termination, if applicable.']
  turnaround_time_sla_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days the CVO must complete primary source verification per service level agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CVO relationship record.']

  Note: 'Master record representing the relationship between the health plan and a Credentials Verification Organization (CVO) engaged to perform PSV and credentialing file preparation services. Captures CVO organization name, NCQA CVO certification status and expiration, contracted services scope (PSV only, full file preparation, ongoing monitoring), contract effective date, contract expiration date, turnaround time SLAs, and relationship status. Distinct from delegated credentialing entities — CVOs perform verification services but the health plan retains the credentialing decision authority.'
}

Table credential.expedited_credential {
  expedited_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for expedited_credential']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Captures the credentialing employee approving expedited requests; critical for urgent care provider onboarding and audit of fast‑track decisions.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider for whom expedited credentialing is requested.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Expedited credential requests are for a credential record; provider_id redundant.']
  approval_committee varchar [note: 'Name of the credentialing committee or board that approved the provisional request.']
  attestation_received boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the required attestation documentation has been received.']
  attestation_received_date date [note: 'Date the attestation was received.']
  clinical_urgency_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the clinical urgency driving the provisional credential request.']
  committee_decision_date date [note: 'Date the approval committee rendered its decision.']
  conditions_of_participation varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or limitations imposed on the provider during the provisional period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the expedited credentialing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for the currency of the provisional fee.']
  dea_license_number varchar [note: 'Drug Enforcement Administration license number for controlled substance prescribing.']
  expedited_credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the expedited credentialing request.']
  expedited_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating the reason for expedited processing.']
  final_credentialing_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the full credentialing process after the provisional period.']
  hospital_privileges_verified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating verification of hospital privileges for the provider.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the provider\'s DEA or state license.']
  malpractice_claims_count varchar [note: 'Number of malpractice claims recorded against the provider.']
  malpractice_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider has a known malpractice history.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the expedited request.']
  outcome_date date [note: 'Date the final credentialing outcome was determined.']
  provider_npi varchar [note: 'Standard 10‑digit identifier for the provider.']
  provisional_duration_days varchar [note: 'Calculated length of the provisional period in days.']
  provisional_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the provisional participation period.']
  provisional_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged for granting provisional credentialing, if applicable.']
  provisional_start_date date [note: 'First day the provider is allowed to deliver services under provisional status.']
  psv_verification_date date [note: 'Date primary source verification was performed.']
  psv_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether primary source verification of credentials has been completed.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the expedited credentialing request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the expedited credentialing request was submitted.']
  requesting_entity_reference varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the requesting entity.']
  requesting_entity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity that initiated the expedited request (e.g., hospital, medical group).']
  sanction_screening_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OIG/SAM sanction screening has been performed.']
  sanction_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the sanction screening process.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the record (e.g., ProviderSource).']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'State medical license number for the provider.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the expedited credentialing record.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the request based on clinical need.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for expedited or provisional credentialing requests where a provider needs temporary network participation authorization before the full credentialing process is complete. Captures request date, requesting entity (hospital, medical group, health plan), clinical urgency justification, provisional start date, provisional end date (typically 120 days), conditions of provisional participation, attestation received indicator, and final credentialing outcome. NCQA permits provisional credentialing under specific conditions. Tracks the provisional period and ensures timely conversion to full credential.'
}

Table credential.credential_appeal {
  credential_appeal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credentialing appeal record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory compliance reporting requires each credential appeal to reference the specific regulatory obligation that triggered the appeal, enabling obligation‑specific tracking.']
  decision_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal decision document stored in the document management system.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the provider submitting the appeal.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credential record that is the subject of the appeal.']
