  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Identifies the employee who adjudicates each credentialing appeal; required for appeal outcome reporting and OIG compliance.']
  appeal_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the appeal for tracking.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the appeal.']
  appeal_type varchar [note: 'Category of the appeal based on procedural pathway.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for any monetary amounts.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date the appeal decision was rendered.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal after hearing.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date the appeal was escalated.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal was escalated to a higher authority.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the provider for processing the appeal, if applicable.']
  ground varchar [note: 'Reason(s) provided by the provider for contesting the credentialing decision.']
  hearing_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the appeal hearing, if applicable.']
  hearing_panel_members varchar [note: 'List of individuals (names or IDs) comprising the hearing panel.']
  hearing_panel_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the hearing panel is internal to the organization or external.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes regarding the appeal processing.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date the decision notification was sent to the provider.']
  original_decision_date date [note: 'Date of the original credentialing decision that is being appealed.']
  original_decision_type varchar [note: 'Type of the original adverse credentialing decision.']
  outcome_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the decision outcome.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appeal record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the appeal record.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or statute governing the appeal (e.g., HCQIA, state DOI).']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken as a result of the appeal (e.g., credential reinstated, conditions added).']
  review_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the appeal must be reviewed per regulatory timelines.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the appeal originated (e.g., Provider Management System).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the provider submitted the appeal.']
  supporting_documentation varchar [note: 'Reference to files or descriptions of documents submitted with the appeal.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for a provider\'s formal appeal of an adverse credentialing decision (denial, revocation, suspension). Captures appeal submission date, appeal type (informal reconsideration, formal hearing, external review), grounds for appeal, supporting documentation submitted, hearing date, hearing panel composition, appeal decision (upheld, overturned, modified), appeal decision date, and notification sent date. Distinct from the member/claim appeal domain — this is provider-side credentialing due process. Required by HCQIA fair hearing rights and state DOI regulations.'
}

Table credential.contract_link {
  contract_link_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the credential_contract_link association']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential record']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor contract']
  contract_role varchar [note: 'Role of the contract for this credential (e.g., primary, supplemental)']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the contract coverage becomes effective for this credential']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the contract coverage expires for this credential']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a vendor contract to a provider credential record. Each row captures the contract role (primary, supplemental, etc.) and the period during which the contract covers the credential.. Existence Justification: A credential record represents a provider\'s credentialing status with a health plan. Multiple vendor contracts can cover the same credential record (e.g., primary and supplemental credentialing services), and each vendor contract can cover many credential records across providers. The business actively creates, updates, and deletes these links and tracks role, effective and expiration dates for each coverage.'
}

Table credential.obligation_mapping {
  obligation_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the credential_obligation_mapping association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory_obligation']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential_record']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the credential starts satisfying the regulatory obligation']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the credential ceases to satisfy the regulatory obligation']

  Note: 'This association product represents the mapping between a credential record and a regulatory obligation. It captures the effective and expiration dates that define when a specific credential satisfies a particular regulatory requirement.. Existence Justification: A credential record can satisfy multiple regulatory obligations (e.g., NCQA, HIPAA) and a single regulatory obligation must be satisfied by many credential records across providers. The business actively tracks each credential‑obligation pairing with effective and expiration dates, making the relationship a true many‑to‑many operational entity.'
}

Table credential.finding {
  finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the credential_finding association']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the audit finding.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the credential record.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit finding is compliant with the specific credential.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the regulation or standard that the finding references for this credential.']

  Note: 'Represents the operational link between a provider\'s credential record and an audit finding. Each row records that a particular finding applies to a specific credential and captures attributes that belong only to this relationship.. Existence Justification: During audit engagements, auditors associate each audit finding with the specific provider credential records that are impacted. A single finding can affect multiple credential records, and a single credential record can be the subject of multiple findings over time. The link is actively created, updated, and tracked as part of the audit remediation process.'
}

Table credential.committee {
  committee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for committee']
  committee_approval_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the approval document in the document management system.']
  committee_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the committee type, referencing the Committee Type reference table.']
  parent_committee_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on committee (parent_committee_id)']
  committee_approval_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the committee.']
  committee_approval_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided during the approval process.']
  committee_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the committee received formal approval.']
  committee_approval_document_checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum value for integrity verification of the approval document.']
  committee_approval_document_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval document was created.']
  committee_approval_document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the approval document (e.g., PDF, DOCX).']
  committee_approval_document_size varchar [note: 'Size of the approval document in bytes.']
  committee_approval_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the approval document (e.g., PDF, DOCX).']
  committee_approval_document_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the approval document.']
  committee_approval_document_url varchar [note: 'URL to the approval document stored in the document management system.']
  committee_approval_document_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the approval document.']
  committee_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the approval process (Pending, Approved, Rejected).']
  committee_contact_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the committee contact person.']
  committee_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for committee communication.']
  committee_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person for the committee.']
  committee_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for committee contact.']
  committee_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the committee record was first created.']
  committee_created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the committee record.']
  committee_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the committee\'s purpose and scope.']
  committee_end_date date [note: 'Date when the committee was dissolved or ceased operations, if applicable.']
  committee_is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the committee is currently active.']
  committee_is_approved boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the committee has been formally approved.']
  committee_is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this committee is the default for certain processes.']
  committee_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or office where the committee is based.']
  committee_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the committee.']
  committee_start_date date [note: 'Date when the committee became operational.']
  committee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the committee (Active, Inactive, Pending).']
  committee_type varchar [note: 'Category of the committee (e.g., Credentialing, Compliance, Quality).']
  committee_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the committee record.']
  committee_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the committee record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for committee. Referenced by committee_id.'
}

Table credential.decision_document {
  decision_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for decision_document']
  decision_approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who approved the decision.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who authored the decision.']
  parent_decision_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on decision_document (parent_decision_document_id)']
  decision_approval_date date [note: 'Date the decision was approved.']
  decision_approval_time timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the decision was approved.']
  decision_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the staff member who approved the decision.']
  decision_approver_role varchar [note: 'Role or title of the staff member who approved the decision.']
  decision_author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the staff member who authored the decision.']
  decision_author_role varchar [note: 'Role or title of the staff member who authored the decision.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date the decision was made.']
  decision_document_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decision document record was first created.']
  decision_document_created_by varchar [note: 'User who created the decision document record.']
  decision_document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the decision document (PDF, DOCX, HTML).']
  decision_document_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the decision document for integrity verification.']
  decision_document_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the decision document is written.']
  decision_document_revision varchar [note: 'Revision number of the decision document.']
  decision_document_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the decision document in bytes.']
  decision_document_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that originated the decision document.']
  decision_document_source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system record.']
  decision_document_source_system_description varchar [note: 'Description of the source system record.']
  decision_document_source_system_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the owner of the source system.']
  decision_document_source_system_owner_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the source system owner.']
  decision_document_source_system_owner_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system owner.']
  decision_document_source_system_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the source system owner.']
  decision_document_source_system_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the source system owner.']
  decision_document_source_system_owner_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the source system owner.']
  decision_document_source_system_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the source system record.']
  decision_document_source_system_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the source system record.']
  decision_document_source_system_version varchar [note: 'Version of the source system record.']
  decision_document_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decision document record was last updated.']
  decision_document_updated_by varchar [note: 'User who last updated the decision document record.']
  decision_document_url varchar [note: 'Web location where the decision document can be accessed.']
  decision_document_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the decision document.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the decision (Approved, Denied, Conditional).']
  decision_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why the decision was made.']
  decision_time timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the decision was made.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible number assigned to the decision document.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document (Draft, Pending Review, Approved, Rejected, Archived).']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the decision document.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the document (e.g., Credentialing Decision, Recredentialing Decision, Sanction Decision).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the decision becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the decision expires or is no longer valid.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for decision_document. Referenced by decision_document_id.'
}

Table credential.credential_document {
  credential_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for credential_document']

  credential_credentialing_event_date date [note: 'Date of the credentialing event (initial, renewal, etc.).']
  credential_credentialing_event_notes varchar [note: 'Notes related to the credentialing event.']
  credential_credentialing_event_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the credentialing event.']
  credential_credentialing_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of credentialing event.']
  credential_document_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credential document record was created.']

  credential_issuing_authority_city varchar [note: 'City where the issuing authority is located.']

  credential_number varchar [note: 'Unique professional license or certification number issued to the provider.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the provider\'s credential.']
  credential_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current credential status.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Professional designation or qualification type (e.g., MD, RN, PhD).']
  credential_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the credential verification was completed.']
  credential_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the credential (e.g., PSV, NCQA, State License Check).']
  credential_verification_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments from the verification process.']
  credential_verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the credential verification process.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for credential_document. Referenced by related_document_id.'
}

Table credential.committee_type {
  committee_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for committee_type']
  parent_committee_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on committee_type (parent_committee_type_id)']
  allows_virtual_participation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of this committee type are permitted to participate and vote remotely via teleconference or video, per organizational bylaws and applicable state regulations.']
  authority_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the decision-making authority of this committee type — whether it renders final credentialing decisions, makes recommendations to a higher body, serves in an advisory capacity, or operates under delegated authority from the governing board.']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level functional category grouping committee types for reporting and governance purposes. Distinguishes credentialing decision-making committees from peer review, quality oversight, advisory, and appeals bodies.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the committee type, used as a business-facing natural key in credentialing workflows and system integrations (e.g., CRED_COMM, PEER_REV, MED_ADV).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and responsibilities of this committee type within the credentialing and provider oversight lifecycle.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order controlling the presentation sequence of committee types in user interfaces, dropdown lists, and reports. Lower values appear first.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this committee type is no longer valid for new assignments. Null indicates the committee type is currently open-ended and in effect. Used for reference data lifecycle management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this committee type became effective and available for use in credentialing governance structures. Supports temporal validity tracking for reference data management.']
  meeting_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard frequency at which this committee type convenes to review credentialing files and render decisions. NCQA requires timely processing, so meeting cadence directly impacts credentialing cycle time.']
  minimum_quorum_count varchar [note: 'The minimum number of voting members required to be present for the committee to conduct official business and render binding credentialing decisions, per organizational bylaws and NCQA standards.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the committee type (e.g., \'Credentials Committee\', \'Peer Review Committee\', \'Medical Advisory Committee\', \'Quality Improvement Committee\').']
  ncqa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this committee type is explicitly required by NCQA credentialing accreditation standards. Used to distinguish mandatory committee structures from optional or organization-specific governance bodies.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Citation or reference to the specific regulatory requirement, accreditation standard, or organizational bylaw that mandates or authorizes this committee type (e.g., \'NCQA CR 1 Element A\', \'42 CFR 438.214\', state-specific credentialing statute).']
  requires_physician_chair boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this committee type requires a licensed physician (MD/DO) to serve as chairperson, as mandated by NCQA credentialing standards and many state regulations for credentialing decision-making bodies.']
  review_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of credentialing activities this committee type is authorized to review — initial credentialing, recredentialing, both, sanctions/disciplinary actions, or appeals of adverse decisions.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this committee type reference record. Active types are available for assignment to credentialing workflows; inactive or deprecated types are retained for historical reference but cannot be assigned to new committees.']
  voting_method varchar [note: 'The voting methodology used by this committee type to reach credentialing decisions — simple majority, supermajority (two-thirds), unanimous, or consensus-based.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for committee_type. Referenced by committee_type_id.'
}

Table network.provider_network {
  provider_network_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the provider network. Primary key for the provider network entity.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Needed to post network‑level financial transactions (fees, reimbursements) to a dedicated general ledger for audit and reporting.']
  network_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Network Management Vendor Assignment report requires linking each provider network to its contracted vendor for credentialing and data services.']
  aco_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this network includes participation in ACO arrangements. True if ACO providers are part of the network, False otherwise.']
  cms_network_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned by CMS for Medicare Advantage and Part D networks. Required for all Medicare networks. Format: one letter followed by five digits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this provider network record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this provider network becomes active and available for member enrollment and provider participation. Start of the network\'s operational lifecycle.']
  facility_count varchar [note: 'The number of hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, and other institutional providers in this network. Used for facility access adequacy assessments.']
  geographic_footprint varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of states, counties, or service areas where this network operates. Defines the geographic scope of provider availability and member eligibility.']
  last_adequacy_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent internal or regulatory network adequacy review. Used to track compliance with annual review requirements.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'The primary business segment this network serves. Commercial (employer-sponsored), Medicare (federal beneficiaries), Medicaid (state beneficiaries), Exchange (ACA marketplace), ASO (Administrative Services Only self-funded).']
  member_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'The current number of members enrolled in plans associated with this network. Used for provider-to-member ratio calculations and capacity planning.']
  ncqa_accreditation_date date [note: 'The date when NCQA accreditation was granted or last renewed for this network. Null if not accredited.']
  ncqa_accreditation_status varchar [note: 'The current NCQA accreditation status for this network. Accredited (full accreditation), Provisional (conditional approval), Denied (failed accreditation), Not Applicable (not seeking accreditation), Pending (under review).']
  ncqa_expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the current NCQA accreditation expires and renewal is required. Null if not accredited.']
  network_adequacy_filing_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent network adequacy filing was submitted to CMS or state regulators. Required annually for Medicare Advantage and Exchange plans.']
  network_adequacy_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory assessment of whether the network meets time-and-distance and provider-to-member ratio standards. Adequate (meets all standards), Deficient (fails standards), Conditional (approved with corrective action plan), Pending Review (under regulatory evaluation).']
  network_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the network in operational systems, claims processing, and provider directories. Externally-known business identifier.']
  network_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the network\'s design, provider composition, geographic coverage, and unique features. Used in member materials and regulatory filings.']
  network_directory_url varchar [note: 'The web URL where members and providers can access the online provider directory for this network. Required for ACA and Medicare Advantage compliance.']
  network_name varchar [note: 'The official business name of the provider network (e.g., \'HMO Narrow Network\', \'PPO Broad Network\', \'Medicare Advantage Network\'). Human-readable identifier used in member communications and plan documents.']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the provider network. Active (accepting enrollments and claims), Inactive (closed to new enrollments), Suspended (temporary hold), Pending (awaiting regulatory approval), Terminated (permanently closed).']
  network_type varchar [note: 'The structural classification of the provider network. HMO (Health Maintenance Organization) requires PCP and referrals; PPO (Preferred Provider Organization) allows out-of-network access; EPO (Exclusive Provider Organization) no out-of-network except emergency; POS (Point of Service) hybrid model; HDHP (High Deductible Health Plan) network; ACO (Accountable Care Organization) value-based network.']
  next_adequacy_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next network adequacy review. Typically annual for Medicare Advantage and Exchange networks.']
  out_of_network_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the network allows members to access out-of-network providers with cost-sharing. True for PPO and some POS networks, False for HMO and EPO.']
  pcp_count varchar [note: 'The number of primary care physicians in this network. Key metric for network adequacy and member-to-PCP ratio calculations.']
  pcp_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members enrolled in this network are required to select a primary care physician. True for HMO and POS networks, False for PPO and EPO.']
  provider_count varchar [note: 'The total number of unique providers (physicians, facilities, ancillary) participating in this network. Used for network adequacy calculations and member communications.']
  referral_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialist visits require a referral from the PCP. True for HMO networks, False for PPO, EPO, and most POS networks.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'The date when the network received regulatory approval from CMS or state Department of Insurance to begin operations.']
  service_area_type varchar [note: 'The geographic granularity at which this network is defined. Statewide (entire state), Regional (multi-county region), County (county-level), ZIP (ZIP code level), National (multi-state or nationwide).']
  specialist_count varchar [note: 'The number of specialist physicians in this network. Used for specialty access adequacy assessments.']
  star_rating decimal(2,1) [note: 'The CMS Star Rating for Medicare Advantage networks, ranging from 1.0 to 5.0 stars. Based on quality measures including network access, member satisfaction, and clinical outcomes. Null for non-Medicare networks.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date when this provider network ceases operations and is no longer available for new enrollments. Nullable for open-ended networks. Existing members may have run-out periods.']
  tier_classification varchar [note: 'The tiering structure of the network for cost-sharing purposes. Tier 1 (lowest cost-sharing, narrow network), Tier 2 (moderate cost-sharing), Tier 3 (highest cost-sharing, broadest access), Single Tier (no tiering), Tiered (multi-tier structure).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this network record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this provider network record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  vbc_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this network includes value-based care arrangements with providers (e.g., shared savings, bundled payments, capitation). True if VBC arrangements exist, False otherwise.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this network record. Used for audit and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a named provider network — the structural grouping of providers under a specific network product (e.g., HMO Narrow Network, PPO Broad Network, Medicare Advantage Network). Owns network identity, network type (HMO, PPO, EPO, POS), line of business association, effective/termination dates, geographic footprint, CMS network ID, NCQA accreditation status, and tier classification. SSOT for network identity — distinct from plan (benefit design), provider (provider identity), and contract (reimbursement terms).'
}

Table network.tier {
  tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network tier. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this network tier record.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network tier definitions are scoped to a specific provider network; adding provider_network_id FK establishes the parent relationship.']
  coinsurance_differential_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage coinsurance differential for this tier. Used when cost_share_differential_type is coinsurance or hybrid. Null if not applicable.']
  copay_differential_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar copay differential for this tier compared to baseline tier. Used when cost_share_differential_type is copay or hybrid. Null if not applicable.']
  cost_share_differential_type varchar [note: 'Type of cost-sharing differential applied to this tier (copay, coinsurance, deductible, hybrid, or none). Determines how member out-of-pocket (OOP) costs are calculated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network tier record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_applies_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan deductible applies to services rendered by providers in this tier before cost-sharing begins.']
  display_label varchar [note: 'Member-facing display label for the tier shown in EOB, member portal, and Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC). May differ from internal tier name for clarity.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this network tier configuration becomes active and applicable to member cost-sharing and provider network assignments.']
  facility_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Defines which facility types this tier applies to (all, hospital, Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF), Durable Medical Equipment (DME), lab, imaging, pharmacy, home health, or custom list). Used to segment tier assignment rules by facility type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|hospital|snf|dme|lab|imaging|pharmacy|home_health|custom — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes or comments about this tier configuration. Not visible to members or external parties.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this network tier record.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network tier record was last modified.']
  member_steerage_incentive_description varchar [note: 'Description of any member steerage incentives or rewards offered for using providers in this tier (e.g., reduced cost-sharing, waived deductible, wellness credits).']
  network_adequacy_credit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in this tier count toward CMS network adequacy standards and state Department of Insurance (DOI) access requirements.']
  oop_max_applies_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether member cost-sharing for this tier counts toward the plan Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP) limit.']
  prior_authorization_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether services from providers in this tier require Prior Authorization (PA) or Utilization Management (UM) review.']
  quality_tier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier is designated as a quality tier based on HEDIS, STAR ratings, or other quality performance metrics.']
  referral_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must obtain a referral from their Primary Care Provider (PCP) to access providers in this tier. Typically applies to HMO and POS plans.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the state Department of Insurance (DOI) or CMS filing that approved this tier structure. Used for compliance audit trails.']
  sbc_disclosure_text varchar [note: 'Standardized disclosure text for this tier that appears in the Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) document provided to members under ACA requirements.']
  specialty_applicability varchar [note: 'Defines which provider specialties this tier applies to (all, primary care only, specialist only, facility, ancillary, behavioral health, or custom list). Used to segment tier assignment rules by provider type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|primary_care|specialist|facility|ancillary|behavioral_health|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this network tier configuration is terminated and no longer applicable. Null if currently active.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the network tier (e.g., TIER1, TIER2, TIER3, OON). Used in system integrations and member communications.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the network tier as defined in plan documents and contracts.']
  tier_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the tier indicating preference and cost-share level. Lower rank indicates preferred tier with lower member cost-sharing (e.g., 1=Tier 1 Preferred, 2=Tier 2 Standard, 3=Tier 3 Specialty, 99=Out-of-Network).']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the network tier configuration (active, inactive, pending approval, suspended, or retired).']
  tier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tier indicating its strategic purpose within the network design (preferred, standard, specialty, out-of-network, value-based, narrow network).']
  vbc_arrangement_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier is eligible for Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) performance incentives and shared savings arrangements.']

  Note: 'Defines the tier structure within a provider network — Tier 1 (preferred/lowest cost-share), Tier 2 (standard), Tier 3 (specialty/higher cost-share), and out-of-network designations. Captures tier name, tier rank, cost-share differential rules, member-facing display label, and applicability by specialty or facility type. Used to drive member cost-sharing calculations and EOB generation. Distinct from contract fee schedules (owned by contract domain).'
}

Table network.network_provider {
  network_provider_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network-provider association record. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating provider network expenses to a cost center for accounting and regulatory expense reporting.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: When credentialing is delegated, Network Provider must reference the Delegated Credentialing Entity that performed the credentialing, required for the Delegated Credentialing Compliance Report.']
  liaison_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Network Provider Management: each provider is assigned a liaison employee who oversees contracts and compliance; required for provider relationship reporting.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider participating in this network. Links to the provider master entity.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network to which this provider participates. Links to the network master entity.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Network Provider participation requires linking to the specific Credential Record used for credentialing, enabling the Network Participation Report and compliance audit.']
  telehealth_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Telehealth Service Vendor Tracking records which external vendor supplies the telehealth platform for each network provider.']
  accepting_new_patients_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider is currently accepting new patients from this network. True indicates accepting new patients.']
  accessibility_ada_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider\'s facilities meet ADA accessibility requirements for members with disabilities. True indicates ADA compliance.']
  aco_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider participates in an ACO arrangement within this network. True indicates ACO participation.']
  after_hours_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider offers after-hours or extended-hours services to members of this network. True indicates after-hours availability.']
  continuity_of_care_end_date date [note: 'The date through which existing members may continue to see this provider under in-network benefits after network termination, per continuity of care requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this network-provider association record was first created in the system.']
  credentialing_date date [note: 'The date on which the provider was most recently credentialed for participation in this network.']
  credentialing_status varchar [note: 'Current credentialing status of the provider for participation in this network. Credentialed indicates all requirements met.']
  current_panel_size varchar [note: 'The current number of members assigned to or served by this provider within this network.']
  directory_last_verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the provider\'s directory information was last verified for accuracy. CMS requires quarterly verification.']
  directory_listing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider should be included in the published provider directory for this network. True indicates directory inclusion.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the provider\'s participation in this network became or will become effective.']
  geographic_service_area varchar [note: 'The geographic area or region within which this provider serves members of this network. May be county, ZIP code range, or custom service area code.']
  in_network_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider is currently in-network for this network. True indicates in-network status.']
  language_services_available varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages in which this provider offers services to members of this network, supporting cultural and linguistic access requirements.']
  network_adequacy_category varchar [note: 'Classification of this provider\'s role in meeting network adequacy requirements. Essential indicates critical for minimum adequacy standards.']
  network_participation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the provider\'s participation relationship with this network. PAR indicates participating provider with contracted rates; non-PAR indicates non-participating.']
  npi varchar [note: 'The 10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned by CMS to uniquely identify this provider in the network.']
  panel_capacity varchar [note: 'The maximum number of members this provider can serve within this network. Used for network adequacy calculations.']
  panel_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the provider\'s patient panel for this network. Open indicates capacity for new members; closed indicates no capacity.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the provider\'s participation in this network. Active indicates the provider is currently in-network and accepting members.']
  pcp_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider serves as a Primary Care Provider within this network. True indicates PCP designation.']
  quality_tier_designation varchar [note: 'Quality-based tier assignment for this provider within the network, based on HEDIS or Star Ratings performance metrics.']
  record_active_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this network-provider association record is currently active in the system. False indicates a logically deleted or superseded record.']
  recredentialing_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the provider must complete recredentialing to maintain participation in this network. Typically every 3 years per NCQA standards.']
  risk_sharing_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider participates in risk-sharing financial arrangements within this network. True indicates risk-sharing participation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system from which this network-provider association record originated (e.g., Cactus, ProviderSource, Facets).']
  specialist_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider is designated as a specialist within this network. True indicates specialist designation.']
  telehealth_available_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider offers telehealth services to members of this network. True indicates telehealth availability.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the provider\'s participation in this network ended or will end. Null for ongoing participation.']
  termination_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Indicates which party initiated the termination of the provider\'s network participation. Null for active participation.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for termination of the provider\'s participation in this network. Null for active participation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|contract_expiration|quality_issues|credentialing_failure|network_restructure|provider_request|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tier_assignment varchar [note: 'The benefit tier assigned to this provider within the network, determining member cost-sharing levels. Tier 1 typically represents lowest member cost-sharing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this network-provider association record was most recently updated in the system.']
  vbc_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider participates in value-based care arrangements within this network. True indicates VBC participation.']
  weekend_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this provider offers weekend services to members of this network. True indicates weekend availability.']

  Note: 'Association entity representing the participation of a specific provider (NPI) within a specific provider network, capturing in-network status, effective and termination dates, tier assignment, accepting-new-patients flag, telehealth availability, panel status (open/closed), panel capacity, and credentialing linkage. This is the SSOT for the structural relationship between a provider and a network — distinct from provider identity (provider domain), contract terms (contract domain), and ACO-specific participation (aco_provider). Supports provider directory publication, CMS network adequacy filings, and member eligibility verification.'
}

Table network.plan_association {
  plan_association_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the plan association record. Primary key for this entity.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Links each plan‑network association to its annual budget, enabling budget‑vs‑actual variance analysis for network participation.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan product that is associated with this provider network. Links to the plan and benefit domain\'s plan entity.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic service area where this plan-network association is valid. Defines the counties, ZIP codes, or regions where members can access this network under this plan.']
  primary_plan_cms_filing_id varchar [note: 'The CMS-assigned identifier for the network adequacy filing or Plan Benefit Package (PBP) submission that includes this plan-network association. Required for Medicare Advantage and Marketplace plans.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network being associated with the health plan product. Links to the network domain\'s network configuration entity.']
  aco_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan-network association includes participation in an Accountable Care Organization arrangement. True means ACO providers are included in this network for this plan; false means no ACO participation.']
  adequacy_certification_date date [note: 'The date when this plan-network association was certified as meeting network adequacy requirements by the regulatory authority or internal compliance review.']
  adequacy_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent network adequacy review or assessment for this plan-network association. Used to track compliance monitoring cycles.']
  association_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for this plan-to-network association, used in eligibility verification (270/271 EDI transactions) and claims adjudication routing.']
  association_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for this plan-to-network association, typically combining plan name and network name for operational reference.']
  association_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the network\'s role for this plan. Primary indicates the main network for member access; supplemental provides additional coverage; specialty covers specific service categories; out-of-area supports members outside primary service area; reciprocal represents shared network arrangements; tertiary provides fallback coverage.']
  auto_assignment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can be automatically assigned to providers in this network under this plan. Relevant for Medicaid and some Medicare programs that use auto-assignment for members who do not actively select a provider. True means eligible for auto-assignment; false means member must actively select.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master contract or agreement document that governs this plan-network association. Links to contract management systems for terms, rates, and legal provisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this plan-network association record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  directory_publication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in this network for this plan should be published in member-facing provider directories and online search tools. True means include in directory; false means exclude (e.g., for internal or specialty networks).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this plan-to-network association becomes active and members can begin accessing providers in this network under this plan.']
  lob varchar [note: 'The line of business this plan-network association serves. Commercial covers employer-sponsored and individual plans; Medicare includes Medicare Advantage; Medicaid covers state programs; Marketplace represents ACA exchange plans; dual eligible serves Medicare-Medicaid beneficiaries; CHIP covers Children\'s Health Insurance Program.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'The market segment this plan-network association targets. Individual covers direct-to-consumer; small group typically 2-50 employees; large group 51-999 employees; jumbo group 1000+ employees; government includes federal and state employee plans; student covers university and college plans.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'The number of members currently enrolled in this plan who have access to this network. Used for network adequacy calculations and capacity planning.']
  network_adequacy_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this plan-network association meets regulatory network adequacy standards for provider access, time-and-distance requirements, and specialty coverage. Compliant meets all standards; non-compliant fails requirements; under review is being evaluated; exempt has regulatory waiver; conditional meets standards with restrictions.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'The tier designation of this network within the plan\'s tiered network structure. Tier 1 typically offers lowest cost-sharing; tier 2 moderate; tier 3 highest. Preferred, standard, basic, and premium are alternative tier naming conventions used by some plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|preferred|standard|basic|premium — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this plan-network association. Used for operational notes that do not fit structured fields.']
  out_of_network_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan provides any coverage for out-of-network services when this network is the member\'s assigned network. True means out-of-network benefits exist (typical for PPO); false means no out-of-network coverage (typical for HMO and EPO).']
  pcp_selection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members enrolled in this plan with this network must select a primary care provider. Typically true for HMO and POS plans; false for PPO and EPO plans.']
  plan_association_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan-network association. Active indicates operational; inactive means temporarily disabled; pending awaits regulatory approval or contract execution; suspended indicates temporary hold; terminated means permanently ended.']
  prior_authorization_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether services rendered by providers in this network under this plan require prior authorization. True means PA is required; false means no PA requirement. This is a network-level default that may be overridden by specific service or provider rules.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the priority order of this network when multiple networks are associated with the same plan. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used in claims adjudication routing when a member has access to multiple networks.']
  provider_count varchar [note: 'The number of unique providers (individual practitioners and facilities) participating in this network for this plan. Used for network adequacy reporting and member communications.']
  referral_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must obtain a referral from their primary care provider to access specialists in this network under this plan. Typically true for HMO plans; false for PPO and EPO plans.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'The date when the regulatory authority (CMS or state DOI) approved this plan-network association for use. Required before the association can become effective.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record that originated this plan-network association data. Typically the Core Administration Platform (Facets, QNXT) or Provider Management System (Cactus, ProviderSource).']
  star_rating_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether provider performance in this network impacts the plan\'s CMS Star Ratings. Applicable primarily to Medicare Advantage plans. True means network performance affects Star metrics; false means no Star impact.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date when this plan-to-network association ends. Null indicates an open-ended association. After this date, members can no longer access providers in this network under this plan for new services.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or system account that last modified this plan-network association record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this plan-network association record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  vbc_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan-network association operates under a value-based care payment model. True means providers in this network are reimbursed based on quality and outcomes; false means traditional fee-for-service.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or system account that created this plan-network association record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a provider network to one or more health plan products, capturing the effective period, LOB, market segment (individual, small group, large group, Medicare, Medicaid), and whether the network is the primary or supplemental network for that plan. Enables plan-to-network resolution for eligibility verification (270/271 EDI) and claims adjudication routing. Owned by the network domain as the structural bridge between network configurations and plan offerings.'
}

Table network.network_service_area {
  network_service_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the geographic service area. Primary key.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network to which this service area belongs.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory filing (e.g., SERFF filing, CMS HPMS submission) that established or modified this service area.']
  cms_service_area_code varchar [note: 'CMS-assigned identifier for the service area, used in Medicare Advantage and Marketplace (QHP) filings.']
  commercial_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service area is approved for commercial (employer-sponsored and individual) plan enrollment.']
  county_fips_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit Federal Information Processing Standards code uniquely identifying the county. Null for non-county-based service areas.']
  county_name varchar [note: 'Name of the county included in this service area. Null for non-county-based service areas.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service area record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this service area configuration becomes active and available for member enrollment.']
  geographic_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the service area boundaries, used for custom or complex geographic definitions not captured by standard codes.']
  medicaid_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service area is approved for Medicaid managed care enrollment.']
  medicare_advantage_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service area is approved for Medicare Advantage plan enrollment.']
  member_residency_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must reside within this service area to be eligible for enrollment in the associated network.']
  msa_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit code identifying the Metropolitan Statistical Area. Null for non-MSA-based service areas.']
  msa_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Metropolitan Statistical Area (e.g., \'San Francisco-Oakland-Hayward, CA\'). Null for non-MSA-based service areas.']
  network_adequacy_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the network adequacy filing was approved by CMS or the state Department of Insurance for this service area.']
  network_adequacy_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent network adequacy filing was submitted to CMS or the state Department of Insurance for this service area.']
  network_adequacy_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the service area meets CMS and state network adequacy standards for provider access: compliant, non-compliant, under review, waiver granted, or not applicable.']
  network_service_area_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service area: active (available for enrollment), inactive (not available), pending approval (awaiting regulatory approval), suspended (temporarily unavailable), or terminated (permanently closed).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, exceptions, or special instructions related to this service area configuration.']
  out_of_area_coverage_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan allows coverage for services received outside this service area (e.g., for travel or temporary relocation).']
  population_count varchar [note: 'Estimated total population residing in this service area, used for network adequacy calculations and market sizing.']
  qhp_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service area is approved for Qualified Health Plan (Marketplace/Exchange) enrollment under the Affordable Care Act.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by CMS or the state Department of Insurance when approving this service area configuration.']
  service_area_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the service area, used in contracts, filings, and member communications.']
  service_area_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service area (e.g., \'Northern California Region\', \'Metro Atlanta Service Area\').']
  service_area_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the service area is defined geographically: county-based, ZIP code-based, Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA)-based, state-based, custom boundary, or multi-state.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state abbreviation where the service area is located (e.g., \'CA\', \'TX\', \'NY\'). For multi-state service areas, this represents the primary state.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this service area configuration is no longer active. Null for currently active service areas.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this service area record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service area record was last modified.']
  urban_rural_designation varchar [note: 'Classification of the service area as urban, suburban, rural, frontier (very low population density), or mixed.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit ZIP code included in this service area. Null for non-ZIP-based service areas.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this service area record.']

  Note: 'Defines the geographic service area for a provider network — the counties, ZIP codes, or metropolitan statistical areas (MSAs) where the network is available and where members must reside to enroll. Captures state, county FIPS code, ZIP code, service area type (county-based, ZIP-based), CMS service area ID, effective dates, and whether the area is approved for QHP/Medicare Advantage enrollment. Critical for CMS network adequacy filings and state DOI market conduct compliance.'
}

Table network.adequacy_standard {
  adequacy_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network adequacy standard record. Primary key.']
  adequacy_standard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this adequacy standard: active (currently enforced), inactive (not currently enforced), proposed (pending regulatory approval), superseded (replaced by newer standard), or under-review (being evaluated for changes).']
  after_hours_availability_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether after-hours availability (evenings, weekends, holidays) is required for this specialty and geography. True if after-hours access is mandated, false otherwise.']
  after_hours_definition varchar [note: 'Definition of what constitutes after-hours for this standard (e.g., weekdays after 5pm, weekends, 24/7 access). Null if after-hours availability is not required.']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Type of appointment this wait-time standard applies to: routine (non-urgent care), urgent (same-day/next-day need), preventive (wellness visits), behavioral-health (mental health/substance abuse), specialist (referral appointments), or emergency. Null if standard is not appointment-access based.']
  compliance_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often compliance with this standard must be assessed and reported to regulators: annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly, or on-demand (event-triggered).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this adequacy standard record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this adequacy standard becomes effective and must be met. Standards change over time as regulations evolve.']
  exception_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions under which an exception or waiver to this standard may be granted (e.g., provider shortage area designation, rural exception, temporary hardship). Null if no exceptions allowed.']
  geographic_classification varchar [note: 'Geographic area classification for which this standard applies: urban (metropolitan areas), suburban (surrounding metro areas), rural (non-metropolitan), or frontier (very low population density areas). Standards vary by geography.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this adequacy standard record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Health insurance line of business this standard applies to: medicare-advantage (MA plans), medicaid (state programs), commercial (employer-sponsored), marketplace (ACA exchange plans), dual-eligible (Medicare-Medicaid), or all (applies to all LOBs).']
  maximum_distance_miles decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable distance in miles from member residence to nearest in-network provider of this specialty in this geographic area. Null if standard is not distance-based.']
  maximum_travel_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable travel time in minutes from member residence to nearest in-network provider of this specialty in this geographic area. Null if standard is not time-based.']
  maximum_wait_time_days varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable wait time in days from appointment request to appointment date for this appointment type and specialty. Null if standard is not appointment-access based.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of how compliance with this standard is measured (e.g., GeoAccess analysis, member survey, claims-based appointment tracking, provider attestation). Defines the assessment approach.']
  minimum_provider_count varchar [note: 'Absolute minimum number of providers of this specialty required in the network for this geography, regardless of member count. Null if not applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or special instructions related to this adequacy standard. May include implementation guidance, historical context, or regulatory interpretation.']
  penalty_for_non_compliance varchar [note: 'Description of regulatory penalties or consequences for failing to meet this standard (e.g., corrective action plan, fines, enrollment suspension, Star rating impact). Null if no specific penalty defined.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of health plan this standard applies to: HMO (Health Maintenance Organization), PPO (Preferred Provider Organization), EPO (Exclusive Provider Organization), POS (Point of Service), HDHP (High Deductible Health Plan), or all (applies to all plan types). Standards may vary by plan structure.']
  provider_to_member_ratio varchar [note: 'Required ratio of providers to members for this specialty and geography, expressed as ratio string (e.g., 1:2000 for PCPs, 1:5000 for specialists). Null if standard is not ratio-based.']
  regulatory_source varchar [note: 'Governing body or regulatory authority that mandates this standard: CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services), state-DOI (State Department of Insurance), NCQA (National Committee for Quality Assurance), URAC, ACA (Affordable Care Act), or internal (company-defined standard exceeding regulatory minimums).']
  service_area_type varchar [note: 'Geographic unit for which this standard is defined: county (county-level), zip (ZIP code level), state (state-wide), region (multi-state region), or national (nationwide standard).']
  specialty_category varchar [note: 'Provider specialty or facility type this standard applies to (e.g., PCP, cardiology, behavioral-health, hospital, SNF, DME). May be broad category or specific specialty code.']
  standard_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the adequacy standard type: time-distance (geographic access), provider-ratio (provider-to-member ratios), appointment-access (wait time standards), after-hours (availability requirements), telehealth-equivalency (virtual care standards), or network-composition (network mix requirements).']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the adequacy standard (e.g., PCP_URBAN_TIME, BH_RURAL_RATIO). Used for external reference and reporting.']
  standard_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the adequacy standard (e.g., Primary Care Physician Urban Time-Distance Standard).']
  standard_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this standard, used to track changes over time (e.g., 2024.1, v3.0). Supports historical analysis and regulatory audit trails.']
  telehealth_equivalency_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether telehealth/virtual care providers can be counted toward meeting this adequacy standard. True if telehealth equivalency is permitted, false if only in-person providers count.']
  telehealth_percentage_cap decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage of the adequacy requirement that can be met through telehealth providers (e.g., 25.00 means up to 25% of required providers can be telehealth-only). Null if no cap or telehealth not allowed.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this adequacy standard is no longer in effect, either due to regulatory change or supersession by a new standard. Null if currently active with no planned end date.']
  threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of members or geographic area that must meet this standard (e.g., 90.00 means 90% of members must have access within the specified time/distance). Used for compliance measurement.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining all network compliance standards by specialty, facility type, and geographic area — encompassing time-distance standards (e.g., 30 miles/30 minutes for PCPs), provider-to-member ratios, appointment access wait-time standards (routine, urgent, preventive, behavioral health), after-hours availability requirements, and telehealth equivalency rules. Captures regulatory source (CMS, state DOI, NCQA, URAC), standard category (time-distance, provider-ratio, appointment-access, after-hours, telehealth-equivalency), specialty category, geographic classification (urban, suburban, rural, frontier), maximum wait time in days, threshold values, and after-hours availability flag. This is the single source of truth for all network adequacy and access compliance standards — both geographic access (time-distance, ratios) and appointment access (wait times, after-hours). Used to evaluate compliance in adequacy assessments, access surveys, and CMS/state regulatory filings.'
}

Table network.adequacy_assessment {
  adequacy_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network adequacy assessment record. Primary key.']
  adequacy_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.adequacy_standard. Business justification: Each adequacy assessment applies a specific adequacy standard; linking via adequacy_standard_id enables reference to the standard definition.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Adequacy assessments are performed to meet a specific regulatory obligation for network adequacy compliance.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Adequacy assessments are performed per contract to verify network adequacy obligations; link enables contract‑level performance dashboards.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Captures costs of network adequacy assessments in a cost center to support regulatory cost reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan associated with this network adequacy assessment.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the geographic service area evaluated in this assessment.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network being assessed for adequacy compliance.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Network adequacy assessments require risk pool data to evaluate member health risk distribution for compliance and planning.']
  accepting_new_patients_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in the assessed specialty or facility type are accepting new patients. True if accepting, False otherwise.']
  appointment_type_requested varchar [note: 'Type of appointment requested during mystery shopper or survey assessment: routine care, urgent care, preventive visit, follow-up visit, new patient intake, or specialist referral.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the network adequacy assessment was conducted or completed.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the adequacy assessment, used for external reference and regulatory submissions.']
  assessment_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the evaluation period covered by this assessment.']
  assessment_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the evaluation period covered by this assessment.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of network compliance evaluation performed: adequacy (provider count and ratio), access-survey (member experience survey), mystery-shopper (secret shopper call study), gap-analysis (deficiency identification), time-distance (geographic access compliance), or appointment-availability (wait time measurement).']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for conducting the network adequacy assessment.']
  assessor_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or department that conducted the assessment (e.g., internal network management team, third-party consultant, regulatory auditor).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context related to the network adequacy assessment, including qualitative findings or special circumstances.']
  compliance_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall compliance result of the network adequacy assessment: compliant (meets all standards), non-compliant (fails standards), partially-compliant (meets some but not all standards), under-review (pending final determination), or remediation-required (corrective action needed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adequacy assessment record was first created in the system.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Type of healthcare facility evaluated in this assessment (e.g., hospital, urgent care, skilled nursing facility (SNF), ambulatory surgery center, imaging center).']
  gap_identified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether network gaps or deficiencies were identified during the assessment. True if gaps exist, False otherwise.']
  gap_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of identified network gaps: critical (immediate member access risk), high (significant access limitation), moderate (minor access concern), or low (minimal impact).']
  member_to_provider_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated ratio of enrolled members to available providers in the assessed specialty or facility type, expressed as members per provider.']
  provider_count_available varchar [note: 'Number of in-network providers or facilities available in the assessed specialty or facility type within the service area.']
  provider_count_required varchar [note: 'Minimum number of providers or facilities required by regulatory standards to meet network adequacy thresholds for the assessed specialty or facility type.']
  regulatory_submission_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory body or framework for which this assessment is being conducted: CMS Medicare, CMS Marketplace, State Department of Insurance (DOI), NCQA accreditation, internal monitoring, or ACA Qualified Health Plan (QHP) certification.']
  remediation_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions taken to address identified network gaps, such as provider recruitment, contract expansion, telehealth deployment, or member outreach.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation actions must be completed to resolve identified network gaps and achieve compliance.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation efforts: not-started (action plan pending), in-progress (corrective actions underway), completed (actions finished), verified (compliance confirmed), or overdue (past due date).']
  specialty_type varchar [note: 'Medical specialty or provider type being evaluated in this assessment (e.g., primary care, cardiology, orthopedics, behavioral health, pediatrics).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the network adequacy assessment was submitted to the regulatory body (CMS, State DOI, NCQA).']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory submission: draft (not yet submitted), submitted (under review), accepted (approved by regulator), rejected (not approved), or resubmission-required (corrections needed).']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the assessment: member survey (CAHPS or custom), secret shopper call (mystery shopper study), provider survey (direct provider inquiry), administrative data (claims and enrollment analysis), or geospatial analysis (GIS mapping).']
  telehealth_offered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether telehealth or virtual care options were offered as an alternative during the assessment. True if telehealth was available, False otherwise.']
  time_distance_compliance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of members in the service area who have access to the assessed provider specialty or facility type within the required time and distance standards.']
  time_distance_standard_miles decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum distance in miles that members should travel to access the assessed provider specialty or facility type, as defined by regulatory standards.']
  time_distance_standard_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum travel time in minutes that members should travel to access the assessed provider specialty or facility type, as defined by regulatory standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adequacy assessment record was last modified in the system.']
  wait_time_days_routine varchar [note: 'Average number of days members wait for a routine appointment with the assessed provider specialty, measured through survey or mystery shopper methodology.']
  wait_time_days_urgent varchar [note: 'Average number of days members wait for an urgent appointment with the assessed provider specialty, measured through survey or mystery shopper methodology.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a network compliance evaluation — including network adequacy assessments, access surveys, mystery shopper studies, and gap identification. Captures assessment type (adequacy, access-survey, mystery-shopper), assessment date, regulatory submission type (CMS, state DOI), specialty/facility type evaluated, provider counts, member-to-provider ratios, time-distance compliance results, wait-time measurements, survey method (member survey, secret shopper call), appointment type requested, telehealth offered flag, and overall compliance outcome. Includes detail-level gap findings as child records: specific specialty or facility type shortfalls within a geographic area, gap severity, number of providers available vs. required, remediation actions taken, and resolution status. Supports CMS network adequacy filings, NCQA HEDIS access measures (Getting Care Quickly), CAHPS survey supplementation, and targeted network development. This is the SSOT for all network compliance evaluations and their associated gap findings.'
}

Table network.adequacy_gap {
  adequacy_gap_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network adequacy gap record. Primary key.']
  adequacy_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent network adequacy assessment that identified this gap.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Identified adequacy gaps are addressed within the context of the specific provider contract; required for remediation tracking.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan affected by this network adequacy gap.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic service area where this adequacy gap exists.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network in which this adequacy gap exists.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Actual date on which this adequacy gap was resolved and the network met the applicable adequacy standard. Null if gap remains unresolved.']
  affected_member_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of plan members residing in the affected geographic area who are impacted by this adequacy gap.']
  available_provider_count varchar [note: 'Number of providers or facilities of the specified type currently available in the network in this geographic area. Null if standard is not count-based.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this adequacy gap record was first created in the system.']
  exception_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory exception or access plan waiver was approved by CMS or the state DOI. Null if no exception has been granted.']
  exception_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the granted regulatory exception or access plan waiver expires and the network must meet the full adequacy standard. Null if no exception has been granted or if exception is permanent.']
  exception_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory exception or access plan waiver has been granted by CMS or the state DOI for this adequacy gap.']
  exception_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and supporting documentation for the regulatory exception or access plan waiver request (e.g., rural area with limited provider availability, specialty shortage area designation, alternative access arrangements).']
