  minimum_savings_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage savings threshold that must be achieved before shared savings are paid. MSR = Minimum Savings Rate.']
  performance_period_end_date date [note: 'The date when the performance measurement period for this VBC program ends.']
  performance_period_start_date date [note: 'The date when the performance measurement period for this VBC program begins.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of value-based arrangement that this program represents. ACO = Accountable Care Organization.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the VBC program used across systems and external reporting.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the VBC program objectives, structure, and key features.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the value-based care program.']
  program_owner varchar [note: 'The internal business unit or department responsible for managing and administering this VBC program.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the VBC program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the VBC program model. MSSP = Medicare Shared Savings Program, REACH = Realizing Equity Access and Community Health, NextGen = Next Generation ACO, P4P = Pay for Performance.']
  program_url varchar [note: 'Web address for the program information page, provider portal, or documentation site.']
  program_year varchar [note: 'The calendar or performance year for which this VBC program design is configured.']
  quality_measure_set varchar [note: 'The standardized set of quality measures used to evaluate performance (e.g., HEDIS, STAR, CMS Quality Measures). HEDIS = Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set.']
  quality_performance_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum quality score threshold required to qualify for shared savings or incentive payments, expressed as a percentage.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory filing or approval associated with this VBC program.']
  risk_model varchar [note: 'The financial risk arrangement structure. Upside Only = shared savings only, Two-Sided = shared savings and losses, Downside Only = shared losses only.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often financial reconciliation and shared savings/loss settlements are calculated and paid.']
  shared_loss_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of losses above benchmark that is shared with participating providers, expressed as a percentage. Null for upside-only models.']
  shared_savings_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of savings below benchmark that is shared with participating providers, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  sponsoring_entity varchar [note: 'The organization or regulatory body that sponsors and governs this VBC program. CMS = Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date when this VBC program design is terminated or discontinued. Null for active programs.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this record.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a Value-Based Care (VBC) program design — the template or program structure under which ACO arrangements, pay-for-performance contracts, and shared savings models are organized. Captures program name, program type (MSSP, REACH, commercial P4P, episode-based payment, capitation), sponsoring entity (CMS, state Medicaid, commercial), program year, quality measure set, risk model (upside-only, two-sided), and benchmark methodology. Distinct from aco_arrangement (which is a specific participation instance) — vbc_program is the program definition.'
}

Table network.network_recruitment {
  network_recruitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the provider recruitment activity record.']
  adequacy_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this recruitment activity to a specific network adequacy gap assessment finding that triggered the recruitment need.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider contract that resulted from successful recruitment, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Records recruitment expenses for network provider outreach against a cost center for budgeting and expense tracking.']
  primary_network_cms_filing_id varchar [note: 'The CMS filing identifier associated with the network adequacy gap that this recruitment addresses, if applicable.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network for which recruitment is being conducted.']
  recruiter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the recruiter assigned to manage this recruitment activity.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Provider recruitment decisions use risk pool characteristics to prioritize filling gaps for high‑risk member segments, supporting network adequacy.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Provider Recruitment Vendor Management links recruitment activities to external recruiting vendors for reporting and cost allocation.']
  adequacy_gap_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of network adequacy gap this recruitment is intended to address, if applicable.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'The effective date of the provider contract resulting from this recruitment, if successfully contracted.']
  contracting_referral_date date [note: 'The date when the recruitment activity was referred to the contracting team for formal contract negotiation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this recruitment record was first created in the system.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'The final outcome or current status of the recruitment activity.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'The date when the recruitment activity reached its final disposition.']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation or reason code for the final disposition outcome (e.g., rate disagreement, geographic mismatch, credentialing issues).']
  estimated_annual_claims_volume decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected annual claims volume in dollars expected from this provider if successfully recruited.']
  estimated_member_impact varchar [note: 'Estimated number of members who would benefit from adding this provider to the network.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for follow-up contact with the provider.']
  lob varchar [note: 'The line of business for which this provider recruitment is being conducted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, conversation details, or special considerations related to the recruitment activity.']
  outreach_date date [note: 'The date when initial outreach was made to the target provider.']
  outreach_method varchar [note: 'The communication channel or method used for initial provider outreach.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency or priority assigned to this recruitment activity based on business need and regulatory risk.']
  provider_response varchar [note: 'The initial response received from the target provider following outreach.']
  reason varchar [note: 'The primary business reason driving this recruitment activity.']
  recruiter_assigned varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the network development recruiter assigned to this recruitment activity.']
  recruitment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the recruitment activity, typically formatted as REC-YYYYMMDD or similar convention.']
  recruitment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the recruitment activity in the pipeline.']
  regulatory_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful recruitment of this provider requires a regulatory filing or network adequacy update with state or federal authorities.']
  response_date date [note: 'The date when the provider responded to the recruitment outreach.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this recruitment data originated (e.g., Provider Management System, CRM).']
  target_county_fips varchar [note: 'The 5-digit FIPS code identifying the target county for recruitment.']
  target_geographic_area varchar [note: 'The geographic area or service area where the provider is needed, typically defined by county, ZIP code, or Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA).']
  target_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the provider or provider organization being recruited.']
  target_provider_npi varchar [note: 'The 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the specific provider being recruited, if known at outreach time.']
  target_provider_tin varchar [note: 'The 9-digit Tax Identification Number (TIN) of the provider organization or practice being recruited.']
  target_specialty varchar [note: 'The medical specialty or provider type being recruited to address network adequacy gaps (e.g., Cardiology, Primary Care, Pediatrics).']
  target_zip_code varchar [note: 'The 5-digit ZIP code identifying the target geographic area for recruitment.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last updated this recruitment record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this recruitment record was last modified.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this recruitment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking provider recruitment activities to address network adequacy gaps or strategic expansion. Captures target provider NPI/TIN, target specialty, target geographic area, recruitment reason (adequacy gap closure, strategic expansion, member request, competitor response), outreach date, outreach method, recruiter assigned, provider response, contracting referral date, and disposition (contracted, declined, no response). Links to adequacy assessment gap findings for remediation tracking. Supports network development pipeline management and regulatory gap closure evidence.'
}

Table network.termination {
  termination_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the provider network termination record. Primary key for the termination entity.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Termination Appeal Process allows providers or members to appeal a termination; the termination record must reference the related appeal case.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Termination of network participation is tied to the underlying provider contract; needed for settlement and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocates termination settlement and related financial impacts to a cost center for accurate financial statements.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Party that initiated the termination: provider (voluntary), health plan (involuntary), mutual (both parties agreed), or regulatory authority (mandated by state DOI or CMS).']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider being terminated from the network.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network from which the provider is being terminated.']
  active_treatment_count varchar [note: 'Number of members currently in active treatment episodes (e.g., ongoing therapy, pregnancy, post-surgical care) with the terminated provider. These members may be eligible for extended continuity-of-care benefits.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the provider appeal: upheld (termination stands), overturned (termination reversed), modified (terms changed), withdrawn (provider withdrew appeal), or pending (not yet resolved).']
  appeal_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which any provider appeal or grievance was resolved. Null if no appeal was filed or if appeal is still pending.']
  appeals_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider filed an appeal or grievance contesting the termination. True = appeal filed; False = no appeal filed.']
  claims_runout_end_date date [note: 'Final date through which claims for pre-termination services will be accepted at in-network rates. Calculated as termination effective date plus claims runout period days.']
  claims_runout_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after termination effective date during which claims for services rendered prior to termination will be accepted and processed at in-network rates. Typically 90-180 days per contract terms.']
  continuity_of_care_end_date date [note: 'Date through which existing patients may continue to receive in-network benefits from the terminated provider under continuity-of-care provisions. Required under No Surprises Act and state regulations.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Final date of the underlying provider contract. May differ from termination effective date if contract allows for early termination or extension provisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the termination record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for compliance and operational tracking.']
  credentialing_related_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the termination was due to credentialing issues such as license revocation, malpractice history, sanctions, or failure to meet credentialing standards. True = credentialing-related; False = other reason.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the provider termination becomes effective and the provider is no longer considered in-network for new services. Critical for claims adjudication and member communication.']
  financial_settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which final financial settlement was completed between the health plan and terminated provider.']
  financial_settlement_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial settlement or reconciliation is required between the health plan and provider upon termination. True = settlement required; False = no settlement required. Common for capitated or risk-sharing arrangements.']
  for_cause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the termination was for-cause due to contract breach, quality issues, fraud, or other serious violations. True = for-cause termination; False = without-cause termination.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last updated the termination record. Part of audit trail.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the termination record. Part of audit trail for compliance and operational tracking.']
  member_impact_count varchar [note: 'Number of active members assigned to or receiving care from the terminated provider at the time of termination. Used to assess continuity-of-care obligations and member notification requirements.']
  member_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which affected members were notified of the provider termination. Required for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  member_notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify affected members of the termination: mail (postal letter), email, member portal, phone call, or multiple channels.']
  member_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether member notification is required by regulation or contract. True = notification required; False = no notification required. CMS and state DOI regulations mandate notification for certain termination types.']
  network_adequacy_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the termination impacts network adequacy standards for time-and-distance or provider-to-member ratios. True = adequacy impact; False = no adequacy impact. Triggers network adequacy re-assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, internal notes, or special handling instructions related to the termination. May include details of negotiations, legal considerations, or operational impacts.']
  notice_date date [note: 'Date on which formal written notice of termination was provided to the provider or plan, as required by contract terms and regulatory requirements.']
  npi varchar [note: 'Ten-digit unique identifier assigned by CMS to healthcare providers. Denormalized for operational efficiency and regulatory reporting.']
  provider_directory_removal_date date [note: 'Date on which the provider was removed from online and printed provider directories. CMS requires directory updates within specified timeframes.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific reason for termination. Examples include quality concerns, credentialing failure, contract breach, provider relocation, retirement, network restructuring, or regulatory action.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the termination reason, providing context beyond the reason code. May include specific incidents, performance issues, or business rationale.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the termination was reported to the applicable regulatory authority (state DOI, CMS, NCQA). Required for compliance audit trail.']
  regulatory_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or confirmation code from the regulatory authority acknowledging receipt of the termination report.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the termination must be reported to regulatory authorities such as state DOI or CMS. True = reporting required; False = no reporting required. For-cause and credentialing-related terminations typically require reporting.']
  replacement_provider_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health plan must recruit a replacement provider to maintain network adequacy. True = replacement required; False = no replacement required.']
  termination_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique reference number for the termination event, used for tracking and correspondence.']
  termination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the termination record: pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized but not yet effective), effective (termination is active), completed (all post-termination obligations fulfilled), cancelled (termination rescinded).']
  termination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the termination event indicating whether it was voluntary (provider-initiated), involuntary (plan-initiated), for-cause (breach or quality issue), contract non-renewal, credentialing revocation, or mutual agreement.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created the termination record. Part of audit trail.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal termination of a provider from a network — including voluntary and involuntary terminations. Records termination type (voluntary, for-cause, contract non-renewal, credentialing revocation), effective date, notice date, termination reason code, member impact count, continuity-of-care obligation period, member notification status, and regulatory reporting status. Supports CMS and state DOI termination notification requirements and continuity-of-care compliance under the No Surprises Act.'
}

Table network.par_agreement {
  par_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the provider participation agreement.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: PAR agreements are executed under a specific provider contract; linking enables contract‑level reporting and compliance tracking.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan associated with the agreement, if plan‑specific.']
  parent_par_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on par_agreement (parent_par_agreement_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider participating in the network.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network to which the agreement applies.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the agreement by the health plan.']
  agreement_scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the services, specialties, and geographic areas covered.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the agreement as individual provider, group practice, or other entity.']
  agreement_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the agreement document (e.g., v1, v2).']
  amendment_effective_date date [note: 'Date the amendment becomes effective.']
  amendment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents an amendment to a prior agreement.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the amendment (e.g., A1, A2).']
  amendment_termination_date date [note: 'Date the amendment expires, if different from the base agreement.']
  compliance_status_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the broader provider contract that governs reimbursement terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the agreement becomes binding and in‑force.']
  electronic_signature_flag boolean [note: 'True if the agreement was signed electronically.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the agreement.']
  paper_signature_flag boolean [note: 'True if the agreement was signed on paper.']
  par_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement.']
  provider_credentialing_date date [note: 'Date the provider completed credentialing for the network.']
  provider_credentialing_status varchar [note: 'Credentialing state of the provider for network participation.']
  provider_directory_last_verified_date date [note: 'Date the provider\'s directory information was last verified.']
  provider_directory_listing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is listed in the public network directory.']
  provider_network_status varchar [note: 'Current network participation status of the provider.']
  provider_participation_role varchar [note: 'Role of the provider within the network (e.g., primary care, specialist).']
  provider_recredentialing_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for provider re‑credentialing.']
  provider_tier_assignment varchar [note: 'Tier code assigned to the provider within the network (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2).']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date the agreement received regulatory approval, if required.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for any required regulatory filing (e.g., CMS network adequacy filing).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Planned date for renewal of the agreement, if applicable.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement is set for automatic renewal.']
  signature_date date [note: 'Date the agreement was signed by the provider.']
  signed_by_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual who signed the agreement on behalf of the provider.']
  signed_by_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the signing individual.']
  signed_by_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the signing individual.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the agreement ends, either by expiration or early termination.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the agreement was terminated.']
  termination_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the termination reason.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Provider participation agreement record — the formal agreement a provider signs to participate in a network. Captures agreement type (individual, group), signature date, effective date, termination provisions, and agreement version. Distinct from the broader provider_contract which covers reimbursement terms.'
}

Table employer.group {
  group_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the employer group record.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broker or agency that sourced the group.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Group Risk Pool Assignment report requires each employer group to be linked to its risk pool for rating, reinsurance and regulatory compliance.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the employer\'s headquarters.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  average_claim_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average cost per claim historically incurred by the group.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the employer\'s address.']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate employer contributions to premiums.']
  country varchar [note: 'Country of the employer\'s headquarters (ISO‑3 code).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employer group record was first created in the system.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA under which the employer operates, if different from legal name.']
  domicile_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the employer is legally domiciled.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the employer group contract becomes binding.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for employer communications.']
  enrollment_count_ec varchar [note: 'Number of employees plus dependent children enrolled.']
  enrollment_count_ef varchar [note: 'Number of employees enrolled with full family coverage (employee, spouse, and children).']
  enrollment_count_eo varchar [note: 'Number of employees enrolled in employee‑only coverage.']
  enrollment_count_es varchar [note: 'Number of employees plus spouses enrolled.']
  erisa_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the group is subject to ERISA regulations.']
  funding_arrangement varchar [note: 'How the group pays for coverage: fully insured, ASO, or self‑funded.']
  gfc_code varchar [note: 'Link to the financial control entity responsible for the group\'s accounting.']
  group_number varchar [note: 'Internal identifier assigned to the employer group by the insurer.']
  group_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the employer group relationship.']
  headcount_current varchar [note: 'Current total number of employees covered under the group plan.']
  headcount_last_month varchar [note: 'Employee headcount as of the end of the previous month.']
  headcount_last_year varchar [note: 'Employee headcount as of the same month in the prior calendar year.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status field last changed value.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the employer organization as registered.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Primary product line(s) offered to the employer group.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Regulatory market segment classification for the group.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six‑digit code representing the employer\'s industry sector.']
  participation_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum employee participation level required for the group plan.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number for the employer.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code for the employer\'s address.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for contract renewal negotiations.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factor used in risk‑adjusted pricing for the group.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the employer\'s primary industry.']
  size_tier varchar [note: 'Categorization of the employer based on employee headcount.']
  state varchar [note: 'State component of the employer\'s address.']
  tax_id_ein varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number for the employer, used for reporting and compliance.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employer group contract is terminated or expires.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employer group record.']

  Note: 'Master record for employer group accounts — the B2B commercial customers who sponsor health coverage for their employees. Captures group demographics, legal entity name, tax ID (EIN), SIC/NAICS industry classification, group size tier, historical headcount and enrollment counts by coverage tier (EO/ES/EC/EF) tracked at monthly intervals for ACA small/large group market classification, line of business (LOB), funding arrangement (fully-insured vs ASO), ERISA status, domicile state, effective and termination dates, group financial control (GFC) identifiers, and complete lifecycle status transitions (prospect, active, suspended, terminated, reinstated) with status history. Single source of truth for employer group identity, size classification, headcount history, and status history.'
}

Table employer.group_location {
  group_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each group location record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Group locations belong to an employer group; linking enables address normalization and removes duplicate address fields from employer_group.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.service_area. Business justification: Needed for Network Adequacy Compliance audit: links employer location to the network service area covering that geography.']
  on_site_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Links employer on‑site clinic locations to provider facilities, enabling employee access reporting and utilization analytics.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address, if applicable.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the purpose of the address (headquarters, billing, satellite, mailing).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the location.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code (e.g., USA).']
  county_fips varchar [note: 'Federal Information Processing Standard code for the county.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the location ceases to be effective; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the location becomes effective for billing and regulatory purposes.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Indicates the precision of the latitude/longitude coordinates.']
  group_location_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the location record.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary address for the employer group.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the location in decimal degrees.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the location used in billing and regulatory filings.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the location (e.g., Headquarters, West Coast Office).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the location in decimal degrees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks about the location.']
  rating_area varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area used for premium calculations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system (e.g., Facets, QNXT) that supplied the record.']
  state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state abbreviation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit postal code.']
  zip_plus4 varchar [note: 'Extended ZIP+4 postal code.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing addresses associated with an employer group, including headquarters, billing address, and satellite office locations. Tracks address type, street, city, state, ZIP+4, county FIPS code, and effective date range. Supports geographic rating, state regulatory filings, and premium billing address routing.'
}

Table employer.group_contact {
  group_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the group contact record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group to which this contact belongs.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the contact\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the contact\'s mailing address, if applicable.']
  authorization_level varchar [note: 'Level of authority the contact has for enrollment and billing actions.']
  can_bill boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact is authorized to initiate billing or premium payment actions.']
  can_enroll boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact is authorized to submit enrollment transactions.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the contact\'s mailing address.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact\'s functional role for the employer group.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the contact\'s address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit where the contact works.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contact\'s authorization expires or is terminated; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contact\'s authorization becomes effective.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for electronic communications with the contact.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the contact person.']
  group_contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the designated primary point of contact for the group.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the contact.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the contact.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the contact\'s mailing address.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Contact\'s preferred method for receiving communications.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the contact data (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  source_system_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the contact as defined in the source system.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the contact\'s mailing address.']
  title varchar [note: 'Professional title or position of the contact within the employer organization.']

  Note: 'Authorized contacts associated with an employer group, including HR administrators, benefits coordinators, payroll contacts, and executive sponsors. Captures contact role type, name, title, phone, email, preferred communication channel, and authorization level for enrollment and billing transactions.'
}

Table employer.group_division {
  group_division_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the group division record.']
  parent_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent employer group to which this division belongs.']
  primary_group_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Preferred Provider Organization (PPO) plan associated with this division, if applicable.']
  quaternary_group_pos_plan_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Point of Service (POS) plan associated with this division, if applicable.']
  tertiary_group_epo_plan_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Exclusive Provider Organization (EPO) plan associated with this division, if applicable.']
  billing_entity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the division is billed as a separate entity from the parent group.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Funding arrangement classification for the division.']
  contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount contributed by employee per pay period for this division (if applicable).']
  contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of premium contributed by employee for this division (if applicable).']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate employee contributions for this division.']
  covered_member_count varchar [note: 'Number of members (including dependents) covered under this division\'s benefits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the division record was first created in the system.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned to the division for internal reference and billing.']
  division_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the division or subsidiary within the employer group.']
  division_type varchar [note: 'Category of the division indicating its organizational role.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s benefit configuration ends or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s benefit configuration becomes effective.']
  eligibility_rule_set varchar [note: 'Reference to the eligibility rule set applied to members in this division.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees assigned to this division.']
  flex_spending_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for the division\'s Flexible Spending Account.']
  fsa_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for the division\'s Flexible Spending Account.']
  group_division_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the division.']
  hsa_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for the division\'s Health Savings Account.']
  is_eligible_for_flex_spending boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division participates in a Flexible Spending Account (FSA) program.']
  is_eligible_for_fsa boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division offers a Flexible Spending Account (FSA) to its members.']
  is_eligible_for_hsa boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division offers a Health Savings Account (HSA) to its members.']
  is_eligible_for_subsidy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division qualifies for employer-sponsored subsidy programs.']
  is_eligible_for_waiver boolean [note: 'Indicates if the division is eligible for premium waivers under certain conditions.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s contract is up for renewal.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar subsidy amount applied to employee premiums for this division.']
  subsidy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage subsidy applied to employee premiums for this division.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the division\'s agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the division record.']

  Note: 'Organizational sub-units within an employer group — divisions, subsidiaries, cost centers, or departments — that have distinct benefit configurations, billing aggregation, or eligibility rules. Tracks division name, division code, parent group reference, effective dates, and whether the division is a separate billing entity. Supports multi-site and multi-subsidiary group structures in Facets.'
}

Table employer.group_plan_offering {
  group_plan_offering_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the group plan offering record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group that sponsors this offering.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying health plan catalog record.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_tier. Business justification: Supports Plan Tier Assignment process: defines which network tier (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2) an employer\'s plan offering uses for cost‑share calculations.']
  contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employer contributes per employee when contribution_type = \'flat\'.']
  contribution_effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the defined contribution strategy expires; null if ongoing.']
  contribution_effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the defined contribution strategy becomes effective.']
  contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium the employer pays when contribution_type = \'percentage\'.']
  contribution_strategy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of how employer contributions are calculated.']
  contribution_tier varchar [note: 'Textual description of tiered contribution rules (e.g., "Tier 1: 70%", "Tier 2: 80%").']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'Method the employer uses to calculate its contribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the offering record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the offering becomes binding for the employer group.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the offering ends; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for employee‑only coverage.']
  family_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount for family coverage.']
  group_plan_offering_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the offering.']
  hra_seed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑funded seed contribution to employee HRA accounts.']
  hsa_seed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑funded seed contribution to employee HSA accounts.']
  is_affordable boolean [note: 'True if the offering meets ACA affordability testing for the employer group.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'End of the period used to evaluate participation compliance.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'Start of the period used to evaluate participation compliance.']
  minimum_enrolled_headcount varchar [note: 'Absolute minimum number of participants required for the offering.']
  minimum_participation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required percentage of eligible employees that must enroll for the offering to remain active.']
  offering_code varchar [note: 'External code used by the employer and carriers to reference this specific plan offering.']
  offering_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the plan offering, including benefits highlights.']
  offering_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the plan offering as displayed to employees.']
  offering_type varchar [note: 'Category of the health plan offering (e.g., QHP, HMO, PPO, EPO, HDHP, Dental, Vision). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qhp|hmo|ppo|epo|hdhp|dental|vision — promote to reference product]']
  open_enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last day employees can enroll in the offering.']
  open_enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First day employees can enroll in the offering.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance result for the offering\'s participation thresholds.']
  plan_catalog_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the plan catalog used for this offering.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which the offering applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the offering record.']
  waiver_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the conditions under which a waiver may be granted.']
  waiver_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer allows waiver of spousal or other coverage.']

  Note: 'The set of health plan products offered by an employer group to its eligible employees during a plan year — the single source of truth for what plans are available, how the employer contributes, and what participation rules apply. Captures which QHP, HMO, PPO, EPO, HDHP, dental, and vision plans are available; employer contribution strategy per plan including contribution type (flat dollar, percentage of premium, tiered by coverage tier), employee-only vs family contribution amounts, HSA/HRA employer seed amounts, and contribution effective date ranges; minimum participation percentage, minimum enrolled headcount, waiver eligibility criteria (e.g., spousal coverage waivers), measurement period, and participation compliance status; and the open enrollment window linkage. Consolidates contribution strategies and participation requirements as attributes of the plan offering. Links employer groups to the plan catalog and drives the enrollment eligibility matrix, premium billing split calculations, and ACA affordability compliance testing.'
}

Table employer.contribution_strategy {
  contribution_strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contribution strategy record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employer group to which this contribution strategy applies.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health plan that the contribution rules are associated with.']
  affordability_test_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contribution satisfies the ACA affordability requirement.']
  contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employer contributes per employee when contribution_type is \'flat\'.']
  contribution_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the contribution strategy in contracts and communications.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the employer contribution is applied to premium billing.']
  contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the premium the employer pays when contribution_type is \'percentage\'.']
  contribution_rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the contribution rule for reporting and UI display.']
  contribution_strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contribution strategy.']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the employer contribution: flat dollar amount, percentage of premium, or tiered based on coverage tier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contribution strategy record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the contribution strategy expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the contribution strategy becomes active.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of employee eligibility rules (e.g., full‑time status, tenure) for this contribution.']
  employer_contribution_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount the employer will contribute per employee per billing period.']
  hra_employer_seed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑funded seed contribution to a Health Reimbursement Arrangement for eligible employees.']
  hsa_employer_seed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑funded seed contribution to a Health Savings Account for eligible employees.']
  is_post_tax boolean [note: 'True if the employer contribution is made on a post‑tax basis.']
  is_pre_tax boolean [note: 'True if the employer contribution is made on a pre‑tax basis.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the contribution strategy was last reviewed for compliance or policy updates.']
  maximum_employee_contribution decimal(12,2) [note: 'Highest amount an employee may be required to pay after employer contribution is applied.']
  minimum_employee_contribution decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lowest amount an employee must pay after employer contribution is applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or special instructions related to the contribution strategy.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance review.']
  tax_credit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contribution qualifies for a tax credit under applicable regulations.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the employee coverage tier (e.g., employee‑only, family) that the contribution amount applies to.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the contribution strategy record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Employer contribution rules defining how much the employer pays toward employee and dependent premiums for each offered plan. Captures contribution type (flat dollar, percentage of premium, tiered by coverage tier), employee-only vs family contribution amounts, HSA/HRA employer seed amounts, and effective date ranges. Supports premium billing split calculations and ACA affordability compliance testing.'
}

Table employer.group_renewal {
  group_renewal_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the group renewal record.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broker representing the group.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employer group associated with this renewal.']
  tpa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third‑party administrator handling the group.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments applied to this renewal record.']
  amendment_flag boolean [note: 'True if any amendment has been applied to the renewal.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the renewal record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the renewal record.']
  benefit_plan_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of benefit plan identifiers attached to the renewal.']
  compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory compliance check was performed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the compliance validation for the renewal.']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'How employer contributions are calculated for the renewal period.']
  erisa_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the Employee Retirement Income Security Act applies to the group.']
  funding_arrangement varchar [note: 'Method by which the group finances coverage.']
  group_size varchar [note: 'Total number of covered individuals in the group for the renewal year.']
  latest_amendment_after_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Value of the changed attribute after the amendment (stored as text).']
  latest_amendment_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the most recent amendment.']
  latest_amendment_before_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Value of the changed attribute before the amendment (stored as text).']
  latest_amendment_effective_date date [note: 'Effective date of the most recent amendment.']
  latest_amendment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code for the most recent amendment.']
  latest_amendment_type varchar [note: 'Type of the most recent amendment applied to the renewal.']
  participation_requirement_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the group met the minimum participation threshold for the renewal.']
  participation_requirement_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the participation requirement evaluation.']
  premium_rate_prior_year decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base premium rate applied in the prior renewal year.']
  premium_rate_renewal_year decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base premium rate applied for the renewal year.']
  prior_renewal_effective_date date [note: 'Effective date of the immediately preceding renewal.']
  rate_change_percentage decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percent change between prior year and renewal year premium rates.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the renewal passed all required regulatory validations.']
  renewal_cycle_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the renewal takes effect.']
  renewal_effective_date date [note: 'First day of coverage under the renewed contract.']
  renewal_end_date date [note: 'Last day of coverage for the renewal term.']
  renewal_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by users during the renewal process.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the renewal.']
  renewal_status_date date [note: 'Date when the current renewal status was set.']
  retention_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the renewal in terms of group retention.']
  retention_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing why a group was retained or lost.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit industry classification code for the employer.']

  Note: 'Annual renewal lifecycle record and single source of truth for all group configuration changes — both at renewal and between renewal cycles. Captures renewal effective date, prior year vs renewal year premium rates, rate change percentage, benefit modifications, participation requirement outcomes, renewal status (pending, proposed, accepted, declined), and retention outcome. Also captures mid-year and off-cycle amendments including amendment type (benefit change, plan add/drop, contribution change, address update, contact change), amendment effective date, reason code, approval status, and before/after values of changed attributes. Consolidates group amendments as lifecycle events within the renewal record — amendments are tracked against the current plan year\'s renewal. Provides complete audit trail for group retention management, regulatory compliance, and configuration change history.'
}

Table employer.participation_requirement {
  participation_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the participation requirement record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group to which this participation requirement applies.']
  compliance_review_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next compliance review of the participation requirement must be completed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance outcome of the participation requirement.']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'How premium contributions are allocated for the group (employer, employee, or shared).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the participation requirement record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the participation requirement becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the participation requirement expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  enrolled_headcount varchar [note: 'Current number of members enrolled under the group.']
  erisa_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employer group is subject to ERISA regulations.']
  funding_arrangement varchar [note: 'Funding model for the group (Administrative Services Only, Fully Insured, or Self‑Funded).']
  group_size varchar [note: 'Total number of eligible members in the employer group.']
  last_evaluated_date date [note: 'Date when the participation requirement was last evaluated for compliance.']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which participation is measured for compliance.']
  minimum_enrolled_headcount varchar [note: 'Minimum number of enrolled individuals required for the group to remain eligible.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments entered by administrators.']
  participation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of eligible members that must enroll to satisfy the requirement.']
  participation_requirement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context and details about the participation requirement.']
  participation_requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the participation requirement.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this participation requirement must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., CMS, NAIC).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next renewal evaluation of the participation requirement.']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the participation requirement.']
  requirement_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the participation requirement.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requirement (e.g., minimum participation, waiver eligibility, headcount threshold).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the participation requirement record.']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the group permits waivers (e.g., spousal coverage waivers).']
  waiver_percentage_allowed decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage of eligible members that may be waived while maintaining compliance.']

  Note: 'Minimum participation rules that an employer group must satisfy to maintain group coverage eligibility. Captures minimum participation percentage, minimum enrolled headcount, waiver eligibility criteria (e.g., spousal coverage waivers), measurement period, and compliance status. Used during underwriting, renewal, and open enrollment to validate group eligibility for continued coverage.'
}

Table employer.broker_assignment {
  broker_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the broker assignment record.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'FK to employer.broker']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the employer group (client) in the core administration system.']

  broker_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the broker assignment.']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'Business metric on which the commission is calculated.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate applicable to the broker for this employer group, expressed as a percentage.']
  commission_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate broker commission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the broker assignment record was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the broker assignment terminates or expires. Null if ongoing.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the broker assignment becomes effective.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this broker is the primary broker for the employer group.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or remarks about the broker assignment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system where the broker assignment originated (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the broker assignment record.']

  Note: 'Association between an employer group and its licensed broker(s) or general agent(s), capturing the broker NPN, agency name, commission arrangement type, commission rate, effective and termination dates, and primary vs secondary broker designation. Supports broker compensation processing, group servicing accountability, and regulatory disclosure requirements.'
}

Table employer.tpa_arrangement {
  tpa_arrangement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the TPA arrangement record.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'FK to employer.broker']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: TPA arrangement is specific to an employer group; linking provides association.']
  tpa_id varchar [note: 'FK to employer.tpa']
  arrangement_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the TPA arrangement.']
  arrangement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number or code assigned to the TPA arrangement.']
  arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the arrangement indicating funding model.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'External reference to the legal contract document.']
  contribution_rate_pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Per member per month contribution amount defined by the employer.']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate employer contributions to the self‑funded arrangement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the arrangement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the arrangement terminates or expires; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the arrangement becomes binding.']
  erisa_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the arrangement is subject to ERISA regulations.']
  fee_schedule_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount payable for the fee schedule within a billing period.']
  fee_schedule_rate_pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Per member per month rate charged for the specified service type.']
  fee_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Service category for which the fee schedule applies.']
  gfc_control_flag boolean [note: 'True if the arrangement is subject to Group Financial Control oversight.']
  group_size_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of covered lives allowed under the arrangement.']
  group_size_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of covered lives required for the arrangement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the arrangement is scheduled for renewal.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., Facets, QNXT) that supplied the record.']
  stop_loss_aggregate_corridor decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional amount above the aggregate deductible that the employer must pay before the carrier reimburses.']
  stop_loss_aggregate_deductible decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total deductible amount for the group before stop‑loss coverage applies.']
  stop_loss_carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier providing stop‑loss coverage.']
  stop_loss_coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Benefit categories covered by the stop‑loss policy.']
  stop_loss_effective_date date [note: 'Date the stop‑loss coverage becomes active.']
  stop_loss_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the stop‑loss coverage ends.']
  stop_loss_individual_attachment_point decimal(12,2) [note: 'Deductible amount at which stop‑loss coverage begins for an individual member.']
  stop_loss_laser_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the carrier will pay for any single member\'s claim after the individual attachment point is met.']
  stop_loss_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy identifier for the stop‑loss contract.']
  stop_loss_premium decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual premium paid to the stop‑loss carrier for the coverage.']
  tpa_arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the TPA arrangement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the arrangement record.']

  Note: 'Third Party Administrator (TPA) and Administrative Services Only (ASO) arrangement record for self-funded employer groups — the single source of truth for all ASO/self-funded configuration. Captures TPA entity name, TPA NPI/tax ID, ASO contract reference; administrative fee schedule components by service type (claims processing, network access, care management, PBM, utilization review) with PMPM rates, effective dates, and contractual fee caps; stop-loss (excess loss) insurance configuration including stop-loss carrier name, policy number, specific deductible (individual attachment point), aggregate deductible, aggregate corridor, lasering provisions with member-level laser amounts, stop-loss premium, covered benefits scope, and stop-loss policy effective/expiration dates; and overall arrangement effective dates. Consolidates ASO fee schedules and stop-loss policies as sub-configurations of the TPA arrangement, aligned with Facets/QNXT ASO module patterns. Drives ASO revenue recognition in the finance domain and self-funded group financial risk management.'
}

Table employer.open_enrollment_window {
  open_enrollment_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for open_enrollment_window']

  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefits offered in this enrollment window.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment window record was first created in the system.']
  eligible_employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees who meet eligibility criteria for this enrollment window.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment window closes; no further changes are accepted after this date.']
  enrollment_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which employees may submit their elections (online portal, paper forms, or EDI 834).']
  enrollment_window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment window.']
  enrollment_window_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the window (annual open enrollment, new‑hire election, COBRA election, or special election).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or special instructions related to the window.']
  participation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (to two decimal places) of eligible employees who completed an election.']
  plan_selection_method varchar [note: 'Whether employees may select a single plan or multiple plans during the window.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the enrollment window record (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment window becomes effective for employee elections.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which an employee must submit an election to be processed.']
  total_employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees in the employer group at the start of the window.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment window record.']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees may waive coverage during this window.']
  waiver_deadline date [note: 'Date by which a waiver must be submitted if waivers are permitted.']
  window_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the enrollment window (e.g., OE2024Q1).']

  Note: 'Scheduled open enrollment windows defined by an employer group during which employees may elect, change, or waive coverage. Captures enrollment period type (annual open enrollment, new hire window, COBRA election), start and end dates, eligible plan offerings, enrollment method (online portal, paper, EDI 834), and enrollment completion status. Drives eligibility event processing in the enrollment domain.'
}

Table employer.employer_underwriting_case {
  employer_underwriting_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the underwriting case.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broker facilitating the employer’s enrollment.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employer group that is the subject of the underwriting case.']
  tpa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the TPA associated with the underwriting case, if applicable.']
  aca_adjustment_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Adjustment applied to ensure compliance with Affordable Care Act rating rules.']
  actuarial_basis_document varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the actuarial documentation supporting the rating.']
  age_gender_composite_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Composite factor derived from the group’s age and gender distribution.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the underwriting case, used for tracking and communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the underwriting case record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the premium estimate.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Last day the proposed premium rates are valid; after this date a new quote is required.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First day the proposed premium rates become effective if the quote is accepted.']
  experience_rating_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor based on the employer’s prior loss experience and credibility weighting.']
  geographic_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor reflecting cost differentials across geographic rating areas.']
  group_average_age decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average age of covered members in the group.']
  group_census_size varchar [note: 'Number of covered lives in the employer group at the time of underwriting.']
  group_female_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of female members in the group census.']
  industry_risk_factor varchar [note: 'Code representing the industry classification risk factor applied to the group.']
  manual_rate_basis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate was manually set rather than algorithmically derived.']
  pmpm_estimate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated premium cost per covered member per month.']
  prior_carrier_loss_experience varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of loss experience with the previous carrier.']
  quote_expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quote expires if not accepted.']
  quote_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the premium quote was generated.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the premium quote within the underwriting workflow.']
  rating_area_code varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area identifier used for regional premium adjustments.']
  rating_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate rates: community rated, experience rated, or blended.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the employer group based on underwriting analysis.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the underwriting case was initially submitted.']
  total_premium_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate estimated premium for the entire employer group for the quoted period.']
  underwriting_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision outcome for the case after review.']
  underwriting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the underwriting case.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the underwriting case record.']

  Note: 'Underwriting evaluation record and single source of truth for the complete risk assessment process from submission through rating through quote generation through decision for a new or renewing employer group. Tracks case submission date, group census data, prior carrier loss experience, medical underwriting status, risk tier assignment, manual rate basis; rating factors including age/gender composite factors, geographic area rating factors, industry risk factors, experience rating credibility weights, ACA-compliant rating adjustments, and actuarial basis documentation; underwriting decision (approved, declined, modified); and premium rate quotes with proposed rates by plan, coverage tier, and rating area, quote effective and expiration dates, rating methodology (community rated, experience rated, blended), proposed PMPM rates, total group premium estimate, and quote status (draft, presented, accepted, expired). Consolidates rating factors and rate quotes as stages within the underwriting case lifecycle. Links to the risk domain for RAF and HCC-based risk scoring at the group level. Supports the sales and renewal pipeline.'
}

Table employer.rate_quote {
  rate_quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the premium rate quote.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broker or sales agent handling the quote.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group for which the quote is generated.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan to which the quote applies.']
  tpa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Third‑Party Administrator associated with the quote, if any.']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Employer contribution approach (e.g., fixed amount, percentage of premium).']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of coverage being quoted (e.g., employee only, family).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the quote (e.g., USD).']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any discounts applied to the gross premium.']
  effective_date date [note: 'First date on which the quoted rates become effective if the quote is accepted.']
  erisa_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employer group is subject to ERISA regulations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the quote is no longer valid.']
  gross_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total premium before any discounts or adjustments, expressed in the quote currency.']
  group_sic_code varchar [note: 'Standard Industrial Classification code describing the employer\'s industry.']
  group_size varchar [note: 'Total number of employees eligible for coverage under the employer group.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Funding arrangement for the group: Administrative Services Only (ASO) or Fully Insured.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the quote was formally issued to the employer.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Number of covered lives in the employer group for the quoted period.']
  net_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final premium after discounts, the amount the employer is expected to pay.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which the quoted plan benefits apply.']
  pmpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Proposed premium rate expressed as a per‑member‑per‑month amount.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the quote for tracking and communication with the employer.']
  quote_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the quote, incremented on each revision.']
  rate_quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quote (e.g., draft, presented, accepted, expired, rejected).']
  rating_area varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area used to calculate the premium.']
  rating_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate rates: community, experience, or blended.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the current policy term ends and renewal is expected.']
  total_group_premium_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total premium for the entire employer group based on the quoted rates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quote record.']

  Note: 'Premium rate quote issued to a prospective or renewing employer group, capturing proposed rates by plan, coverage tier, and rating area. Includes quote effective date, expiration date, rating methodology (community rated, experience rated, blended), proposed PMPM rates, total group premium estimate, and quote status (draft, presented, accepted, expired). Supports the sales and renewal pipeline.'
}

Table employer.group_amendment {
  group_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each group amendment record.']
