  after_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'JSON‑encoded snapshot of the group attributes after the amendment was applied.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible sequential number assigned to the amendment.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the amendment.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the amendment indicating the nature of the change.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the amendment received final approval.']
  approval_user_role varchar [note: 'Business role of the user who performed the approval.']
  before_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'JSON‑encoded snapshot of the group attributes before the amendment was applied.']
  contribution_change_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net monetary change to the employer contribution resulting from the amendment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the amendment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for monetary fields.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment becomes effective for the group.']
  is_critical_change boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the amendment is considered critical for compliance or financial reporting.']
  new_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount after the amendment (per member per month).']
  original_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer contribution amount before the amendment (per member per month).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the amendment was initiated.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the amendment reason.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the amendment was submitted for review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the amendment record.']

  Note: 'Mid-year or off-cycle changes to an employer group\'s coverage terms, benefit configuration, or administrative parameters. Captures amendment type (benefit change, plan add/drop, contribution change, address update, contact change), effective date, reason code, approval status, and the before/after values of changed attributes. Provides a complete audit trail of group configuration changes between renewal cycles.'
}

Table employer.cobra_administration {
  cobra_administration_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the COBRA administration record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group to which this COBRA administration applies.']
  administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity responsible for managing COBRA administration.']
  administrator_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether COBRA administration is self‑administered or handled by a third‑party TPA.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the COBRA coverage type applicable to the group.']
  cobra_administration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the COBRA administration record.']
  cobra_agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the COBRA agreement for the employer group.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the group with respect to COBRA obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the COBRA administration record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the COBRA coverage obligations become effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the COBRA coverage obligations terminate or are superseded.']
  election_end_date date [note: 'End date of the COBRA election window for a qualifying event.']
  election_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days participants have to elect COBRA coverage after a qualifying event.']
  election_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the COBRA election window for a qualifying event.']
  group_cobra_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer group meets criteria to be subject to COBRA regulations.']
  last_compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent compliance audit or review was performed.']
  max_employee_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of employees for which the group remains subject to COBRA (used for state mini‑COBRA thresholds).']
  min_employee_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of employees required for the group to be covered by federal COBRA.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information or comments.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for delivering COBRA notifications to participants.']
  notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether participants must be notified of COBRA rights and election periods.']
  premium_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated COBRA premium amount per participant per period.']
  premium_rate_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the group premium rate to calculate the COBRA premium (typically 102%).']
  qualifying_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualifying event that triggers COBRA eligibility for a participant.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the COBRA administration data.']
  state_cobra_law varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code indicating which state’s mini‑COBRA law applies to the group.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the COBRA administration record.']

  Note: 'COBRA (Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act) administration configuration for an employer group, defining the group\'s COBRA obligations, qualifying event types, election period durations, premium rates (102% of group rate), and COBRA administrator designation (self-administered vs third-party COBRA TPA). Tracks group-level COBRA compliance status, election window configurations, and notification requirements. Required for all employer groups with 20+ employees under federal COBRA or state mini-COBRA equivalents.'
}

Table employer.wellness_program {
  wellness_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each wellness program.']
  primary_wellness_vendor_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the vendor partner.']
  aca_compliance_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the program under ACA wellness rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the program record was first created in the system.']
  current_participant_count varchar [note: 'Number of members currently enrolled in the program.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the program ceases to be offered (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the program becomes active for eligible members.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual definition of the criteria members must meet to enroll.']
  enrollment_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of participants allowed for the program.']
  incentive_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the incentive offered to participants.']
  incentive_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the incentive amount.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the incentive is delivered.']
  is_eligible_for_tax_credit boolean [note: 'True if the program qualifies for a tax credit under applicable regulations.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participation is required for covered employees.']
  participation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture member participation data.']
  program_actual_participation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed percentage of eligible members who have participated.']
  program_budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for the program during its effective period.']
  program_budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO currency code for the program budget.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping similar wellness programs.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the program across systems.']
  program_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for regulatory or audit comments related to the program.']
  program_effective_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the program becomes effective.']
  program_end_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the program was terminated or discontinued.']
  program_last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent governance or compliance review.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the wellness program.']
  program_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the program\'s compliance or policy review.']
  program_risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor used in actuarial models to adjust risk based on program participation.']
  program_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the program record.']
  program_subtype varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the program category.']
  program_target_participation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired percentage of eligible members to participate.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Category of the wellness program indicating its primary focus.']
  program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the program definition (e.g., v1.0).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the program record.']
  wellness_program_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the program\'s purpose and activities.']
  wellness_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program.']

  Note: 'Employer-sponsored wellness and health promotion programs offered to enrolled members, including biometric screening, smoking cessation, weight management, EAP (Employee Assistance Program), and chronic disease prevention initiatives. Captures program name, type, vendor partner, incentive structure (premium discount, HSA contribution, gift cards), participation tracking method, program effective dates, and ACA wellness program compliance classification (participatory vs health-contingent). Supports employer value-add differentiation and group retention strategy.'
}

Table employer.group_document {
  group_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the group document record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group to which the document belongs.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date the amendment became effective.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the amendment version for amendment documents.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Serialized audit log of changes to the document metadata.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA‑256) for integrity verification of the document.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document meets current regulatory compliance requirements.']
  confidential boolean [note: 'True if the document contains confidential or restricted information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was created in the system.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level category grouping for the document.']
  document_language varchar [note: 'Language code of the document content.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the document.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document (e.g., contract, summary of benefits, ERISA plan document). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: contract|plan|spd|sbc|erisa|trust|fiduciary|filing|renewal|broker_letter — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the document becomes effective.']
  erisa_plan_administrator varchar [note: 'Name of the plan administrator designated under ERISA for the document.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the document expires or is no longer in force.']
  expiration_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing what happens when the document expires.']
  fiduciary_designation varchar [note: 'Designation of fiduciary(s) associated with the document.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored document.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes.']
  group_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document.']
  is_electronic boolean [note: 'True if the document is stored electronically rather than paper.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the document is required by regulation or policy for the group.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'True if this document is the primary version for its type within the group.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date the document was last reviewed for accuracy/compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the document.']
  physical_location varchar [note: 'Physical storage location reference for paper documents.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Plan year to which the document applies.']
  retention_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the document must be retained per policy.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or role that performed the last review.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the document metadata.']
  source_system_document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the document in the source system.']
  storage_uri varchar [note: 'URI pointing to the document location in the enterprise content repository.']
  trust_agreement_details varchar [note: 'Key details of the trust agreement referenced by the document.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the document, following the organization’s versioning scheme.']

  Note: 'Repository and single source of truth for all documents associated with an employer group, including executed group contracts, plan documents, SPDs (Summary Plan Descriptions), SBCs (Summary of Benefits and Coverage), ERISA wrap documents, trust agreements, named fiduciary designations, plan administrator information, DOL Form 5500 filings, ERISA plan amendment history, fiduciary liability insurance records, DOI filings, renewal agreements, broker appointment letters, and all ERISA compliance artifacts. Captures document type, document title, version, effective date, expiration date, storage reference URI, document status, plan year, and ERISA-specific metadata (plan administrator, fiduciary designations, amendment history, trust agreement details, plan year). Consolidates all group documentation including ERISA plan documents — ERISA documents are distinguished by document type classification, not by separate entity. Supports regulatory audit readiness, ERISA fiduciary obligations, DOL reporting, and group servicing.'
}

Table employer.aso_fee_schedule {
  aso_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the ASO fee schedule record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: ASO fee schedule applies to a particular employer group; linking removes need for separate group reference.']
  aso_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the fee schedule purpose and any special conditions.']
  aso_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the fee is invoiced to the employer group.']
  cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount that can be charged for this component within a billing period.']
  cap_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the cap amount is applied (per member, per claim, or overall).']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Category of administrative service for which the fee is charged (e.g., claims processing, network access).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in this schedule.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule becomes effective and billable.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which employer groups or members qualify for this fee schedule.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is subject to tax.']
  minimum_participation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of eligible members that must enroll for the schedule to apply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the fee schedule.']
  pm_per_member_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base administrative fee charged per covered member per month for the specified component.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the fee schedule in contracts and communications.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the fee schedule data (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage when is_taxable is true.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee schedule record.']

  Note: 'Administrative fee schedule for ASO (Administrative Services Only) self-funded employer groups, defining the per-member-per-month (PMPM) administrative fees charged for claims processing, network access, care management, pharmacy benefit management, and other administrative services. Captures fee component type, PMPM rate, effective date, and contractual cap amounts. Drives ASO revenue recognition in the finance domain.'
}

Table employer.stop_loss_policy {
  stop_loss_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the stop‑loss policy record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Stop‑loss policy is tied to an employer group; linking creates proper relationship.']
  aggregate_attachment_point decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total deductible amount the employer group must incur before the carrier pays for excess claims.']
  aggregate_deductible_reset_period varchar [note: 'Period after which the aggregate attachment point resets (e.g., annually).']
  attachment_point_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether attachment points are calculated per individual claim or on an annual basis.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier that provides the stop‑loss coverage.']
  claim_payment_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the carrier will pay for a single claim under the stop‑loss policy.']
  claim_payment_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the claim payment limit.']
  contribution_strategy varchar [note: 'Method the employer uses to contribute toward the stop‑loss premium (fixed amount, percentage of premium, or tiered).']
  covered_benefit_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of benefit codes (e.g., CPT, DRG) that are covered under the stop‑loss policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the stop‑loss policy record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'General deductible amount defined in the stop‑loss contract (may differ from attachment points).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the stop‑loss coverage becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the stop‑loss coverage expires (if not renewed).']
  individual_attachment_point decimal(12,2) [note: 'Deductible amount an individual member must incur before the stop‑loss carrier pays.']
  lasering_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy includes a lasering provision (reinstatement after a loss).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions related to the policy.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'External policy number assigned by the carrier; used for billing and claims.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the policy provides individual attachment point coverage, aggregate coverage, or both.']
  premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Periodic premium charged by the carrier for the stop‑loss coverage.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the premium amount.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for policy renewal negotiations.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factor applied to the premium based on the employer group\'s risk profile.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the stop‑loss policy data (e.g., Facets, QNXT).']
  stop_loss_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stop‑loss policy.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the policy was terminated prior to its scheduled expiration, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the stop‑loss policy record.']

  Note: 'Stop-loss (excess loss) insurance policy associated with a self-funded ASO employer group, providing financial protection against catastrophic claims. Captures stop-loss carrier name, policy number, specific deductible (individual attachment point), aggregate deductible, lasering provisions, covered benefits, policy effective and expiration dates, and premium amounts. Critical for ASO group financial risk management.'
}

Table employer.group_rating_factor {
  group_rating_factor_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the rating factor record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group to which this rating factor applies.']
  actuarial_basis varchar [note: 'Underlying actuarial methodology used to derive the factor.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any manual adjustment to the factor value.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the factor record was first created in the data lake.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the rating factor ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rating factor becomes effective for premium calculations.']
  factor_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the rating factor within the group.']
  factor_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the factor purpose and calculation methodology.']
  factor_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rating factor (e.g., age/gender composite, geographic area, industry risk, experience rating, ACA adjustment).']
  factor_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the factor (e.g., 1.025 for a 2.5% increase).']
  group_rating_factor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the factor record.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'True if the factor value has been manually overridden from the actuarial default.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this factor is the default for its type when no group‑specific factor exists.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business notes about the factor.']
  rating_period_end date [note: 'End date of the rating period to which the factor applies.']
  rating_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the rating period to which the factor applies.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or guideline that mandates or influences the factor.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the factor (e.g., Facets, QNXT, or custom actuarial system).']
  source_system_factor_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the factor in the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the factor record.']
  value_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the factor value: percentage, multiplier, or dollar amount.']

  Note: 'Rating factors applied to an employer group for premium calculation, including age/gender composite factors, geographic area rating factors, industry risk factors, experience rating credibility weights, and ACA-compliant rating adjustments. Captures factor type, factor value, rating period, and the actuarial basis for each factor. Used by the billing domain for premium calculation and by the risk domain for group-level risk adjustment.'
}

  effective_until date [note: 'Date the document ceases to be effective (null for open‑ended).']

  trust_state varchar [note: 'State component of the trust address (US state code).']
  trust_zip varchar [note: 'ZIP/Postal code for the trust address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']

  Note: 'ERISA-governed plan document record for self-funded employer groups, capturing the formal plan document, trust agreement, named fiduciary designations, plan administrator information, plan year, and ERISA wrap document details. Tracks DOL Form 5500 filing obligations, plan amendment history, and fiduciary liability insurance. Distinct from group_document in that it specifically governs ERISA compliance obligations and fiduciary responsibilities.'
}

Table employer.regulatory_compliance_record {
  regulatory_compliance_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the RegulatoryComplianceRecord association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory_obligation']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer_group']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the employer group for this obligation']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment for this group‑obligation pair']

  Note: 'This association product captures the compliance relationship between an employer group and a regulatory obligation. It records the compliance status and the date of the most recent assessment for each group‑obligation pair.. Existence Justification: Each employer group must satisfy many regulatory obligations, and each regulatory obligation (e.g., HIPAA, ACA) applies to many employer groups. The insurer tracks compliance status and assessment dates for every group‑obligation pair, creating, updating, and deleting these records as part of ongoing compliance management.'
}

Table employer.employer_contract {
  employer_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the Contract association']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer_group']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor']
  annual_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the annual contract review']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the contract']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the contract becomes effective']
  employer_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract (e.g., active, pending, terminated)']
  total_contract_value decimal [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual relationship between an employer group and a vendor. It captures contract-specific details that exist only in the context of this relationship, linking one employer_group to one vendor per record.. Existence Justification: Employer groups negotiate separate contracts with multiple vendors, and each vendor can serve many employer groups. The contract itself carries attributes such as contract number, effective dates, status, total value, and review schedule, which are not owned by either the employer group or the vendor.'
}

Table employer.broker {
  broker_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for broker']

  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of broker\'s physical address.']
  agreement_end_date date [note: 'End date of the broker agreement.']
  agreement_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the broker agreement.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the broker agreement.']
  agreement_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed terms and conditions of the broker agreement.']
  broker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of broker (e.g., independent, captive).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of broker\'s address.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Commission amount paid to the broker.']
  commission_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the commission is paid.']
  commission_end_date date [note: 'End date of the commission period.']
  commission_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Commission rate agreed with the broker.']
  commission_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the commission period.']
  country varchar [note: 'Country of broker\'s address.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for broker communication.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the broker relationship ended or is scheduled to end.']
  fax varchar [note: 'Fax number for broker contact.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'License number issued by regulatory authority.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the broker.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for broker contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of broker\'s address.']
  rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Credit rating or performance score assigned to the broker.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the broker record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the broker record was last updated.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the broker operates.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned to the broker.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for renewing the broker agreement.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the renewal process.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the broker relationship began.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of broker\'s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the broker.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Tax ID used for regulatory reporting.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for terminating the broker relationship.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for broker. Referenced by broker_id.'
}

  tpa_coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the coverage.']
  tpa_coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of coverage provided by the TPA.']
  tpa_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the TPA record was first captured in the system.']

  tpa_sla_notes varchar [note: 'Notes or remarks about the SLA.']
  tpa_sla_start date [note: 'Start date of the current SLA with the TPA.']
  tpa_sla_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SLA.']
  tpa_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the TPA\'s primary location.']
  tpa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the third-party administrator.']
  tpa_tax_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number assigned to the TPA.']
  tpa_termination_date date [note: 'Date when the TPA relationship was terminated, if applicable.']
  tpa_termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination of the TPA relationship.']
  tpa_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the third-party administrator\'s role within the health insurance ecosystem.']
  tpa_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the TPA record.']
  tpa_zip varchar [note: 'Postal ZIP code of the TPA\'s primary location.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for tpa. Referenced by tpa_id.'
}

Table employer.broker_agreement {
  broker_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for broker_agreement']
  parent_broker_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on broker_agreement (parent_broker_agreement_id)']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the broker agreement, used in correspondence, commission statements, and regulatory filings.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the broker agreement indicating the nature of the intermediary relationship (e.g., general agent, broker, sub-broker, managing general agent, consultant, or third-party administrator).']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments or addenda that have been executed against this broker agreement since its original effective date.']
  appointment_date date [note: 'Date on which the broker was formally appointed by the carrier to represent and sell its health insurance products.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the broker\'s formal appointment with the carrier, indicating whether the broker is currently appointed, pending appointment, lapsed, or revoked.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the broker agreement automatically renews at the end of each term period without requiring explicit re-execution.']
  base_commission_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Standard base commission rate (expressed as a decimal percentage, e.g., 0.0500 = 5%) applicable under this agreement for new business production.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broker or producer entity that is party to this agreement.']
  broker_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the broker or brokerage firm associated with this agreement, as registered with the state department of insurance.']
  broker_npn varchar [note: 'National Producer Number assigned by the National Insurance Producer Registry (NIPR), uniquely identifying the licensed producer across all states.']
  broker_tin varchar [note: 'Federal Taxpayer Identification Number (EIN or SSN) of the broker, used for IRS 1099 reporting of commission payments.']
  commission_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Type of commission schedule governing broker compensation under this agreement, such as flat rate, tiered, percentage of premium, per-member-per-month, bonus-eligible, or override commission.']
  compensation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the broker is compensated under this agreement, such as commission-based, fee-for-service, salary, or a hybrid arrangement.']
  compliance_certification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance certification or attestation completed by the broker, confirming adherence to carrier policies, ACA requirements, and anti-fraud provisions.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the broker agreement contract template used, supporting tracking of contract amendments and revisions over time.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the broker agreement becomes legally binding and the broker is authorized to solicit and sell health insurance products on behalf of the carrier.']
  employer_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific employer group account this broker agreement is associated with, if the agreement is group-specific rather than a general agency agreement.']
  errors_omissions_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the broker\'s current Errors and Omissions (E&O) insurance policy on file with the carrier.']
  errors_omissions_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the broker is required to maintain Errors and Omissions (E&O) professional liability insurance as a condition of this agreement.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an exclusive agreement where the broker is restricted from selling competing carriers\' products in the same market segment and territory.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the broker agreement was formally signed and executed by both parties.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the broker agreement is scheduled to expire. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen agreements that auto-renew.']
  general_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the general agent or managing general agent under whose hierarchy this broker agreement falls, if applicable.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Health insurance market segment that the broker is authorized to sell under this agreement, such as small group, large group, individual, Medicare, Medicaid, or exchange marketplace.']
  minimum_production_requirement decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual premium production volume or number of enrolled lives the broker must achieve to maintain active status under this agreement.']
  non_compete_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the non-compete or non-solicitation clause that applies after termination of this agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the broker agreement, capturing special terms, negotiated exceptions, or administrative remarks.']
  override_commission_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Override or bonus commission rate paid to general agents or managing brokers on production generated by sub-brokers under their hierarchy.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which commission payments are disbursed to the broker under this agreement.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Line of health insurance products the broker is authorized to sell under this agreement, such as medical, dental, vision, life, disability, or supplemental coverage.']
  renewal_commission_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Commission rate applicable to renewal business under this agreement, often different from the new business rate.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Next scheduled renewal date for the broker agreement, used for renewal management workflows and commission schedule reviews.']
  state_of_licensure varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the broker holds an active insurance producer license, as required for this agreement to be valid.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the broker agreement indicating whether it is draft, active, suspended, terminated, expired, or pending renewal.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the broker agreement was terminated, if terminated prior to the scheduled expiration date. Null if the agreement has not been terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early termination of the broker agreement, such as voluntary withdrawal, for-cause termination, non-renewal, regulatory action, mutual agreement, or carrier-initiated cancellation.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic sales territory or service area in which the broker is authorized to operate under this agreement.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for broker_agreement. Referenced by agreement_id.'
}

Table contract.contract_provider_contract {
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the provider contract record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for all contractual agreements between the health plan and providers.']
  contract_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Contract Owner assignment report; links each provider contract to the employee responsible for its management.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budgeting process assigns each provider contract to a cost center for expense allocation and financial reporting; insurers track contract costs by cost center.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: GL posting of payments and adjustments under a provider contract uses a specific ledger; accounting requires contract‑to‑ledger mapping for audit trails.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider (physician, hospital, clinic, ancillary provider, or DME supplier) who is party to this contract. Sourced from Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider network that owns or administers this contract (e.g., HMO network, PPO network, EPO network). Determines which network directory listings are governed by this contract.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Contract underwriting ties each provider contract to the credential record reviewed for compliance; needed for audit, renewal, and regulatory reporting.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: UNDERWRITING: Provider contracts must be tied to a risk pool for rate setting and risk‑adjustment reporting required by CMS.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Claims outsourcing uses a TPA vendor; provider contract must reference the TPA vendor for payment methodology, audit, and regulatory reporting.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Effective date of the most recent amendment to this contract. Used to determine which contract terms apply for a given date of service. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the most recent amendment to this contract. Null for original, unamended contracts. Tracks the amendment sequence for audit and compliance purposes. Sourced from Cactus/ProviderSource.']
  aso_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract operates under an Administrative Services Only (ASO) arrangement where the employer self-funds claims and the health plan provides administrative services only. True = ASO arrangement.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract is configured to automatically renew upon reaching its termination date without requiring re-execution. True = auto-renews; False = requires manual renewal action.']
  capitation_rate_pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fixed monthly payment amount per enrolled member (Per Member Per Month - PMPM) paid to the provider under capitation contracts. Applicable only when payment_methodology = capitation. Expressed in USD.']
  carve_out_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specific services or benefit categories are carved out of this contract and managed under a separate arrangement (e.g., behavioral health carve-out, pharmacy carve-out). True = carve-outs exist.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the contract as recorded in the Provider Management System. Used for display in contracting workflows, provider communications, and reporting dashboards.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract identifier assigned by the health plan contracting department. Used in correspondence, amendments, and provider communications. Sourced from Cactus/ProviderSource contract module.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the provider contract. Drives eligibility verification, claims adjudication routing, and provider directory inclusion. Values: draft, pending_execution, active, suspended, terminated, expired, under_amendment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_execution|active|suspended|terminated|expired|under_amendment — promote to reference product]']
  contract_tier varchar [note: 'Tiering classification assigned to this contract, reflecting the provider\'s preferred status and associated member cost-sharing levels. Tier 1/preferred providers typically have lower member cost-sharing. Used in benefit design and member-facing provider directory.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractual arrangement by payment and relationship structure. Determines reimbursement methodology and applicable rate tables. Values: fee_for_service (FFS), capitation, bundled_payment, value_based_care (VBC/ACO), letter_of_agreement (LOA), single_case_agreement (SCA), out_of_network_gap_fill. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|bundled_payment|value_based_care|letter_of_agreement|single_case_agreement|out_of_network_gap_fill — promote to reference product]']
  cpt_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending CPT or HCPCS procedure code defining the upper bound of the service scope covered under this contract. Used with cpt_range_start to define the range of covered procedures.']
  cpt_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting CPT or HCPCS procedure code defining the lower bound of the service scope covered under this contract. Used with cpt_range_end to define the range of covered procedures. Sourced from AMA CPT code set.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this provider contract record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credentialing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider must maintain active credentialing status as a condition of this contract. True = credentialing is a contractual requirement. Linked to NCQA credentialing standards.']
  delegation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credentialing, utilization management, or other health plan functions have been delegated to the provider organization under this contract. True = delegation arrangement exists. Requires NCQA delegation oversight.']
  drg_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) code defining the upper bound of inpatient service scope covered under this contract. Used with drg_range_start to define the range of covered DRGs.']
  drg_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting Diagnosis Related Group (DRG) code defining the lower bound of inpatient service scope covered under this contract. Applicable for hospital contracts with DRG-based payment methodology.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and reimbursement terms take effect. Used in claims adjudication to determine applicable rate schedule. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which all parties signed and the contract was fully executed. May differ from effective_date for retroactive or prospective contracts. Sourced from Cactus/ProviderSource contract module.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule (rate table) governing reimbursement amounts for specific CPT/HCPCS procedure codes under this contract. Applicable for fee-for-service and bundled payment contracts.']
  lob_applicability varchar [note: 'Line of Business (LOB) to which this contract applies. Determines which member populations and benefit plans are governed by this contract\'s reimbursement terms. Values: commercial, medicare_advantage, medicaid, marketplace, cobra, tricare, all. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_advantage|medicaid|marketplace|cobra|tricare|all — promote to reference product]']
  npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the contracting provider as assigned by CMS NPPES. 10-digit unique identifier used in EDI transactions (837/835) and claims adjudication to identify the contracted provider.']
  payment_methodology varchar [note: 'Specific reimbursement calculation methodology governing how the health plan pays the provider under this contract. Distinct from contract_type in that it specifies the payment calculation approach. Examples: fee_for_service (FFS), capitation (PMPM), per_diem, case_rate, DRG-based, bundled, Reference Based Pricing (RBP), global_budget, salary. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|per_diem|case_rate|drg_based|bundled|rbp|global_budget|salary — promote to reference product]']
  provider_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contracted provider entity type. Drives applicable reimbursement rules, credentialing requirements, and service scope definitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physician|hospital|clinic|ancillary|dme_supplier|behavioral_health|skilled_nursing|home_health|laboratory|radiology — promote to reference product]']
  quality_incentive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes quality-based incentive payments tied to HEDIS, STAR ratings, CAHPS, or other quality performance measures. True = quality incentive provisions exist in the contract.']
  raf_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) / Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) coding obligations apply to the provider under this contract. True = provider has contractual HCC coding and encounter data submission requirements.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to the termination date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Contractually mandated notice period for non-renewal. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days.']
  revenue_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending UB-04 revenue code defining the upper bound of facility service categories covered under this contract. Used with revenue_code_range_start to define the range of covered revenue codes.']
  revenue_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting UB-04 revenue code defining the lower bound of facility service categories covered under this contract. Used for hospital and facility contracts to define covered service lines.']
  risk_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Specifies the financial risk-sharing arrangement between the health plan and provider under VBC or ACO contracts. Determines how savings and losses are allocated. Values: none, shared_savings, shared_risk, full_risk, upside_only, downside_only.']
  service_exclusion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of specific services, procedures, or conditions explicitly excluded from the scope of this contract. Supplements the CPT/DRG/revenue code ranges with narrative exclusion details.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this contract record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Provider Management System and Core Administration Platform.']
  source_system_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Native contract identifier from the originating operational system (Cactus, ProviderSource, Facets, or QNXT). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the contracting provider entity. Used for 1099 reporting, payment processing, and IRS compliance. 9-digit numeric value.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard contract template used as the basis for this agreement. Enables tracking of which template version governs the contract terms and supports template-level compliance audits.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended contracts. Used to determine provider in-network status for claims with dates of service at or before this date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the contract for convenience (without cause). Distinct from renewal_notice_days which applies at contract expiry.']
  tpa_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract is administered by a Third Party Administrator (TPA) on behalf of the health plan. True = TPA-administered; False = directly administered by the health plan. Relevant for ASO arrangements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this provider contract record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for incremental load detection and change data capture. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  vbc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes Value-Based Care (VBC) components such as quality bonuses, shared savings, or risk-sharing arrangements beyond standard fee-for-service reimbursement.']

  Note: 'Master record for all contractual agreements between the health plan and providers (physicians, hospitals, clinics, ancillary providers, DME suppliers), including formal contracts, Letters of Agreement (LOAs), Letters of Intent (LOIs), single-case agreements (SCAs), and out-of-network gap-fill arrangements. Captures contract type (fee-for-service, capitation, bundled payment, VBC/ACO, LOA, SCA), contract status, effective and termination dates, line of business (LOB) applicability, TPA/ASO designations, auto-renewal flags, contract tier, owning network, template reference, payment methodology classification, and service scope definitions (covered/excluded service categories, CPT/HCPCS ranges, revenue code ranges, DRG ranges, carve-out indicators, inclusion/exclusion flags). SSOT for all provider reimbursement agreements, interim arrangements, and contracted service scope. Sourced from Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource) and Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).'
}

Table contract.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each party record in the provider contract domain. Serves as the primary key for all contractual party references across the health plan enterprise.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider contract this party is bound by. Links the party record to its governing contractual agreement within the contract domain.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Party represents a provider entity in a contract; linking to its credential record enables tracking of credential status and compliance per party.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Contract Party Management report requires linking party records to vendor master to identify vendor parties (e.g., TPAs) for compliance and payment processing.']
  aco_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is an Accountable Care Organization (ACO) participating in a value-based care (VBC) arrangement. Triggers ACO-specific contract terms, shared savings calculations, and CMS reporting requirements.']
  capitation_eligible_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is eligible to receive capitation payments (Per Member Per Month - PMPM) under the contract. Drives capitation payment processing and actuarial PMPM rate application.']
  contract_party_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this party within the contract management system (e.g., Cactus or ProviderSource provider contract number). Used for cross-system reconciliation, EDI transactions, and provider directory publication.']
  credentialing_date date [note: 'Date on which the party was most recently credentialed or re-credentialed by the health plan. NCQA requires re-credentialing every 36 months. Used to trigger re-credentialing workflows and network compliance reporting.']
  credentialing_status varchar [note: 'Current credentialing status of the party as maintained in the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource). Determines whether the party is eligible to provide services to plan members. Credentialing must be current for claims to be adjudicated as in-network.']
  delegation_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this party is a delegated entity authorized to perform functions on behalf of the health plan (e.g., credentialing, utilization management, claims processing). Triggers delegation oversight and audit requirements per NCQA and state DOI standards.']
  delegation_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies the functional scope of delegation authority granted to this party. Applicable only when delegation_indicator is true. Defines which health plan functions the delegated entity is authorized to perform on behalf of the payer.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the party operates, if different from the legal entity name. Commonly used for group practices and hospital systems that operate under a brand name distinct from their legal registration.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this party\'s participation in the contract becomes effective. Drives claims adjudication eligibility windows, network directory publication, and provider credentialing timelines.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the contracting party as registered with state or federal authorities. Used for contract execution, credentialing, and regulatory reporting. May be an individual provider name, group practice name, IPA, ACO, or hospital system name.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the party\'s primary state professional or business license expires. Triggers credentialing re-verification workflows and may result in contract suspension if not renewed. Critical for NCQA and URAC credentialing compliance.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation where the party holds its primary professional or business license. Used for network adequacy reporting, credentialing, and state-specific regulatory compliance.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Health plan line of business under which this party is contracted. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, reimbursement methodologies, and network adequacy standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_advantage|medicaid|marketplace|cobra|fehb — promote to reference product]']
  malpractice_coverage_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party maintains active professional liability (malpractice) insurance coverage as required by the contract. Failure to maintain coverage may trigger contract suspension or termination per NCQA credentialing standards.']
  malpractice_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the party\'s professional liability (malpractice) insurance policy expires. Triggers credentialing re-verification and contract compliance monitoring workflows.']
  network_participation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the party is a participating (PAR) in-network provider, a non-participating (non-PAR) provider, or an out-of-network provider. Drives member cost-sharing calculations, claims adjudication rules, and EOB generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing supplemental information about the party that does not fit structured attributes. May include contract negotiation notes, special arrangement details, or credentialing exceptions. Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of sensitive business information.']
  npi varchar [note: 'The 10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned by CMS to uniquely identify the provider party. Type 1 NPI for individual providers; Type 2 NPI for organizational providers. Required for all HIPAA-covered transactions including 837 claims and 270/271 eligibility inquiries.']
  npi_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the NPI is Type 1 (individual/solo practitioner) or Type 2 (organizational entity such as group practice, hospital, or IPA). Determines credentialing requirements and contract structure.']
  oig_exclusion_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party appears on the OIG List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE). Excluded parties are prohibited from participating in Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal healthcare programs. Checked monthly per CMS compliance requirements.']
  party_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of this party within the contract relationship. Identifies whether the party is the health plan (payer), a contracting provider, a delegated entity, an Independent Practice Association (IPA), an Accountable Care Organization (ACO), a hospital system, or a group practice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: payer|provider|delegated_entity|ipa|aco|hospital_system|group_practice — promote to reference product]']
  party_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party within the contract. Indicates whether the party is actively bound by the contract, pending execution, suspended, terminated, or inactive. Drives eligibility verification and claims adjudication routing.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the party as an individual (natural person, e.g., solo practitioner with individual NPI) or an organization (legal entity, e.g., group practice, hospital, IPA, ACO). Drives NPI type assignment and credentialing workflow.']
  primary_specialty_code varchar [note: 'NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code representing the party\'s primary clinical specialty. Used for network adequacy analysis, claims routing, prior authorization rules, and provider directory classification.']
  raf_applicable_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) scoring applies to this party\'s contract arrangement. Relevant for Medicare Advantage and ACA marketplace contracts where Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) risk scores drive capitation payments.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this party record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this party record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and SCD Type 2 processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  recredentialing_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the party must complete re-credentialing to maintain active network participation. Calculated as 36 months from the credentialing_date per NCQA standards. Drives automated re-credentialing workflow initiation.']
  sanction_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has an active sanction, exclusion, or adverse action recorded by OIG, SAM, or state licensing boards. A true value triggers contract suspension review and claims payment hold per CMS and HIPAA compliance requirements.']
  secondary_specialty_code varchar [note: 'NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code representing the party\'s secondary clinical specialty, if applicable. Supports multi-specialty provider contracting and network adequacy reporting.']
  service_area_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation representing the primary state in which this party provides services under the contract. Used for network adequacy reporting, state regulatory compliance, and geographic claims routing.']
  service_area_zip_code varchar [note: 'Primary ZIP code of the party\'s principal service location. Used for network adequacy geo-mapping, member-to-provider distance calculations, and CMS network adequacy submissions.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized individual who signed or has authority to sign the contract on behalf of this party. Required for contract execution validation and legal enforceability. Typically the CEO, CMO, or designated contracting officer.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Official title or position of the authorized signatory (e.g., Chief Executive Officer, Medical Director, Contracting Officer). Validates the authority level of the individual executing the contract on behalf of the party.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this party record originated (e.g., Cactus, ProviderSource, Facets, QNXT). Supports data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and ETL audit in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_party_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this party record in the originating operational system (e.g., Cactus provider ID, ProviderSource contract party key). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued professional or business license number for the contracting party. Required for credentialing verification and network participation. For individual providers, this is the state medical license; for organizations, the state business license or certificate of authority.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this party\'s participation in the contract ends. Null for open-ended or currently active parties. Used to determine network participation windows, claims payment eligibility, and member notification requirements.']
  tin varchar [note: 'Federal Tax Identification Number (EIN or SSN) used for payment processing, 1099 reporting, and IRS compliance. Critical for remittance and premium payment routing. Stored as 9-digit numeric string without hyphens.']
  vbc_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the payment methodology or value-based care arrangement type applicable to this party under the contract. Drives reimbursement calculation logic, risk-sharing model application, and financial reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|bundled_payment|shared_savings|global_risk — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Identifies all parties bound by a provider contract — the health plan entity, the contracting provider (individual NPI or organizational NPI), group practice, IPA, ACO, or hospital system. Tracks party role (payer, provider, delegated entity), NPI, TIN, legal entity name, signatory authority, and party effective dates. Supports multi-party and delegated contracting arrangements.'
}

Table contract.term {
  term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a contract term record in the provider contract management system (Facets/QNXT or Provider Management System). Serves as the primary key for the term entity.']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract amendment that introduced or modified this term, if applicable. Null if the term originates from the base contract rather than an amendment.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent provider contract to which this term belongs. Links the term to its governing contract in the Provider Network Management system.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule associated with this term when payment_methodology is fee_for_service. Links to the rate table governing allowed amounts for procedure codes under this term.']
  audit_rights_period_years varchar [note: 'The number of years the health plan retains the right to audit provider records under this audit rights term. Typically 7 years per HIPAA and CMS requirements. Applicable when term_type is \'audit_rights\'.']
  auto_renew boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term automatically renews upon expiration without requiring renegotiation (True). Supports contract lifecycle management and renewal notification workflows.']
  capitation_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The Per Member Per Month (PMPM) capitation rate defined by this term when payment_methodology is capitation. Represents the fixed monthly payment per enrolled member to the provider regardless of services rendered. Classified confidential as proprietary pricing.']
  compliance_checkpoint_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit checkpoint associated with this term. Used by the compliance team to track regulatory obligations, CMS audit readiness, and DOI examination preparation.']
  cpt_range_end varchar [note: 'The ending CPT or HCPCS procedure code of the range to which this reimbursement rule term applies. Used in conjunction with cpt_range_start to define the applicable procedure code range for adjudication.']
  cpt_range_start varchar [note: 'The starting CPT or HCPCS procedure code of the range to which this reimbursement rule term applies. Used in conjunction with cpt_range_end to define the applicable procedure code range for adjudication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract term record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record retention requirements.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed method for resolving disputes arising under this term: Arbitration, Mediation, Litigation, or Internal Review. Applicable for hold-harmless, indemnification, and audit rights terms.']
  drg_grouper_version varchar [note: 'The DRG grouper version (e.g., \'MS-DRG v41.0\', \'APR-DRG v39\') applicable to this term when payment_methodology is \'drg_based\'. Ensures adjudication uses the correct DRG weight tables for inpatient reimbursement.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this contract term becomes legally binding and operationally enforceable. Used to determine applicability during claims adjudication in Facets/QNXT.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this contract term ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended terms with no defined end date. Used in adjudication date-range checks.']
  governing_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code whose laws govern this contract term. Determines applicable state DOI regulations, insurance code requirements, and state-specific provider contract mandates.']
  hipaa_baa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term constitutes or requires a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) (True). Triggers compliance tracking and ensures Protected Health Information (PHI) handling obligations are documented per HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.']
  icd_version varchar [note: 'Specifies the ICD diagnosis coding version applicable to this term when the term references diagnosis-based reimbursement rules or Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) risk adjustment provisions.']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum dollar amount of quality incentive payment available to the provider under this term upon meeting quality_metric_threshold. Applicable for VBC, ACO, and pay-for-performance contract terms. Classified confidential as proprietary financial terms.']
  is_adjudication_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term directly drives claims adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT (True). When True, the term\'s reimbursement_policy_subtype, cpt_range_start/end, and reduction_percentage are loaded into the adjudication engine.']
  is_negotiable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract term is open to negotiation (True) or is standard non-negotiable boilerplate language (False). Supports contract negotiation workflow and template management.']
  is_standard_boilerplate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term is sourced from the organization\'s standard contract template library (True) or is a custom-negotiated term specific to this contract (False). Supports template governance and deviation tracking.']
  lob_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies which Line of Business (LOB) this contract term applies to. Enables selective application of reimbursement rules and contractual obligations across Commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, Marketplace (ACA/QHP), or all lines of business.']
  mac_pricing_basis varchar [note: 'The drug pricing benchmark used for pharmacy reimbursement terms: Average Wholesale Price (AWP), Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC), Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC), National Average Drug Acquisition Cost (NADAC), or a custom contracted rate. Applicable for terms covering pharmacy benefits.']
  payment_methodology varchar [note: 'The reimbursement payment methodology defined by this term when term_type is \'payment_methodology\' or \'reimbursement_rule\'. Drives adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT. Includes Fee-for-Service (FFS), Capitation, Bundled Payment, Case Rate, Per Diem, Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)-based, Reference Based Pricing (RBP), and Value-Based Care (VBC). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_for_service|capitation|bundled_payment|case_rate|per_diem|drg_based|rbp|vbc — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pos_code varchar [note: 'The CMS Place of Service (POS) code to which this term\'s reimbursement differential applies, when reimbursement_policy_subtype is \'pos_differential\'. Two-digit code per CMS POS code set (e.g., \'11\' for Office, \'21\' for Inpatient Hospital).']
  quality_metric_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum quality performance threshold (expressed as a percentage or score) that the provider must achieve to qualify for incentive payments or avoid penalties under quality incentive terms. Linked to HEDIS or STAR measures.']
  reduction_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage reduction applied to the allowed amount under this reimbursement rule term (e.g., 50.00 for a 50% reduction on secondary procedures under multiple procedure reduction policy). Applicable when reimbursement_policy_subtype is set. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary pricing.']
  reimbursement_policy_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification of the reimbursement rule when term_type is \'reimbursement_rule\'. Specifies the specific adjudication policy: Multiple Procedure Reduction, Bilateral Procedure, Assistant Surgeon, Global Surgery, Unbundling Rules, Place-of-Service (POS) Differential, or Modifier Impact. Directly maps to adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: multiple_procedure_reduction|bilateral_procedure|assistant_surgeon|global_surgery|unbundling|pos_differential|modifier_impact — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days prior to expiration_date by which either party must provide notice of intent not to renew this term. Applicable when auto_renew is True.']
  risk_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Defines the risk-sharing arrangement under Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) contract terms. Specifies whether the provider participates in shared savings only (upside), shared risk (two-sided), or full risk (global capitation).']
  source_system_term_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this term record in the originating operational system (e.g., Cactus contract clause ID, QNXT reimbursement rule ID, or ProviderSource term reference). Supports data lineage and reconciliation with source systems.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Plain-language operational summary of the term\'s intent and impact, authored by the contracting team. Used for training, audits, and quick reference without reading full legal text.']
  term_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable external identifier for the term, typically formatted as a section or clause reference (e.g., \'TERM-2024-0042\' or \'Section 4.2.1\'). Used in contract documents and correspondence.']
  term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract term. \'active\' indicates the term is currently in force; \'superseded\' indicates it has been replaced by an amendment; \'expired\' indicates the effective period has lapsed.']
  term_text varchar [note: 'Full verbatim legal text of the contract term as negotiated and executed. Contains the binding contractual language. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary reimbursement and legal obligations.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the contract term defining its legal or operational purpose. Drives downstream adjudication logic and compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reimbursement_rule|payment_methodology|exclusivity|termination_clause|audit_rights|hold_harmless|indemnification|confidentiality|hipaa_baa|quality_incentive — promote to reference product]']
  termination_cause_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the basis under which termination is permitted by this term: For Cause (material breach, fraud, license revocation), Without Cause (convenience), Mutual Agreement, or Regulatory (required by law or CMS/DOI directive).']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days of advance written notice required to terminate the contract under this termination clause term. Applicable when term_type is \'termination_clause\'. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days per regulatory requirements.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the contract term as it appears in the contract document (e.g., \'Multiple Procedure Reduction Policy\', \'Termination for Cause Clause\'). Supports search and display in contract management UIs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract term record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  withhold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of reimbursement withheld from provider payments pending quality performance or risk settlement under VBC or capitation arrangements. Classified confidential as proprietary financial terms.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific terms, conditions, obligations, and reimbursement policies within a provider contract. Captures term type (payment methodology, exclusivity, termination clause, audit rights, hold-harmless, indemnification, confidentiality, HIPAA BAA, reimbursement rule), term text, effective date, expiration date, and whether the term is negotiable or standard boilerplate. For reimbursement rule terms: captures policy sub-type (multiple procedure reduction, bilateral procedure, assistant surgeon, global surgery, unbundling rules, place-of-service differential), applicable CPT/HCPCS ranges, reduction percentage, and LOB applicability. Enables granular tracking of contractual obligations, compliance checkpoints, and reimbursement adjudication rules. Bridges contract terms to claims adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT.'
}

Table contract.amendment {
  amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each provider contract amendment record. Primary key for the amendment entity.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for Amendment Approval audit; ties each amendment to the employee who approved it, satisfying compliance tracking.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent provider contract that this amendment modifies. Links the amendment to its originating contract record.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule record activated or superseded by this amendment. Links the amendment to the specific rate table used for claims adjudication under the amended terms.']
  regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Required: Amendments are often triggered by regulatory changes; linking tracks the change that caused the amendment.']
  superseded_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a prior amendment record that this amendment replaces or supersedes. Enables traversal of the full amendment history chain for a given contract. Null if this is the first amendment or does not supersede a prior amendment.']
  vbc_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated Accountable Care Organization (ACO) agreement when this amendment relates to an ACO or Value-Based Care (VBC) arrangement. Nullable for non-ACO amendments.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable sequential identifier for the amendment (e.g., AMD-2024-001234-001). Used in correspondence, audit trails, and regulatory submissions. Unique within the parent contract.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the amendment indicating the nature of the modification. Drives downstream processing workflows and audit categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rate_change|term_modification|lob_addition|network_tier_change|scope_change|termination|capitation_change|bundled_payment_change|vbc_modification|addendum — promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the amendment through the negotiation and approval workflow. Tracks progression from draft through execution or withdrawal.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment received final internal approval from all required approvers. Distinct from execution date which reflects provider countersignature.']
  capitation_rate_pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'The Per Member Per Month (PMPM) capitation rate established or modified by this amendment for capitation-based payment arrangements. Applicable when payment_methodology is capitation. Confidential commercial negotiation data.']
  claims_reprocess_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether claims adjudicated under prior contract terms must be reprocessed following execution of this amendment. Triggers downstream claims reprocessing workflows in Facets/QNXT.']
