  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract payment model.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the financial summary record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the contract becomes legally binding.']
  financial_summary_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  ibnr_estimate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated incurred‑but‑not‑reported liability for the period.']
  incentive_earned_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary incentive earned by the provider based on performance metrics.']
  mrl_allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the contract spend allocated to the Medical Loss Ratio calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the financial summary.']
  payment_methodology varchar [note: 'Primary payment approach used for the contract.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite quality performance score used for incentive calculations.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date the reconciliation was completed.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the financial reconciliation process.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for when the row was initially loaded into the lakehouse.']
  risk_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of risk shared between the health plan and provider.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the financial data.']
  summary_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the reporting period covered by this summary.']
  summary_period_start date [note: 'First day of the reporting period covered by this summary.']
  summary_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the financial aggregation.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the contract ends or is scheduled to end; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  total_allowed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate allowed charge amount for services under the contract during the period.']
  total_paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount actually paid to the provider for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the financial summary record.']
  withhold_pool_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount retained pending quality or performance verification.']

  Note: 'Contract-level financial summary and accrual tracking record aggregating total contracted spend, paid amounts, outstanding liabilities, capitation accruals, withhold balances, incentive payouts, and reconciliation status for each provider contract by performance period. Captures summary period (month/quarter/year), total allowed amount, total paid amount, capitation accrual balance, withhold pool balance, incentive earned amount, IBNR estimate contribution, and reconciliation status. Supports finance department contract-level P&L reporting, IBNR estimation inputs, total-cost-of-care tracking for VBC contracts, medical loss ratio (MLR) allocation, and regulatory financial reporting. SSOT for contract-level financial position. Links to provider_contract as the parent entity.'
}

Table contract.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the contract_obligation association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the provider contract']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the contract for this obligation']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the contract became subject to the regulatory obligation']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the contract ceased to be subject to the regulatory obligation']

  Note: 'This association product records the compliance relationship between a provider contract and a regulatory obligation. It captures the compliance status and the effective/termination dates specific to each contract‑obligation pairing.. Existence Justification: Each provider contract must satisfy multiple regulatory obligations (e.g., HIPAA, ACA, state mandates) and each regulatory obligation applies to many provider contracts. The compliance status, effective and termination dates of the obligation for a specific contract are tracked, making the relationship a managed business entity.'
}

Table contract.party_regulatory_obligation_compliance {
  party_regulatory_obligation_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the PartyRegulatoryObligationCompliance association']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation record']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the party record']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status for this party‑obligation pair']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the compliance requirement became effective for this party']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the compliance requirement was terminated or satisfied for this party']

  Note: 'Represents the compliance relationship between a contract party and a regulatory obligation. Each record captures the compliance status and the effective/termination dates for that specific party‑obligation pairing.. Existence Justification: Each provider party must satisfy multiple regulatory obligations, and each regulatory obligation applies to many provider parties. The organization tracks compliance status, effective and termination dates for each party‑obligation pair, which is managed as a distinct record.'
}

Table appeal.appeal_grievance {
  appeal_grievance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the grievance record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: A grievance that is an appeal should reference the related appeal case for traceability.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or case manager assigned to the grievance.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_event. Business justification: Grievance often stems from an enrollment termination or change event; linking provides the Enrollment Event Detail report for compliance.']
  filing_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the party who filed the grievance (member_id, provider_id, etc.).']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employers may file grievances on behalf of employees; linking enables grievance tracking and compliance reporting per employer.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Needed for the \'Grievance Summary by Plan\' report used by plan administrators to monitor grievance volume and resolve plan‑specific issues per ACA requirements.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Grievance triage process uses the member\'s risk score to prioritize high‑risk cases and allocate resources efficiently.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_invoice. Business justification: Grievance filings often concern billing errors; linking grievance to the premium invoice provides the basis for investigation and compliance tracking.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Provider Grievance Management requires linking each grievance to the filing provider for reporting and compliance.']
  related_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with the grievance, if applicable.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date the grievance was acknowledged by the health plan.']
  appeal_grievance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grievance.']
  appeal_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated appeal, if the grievance is an appeal.']
  case_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by case handlers during the grievance lifecycle.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the grievance triggered a compliance review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the grievance record was created in the system.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Escalation tier if the grievance required higher-level review.']
  external_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of any external review process.']
  external_review_requested boolean [note: 'True if an external review (e.g., state fair hearing) was requested.']
  filing_channel varchar [note: 'Method through which the grievance was submitted.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the grievance was received.']
  filing_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance was filed by a member, provider, employer, or other party.']
  grievance_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the grievance.']
  grievance_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the grievance.']
  investigation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation concluded.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation of the grievance began.']
  is_appeal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance is also an appeal of a specific adverse benefit determination.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the grievance contains confidential information requiring restricted handling.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the grievance for handling.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category under which the grievance is reported.']
  related_encounter_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical encounter linked to the grievance, if applicable.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the grievance was resolved.']
  resolution_detail varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the resolution outcome.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Outcome type of the grievance resolution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system where the grievance was first recorded. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: facets|qnxt|cactus|provider_source|rxclaim|interqual|casenet|salesforce|healthedge — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the grievance originated.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief title summarizing the grievance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the grievance record.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal ZIP code of the member/provider address related to the grievance.']

  Note: 'Master record for all member and provider grievances — formal expressions of dissatisfaction with health plan decisions, service quality, or administrative actions that do not contest a specific adverse benefit determination. Distinct from appeals (which contest specific ABDs) per CMS and state DOI classification rules. Tracks grievance type (quality of care, access, billing, customer service, provider behavior), filing party, filing channel, acknowledgment date, investigation findings, resolution date, resolution type, regulatory classification (CMS CTM categories for Medicare, state DOI categories for commercial), and case notes. SSOT for grievance identity and lifecycle in the appeal domain. Supports ACA, Medicare Part C/D CTM, and state DOI grievance reporting requirements.'
}

Table appeal.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the appeal case record.']
  assigned_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Appeal case assignment process requires linking each case to the employee handling it for tracking case manager workload and regulatory reporting.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim that triggered the appeal.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each appeal case is subject to a regulatory obligation (e.g., timeliness); linking enables monitoring of compliance with that obligation.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Appeal adjudication must reference the governing provider contract to apply correct fee schedule, risk‑share and compliance rules per CMS regulations.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Required for employer‑level appeal reporting mandated by ACA regulations to track number of appeals per employer group.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Required for the \'Plan‑Level Appeal Performance Report\' which aggregates appeal outcomes by health plan for regulatory compliance; appeals are always filed under a specific member\'s health plan.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who filed or is subject to the appeal.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Appeal case review report requires the member\'s risk score to assess fraud likelihood and high‑cost patterns, a standard underwriting‑risk analysis step.']
  network_service_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.service_area. Business justification: Service‑area‑specific appeals are evaluated against the service area regulations; the appeal must reference the relevant service_area.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.party. Business justification: Identifying the contract party that filed the appeal enables party‑level appeal metrics and regulatory reporting required by state insurance oversight.']
  plan_election_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.plan_election. Business justification: Many appeals contest plan election outcomes; linking to the specific plan election record supports the Plan Election Appeal audit.']
  premium_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_invoice. Business justification: Required for the Invoice Appeal process; the appeal case must reference the specific premium invoice under dispute, enabling audit and resolution reporting.']
  premium_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.premium_payment. Business justification: Needed for Payment Appeal workflow; links the appeal case to the exact payment being contested, supporting regulatory reporting of disputed payments.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Provider Appeal Summary Report tracks which provider is the respondent for each appeal case.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network Adequacy Appeal Review report requires linking each appeal to the provider network under which the provider was contracted.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Required for the \'Credential Verification for Appeal\' report that checks provider credential status when a claim denial triggers an appeal.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Required for Enrollment Denial Appeal Report: ties each appeal case to the specific enrollment transaction that was denied, enabling audit of enrollment‑related appeals.']
  appeal_assigned_to varchar [note: 'Internal team or individual responsible for handling the appeal.']
  appeal_escalation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the appeal was escalated beyond the initial review level.']
  appeal_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the appeal by the plan.']
  appeal_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the appeal for processing.']
  appeal_review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from filing to final decision.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appeal.']
  appeal_type varchar [note: 'Category of appeal based on level and review pathway.']
  clinical_criteria_applied varchar [note: 'Specific clinical guidelines or criteria used in the decision (e.g., InterQual code).']
  completeness_determination varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal package was deemed complete at intake.']
  decision_author_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the clinician or committee authorizing the decision.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Explanation of the clinical and policy reasoning behind the decision.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the appeal decision was rendered.']
  decision_type varchar [note: 'Outcome of the appeal decision.']
  effective_benefit_change_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit change resulting from the decision becomes effective.']
  expedited_clinical_urgency_basis varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for expedited status (e.g., life‑threatening condition).']
  expedited_trigger boolean [note: 'True if the appeal qualified for expedited processing based on clinical urgency.']
  filing_channel varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the appeal.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'Technical method of filing (e.g., electronic upload, paper mail).']
  filing_party_type varchar [note: 'Entity that filed the appeal.']
  filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the appeal was received by the plan.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the appeal belongs.']
  member_notification_date date [note: 'Date the member was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  original_claim_number varchar [note: 'External claim number associated with the appealed claim.']
  overturn_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the original benefit determination was overturned, if applicable.']
  provider_notification_date date [note: 'Date the provider was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appeal case record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the appeal case record.']
  regulatory_tier varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the appeal rights.']
  related_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD diagnosis code linked to the appealed service.']
  related_drug_ndc varchar [note: 'National Drug Code of the medication involved, if a pharmacy appeal.']
  related_service_code varchar [note: 'CPT code of the service at issue in the appeal.']
  supporting_documentation_checklist varchar [note: 'List of required documents attached to the appeal (e.g., medical records, provider notes).']

  Note: 'Core master record for every formal appeal filed by a member, provider, or authorized representative contesting an adverse benefit determination (ABD). Captures appeal type (first-level internal, second-level internal, expedited, external independent review, state fair hearing), regulatory tier (ACA, ERISA, Medicare Part C/D, Medicaid/CHIP), line-of-business classification, and appeal level tracking for multi-level appeal processes. Includes filing details (channel, filing party type, receipt timestamp, filing method, completeness determination, expedited trigger, supporting documentation checklist), resolution details (decision type — upheld, overturned, partially overturned, withdrawn, dismissed, remanded — decision rationale, clinical criteria applied, decision author NPI or committee ID, decision date, effective date of benefit change, member/provider notification date), and overturn reason. Also tracks expedited appeal attributes (clinical urgency basis, 72-hour clock, treating provider attestation) when applicable. Primary SSOT for appeal identity, filing, resolution, and outcome in the appeal domain. Serves as the binding record of plan decision for regulatory reporting, HEDIS measurement, downstream claim reprocessing triggers, and benefit restoration workflows.'
}

Table appeal.adverse_determination {
  adverse_determination_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the adverse benefit determination record.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Adverse determinations are audited for compliance; linking each determination to its audit finding supports audit trail and corrective actions.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim that triggered the adverse determination.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom the adverse determination applies.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization associated with the determination, if applicable.']
  prior_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization request linked to the determination.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Adverse Determination Provider Report needs a direct FK to provider for audit and risk analysis.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  appeal_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is eligible to appeal the determination under applicable regulations.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the member filed an appeal.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal relative to the original adverse determination.']
  appeal_resolution_date date [note: 'Date the appeal decision was rendered.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal associated with the determination.']
  basis_of_denial varchar [note: 'High‑level category explaining the basis for the adverse determination.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical guideline (InterQual or MCG) applied in the decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the determination record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary fields.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the primary reason for denial (e.g., CMS‑DRG, internal code).']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why the claim or service was denied.']
  determination_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adverse determination was issued.']
  determination_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the determination (e.g., ABD‑2023‑000123).']
  determination_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the determination.']
  determination_type varchar [note: 'Category of the adverse action issued by the health plan.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Diagnosis code associated with the service.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the adverse determination becomes effective for the member.']
  monetary_amount_adjusted decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any monetary adjustment applied after the initial denial (e.g., partial payment).']
  monetary_amount_denied decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of benefit that was denied or reduced.']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Network relationship of the provider for the denied service.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the reviewer or adjudicator.']
  prior_authorization_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a prior authorization was required for the service.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the reviewer who issued the determination.']
  reviewer_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the clinician or reviewer who made the adverse determination.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Procedure code for the service that was denied.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the denied service was rendered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the determination record.']

  Note: 'Records every adverse benefit determination (ABD) issued by the health plan — the triggering event for member and provider appeal rights. Captures determination type (denial, reduction, termination, non-authorization), basis for denial (medical necessity, benefit exclusion, out-of-network, experimental), clinical criteria applied (InterQual/MCG guideline version), issuing reviewer NPI, determination date, effective date, and the specific claim, authorization, or enrollment action being denied. Links to the originating claim or prior authorization in the claim and utilization domains. Owned by the appeal domain as the formal adverse action record.'
}

Table appeal.review {
  review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each appeal review record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the parent appeal case that this review addresses.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Appeal reviewers must reference the provider\'s credential record to assess if credentialing caused the denial, per the \'Appeal Review Credential Check\' process.']
  appeal_case_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the appeal case linked to this review.']
  appeal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the reason for the original appeal.']
  appeal_status_at_review varchar [note: 'Status of the appeal case at the time the review was performed.']
  appeal_submission_date date [note: 'Date the appeal was originally submitted by the member or provider.']
  clinical_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the clinical reasoning behind the review decision.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Result of the NCQA/URAC compliance check for reviewer independence.']
  cpt_codes_reviewed varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of CPT procedure codes examined during the review.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical criteria set (e.g., InterQual v2023) applied during the review.']
  drg_code varchar [note: 'DRG code associated with the claim under review.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes spent on the review from start to completion.']
  icd_codes_reviewed varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ICD diagnosis codes evaluated in the review.']
  is_independent_reviewer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewer was independent of the original adverse determination (true/false).']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the review was conducted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments captured by the reviewer.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the review after clinical evaluation.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible sequential number assigned to the review for tracking and reference.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the review.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review was performed.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Level or tier of the review within the appeal process.']
  reviewer_npi varchar [note: 'Unique NPI of the clinical reviewer who performed the review.']
  reviewer_specialty varchar [note: 'Clinical specialty of the reviewer (e.g., cardiology, orthopedics).']
  reviewer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reviewer role for the review.']

  Note: 'Tracks each discrete review action performed on an appeal case — first-level clinical review, second-level peer review, medical director review, and committee review. Captures reviewer identity (NPI for clinical reviewers), reviewer specialty, review type, review date, review outcome, clinical rationale, criteria applied (ICD/CPT codes reviewed, DRG, InterQual/MCG criteria version), and whether the review was conducted by a reviewer not involved in the original adverse determination. Supports NCQA and URAC reviewer independence requirements.'
}

Table appeal.external_review {
  external_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the external review case.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the IRO to this review case.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the claim that triggered the external review.']
  iro_organization_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the external independent review organization handling the case.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who initiated the external review.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider associated with the claim under review.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: External review organizations need the provider\'s credential record to validate eligibility during independent appeal assessments.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any monetary adjustment (e.g., fee, discount) applied during the review.']
  appeal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the member’s reason for requesting external review.']
  appeal_reason_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the appeal reason.']
  binding_determination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRO decision is binding on the plan (true) or advisory (false).']
  claim_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original claim dollar amount submitted for the service.']
  compliance_action_taken varchar [note: 'Plan‑level corrective or administrative action taken to comply with the IRO decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the external review record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values (e.g., USD).']
  deadline_date date [note: 'Latest date by which the IRO must render a decision per regulatory rules.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the external review as determined by the IRO.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Calendar date the IRO rendered its decision.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the IRO for its decision.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD‑10 diagnosis code associated with the claim.']
  external_review_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the external review request.']
  external_review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the external review case.']
  external_review_type varchar [note: 'Category of service under review.']
  iro_accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the IRO per CMS/ACA requirements.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'True if the review was flagged as urgent due to clinical necessity.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status transition for the external review.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after adjustments, as determined by the IRO.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments captured by the plan staff.']
  overdue_flag boolean [note: 'True if the decision deadline has passed without a decision.']
  patient_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the patient to the member requesting review.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Standard CPT code for the procedure under review.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case has been reported to CMS or state DOI.']
  reporting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory report for this case.']
  reporting_submission_date date [note: 'Date the external review case was submitted to the regulatory reporting system.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the external review request was transmitted to the IRO.']
  review_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the review focus.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned reference number for the external review request.']
  review_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the external review case.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the underlying health service was provided.']
  state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the member resides or the service was provided.']
  transmission_date date [note: 'Calendar date the external review request was sent to the IRO.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the external review record.']

  Note: 'Manages Independent Medical Review (IMR) and External Independent Review Organization (IRO) requests — the federally mandated external review process under ACA §2719 and state external review laws. Tracks IRO assignment, IRO accreditation status, case transmission date, IRO decision date, IRO decision (uphold/overturn), IRO clinical rationale, binding determination flag, and plan compliance action taken. Distinct from internal appeal reviews. Supports CMS external review reporting and state DOI compliance.'
}

Table appeal.timeline {
  timeline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each appeal timeline record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the appeal case this timeline belongs to.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Timeline records need to identify which employer\'s HR team is responsible; required for SLA tracking reports.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the appeal.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Internal team or workgroup responsible for the appeal.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider involved in the appeal.']
  acknowledgment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the insurer must acknowledge receipt of the appeal.']
  actual_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date the insurer actually acknowledged the appeal.']
  actual_decision_date date [note: 'Date the final decision was rendered.']
  actual_expedited_date date [note: 'Date the expedited decision was actually completed.']
  actual_extension_date date [note: 'Date the granted extension became effective.']
  appeal_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the appeal type.']
  appeal_filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the appeal was officially submitted.']
  appeal_origin varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the appeal.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Operational state of the appeal.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'True when the appeal missed a regulatory deadline.']
  breach_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of why the SLA breach happened.']
  clock_start_event varchar [note: 'Narrative of the event that triggers the start of the regulatory clock (e.g., claim denial date).']
  clock_type varchar [note: 'The regulatory framework that defines the appeal\'s timing requirements.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal met its regulatory deadlines.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of remediation steps performed after a breach.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Count of calendar days the appeal missed its deadline.']
  decision_due_date date [note: 'Date by which a final decision on the appeal must be rendered.']
  expedited_due_date date [note: 'Accelerated deadline for decisions that qualify for expedited processing.']
  extension_notification_date date [note: 'Date the member/provider was notified of a deadline extension.']
  extension_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided for why a deadline extension was approved.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'US state code governing the appeal\'s regulatory requirements.']
  last_action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest activity on the appeal.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplemental comments or observations about the appeal timeline.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority for processing the appeal.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date and time the timeline record was initially loaded.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the timeline record.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version used to manage concurrent updates.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Agency or authority that defines the applicable appeal clock.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary reason why a deadline was missed.']
  self_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the organization voluntarily reported the SLA breach.']
  sla_actual_days varchar [note: 'Number of days actually taken to complete the required action.']
  sla_breach boolean [note: 'True when actual days exceed target days.']
  sla_target_days varchar [note: 'Number of days defined by the regulatory clock for the required action.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the timeline data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Facets|QNXT|Cactus|ProviderSource|RxClaim|Argus|InterQual|MCG|Casenet|TruCare|Altruista|Salesforce|Pega|HealthEdge|CustomBilling|OracleFinancials|Milliman — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the record in the originating source system.']

  Note: 'Tracks all regulatory and contractual deadline milestones for each appeal case — acknowledgment due date, decision due date, expedited decision due date, extension notification date, and actual completion dates. Captures regulatory clock type (ACA, ERISA, Medicare Part C/D, Medicaid, state DOI), clock start event, clock pause events, compliance status (on-time, late, extended), and SLA breach details (days overdue, root cause category, corrective action, self-report flag) when milestones are missed. Drives proactive SLA management, regulatory timeliness reporting to CMS and state DOIs, and breach tracking for quality improvement programs.'
}

Table appeal.appeal_document {
  appeal_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the appeal document.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the appeal case this document belongs to.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member associated with the appeal.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider associated with the document.']
  appeal_document_description varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text description or notes about the document.']
  appeal_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events for the document (e.g., access, redaction).']
  considered_in_decision boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the document was used in the final appeal decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was created.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the document, often used in tracking.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document content.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes.']
  format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored document.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates if the document is marked confidential beyond PHI.']
  is_redacted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document has been redacted to remove PHI.']
  last_accessed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent access to the document.']
  phi_classification varchar [note: 'Extent to which the document contains protected health information.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the document was received into the appeal system.']
  redaction_status varchar [note: 'Level of redaction applied to the document.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained per regulatory requirements.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the document, e.g., member upload, provider submission, system generated.']
  storage_path varchar [note: 'Secure storage location reference (e.g., S3 URI).']
  title varchar [note: 'Human readable title or description of the document.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the document if multiple revisions exist.']

  Note: 'Manages all documents associated with an appeal or grievance case — member-submitted evidence, medical records, EOB copies, denial letters, acknowledgment letters, decision letters, IRO submissions, hearing transcripts, and peer-to-peer review notes. Tracks document type, document source, receipt date, document format, storage reference (BLOB/S3 path), PHI classification, redaction status, retention period (7-10 years per CMS and state DOI requirements), and whether the document was considered in the review decision. Supports HIPAA PHI handling requirements, regulatory record retention obligations, and litigation hold compliance.'
}

Table appeal.appeal_communication {
  appeal_communication_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each appeal communication record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Communications are part of an appeal case workflow; linking them to the case enables chronological tracking.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Communications (e.g., status updates) are often sent to the employer; FK supports employer notification logs required for audit.']
  primary_appeal_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the record.']
  accessibility_accommodation varchar [note: 'Any accommodation provided for accessibility (e.g., large print, braille, interpreter).']
  appeal_communication_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the communication (e.g., pending, sent, delivered, failed, returned, closed).']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of attachments included with the communication.']
  attachment_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the communication includes one or more attachments.']
  body_text varchar [note: 'Full textual content of the communication.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Medium used to deliver the communication.']
  communication_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the communication purpose.']
  communication_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the communication for tracking and reference.']
  communication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the communication event occurred (sent or received).']
  communication_type varchar [note: 'Category of the communication (e.g., acknowledgment, extension notice, decision letter, notice of rights, information request, response).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the communication meets all applicable compliance requirements.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes regarding compliance considerations for the communication.']
  created_by_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user who created the record.']
  delivery_confirmation_date date [note: 'Date on which delivery confirmation was received.']
  delivery_confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'True if delivery of the communication was confirmed by the recipient.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the communication was sent to the party (outbound) or received from the party (inbound).']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date the communication was escalated.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the communication has been escalated for higher‑level review.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the communication contains confidential or PHI content.']
  is_physical_mail boolean [note: 'True if the communication was delivered via physical mail.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language code of the communication content.']
  last_modified_by_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user who last modified the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form internal notes related to the communication.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party involved: member, provider, or other.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the communication is marked as high priority.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the communication.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the communication was received (applicable for inbound messages).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the communication record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the communication record.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework that mandates the communication (e.g., ACA, ERISA, state DOI, CMS, HIPAA).']
  regulatory_notice_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the communication satisfies a specific regulatory notice requirement.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which a response is required from the party.']
  response_received_date date [note: 'Date the response was received.']
  response_received_flag boolean [note: 'True if a response to the communication has been received.']
  returned_mail_date date [note: 'Date the mail was returned to sender.']
  returned_mail_flag boolean [note: 'True if the mailed communication was returned as undeliverable.']
  sent_date date [note: 'Date the communication was sent (applicable for outbound messages).']
  subject varchar [note: 'Subject or title of the communication.']

  Note: 'Records all outbound and inbound communications related to appeal and grievance cases — acknowledgment letters, extension notices, decision letters (NOA/NOAD), Notice of Appeal Rights, member rights notices, requests for additional information, and member/provider responses. Tracks communication type, communication channel (mail, phone, secure portal, fax), sent/received date, recipient type, language, accessibility accommodation (large print, Braille, interpreter), regulatory notice compliance flag, regulatory basis, delivery confirmation, and returned mail tracking. Supports ACA §2719, ERISA §503, and state DOI member notice requirements.'
}

Table appeal.coverage_dispute {
  coverage_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the coverage dispute record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Coverage disputes arise within the context of a specific appeal case; linking provides direct access to case details.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim that triggered the dispute.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Coverage disputes may trigger regulatory reporting obligations; linking captures the governing obligation for each dispute.']
  disputing_party_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the party that initiated the dispute.']
  enrollment_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Eligibility disputes reference the exact eligibility span; needed for the Eligibility Dispute Analysis report required by CMS.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group (if dispute is on behalf of an employer).']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the dispute is being processed.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member involved in the dispute.']
  prior_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization associated with the disputed service.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider involved in the dispute.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Coverage disputes often arise from credentialing issues; linking to credential_record enables the \'Dispute Credential Impact\' analysis.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Last date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date the appeal was submitted.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the appeal process.']
  cob_order varchar [note: 'Ordered list of payer responsibilities for the claim (e.g., primary|secondary|tertiary).']
  cob_rule_applied varchar [note: 'COB rule used to determine payer order for the dispute.']
  coordination_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount determined by the coordination of benefits calculation.']
  coverage_dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary fields.']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the issue being disputed.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispute for tracking and communication.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Category of the coverage dispute indicating the underlying issue.']
  disputed_benefit_code varchar [note: 'Code of the benefit, service, or procedure that is the subject of the dispute.']
  disputing_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute was raised by a member, provider, employer group, or other entity.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the dispute became effective (typically the date of filing).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the dispute was closed or otherwise terminated; null if still open.']
  formulary_exception_flag boolean [note: 'True if the dispute relates to a formulary exception request.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute is flagged as high priority due to potential impact.']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the network classification of the service at issue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  other_carrier_code varchar [note: 'System identifier of the secondary carrier.']
  other_carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the secondary carrier involved in a COB dispute.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority for handling the dispute.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the dispute handling.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the dispute was resolved.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the dispute after final adjudication.']
  subrogation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount involved in a subrogation claim.']
  subrogation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the dispute involves a subrogation scenario.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Count of documents attached to the dispute for evidence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dispute record.']

  Note: 'Master record for coverage disputes — formal disagreements between members, providers, or employer groups and the health plan regarding benefit interpretation, plan exclusions, coordination of benefits (COB) determinations, or subrogation decisions. Captures dispute type (benefit interpretation, COB, subrogation, network status, formulary exception), disputing party, disputed benefit or service, plan position, and resolution outcome. For COB disputes, additionally captures other carrier information, COB rule applied (birthday rule, NAIC COB model), disputed payer order, coordination amount, and inter-plan communication records. Supports ERISA plan document interpretation, NAIC COB model law compliance, and state DOI market conduct requirements.'
}

Table appeal.appeal_regulatory_filing {
  appeal_regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the appeal regulatory filing record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory filing of appeal data must reference the specific regulatory obligation it satisfies, required for compliance reporting.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Regulatory filings must include employer identifier to satisfy state/federal reporting of appeal outcomes.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who initiated the appeal.']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider associated with the appeal (if applicable).']
  acknowledgment_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulator\'s acknowledgment was received.']
  acknowledgment_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an acknowledgment of receipt was received from the regulator.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Plan outlining steps to remediate deficiencies noted by the regulator.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filing record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the filing amount.']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Text description of the issues identified by the regulator.']
  deficiency_notice boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the regulator issued a deficiency notice for the filing.']
  filing_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount associated with the filing (e.g., filing fee).']
  filing_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filing was initially submitted to the regulator.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to submit the filing (e.g., EDI transaction, web portal upload).']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'External filing number assigned by the regulatory body for tracking the appeal submission.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the filing within the regulatory workflow.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory filing (e.g., appeal, grievance, external review, state hearing).']
  filing_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the filing document (e.g., v1, v2).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the filing.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'The governing agency or organization to which the filing is submitted.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The calendar or fiscal period the filing covers (e.g., 2023Q1).']
  resubmission_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the resubmitted filing, if applicable.']
  resubmission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a resubmission of the filing is required.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the filing was delivered to the regulator.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the filing record.']

  Note: 'Tracks mandatory regulatory filings and submissions related to appeals and grievances — CMS quarterly appeals data submissions (HPMS), CMS Medicare Part C/D organization determination and appeal reports, state DOI annual grievance and appeal reports, NCQA HEDIS appeals measures data extracts, URAC accreditation appeals documentation packages, and ACA external review annual reports to HHS. Captures filing type, regulatory body, reporting period, submission date, submission method (CMS HPMS, state portal, EDI, manual upload), filing status, acknowledgment or acceptance confirmation, deficiency notices, corrective action plans, and resubmission tracking. Distinct from the compliance domain\'s general regulatory audit tracking — this is appeal-domain-specific regulatory reporting.'
}

Table appeal.iro_organization {
  iro_organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Independent Review Organization.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: REQUIRED: IROs are external vendors; linking enables compliance reports and performance metrics for each IRO contract.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Accrediting organization for the IRO.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the IRO\'s accreditation expires.']
  accreditation_review_notes varchar [note: 'Notes from the latest accreditation review.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the IRO.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the IRO\'s mailing address, if applicable.']
  approved_states varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of state codes where the IRO is approved.']
  assignment_rotation_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to rotate case assignments among IROs (e.g., round-robin, random).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance requirements applicable to the IRO (e.g., ACA §2719).']
  conflict_of_interest_attestation_date date [note: 'Date when the IRO provided a conflict-of-interest attestation.']
  contract_effective_end_date date [note: 'End date of the contract with the IRO.']
  contract_effective_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the contract with the IRO.']
  country varchar [note: 'Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3) of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IRO record was first created in the system.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the IRO.']
  external_review_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of reviews performed by the IRO (e.g., medical, pharmacy, dental).']
  iro_organization_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the Independent Review Organization.']
  iro_organization_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the IRO.']
  is_conflict_of_interest boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a conflict of interest was identified (True/False).']
  is_state_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates if the IRO is currently approved in all required states (True/False).']
  last_accreditation_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent accreditation review.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the IRO record.']
  organization_type varchar [note: 'Category of the IRO based on its role and accreditation.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the IRO.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  rotation_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months in each rotation cycle.']
  specialty_capabilities varchar [note: 'List of medical specialties the IRO is qualified to review.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the IRO\'s mailing address.']
  state_approval_status varchar [note: 'Overall state-level approval status for the IRO.']

  Note: 'Master reference for Independent Review Organizations (IROs) and external review entities contracted or approved to conduct external independent reviews. Captures IRO name, URAC/NCQA accreditation status, accreditation expiration date, state approval status (by state), specialty capabilities, conflict-of-interest attestation date, contract effective dates, and assignment rotation methodology. Required for ACA §2719 external review compliance and state external review law adherence. Distinct from the provider and contract domains — IROs are specialized regulatory entities, not clinical providers.'
}

Table appeal.expedited_review {
  expedited_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each expedited review request.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Expedited reviews are a specialized review type for an appeal case; the existing appeal_reference_number duplicates the case identifier and can be removed.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health claim associated with the expedited review request.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member requesting the expedited review.']
  parent_expedited_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on expedited_review (parent_expedited_review_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Expedited Review tracking requires linking the reviewed provider to the review record for SLA monitoring.']
  actual_decision_date date [note: 'Date the final decision for the expedited review was entered into the system.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustments (e.g., discounts, penalties) applied as a result of the expedited review.']
  claim_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount billed on the original claim.']
  clinical_urgency_classification varchar [note: 'Categorization of the clinical urgency that drives the expedited decision deadline.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the review triggered any compliance or regulatory follow‑up.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the expedited review record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency identifier (e.g., USD).']
  decision_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory or contractual deadline for completing the expedited review.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the expedited review (e.g., claim upheld, denied, partially approved).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Clinical and policy rationale supporting the final decision.']
  expedited_notification_required boolean [note: 'True if the member must receive a notification within the expedited timeline.']
  expedited_reason varchar [note: 'Business‑defined reason that qualifies the request for an expedited timeline.']
  expedited_review_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the processing stage of the expedited review request.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'True if the expedited review record includes protected health information.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after applying adjustments from the expedited review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the expedited review.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member was notified of the decision.']
  notified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the notification was sent to the member.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing agency (e.g., CMS or state authority) that mandates the expedited review.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the member or provider submitted the expedited review request.']
  review_request_number varchar [note: 'External reference number used in communications and reporting for the expedited review.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system (e.g., Utilization Management System) that created the record.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'US state code relevant to the expedited review jurisdiction.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents (e.g., medical records) submitted with the expedited request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the expedited review record was most recently modified.']

  Note: 'Tracks expedited appeal review requests where standard timeframes could jeopardize the members life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. Captures expedited request reason, clinical urgency classification, shortened decision timeline, and expedited notification requirements per CMS and state regulations.'
}

Table appeal.outcome {
  outcome_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the appeal outcome record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original appeal case to which this outcome belongs.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim that was the subject of the appeal.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Outcome reporting per employer is required for financial impact analysis and employer‑specific compliance dashboards.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the appeal.']
  parent_outcome_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on outcome (parent_outcome_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider associated with the appeal.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal outcome complies with applicable regulations and internal policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appeal outcome record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|CAD|GBP|JPY|AUD — promote to reference product]']
  downstream_action varchar [note: 'Action triggered downstream as a result of the appeal outcome.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount restored to the member as a result of the appeal outcome.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the appeal was adjudicated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CA|NY|TX|FL|IL|PA — promote to reference product]']
  member_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the member was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the reviewer or system regarding the outcome.']
  outcome_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the appeal outcome for tracking and reporting.']
  outcome_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appeal outcome record.']
  outcome_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appeal outcome was rendered.']
  outcome_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the final disposition of the appeal.']
  provider_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the provider was notified of the appeal outcome.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for the appeal outcome.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason behind the appeal outcome.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority overseeing the appeal outcome (e.g., CMS, state insurance department).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the appeal outcome record.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the record in the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the appeal outcome record.']

  Note: 'Records the final disposition and outcome of an appeal case after all levels of review are exhausted — including overturn, uphold, partial overturn, and withdrawn outcomes. Captures outcome date, outcome type, financial impact (claim amount restored), member notification date, and downstream action triggers (claim reprocessing, authorization issuance).'
}

Table appeal.penalty {
  penalty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each appeal penalty record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Penalty assessments arise from violations of specific regulatory obligations; linking ensures traceability for audit and reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plan entity that incurred the penalty.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Member whose claim or appeal triggered the penalty, when relevant.']
  parent_penalty_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on penalty (parent_penalty_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Provider linked to the penalty event, when relevant.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value assessed before any adjustments.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Last date the plan or member may contest the penalty.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date the appeal against the penalty was received.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'True if an appeal was submitted, otherwise false.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal after review.']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the penalty became effective.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Path or identifier for detailed audit records related to the penalty.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the underlying process was in compliance when the penalty was issued.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the currency of the penalty.']
  due_date date [note: 'Deadline for payment to avoid additional sanctions.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the penalty\'s enforceability.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date after which the penalty is no longer applicable.']
  interest_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional monetary charge accrued on the penalty.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or contextual information.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the penalty amount was settled.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the penalty has been paid or remains outstanding.']
  penalty_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the penalty purpose.']
  penalty_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned identifier used in regulatory filings and internal tracking.']
  penalty_status varchar [note: 'Workflow state of the penalty record.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the penalty based on its source or nature.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Compact code used by the organization to categorize penalty causes.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why the penalty was assessed.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the row was initially loaded.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Government or oversight entity that issued the penalty.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Legal or regulatory provision that the penalty addresses.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Risk or impact rating assigned to the penalty.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Primary key of the penalty record in the source system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the penalty data.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount payable after interest and any reductions.']

  Note: 'Tracks regulatory penalties, sanctions, and financial consequences imposed on the health plan for non-compliance with appeal processing requirements — including CMS Civil Money Penalties (CMPs), state DOI fines, and auto-approval penalties for missed turnaround times.'
}

Table appeal.evidence {
  evidence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the appeal evidence record.']
  parent_evidence_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on evidence (parent_evidence_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Evidence Source Attribution Report links evidence submitted by a provider to the provider entity.']
  related_appeal_appeal_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the appeal to which this evidence is attached.']
  related_authorization_pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization related to the evidence.']
  related_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with the evidence, if applicable.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the author of the evidence.']
  author_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the author of the evidence, if applicable.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA-256) for file integrity verification.']
  clinical_code_icd10 varchar [note: 'Associated ICD-10 diagnosis code(s) referenced in the evidence.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the evidence contains confidential information requiring restricted handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the evidence record was created in the system.']
  drg_code varchar [note: 'Diagnosis Related Group code associated with the evidence.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the evidence becomes effective for the appeal review.']
  evidence_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the evidence content and purpose.']
  evidence_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the evidence by the source system.']
  evidence_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the evidence.']
  evidence_type varchar [note: 'Category of the evidence submitted for the appeal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical_record|clinical_guideline|peer_reviewed_literature|expert_opinion|lab_result|imaging|authorization|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the evidence is no longer considered valid.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the digital evidence document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|docx|txt|jpg|png|tiff|xml|json — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the evidence file in bytes.']
  guideline_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical guideline cited in the evidence.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'National Drug Code if the evidence pertains to a medication.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the evidence.']
  phi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the evidence includes protected health information.']
  procedure_code_cpt varchar [note: 'Associated CPT procedure code(s) referenced in the evidence.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the evidence was received by the processing system.']
  related_encounter_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical encounter linked to the evidence.']
  relevance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score indicating the relevance of the evidence to the appeal (0-100).']
  source_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity that provided the evidence.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the evidence.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original record in the source system.']
  storage_path varchar [note: 'Logical storage location or URI where the evidence file is stored.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the evidence was submitted to the appeal process.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief title describing the evidence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the evidence record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the evidence document, incremented on updates.']

  Note: 'Master record for clinical and non-clinical evidence submitted or gathered during the appeal review process — medical records, clinical guidelines cited, peer-reviewed literature, and expert opinions. Distinct from appeal_document (which tracks document logistics) by focusing on the evidentiary substance and its weight in the review decision.'
}

Table risk.member_risk_score {
  member_risk_score_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the member risk score record.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member to whom the risk score applies.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Member risk scores are calculated within the context of a risk pool.']
  audit_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who performed the most recent audit action.']
  cms_published_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'RAF score value as published by CMS after reconciliation.']
  cms_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the score\'s submission to CMS.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken to resolve score variance (e.g., code addendum, data correction).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk score record was first created in the data lake.']
  demographic_factor_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Score component derived from member demographics (age, gender, Medicaid status).']
  diagnosis_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct diagnoses captured for the member in the scoring period.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the risk score became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the risk score is no longer valid for payment.']
  hcc_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of HCC codes contributing to the score.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the score was manually overridden.']
  manual_override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for a manual override of the risk score.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Name of the risk adjustment model (e.g., "CMS‑HCC", "RxHCC", "HHS‑HCC").']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the actuarial model used to generate the score (e.g., "CMS‑HCC v2023").']
  payment_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the risk score is used in CMS payment calculations.']
  plan_calculated_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'RAF score calculated internally by the health plan prior to CMS submission.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the risk score record.']
  resubmission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for any resubmitted score file to CMS.']
  risk_adjustment_factor_category varchar [note: 'Category of the risk adjustment factor (e.g., "RAF", "HCC", "RxHCC").']
  risk_score_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the risk score record, often composed of member ID, year, and model version.']
  risk_score_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑100) assigned by the model to the score.']
  risk_score_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label describing the risk score record (e.g., "2023 Medicare Advantage RAF").']
  risk_score_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any additional commentary or observations.']
  risk_score_source varchar [note: 'Data source used to generate the score.']
  risk_score_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the risk score.']
  risk_score_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the score is projected (prospective), derived from current data (concurrent), or the final CMS‑published value.']
  risk_score_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric RAF score calculated for the member (e.g., 1.2345).']
  score_components varchar [note: 'Delimited list or JSON string of component scores (e.g., HCC contributions, demographic adjustments).']
  score_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk score becomes effective for payment calculations.']
  score_variance decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between the plan‑calculated score and the CMS‑published score.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the risk score (e.g., "Milliman MG‑ALFA").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk score record.']
  variance_category varchar [note: 'Root‑cause classification for any variance between plan and CMS scores.']

  Note: 'Authoritative member-level risk score record and reconciliation tracker. Captures RAF (Risk Adjustment Factor) scores, HCC (Hierarchical Condition Category) mappings, CMS reconciliation outcomes, and plan-vs-CMS variance analysis. Stores prospective and concurrent RAF scores derived from actuarial models and encounter data, including score effective dates, payment year, model version (CMS-HCC, RxHCC, HHS-HCC, PACE), score components, CMS submission status, plan-calculated vs. CMS-published RAF variance, root cause category (missing diagnoses, HCC hierarchy differences, demographic factors), corrective actions, and resubmission references. Core SSOT for member-level risk in Medicare Advantage, ACA, and Medicaid risk adjustment programs. Subsumes RAF reconciliation tracking.'
}

Table risk.hcc_mapping {
  hcc_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hcc_mapping']
