  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: HCC mappings are applied per risk pool to reflect pool‑specific model versions.']
  age_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier adjusting the HCC coefficient based on member age.']
  coefficient decimal(10,6) [note: 'Weighting coefficient used in RAF score calculations for the HCC.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the mapping record was first loaded into the lakehouse.']
  demographic_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor adjusting the HCC weight for demographic characteristics (age, gender, etc.).']
  disease_interaction_group varchar [note: 'Identifier for a group of diagnoses that have interaction effects.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this mapping becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this mapping expires or is superseded; null if still active.']
  gender_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier adjusting the HCC coefficient based on member gender.']
  hcc_code varchar [note: 'CMS-assigned HCC identifier linked to the diagnosis.']
  hcc_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the HCC.']
  hcc_mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mapping record.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the HCC within the hierarchical structure (0 = top level).']
  icd10_code varchar [note: 'Standard ICD-10 diagnosis code associated with the mapping.']
  icd10_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the ICD-10 diagnosis.']
  interaction_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the diagnosis interacts with other HCCs (yes) or not (no).']
  is_excluded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the diagnosis is excluded from the current model year.']
  is_mapped boolean [note: 'True if the ICD‑10 code has a valid HCC mapping in this version.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the mapping was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the last update.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the mapping record.']
  mapping_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the mapping data.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the CMS HCC model version to which the mapping applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or audit notes regarding the mapping.']
  parent_hcc_code varchar [note: 'Code of the immediate parent HCC in the hierarchy, if applicable.']
  plan_type_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Adjustment factor for the HCC based on the member\'s health plan type (e.g., HMO, PPO).']
  region_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Adjustment factor reflecting geographic cost variations.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent review process.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Weight applied to the HCC when aggregating RAF scores.']
  source_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the source CMS model (e.g., V24, V28).']

  Note: 'Maps ICD-10 diagnosis codes to Hierarchical Condition Categories (HCCs) per CMS model version. Stores the ICD-to-HCC crosswalk, HCC hierarchy relationships (parent/child HCC hierarchies), coefficient values by model year, disease interaction flags, and demographic adjustment factors. Supports RAF score calculation, RAPS/EDPS submission validation, and RADV audit defense. Reference entity managed per CMS annual model updates (V24, V28 transition). Source: CMS annual HCC model release files.'
}

Table risk.raps_submission {
  raps_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each RAPS submission batch record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who initiated the RAPS submission.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health plan (contract) associated with the RAPS submission.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: RAPS submissions are filed for a specific risk pool\'s members.']
  accepted_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that CMS accepted without error in this submission.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'External batch identifier assigned by the source system for the RAPS submission.']
  cms_acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Acknowledgment status returned by CMS for the submitted batch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the submission record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  error_disposition varchar [note: 'Textual description of any errors or rejections returned by CMS for this batch.']
  payment_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the risk adjustment payment is being calculated.']
  plan_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract number of the plan submitted to CMS for risk adjustment.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of health plan associated with the submission (e.g., HMO, PPO).']
  raps_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the RAPS submission within the CMS workflow.']
  rejected_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that CMS rejected or flagged with errors in this submission.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Aggregated HCC-derived factor used to compute the plan\'s risk adjustment payment.']
  risk_adjustment_year varchar [note: 'Year for which the risk adjustment factors are being reported.']
  risk_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) score calculated for the plan in this submission.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the RAPS submission record.']
  submission_file_checksum varchar [note: 'SHA-256 checksum of the transmitted file to ensure data integrity.']
  submission_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the flat file or payload transmitted to CMS for this batch.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RAPS batch was transmitted to the CMS gateway.']
  submission_user_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the user who submitted the batch.']
  total_record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual member risk adjustment records included in the batch.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the submission record.']

  Note: 'Tracks Risk Adjustment Processing System (RAPS) submissions to CMS for Medicare Advantage risk adjustment. Captures submission batch ID, submission date, plan contract number, payment year, record counts, accepted/rejected record counts, CMS acknowledgment status, and error disposition. Each record represents a RAPS batch transaction submitted via the CMS RAPS gateway. Source: Milliman MG-ALFA and EDI gateway.'
}

Table risk.risk_cms_submission {
  risk_cms_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cms_submission']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: CMS risk‑adjustment submissions must be linked to the governing regulatory obligation; reporting dashboards require this association.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that initiated the submission.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan associated with the risk adjustment data.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: CMS risk adjustment submissions are associated with a risk pool.']
  accepted_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that CMS accepted without error.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary adjustments (e.g., discounts, penalties) applied to the claim total.']
  cms_acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'CMS\'s acknowledgment of the submission batch.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract or Provider Benefit Package (PBP) associated with the submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the submission record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary fields.']
  encounter_type varchar [note: 'Category of the clinical encounter data included in the batch.']
  error_disposition varchar [note: 'Textual description of any errors returned by CMS for the batch.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net dollar amount after adjustments, representing the amount CMS will reimburse.']
  payment_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which CMS payment is being calculated.']
  record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual risk adjustment records submitted in the batch.']
  rejected_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that CMS rejected due to validation errors.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Aggregated Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) factor for the batch.']
  risk_adjustment_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) score calculated for the batch.']
  service_year varchar [note: 'Year of the services/encounters represented in the submission.']
  submission_batch_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the batch of records submitted to CMS.']
  submission_checksum varchar [note: 'MD5 checksum of the submission file for integrity verification.']
  submission_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes entered at submission time.']
  submission_file_name varchar [note: 'File name of the electronic submission payload.']
  submission_file_size varchar [note: 'Size of the submission file in bytes.']
  submission_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the submission (e.g., Milliman MG‑ALFA).']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CMS submission batch.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the batch was transmitted to CMS.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was submitted via the Risk Adjustment Processing System (RAPS) or Encounter Data Processing System (EDPS).']
  submission_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the submitter (e.g., actuarial_analyst, system_process).']
  total_claim_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount of all claims included in the submission.']
  transition_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch complies with the RAPS‑to‑EDPS transition requirements (true = compliant).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the submission record.']

  Note: 'Tracks CMS risk adjustment data submissions for Medicare Advantage via both RAPS (Risk Adjustment Processing System) and EDPS (Encounter Data Processing System) channels. Captures submission batch ID, submission type (RAPS/EDPS), submission date, contract/PBP number, payment year, service year, encounter type, record counts, accepted/rejected counts, CMS acknowledgment status, error disposition, and RAPS-to-EDPS transition compliance flags. Each record represents a batch transaction submitted to CMS for risk adjustment processing. Supports RADV audit readiness and CMS reconciliation. Note: This product should be renamed to cms_submission after the RAPS+EDPS merge.'
}

Table risk.risk_underwriting_case {
  risk_underwriting_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the underwriting case record.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Underwriting cases are subject to audit engagements; audit reports reference the specific case audited.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: Underwriting cases are processed per delegated credentialing entity; linking supports reporting of underwriting outcomes by delegated entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underwriter who made the decision.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Underwriting case must reference the employer group to calculate premiums and satisfy ACA/ERISA reporting requirements.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the applicant (member or group) for which the underwriting case is created.']
  provider_credential_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Network underwriting requires the provider\'s credential record to verify eligibility; linking enables underwriting reports that reference credential status.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Underwriting cases are evaluated for a particular risk pool.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Underwriting cases may be outsourced to external underwriting vendors; linking enables vendor performance tracking and compliance reporting.']
  age_gender_distribution_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Statistical index representing the age and gender mix of the applicant population.']
  applicant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the applicant as individual, small group, large group, etc.']
  chronic_condition_prevalence_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate of chronic conditions among the applicant population.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the underwriting case record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for premium amounts.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the underwriting decision.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the underwriting decision was rendered.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the underwriting decision becomes effective for coverage.']
  expected_claims_pmpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Projected claims cost per member per month for the upcoming period.']
  gross_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total premium before adjustments.']
  industry_risk_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Adjustment factor based on the applicant\'s industry risk profile.']
  is_self_insured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant is self‑insured (ASO) or fully‑insured.']
  large_claimant_count varchar [note: 'Number of members with high-cost claims in the group.']
  lob varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., medical, dental, vision) to which the underwriting case applies.']
  medical_underwriting_status varchar [note: 'Result of medical underwriting review.']
  morbidity_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor reflecting expected morbidity based on health status and demographics.']
  net_premium_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final premium after adjustments.']
  overall_group_risk_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Composite risk score derived from all risk factors for the group.']
  premium_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of discounts, fees, or other adjustments applied to the gross premium.']
  premium_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base premium rate applied to the applicant.']
  premium_rate_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the premium (fixed amount, per member, per employee).']
  prior_year_claims_pmpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Claims cost per member per month from the prior year.']
  rate_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the premium rate becomes effective.']
  rate_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the premium rate expires (null for open‑ended).']
  rating_period varchar [note: 'Period (e.g., FY2024) used for rating calculations.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of required regulatory filings (e.g., CMS, NAIC).']
  reinsurance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case includes reinsurance or stop‑loss pricing.']
  review_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the underwriting case must be reviewed or decided.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the applicant.']
  risk_underwriting_case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the underwriting case.']
  stop_loss_arrangement varchar [note: 'Code or description of any stop‑loss coverage attached to the case.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the underwriting case was submitted to regulators.']
  total_member_months varchar [note: 'Aggregate member-months for the group applicant during the rating period.']
  underwriting_case_number varchar [note: 'External case number assigned to the underwriting decision, used for tracking and communication.']
  underwriting_rule_set varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule set applied during underwriting.']
  underwriting_tier varchar [note: 'Tier level used in underwriting risk segmentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the underwriting case record.']

  Note: 'Represents an underwriting decision record for group or individual market applicants, including the group-level risk profile used in underwriting evaluation and renewal rating. Captures underwriting case number, applicant type (group/individual/small group/large group), LOB, underwriting tier, risk classification, medical underwriting status, final decision (approved/declined/rated), effective date, underwriter ID, decision rationale, and applicable underwriting rules/criteria. For group cases, includes aggregate risk profile: rating period, total member months, age/gender distribution index, morbidity factor, industry risk factor, prior year claims PMPM, expected claims PMPM, large claimant count, chronic condition prevalence rates, and overall group risk score. Supports fully-insured and self-insured (ASO) underwriting workflows, group renewal underwriting, and stop-loss pricing.'
}

Table risk.rate_development {
  rate_development_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each rate development record.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Rate development factors are defined per risk pool; linking to pool enables aggregation and eliminates duplicated pool attributes.']
  administrative_loading decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage added to cover administrative expenses.']
  age_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Multiplicative factor based on member age.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate was formally approved for use.']
  base_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fundamental rate before any rating factors are applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate development record was first created.']
  credibility_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Weighting factor blending experience data with credibility.']
  development_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the rate development for tracking and regulatory filing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the approved rate becomes effective for premium calculations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the rate ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended rates).']
  final_approved_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Rate after all factors, loadings, and regulatory adjustments are applied.']
  gender_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Multiplicative factor based on member gender.']
  geographic_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Factor reflecting cost differences across rating areas.']
  group_size_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Factor based on the size of the employer group.']
  industry_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Factor derived from the employer\'s industry classification.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the rate applies (e.g., health, dental).']
  mlr_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Regulatory MLR target percentage for the product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale entered by actuaries.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of insurance plan for which the rate is calculated.']
  plan_type_loading decimal(6,4) [note: 'Additional loading applied for specific plan designs.']
  profit_margin decimal(6,4) [note: 'Target profit margin applied to the rate.']
  rate_development_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate development record.']
  rate_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the rate (e.g., community rating, experience rating, blended).']
  rating_area varchar [note: 'Geographic region used in rating calculations.']
  rating_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the rating period for which the rate is being developed.']
  rating_period_start date [note: 'First day of the rating period for which the rate is being developed.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the state or federal filing associated with this rate.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the rate record (e.g., Milliman MG‑ALFA).']
  tobacco_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Factor applied for tobacco use status.']
  trend_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Factor used to project future cost trends.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rate development record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the rate development record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Captures actuarial rate development records and their constituent rating factors for premium rate-setting across plan types, market segments, and rating areas. Stores rate development ID, rating period, LOB, plan type, geographic rating area, base rate, and all applicable rating factors (age/gender, geographic area, tobacco use, group size, industry, plan-type loading). Includes trend factor application, credibility-weighted experience blending, administrative loading, profit margin, MLR target, final approved rate, and regulatory filing reference. Supports ACA community rating and experience rating methodologies. Source: Milliman MG-ALFA rate-setting models. Links to state DOI rate filings.'
}

Table risk.ibnr_reserve {
  ibnr_reserve_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ibnr_reserve']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: IBNR reserve estimates are calculated per risk pool; linking provides context and allows roll‑up.']
  actuarial_confidence_level decimal(3,2) [note: 'Confidence level (e.g., 0.95) associated with the interval.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the actuarial confidence interval for the IBNR estimate.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the actuarial confidence interval for the IBNR estimate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reserve record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score indicating quality of input data used for reserve.']
  development_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor applied to projected losses to estimate ultimate cost.']
  expected_loss_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Projected loss ratio used in reserve calculation.']
  external_reserve_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external actuarial systems to reference this reserve.']
  forecast_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Number of months ahead the reserve projection covers.']
  hcc_weighted_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'IBNR amount weighted by Hierarchical Condition Category risk scores.']
  ibnr_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated Incurred But Not Reported reserve amount in USD.']
  ibnr_pmpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'IBNR reserve expressed on a per member per month basis.']
  ibnr_reserve_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reserve estimate.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the line of business for which the reserve is calculated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or rationale for the reserve estimate.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of health insurance plan associated with the reserve.']
  raps_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the reserve is included in RAPS submission to CMS.']
  rbc_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Impact of the reserve on Risk Based Capital calculations.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the reserve is required for regulatory reporting (e.g., GAAP, RBC).']
  reserve_adequacy_flag varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the reserve is adequate.']
  reserve_methodology varchar [note: 'Actuarial method used to calculate the IBNR reserve.']
  reserve_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the reserve estimate.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor applied to adjust reserve for risk considerations.']
  service_month date [note: 'Month of service for which the IBNR reserve is estimated (first day of month).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the reserve estimate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reserve record.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date on which the reserve estimate was calculated.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the reserve estimate for the same valuation date.']

  Note: 'Stores Incurred But Not Reported (IBNR) reserve estimates by LOB, plan type, service month, and valuation date. Captures reserve methodology (chain-ladder, Bornhuetter-Ferguson, Cape Cod), development factors, expected loss ratios, IBNR amount, IBNR PMPM, actuarial confidence interval, and reserve adequacy assessment. Generated from Milliman MG-ALFA reserving models. Critical for financial close, SAP/GAAP reporting, and RBC calculations.'
}

Table risk.reinsurance_arrangement {
  reinsurance_arrangement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reinsurance arrangement record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the reinsurer in the master party table.']
  aggregate_attachment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold for aggregate stop‑loss coverage.']
  aggregate_attachment_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of aggregate losses that must be reached before the reinsurer pays.']
  arrangement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the insurer or reinsurer to identify the treaty.']
  attachment_point decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss amount threshold at which the reinsurer begins to pay.']
  attachment_point_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the attachment point amount.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of losses the reinsurer pays after the attachment point.']
  corridor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage range defining the corridor where losses are shared between insurer and reinsurer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reinsurance arrangement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the reinsurance coverage becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the reinsurance coverage terminates; null for open‑ended treaties.']
  lob_coverage varchar [note: 'Business line(s) to which the reinsurance treaty applies.']
  maximum_liability decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper limit of the reinsurer\'s liability under the treaty.']
  maximum_recovery_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cap on the total amount the insurer can recover from the reinsurer.']
  premium_ceded decimal(18,2) [note: 'Premium amount transferred to the reinsurer for the treaty.']
  reinsurance_arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reinsurance treaty.']
  reinsurer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the reinsurer party to the arrangement.']
  specific_deductible decimal(18,2) [note: 'Deductible applied to each individual claim under a specific stop‑loss treaty.']
  stop_loss_deductible decimal(18,2) [note: 'Deductible applied before stop‑loss coverage becomes payable.']
  stop_loss_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the reinsurer will pay under the stop‑loss arrangement.']
  stop_loss_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss amount that triggers stop‑loss coverage.']
  stop_loss_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the stop‑loss is specific per‑member or aggregate across a group.']
  treaty_terms varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the treaty\'s contractual terms and conditions.']
  treaty_type varchar [note: 'Type of reinsurance treaty governing the risk transfer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reinsurance arrangement record.']

  Note: 'Master record for reinsurance and stop-loss arrangements protecting the health plan against catastrophic or excess losses. Captures treaty type (quota share, excess of loss, aggregate stop-loss, specific stop-loss), reinsurer name, attachment point, specific deductible amount, aggregate attachment factor and amount, corridor percentage, maximum liability, coinsurance percentage, maximum recovery limit, premium ceded, effective and expiration dates, LOB coverage, and treaty terms. Includes stop-loss threshold parameters for self-insured employer groups. Supports both individual specific stop-loss and aggregate stop-loss for self-insured groups.'
}

Table risk.reinsurance_claim {
  reinsurance_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reinsurance claim record.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the claimant (member or group) for whom the reinsurance claim is filed.']
  reinsurance_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.reinsurance_arrangement. Business justification: Reinsurance claims belong to a specific reinsurance arrangement; replace free‑text reference with FK.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reinsurer acknowledged receipt of the claim.']
  attachment_point_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Attachment point amount applied to the loss before reinsurance coverage kicks in.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'External claim number assigned by the reinsurer.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the reinsurance claim.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Type of reinsurance claim: stop‑loss (aggregate) or specific loss.']
  claimant_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the claimant is an individual member or an employer group.']
  covered_end_date date [note: 'End date of the coverage period for which the loss occurred.']
  covered_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the coverage period for which the loss occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  is_aggregated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this claim aggregates multiple underlying losses (true) or represents a single loss (false).']
  loss_category varchar [note: 'Category of loss underlying the claim.']
  loss_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the loss event leading to the reinsurance claim.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by claims adjusters.']
  payment_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when payment was received from the reinsurer.']
  recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount recoverable from the reinsurer after applying attachment point and limits.']
  recovery_status varchar [note: 'Status of the recovery payment from the reinsurer.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the claim must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., CMS).']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'Financial reporting period associated with the claim.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reinsurance claim was submitted to the reinsurer.']
  total_incurred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total incurred loss amount before applying reinsurance terms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of reinsurance recoveries and stop-loss claims submitted to reinsurers. Captures reinsurance claim number, arrangement reference, claimant (member or group), covered period, total incurred amount, attachment point applied, recoverable amount, submission date, reinsurer acknowledgment, payment received date, and recovery status. Tracks both specific and aggregate stop-loss recovery transactions.'
}

Table risk.rbc_calculation {
  rbc_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each RBC calculation record.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: RBC calculations are performed per risk pool; FK creates proper hierarchy.']
  action_threshold_status varchar [note: 'Indicator of which regulatory threshold category the calculated RBC falls into.']
  authorized_control_level_rbc decimal(15,2) [note: 'RBC amount threshold that triggers authorized control actions by regulators.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the RBC calculation (standard NAIC formula or custom adaptation).']
  calculation_number varchar [note: 'External business reference number assigned to the RBC calculation for reporting and audit purposes.']
  calculation_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the regulatory reporting period covered by this RBC calculation.']
  calculation_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the regulatory reporting period covered by this RBC calculation.']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the RBC calculation was performed.']
  company_action_level_rbc decimal(15,2) [note: 'RBC amount threshold at which the company must take corrective action.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insurance company for which the RBC calculation is performed.']
  covariance_adjustment decimal(15,2) [note: 'Adjustment applied to account for covariance among risk components.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RBC calculation record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  h0_asset_risk decimal(15,2) [note: 'Risk charge component for asset risk (H0) as defined by NAIC.']
  h1_underwriting_risk decimal(15,2) [note: 'Risk charge component for underwriting/insurance risk (H1).']
  h2_credit_risk decimal(15,2) [note: 'Risk charge component for credit risk (H2).']
  h3_business_risk decimal(15,2) [note: 'Risk charge component for business risk (H3).']
  h4_admin_expense_risk decimal(15,2) [note: 'Risk charge component for administrative expense risk (H4).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any additional comments or explanations related to the calculation.']
  rbc_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of total risk charges to total adjusted capital, expressed as a decimal.']
  rbc_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status resulting from the RBC calculation indicating required action level.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the RBC calculation data.']
  total_adjusted_capital decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate adjusted capital amount used as the denominator in the RBC ratio.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the RBC calculation record.']

  Note: 'Risk-Based Capital (RBC) calculation records per NAIC RBC formula for health organizations (HRBC). Captures calculation period, company action level RBC, authorized control level RBC, total adjusted capital (TAC), RBC ratio, component risk charges (H0 asset risk, H1 insurance/underwriting risk, H2 credit risk, H3 business risk, H4 administrative expense risk), covariance adjustment, and regulatory action threshold status (no action, company action, regulatory action, authorized control, mandatory control). Required for annual state DOI regulatory reporting and NAIC financial statement filing.'
}

Table risk.adjustment_payment {
  adjustment_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for adjustment_payment']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Adjustment payments must be approved by a finance employee; approval audit requires linking to employee.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan or contract associated with the payment.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose risk adjustment is being recorded.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Adjustment payments are associated with the pool that generated the risk score; linking enables pool‑level reporting.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of any adjustments, fees, or offsets applied to the gross payment.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why an adjustment was made (e.g., coding error, audit correction).']
  adjustment_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the adjustment reason.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp of the business event that generated the payment record.']
  cms_notification_date date [note: 'Date CMS notified the payer of the risk adjustment amount.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment (e.g., USD).']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments or fees.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the payment record.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments; the amount actually transferred or charged.']
  payment_effective_date date [note: 'Date the payment becomes effective for accounting and reporting.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to transfer the payment.']
  payment_source varchar [note: 'Entity that originated the payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payment.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record represents a receipt (inflow) or a charge (outflow).']
  payment_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the risk adjustment payment applies.']
  processing_date date [note: 'Date the payment was processed in the internal system.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'CMS program under which the payment is made: ACA risk adjustment, Medicare Advantage, reinsurance, or cost sharing reduction.']
  reconciliation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the payment has been reconciled with accounting records.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk adjustment score (e.g., RAF) used to calculate the payment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the payment record.']

  Note: 'Records CMS risk adjustment transfer payments and charges for ACA marketplace and Medicare Advantage programs. Captures payment year, program type (ACA RA, MA risk adjustment, reinsurance, CSR), plan/contract identifier, risk score used, transfer payment amount (receipt or charge), CMS notification date, payment effective date, and reconciliation status. Tracks both interim and final risk adjustment settlements.'
}

Table risk.prospective_risk_model {
  prospective_risk_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each prospective risk model configuration record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit or individual responsible for the model.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Prospective risk model runs are scoped to a specific risk pool.']
  cms_model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the CMS model specification that the risk model implements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the model configuration becomes effective for scoring.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the model configuration is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  input_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the source data window used for the model run.']
  input_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the source data window used for the model run.']
  is_production boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the model is approved for production use.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of this model configuration.']
  model_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of the model (e.g., actuarial, underwriting, risk‑adjustment).']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or catalog code assigned to the model for reference in downstream systems.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk model (e.g., "CMS HCC 2024 Model").']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the model configuration.']
  normalization_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Scalar applied to model outputs to align with enterprise scoring conventions.']
  population_segment varchar [note: 'Target member segment for which the model is run.']
  prospective_risk_model_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the model purpose, assumptions, and scope.']
  risk_model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the actuarial model methodology.']
  risk_score_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the score range produced by the model.']
  risk_score_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the score range produced by the model.']
  run_date date [note: 'Date on which the model execution was initiated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the model configuration (e.g., Milliman MG‑ALFA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the model record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version string of the model configuration (e.g., "v1.3.0").']

  Note: 'Stores prospective risk model configurations and run parameters used to generate member-level risk scores. Captures model name, model version, CMS model year, risk model type (CMS-HCC, RxHCC, HHS-HCC, CDPS), population segment (Medicare, Medicaid, Commercial), run date, input data period, normalization factor, and model status. Manages the actuarial model catalog used in Milliman MG-ALFA for risk score production runs.'
}

Table risk.score_run {
  score_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for score_run']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the actuarial professional who approved the run.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Score runs are executed for a specific risk pool; linking enables traceability.']
  actuarial_signoff_date date [note: 'Date the actuarial sign‑off was recorded.']
  average_raf_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Mean RAF score across the member population for the run.']
  cms_model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the CMS model specification applied in the run.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the run record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the overall data quality for the run.']
  data_snapshot_date date [note: 'Date of the data extract snapshot used for scoring.']
  input_data_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the data period used as input for the model.']
  input_data_period_start date [note: 'First day of the data period used as input for the model.']
  member_population_count varchar [note: 'Number of members included in the scoring run.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the actuarial risk model (e.g., CMS-HCC Model).']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk model used in the run.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the risk model used for the run.']
  normalization_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor applied to adjust raw scores to a common scale.']
  population_segment varchar [note: 'Member segment targeted by the run.']
  risk_model_type varchar [note: 'Category of the risk model applied.']
  run_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the run, often used in reporting and audit logs.']
  run_date date [note: 'Date on which the run was initiated.']
  run_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the run.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the run.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the run is a production, test, or reconciliation execution.']
  score_distribution_summary varchar [note: 'JSON-formatted summary of score buckets and frequencies.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that generated the run data (e.g., Milliman MG-ALFA).']
  total_raf_score decimal(18,4) [note: 'Sum of all Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) scores calculated in the run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the run record.']

  Note: 'Represents a risk score model configuration and its production/test execution runs against the member population. Captures model name, model version, CMS model year, risk model type (CMS-HCC, RxHCC, HHS-HCC, CDPS), population segment (Medicare, Medicaid, Commercial), normalization factor, model status, run type (production/test/reconciliation), run date, input data period, data snapshot date, member population count, total and average RAF scores, score distribution summary, run status, and actuarial sign-off. Manages the actuarial model catalog and provides the complete audit trail from model configuration through execution for each risk score production cycle. Source: Milliman MG-ALFA.'
}

Table risk.radv_audit {
  radv_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each RADV audit record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the analyst who created the audit record.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose records were sampled for the audit.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: RADV audits are performed on members within a risk pool; linking provides pool context.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal filed against CMS audit findings.']
  audit_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit reached final settlement.']
  audit_error_description varchar [note: 'Text description of the error condition when audit_error_flag is true.']
  audit_error_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any processing error was detected for this audit record.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by auditors during the audit process.']
  audit_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the audit request – either CMS‑initiated or internally initiated.']
  audit_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit process was initiated.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RADV audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is an initial RADV audit or a follow‑up audit.']
  audit_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the analyst who last updated the audit record.']
  audit_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the RADV audit was performed.']
  cms_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings and observations reported by CMS for this audit.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Medicare Advantage contract subject to the RADV audit.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  extrapolated_payment_error decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary error in payments derived from audit findings, before final settlement.']
  final_settlement_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount finally paid or recovered after audit resolution.']
  hcc_mapping_version varchar [note: 'Version of the HCC mapping algorithm used for validation.']
  medical_record_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the requested medical records were received.']
  medical_record_request_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the request for medical records from providers.']
  record_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first captured in the data warehouse.']
  record_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this audit record.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'RAF score applied to the member cohort for this audit.']
  sampled_member_count varchar [note: 'Number of members selected for review in this audit.']
  validated_hcc_count varchar [note: 'Number of Hierarchical Condition Categories confirmed as accurate after audit.']

  Note: 'Manages CMS Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) audit records for Medicare Advantage contracts. Captures audit year, contract number, sampled member list, medical record request status, medical record receipt date, CMS audit findings, validated HCC count, extrapolated payment error, appeal status, and final settlement amount. Tracks the full RADV audit lifecycle from CMS notification through final payment reconciliation.'
}

Table risk.pool {
  pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pool']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each risk pool must comply with defined regulatory obligations (e.g., ACA, state regulations); compliance reporting tracks this link.']
  reinsurance_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reinsurance or stop‑loss arrangement linked to the pool.']
  aca_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pool meets ACA 45 CFR 156.80 single‑risk‑pool requirements.']
  average_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean risk score (e.g., RAF) for members in the pool.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the risk‑pool record was first created in the source system.']
  data_source_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the source record in the originating system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk pool becomes effective.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the geographic coverage of the pool.']
  is_excluded_from_mlr boolean [note: 'True if the pool is excluded from Medical Loss Ratio calculations.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or actuarial review of the pool.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the pool belongs.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market that the pool serves.']
  member_months varchar [note: 'Cumulative member‑months contributed to the pool during its life.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or annotations about the pool.']
  pmpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average cost per member per month for the pool.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the risk pool in external systems.']
  pool_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the risk pool.']
  pool_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk pool.']
  pool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pool based on composition and market segment.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the pool (e.g., ACA, CMS, State law).']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor applied for risk‑adjustment calculations (e.g., RAF multiplier).']
  risk_pool_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the pool definition, incremented on each change.']
  risk_score_stddev decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical dispersion of risk scores within the pool.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the pool data (e.g., MG‑ALFA).']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state abbreviation for pools limited to a single state.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk pool is terminated or expires (nullable).']
  total_incurred_claims decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount of claims incurred by members in the pool.']
  total_paid_claims decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount of claims paid out from the pool.']
  total_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reserve dollars set aside for future claim payments within the pool.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the risk‑pool record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or process that created the risk‑pool record.']

  Note: 'Defines risk pools used for rate development, experience rating, MLR calculation, and ACA risk adjustment transfer calculations. Captures pool name, pool type (single risk pool, merged market, small group, large group, individual), LOB, market segment, geographic scope, effective date, member months, total incurred claims, pool PMPM, and regulatory basis. Tracks ACA single risk pool compliance per 45 CFR 156.80. Supports state-level market reform pool configurations.'
}

Table risk.actuarial_assumption_set {
  actuarial_assumption_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each actuarial assumption set.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Actuarial assumption sets are often defined per risk pool for rate development.']
  actuarial_assumption_set_code varchar [note: 'External code used in reporting and model documentation to reference the assumption set.']
  actuarial_assumption_set_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose or focus of the assumption set.']
  actuarial_assumption_set_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assumption set.']
  actuarial_assumption_set_type varchar [note: 'Category of assumptions contained in the set.']
  actuarial_signoff_date date [note: 'Date the senior actuary signed off on the assumptions.']
  actuarial_signoff_name varchar [note: 'Name of the senior actuary who approved the assumption set.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the assumption set received final governance approval.']
  compound_trend_factor decimal(12,6) [note: 'Combined factor that incorporates medical cost, utilization, and pharmacy trends.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  credibility_weight decimal(12,6) [note: 'Weight applied to experience data when blending with industry benchmarks.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the underlying data used to derive the assumptions (e.g., Milliman MG‑ALFA, CMS, internal claims data).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the assumptions become effective for actuarial calculations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the assumptions cease to be valid; null for open‑ended sets.']
  is_peer_reviewed boolean [note: 'True when the peer‑review process has been completed and approved.']
  is_regulatory_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the assumption set meets all applicable CMS, NAIC, and HHS regulatory requirements.']
  lapse_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Projected proportion of policies that will lapse each year.']
  loss_development_factor_age_to_age decimal(12,6) [note: 'Factor used to project losses from one age band to the next.']
  loss_development_factor_cumulative decimal(12,6) [note: 'Factor that aggregates loss development across all development periods.']
  methodology_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the actuarial methods and statistical techniques applied.']
  mortality_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual mortality assumption used for life‑related reserves.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the assumption set.']
  peer_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the peer‑review process for the assumption set.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Business reason for creating the set (e.g., rate development, IBNR reserving, RBC calculation).']
  trend_rate_medical_cost decimal(12,6) [note: 'Annual percentage change applied to projected medical costs.']
  trend_rate_pharmacy decimal(12,6) [note: 'Annual percentage change applied to pharmacy spend forecasts.']
  trend_rate_utilization decimal(12,6) [note: 'Annual percentage change applied to service utilization volumes.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number incremented on each change to the set.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the record.']

  Note: 'Versioned, governed set of all actuarial assumptions and factors used across risk domain calculations. Includes trend rates (medical cost, utilization, unit cost, pharmacy) by service category and LOB, loss development factors (age-to-age, cumulative, tail, selected) by development period, credibility weights and factors (Bühlmann, limited fluctuation) by group size threshold, lapse rates, mortality assumptions, and compound trend factors. Captures assumption set name, version, effective date, purpose, data sources, factor selection methodology, actuarial sign-off, peer review status, and approval date. Provides governance, audit trail, and single source of truth for all actuarial model inputs used in Milliman MG-ALFA for rate development, IBNR reserving, RBC calculations, and financial projections.'
}

Table finance.ledger {
  ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ledger record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Ledger entries for risk adjustment factors must be posted against the specific risk pool to produce accurate financial statements.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the GL account.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the GL account.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account for financial reporting.']
  balance_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance.']
  budget_owner varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or group responsible for the account\'s budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ledger record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for monetary amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code for the department within the organization.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Code for the division within the organization.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the account becomes effective.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the account ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  financial_reporting_category varchar [note: 'Category used for external financial reporting and regulatory filings.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the fund segment of the account.']
  gaap_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'True if the account is part of a GAAP ledger.']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the account is included in the budgeting process.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the ledger entry belongs to a consolidated reporting set.']
  ledger_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context for the account.']
  ledger_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the legal entity owning the account.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Identifier for the line of business to which the account belongs.']
  posting_allowed boolean [note: 'True if postings are permitted to this account.']
  segment1 varchar [note: 'First custom segment value for the account (e.g., business unit).']
  segment2 varchar [note: 'Second custom segment value for the account.']
  segment3 varchar [note: 'Third custom segment value for the account.']
  segment4 varchar [note: 'Fourth custom segment value for the account.']
  segment5 varchar [note: 'Fifth custom segment value for the account.']
  statutory_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'True if the account is part of a statutory (SAP) ledger.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ledger record.']

  Note: 'General ledger (GL) master in Oracle Financials representing the chart of accounts, ledger structure, organizational cost center hierarchy, and segment value definitions. Defines account codes, account types (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), natural account segments, cost center segments (department codes, division codes, LOB segments, legal entity assignments, budget owner relationships), fund segments, and GAAP vs SAP statutory ledger designations. Cost center hierarchy supports PMPM cost allocation, MLR segmentation, and statutory expense reporting with drill-down from enterprise to department level. Each ledger instance corresponds to a legal entity or reporting purpose (primary, secondary, statutory). Serves as the authoritative financial accounting backbone and organizational structure master for all journal postings, trial balance generation, period-end close, cost center-based financial accountability, and regulatory financial reporting to state DOIs and NAIC.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {

Business justification: cost_center has legal_entity_code BIGINT and legal_entity_name STRING — both denormalized from legal_entity. Adding legal_entity_id FK normalizes this. legal_entity has both legal_entity_code and lega']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who owns the cost center for budgeting purposes.']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy.']
  primary_cost_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for managing the cost center.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate expenses to the cost center.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage used when allocation_method is \'percentage\'.']

  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the cost center, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount (e.g., USD).']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the purpose of the cost center.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in financial systems and reporting.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the cost center.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center (e.g., department, division, line of business, legal entity, project).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level for the cost center data.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the department that owns the cost center.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the division that owns the cost center.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective for reporting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center was retired or closed (nullable).']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., ERP, HR) to reference the cost center.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'GL account number associated with the cost center for posting transactions.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Logical grouping name for reporting clusters of cost centers.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy (1 = top level).']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the cost center has an assigned budget; false otherwise.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center is used for consolidated reporting across multiple entities.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent budget or structure review for the cost center.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the cost center belongs.']

  pmpm_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost center is used in PMPM cost allocation.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cost center must be reported in statutory filings.']
  reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used in financial reporting packages to reference the cost center.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the cost center budget and structure are reviewed.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor applied to the cost center for risk‑adjusted financial calculations.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center became active in the organization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost center record.']

  Note: 'Organizational cost center hierarchy master managed in Oracle Financials. Defines the financial reporting structure — department codes, division codes, LOB (Line of Business) segments, legal entity assignments, and budget owner relationships. Used for PMPM cost allocation, MLR (Medical Loss Ratio) segmentation, and statutory expense reporting. Supports drill-down from enterprise to department level for financial accountability.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the journal entry.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the journal entry.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Accounting creates journal entries for each appeal case to record adjustments; required for GAAP compliance and audit trail.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center to which the entry is charged.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Journal entries that record employer‑related transactions need the employer_group_id for accurate financial reporting and GAAP compliance.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: journal_entry has legal_entity_code BIGINT representing the legal entity posting the journal entry. Adding legal_entity_id FK normalizes this to the legal_entity master. legal_entity has legal_entity_']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department responsible for the transaction.']
  outcome_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_outcome. Business justification: Journal entries must reference the specific appeal outcome to capture the exact financial impact for reporting and reconciliation.']
  primary_journal_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who prepared the journal entry.']
  reversal_of_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Self‑reference to the journal entry that this entry reverses, if applicable.']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Journal entries for reserve adjustments, reinsurance recoveries, etc., need the associated risk pool for audit trails and reporting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Vendor expense audit report requires linking each journal entry to the originating vendor for expense verification and regulatory compliance (e.g., CMS vendor cost tracking).']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier (e.g., 2023-09) for the financial posting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the entry.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted amount associated with the entry, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the business event that generated the journal entry (e.g., posting time).']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entry (e.g., accrual, reversal, manual adjustment).']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when converting foreign currency to reporting currency.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) of the fiscal period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the journal entry.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the fund or pool of resources associated with the entry.']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry is part of the approved budget.']
  is_consolidation_elimination boolean [note: 'True if the entry is used to eliminate intercompany balances in consolidated statements.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry is an intercompany transaction.']
  is_statistical boolean [note: 'True if the entry is for statistical reporting only (no financial impact).']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or nature of the entry.']
  journal_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the journal entry, often used in reporting and audit trails.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the journal entry pertains (e.g., medical, dental).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount (debits minus credits) representing the entry\'s impact.']
  originating_entity_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity that originated the intercompany transaction.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the entry has been posted to the ledger.']
  receiving_entity_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity that receives the intercompany transaction.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External document number (e.g., invoice, claim) linked to the entry.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module within the source system that generated the entry (e.g., GL).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system (e.g., Oracle Financials).']
  statistical_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount recorded for statistical entries when applicable.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate credit amount for the journal entry.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate debit amount for the journal entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the journal entry record.']

  Note: 'Core GL journal entry in Oracle Financials capturing all financial postings — accruals, reversals, manual adjustments, intercompany eliminations, consolidation entries, and system-generated entries from sub-ledgers (AP, AR, FA). Includes header-level attributes (journal source, category, accounting period, currency, exchange rate, GAAP vs SAP basis, preparer, approver, posting status) and line-level detail (account code combination with natural account + cost center + LOB + fund segments, debit/credit amounts, entered and accounted amounts, statistical amounts, reconciliation references). Intercompany entries carry originating/receiving legal entity identifiers and elimination status for consolidated financial statement preparation. Supports all journal categories including intercompany management fees, cost allocations, intercompany loans, dividends, and consolidation eliminations. Foundation for period-end close, trial balance, statutory financial statements, and all regulatory filings to NAIC and state DOIs.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each journal entry line.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry header to which this line belongs.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL account (or account segment combination) associated with this line.']
  accounted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount posted to the ledger after adjustments, taxes, and currency conversion.']
  additional_segment varchar [note: 'Optional fifth segment for extended chart of accounts (e.g., project or grant code).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cost center to which the transaction is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the line amount is a debit or a credit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the transaction becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  entered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original amount entered for the line before any adjustments.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate applied when the transaction currency differs from the reporting currency.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Fund or financing source code used for the transaction.']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the line amount is part of a budgeted allocation.']
  is_statistical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a statistical (non‑monetary) entry.']
  journal_entry_line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the transaction line purpose or narrative.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line or product line code (e.g., medical, dental, vision).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the journal entry.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the line purpose (e.g., regular transaction, tax, fee).']
  natural_account varchar [note: 'Primary chart of accounts segment representing the account type (e.g., revenue, expense).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the line was posted to the general ledger.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'External reference used for sub‑ledger or bank reconciliation.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component within the source system (e.g., GL Posting, Billing).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source application or system that generated the line (e.g., Oracle Financials, HealthEdge).']
  statistical_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount used for statistical reporting (non‑financial) such as volume or headcount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the journal entry line record.']

  Note: 'Individual debit or credit line within a GL journal entry. Captures account code combination (natural account + cost center + LOB + fund), entered amount, accounted amount, currency, statistical amount, description, and reconciliation reference. Enables granular account-level analysis, sub-ledger reconciliation, and audit trail for GAAP and SAP statutory reporting. Each line maps to a specific ledger account segment combination.'
}

  service_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the service period the invoice covers.']

  vendor_npi varchar [note: 'NPI assigned to provider‑type vendors.']
  vendor_tax_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., EIN) for the vendor.']
  void_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice has been voided.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable (AP) sub-ledger in Oracle Financials representing the full invoice-to-payment lifecycle for all financial payees — healthcare providers receiving capitation or fee-for-service payments, DME suppliers, TPA administrators, PBM partners, reinsurers, IT vendors, and professional service firms. Captures invoice attributes (invoice number, vendor/supplier ID, invoice date, due date, payment terms, amount, tax, currency, approval status, hold reason) and payment/disbursement attributes (payment method — check/EFT/wire, payment date, bank account, payment amount, void status, cleared date). Covers provider capitation payment runs, provider incentive settlements, VBC shared savings distributions, and routine vendor payments. Includes vendor/payee reference fields (vendor name, type, tax ID, NPI for provider-vendors, payment terms, 1099 flag). Feeds the GL upon validation and posting, and reconciles against bank statements. Single source of truth for the complete AP sub-ledger lifecycle from invoice receipt through disbursement and bank clearing.'
}
