  Note: 'Accounts receivable (AR) sub-ledger in Oracle Financials representing the full invoice-to-receipt lifecycle for all premium and fee receivables. Captures invoice attributes (invoice number, customer/group ID, invoice date, due date, premium period, amount, tax, currency, collection status) and receipt/cash application attributes (receipt date, receipt method — ACH/wire/check/lockbox, receipt amount, applied invoice references, unapplied amount, bank deposit batch). Covers premium receivables from employer groups, member premium billings, ASO administrative fee receivables, and government program receivables (CMS Medicare Advantage, Medicaid managed care). Supports cash application, grace period tracking, premium revenue reconciliation, and feeds the GL for revenue recognition. Single source of truth for the complete AR sub-ledger lifecycle from invoice generation through cash receipt and application.'
}

Table finance.ar_receipt {
  ar_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the AR receipt record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that processed the receipt.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (member, employer, government agency) that made the payment.']
  reversal_receipt_ar_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receipt that reverses this transaction, if applicable.']
  ach_trace_number varchar [note: 'Trace number for ACH transactions.']
  applied_invoice_ids varchar [note: 'Delimited list of invoice identifiers to which the receipt has been applied.']
  ar_receipt_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the receipt.']
  ar_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receipt.']
  bank_deposit_batch varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bank deposit batch containing this receipt.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that included this receipt.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Check number associated with the receipt, when method is check.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receipt record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the receipt currency.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'Date the receipt was deposited into the bank.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the receipt.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied when receipt currency differs from functional currency.']
  grace_period_end_date date [note: 'Date after which late fees may be assessed if receipt remains unapplied.']
  is_foreign_currency boolean [note: 'True if receipt was received in a currency other than the functional currency.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates if the receipt is locked for further changes (e.g., after posting).']
  is_reversed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the receipt has been reversed.']
  lockbox_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lockbox where the check was received, if applicable.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax and discount, posted to the account.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the paying party.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Operational channel through which the payment was processed.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the payer (e.g., check number, ACH trace).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payment.']
  receipt_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross amount received before any adjustments.']
  receipt_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cash receipt was actually received.']
  receipt_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the payment.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the receipt by the billing system.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module within the source system that produced the receipt.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the receipt record (e.g., HealthEdge, Oracle Financials).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component included in the receipt.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the receipt.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the receipt not yet applied to an invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the receipt record.']
  wire_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for wire transfer receipts.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable cash receipt transaction in Oracle Financials recording premium payments received from employer groups, individual members, CMS, and state Medicaid agencies. Captures receipt date, receipt method (ACH, wire, check, lockbox), receipt amount, currency, applied invoice references, unapplied amount, and bank deposit batch. Supports cash application, grace period tracking, and premium revenue reconciliation against billing system records.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each fixed asset record.']

  accumulated_depreciation decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized to date for the asset.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original cost incurred to acquire the asset, before any depreciation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was purchased or otherwise capitalized.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the asset (e.g., "IT Infrastructure", "Office Equipment").']

  asset_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number for the asset.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the asset within the organization.']
  asset_subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the asset category (e.g., "Server", "Desktop Computer").']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'External tag or code used to identify the asset on the shop floor or in inventory.']
  assigned_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the asset\'s use and upkeep.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense recognized for the most recent period.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation expense is scheduled to end (typically end of useful life).']
  depreciation_expense_account varchar [note: 'GL account used for recording periodic depreciation expense.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation (e.g., straight‑line, declining balance).']
  depreciation_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which depreciation expense is recognized.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation expense began for the asset.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was removed from service or sold.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount received from the disposal of the asset.']
  disposal_status varchar [note: 'Current disposal state of the asset.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the asset record is considered active for reporting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date after which the asset record is no longer active (null if still active).']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account to which the asset\'s cost and depreciation are posted.']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the insurance coverage for the asset ends.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number covering the asset against loss or damage.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the most recent maintenance activity was performed.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the physical location or site where the asset resides.']
  maintenance_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost incurred for the most recent maintenance activity.']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Standard maintenance cadence (e.g., "Quarterly", "Annual").']
  net_book_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current carrying amount of the asset (cost less accumulated depreciation).']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the purchase order that sourced the asset.']
  salvage_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the asset record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated number of years the asset is expected to provide economic benefit.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the vendor warranty for the asset expires.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset (FA) master and depreciation history in Oracle Financials representing capitalized assets owned by the health plan — IT infrastructure, office equipment, leasehold improvements, software licenses, and medical management systems. Captures asset-level attributes (asset number, category, acquisition date/cost, depreciation method, useful life, salvage value, location, assigned cost center, disposal status, disposal date, disposal proceeds) and periodic depreciation records (depreciation period, depreciation method — straight-line/declining balance, depreciation amount, accumulated depreciation, net book value, GL account distribution). Supports GAAP and SAP statutory balance sheet reporting, monthly close depreciation expense recognition, RBC (Risk-Based Capital) asset valuation calculations, and asset lifecycle management from acquisition through disposal.'
}

Table finance.depreciation_transaction {
  depreciation_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the depreciation transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: depreciation_transaction has denormalized cost_center_code STRING. Adding cost_center_id FK normalizes the depreciation posting to its cost center. cost_center.cost_center_code available via JOIN. Pop']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fixed asset to which this depreciation applies.']

Business justification: depreciation_transaction has legal_entity_code BIGINT representing the legal entity owning the depreciated asset. Adding legal_entity_id FK normalizes this to the legal_entity master. Populated at dep']
  reversal_of_transaction_depreciation_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original depreciation transaction that is being reversed.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the depreciation transaction record was first created.']

  depreciation_basis varchar [note: 'Underlying basis used for depreciation (cost, revaluation, or fair value).']
  depreciation_date date [note: 'The date on which the depreciation expense is recorded.']
  depreciation_life_years varchar [note: 'Total useful life of the asset, in years, as defined for depreciation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to calculate depreciation (e.g., straight‑line, declining balance).']
  depreciation_period varchar [note: 'Code representing the fiscal period (month/quarter) to which the depreciation amount applies.']
  depreciation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate used to calculate depreciation, expressed as a percentage.']
  depreciation_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Frequency at which depreciation is posted (e.g., monthly).']
  depreciation_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence of the depreciation entry within the reporting period.']

  gl_account_credit varchar [note: 'GL account code that receives the offsetting credit (e.g., accumulated depreciation).']
  gl_account_debit varchar [note: 'GL account code to which the depreciation expense is debited.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'True if the depreciation amount is an estimate rather than a final figure.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., health, dental, vision) that owns the asset.']
  net_book_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Asset\'s carrying amount after subtracting accumulated depreciation.']
  posted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the depreciation entry has been posted to the general ledger.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the depreciation entry was posted to the general ledger.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the posting process for the depreciation transaction.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if this transaction reverses a prior depreciation entry.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component that generated the transaction (e.g., Fixed Asset Module).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating source system (e.g., Oracle Financials).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component attributable to the depreciation expense.']
  tax_effect_flag boolean [note: 'True if the depreciation expense has a tax effect.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the depreciation transaction, used for tracking and reporting.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the depreciation transaction record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Periodic depreciation transaction in Oracle Financials recording the systematic allocation of fixed asset cost over useful life. Captures asset ID, depreciation period, depreciation method (straight-line, declining balance), depreciation amount, accumulated depreciation balance, net book value after depreciation, and GL account distribution. Supports monthly close, GAAP expense recognition, SAP statutory reporting, and RBC asset valuation adjustments.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']
  vbc_settlement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the settlement.']

  Note: 'Value-Based Care (VBC) settlement transaction recording shared savings distributions, shared risk settlements, and performance incentive payments under ACO, bundled payment, and pay-for-performance contracts. Captures contract ID, performance period, provider/ACO entity, benchmark expenditure, actual expenditure, savings/loss amount, shared savings percentage, quality score, final settlement amount, and payment status. Supports VBC financial reconciliation and provider incentive accounting.'

Table finance.mlr_financial_entry {
  mlr_financial_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each MLR financial entry record.']
  doi_submission_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the state Department of Insurance to the submitted MLR report.']
  mlr_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.mlr_calculation. Business justification: MLR financial entry must be tied to the exact MLR calculation for accurate reporting and audit.']
  audit_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initially created the MLR entry.']
  audit_user_update varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update to the MLR entry.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'Date on which the MLR calculation was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MLR entry was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the period for which the MLR calculation is effective.']
  effective_to date [note: 'End date of the period for which the MLR calculation is effective; null if open‑ended.']
  incurred_claims_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of claims incurred during the reporting period.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the MLR calculation applies (e.g., Medical, Dental, Vision).']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market for which the MLR is calculated.']
  minimum_mlr_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Regulatory minimum MLR threshold that must be met for the jurisdiction.']
  mlr_financial_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the MLR entry.']
  mlr_financial_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MLR entry.']
  mlr_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated MLR percentage (incurred claims ÷ earned premium).']
  quality_improvement_expenses decimal(15,2) [note: 'Qualified expenses incurred for quality improvement activities that count toward the MLR denominator.']
  rebate_liability_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of rebate liability owed to members if MLR falls below the minimum threshold.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the MLR is reported.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code for the jurisdiction of the MLR calculation.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the MLR filing was submitted to the regulator.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the MLR filing with CMS or the state Department of Insurance.']
  total_earned_premium decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total earned premium revenue for the reporting period, used as the MLR denominator.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MLR entry.']

  Note: 'Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) calculation record per ACA Section 2718 requirements, computed by LOB, market segment (individual, small group, large group), and state. Captures reporting year, incurred claims amount, quality improvement expenses, total earned premium, MLR percentage, minimum MLR threshold, rebate liability amount, and CMS/state DOI submission status. Drives ACA MLR rebate determinations and regulatory filings. Distinct from analytics — this is the authoritative operational record of each MLR computation.'
}

Table finance.actuarial_reserve {
  actuarial_reserve_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for actuarial_reserve']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Actuarial reserve records are tied to a specific cost center for financial reporting; adding cost_center_id FK eliminates the silo and enables proper joins.']
  actuarial_reserve_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reserve record.']
  actuary_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the certified actuary responsible for the reserve estimate.']
  actuary_signature_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the actuary signed off on the reserve estimate.']
  confidence_interval_high decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the reserve estimate.']
  confidence_interval_low decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the reserve estimate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reserve record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts.']
  development_method varchar [note: 'Actuarial technique used to project the reserve (e.g., Chain‑Ladder, Bornhuetter‑Ferguson, Frequency‑Severity).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the reserve estimate becomes effective.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the reserve estimate expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended).']
  gl_posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking the reserve to the corresponding GL posting.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment (e.g., Medical, Dental, Vision) for which the reserve is calculated.']
  mlr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserve is included in MLR calculations (true/false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or annotations entered by the actuary or reviewer.']
  paid_claims_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of claims that have been paid to date for the period.']
  paid_lae_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of loss‑adjustment expenses that have been paid to date.']
  pmpm_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserve amount is reported on a per‑member‑per‑month basis.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Specific product or plan type (e.g., HMO, PPO, HDHP) associated with the reserve.']
  reserve_estimate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actuarial estimate of the liability for the reserve period before adding confidence bounds.']
  reserve_period date [note: 'First day of the accounting period to which the reserve estimate applies.']
  reserve_type varchar [note: 'Category of reserve: Incurred But Not Reported (IBNR), Allocated Loss Adjustment Expense (ALAE), or Unallocated Loss Adjustment Expense (ULAE).']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor applied to the reserve to reflect risk adjustments required by GAAP or SAP.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the reserve record (e.g., Milliman MG‑ALFA).']
  total_liability_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of paid amounts and reserve estimate representing the total projected liability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reserve record.']

  Note: 'Actuarial reserve record encompassing both Incurred But Not Reported (IBNR) claims reserves and Loss Adjustment Expense (LAE) reserves — including allocated LAE (ALAE) and unallocated LAE (ULAE). Produced by the actuarial system (Milliman MG-ALFA) representing estimated liabilities for claims incurred but not yet submitted/adjudicated and the costs to investigate, adjudicate, and settle those claims. Captures reserve period, LOB, product type, reserve type (IBNR, ALAE, ULAE), development method (chain-ladder, Bornhuetter-Ferguson), paid claims/LAE to date, reserve estimate, total liability, confidence interval, actuary sign-off/certification, and GL posting reference. Critical for GAAP and SAP statutory balance sheet accuracy, RBC calculations, NAIC Annual Statement Schedule P reporting, and state DOI solvency examinations.'
}

Table finance.finance_regulatory_filing {

  filing_status_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text field providing additional detail about the filing status.']

  filing_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory filing (e.g., NAIC annual statement, CMS MLR report).']
  filing_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the filing document.']
  final_tax_due_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount due after any adjustments or penalties.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter code of the state or jurisdiction for the filing.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who prepared the filing.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority receiving the filing.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who reviewed and approved the filing.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Latest date the filing must be submitted to remain compliant.']
  tax_liability_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated tax liability for the filing.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base amount on which the filing tax is calculated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the filing record.']

  Note: 'Financial regulatory and tax filing record tracking all statutory financial reports and tax submissions to state DOIs, NAIC, CMS, HHS, IRS, and state tax authorities. Captures filing type (NAIC Annual Statement, NAIC Quarterly Statement, CMS MLR Report, state DOI filing, RBC report, federal income tax Form 1120, state premium tax, health insurer fee under ACA, vendor 1099 reporting), filing period, regulatory/tax jurisdiction, submission date, filing status, taxable base amount, tax rate, tax liability, estimated payments made, final tax due, confirmation number, preparer, reviewer, acceptance confirmation, and deficiency notices. Serves as the single authoritative log of all financial regulatory submissions and tax compliance filings and their disposition. Consolidates what were previously separate regulatory and tax filing tracking systems into one SSOT.'
}

Table finance.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the budget record.']
  budget_approval_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the approval.']
  budget_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal person responsible for the budget.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: budget has denormalized cost_center_code STRING. Adding cost_center_id FK normalizes the budget to its owning cost center. cost_center.cost_center_code available via JOIN. Populated at budget creation']
  risk_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Budgeting of premiums, reserves, and expenses is performed per risk pool; the FK supports pool‑specific budget creation and variance analysis.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget was formally approved.']
  approval_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who approved the budget.']
  budget_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the budget purpose and scope.']
  budget_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable budget number assigned by finance for tracking and reporting.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Category of the budget indicating planning horizon.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the department responsible for the budget.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Organizational division code associated with the budget.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the budget expires; null for open‑ended budgets.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the budget becomes effective for spending.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., medical, dental, vision) the budget supports.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during budgeting or reforecast cycles.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the budget owner.']
  prior_year_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual spend recorded for the same fiscal period in the previous year.']
  total_adjusted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all approved adjustments (increases or decreases) to the original budget.']
  total_budgeted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount originally budgeted for the period.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net budget amount after adjustments (budgeted + adjustments).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between total net budget and prior year actual (positive = over, negative = under).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the budget (e.g., v1, v2) used during re‑forecast cycles.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the budget applies.']

  Note: 'Annual and multi-year financial budget in Oracle Financials capturing approved spending plans by cost center, LOB, account, and fiscal period. Includes header-level attributes (budget version, budget year, budget owner, approval status, budget type) and line-level detail (fiscal period, account code combination, cost center, budgeted amount, prior year actual, variance to prior year, reforecast adjustments, notes). Supports variance analysis against actuals, PMPM budget tracking, monthly budget-to-actual drill-down by department and LOB, reforecast cycles, and financial planning for premium rate setting and actuarial projections. Single source of truth for all budget headers and their period-level line allocations.'
}

Table finance.budget_line {
  budget_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each budget line item.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the budget record to which this line belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: budget_line has denormalized cost_center_code STRING. Adding cost_center_id FK normalizes the budget line to its cost center. cost_center.cost_center_code available via JOIN. Populated at budget line ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who approved the budget line.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Annual budgeting of vendor contracts allocates budget to specific vendors; linking budget_line to vendor enables vendor‑specific budget planning and cost control.']
  account_code varchar [note: 'Chart of accounts code that the budget amount is charged to.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Rule used to distribute the budget amount across cost centers or projects.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of the total amount allocated to this line (if allocation_method = percentage).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the budget line\'s approval process.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the budget line received final approval.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount allocated in the current budget cycle.']
  budget_line_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the budget line item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the budget line record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency identifier for the amounts.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Internal division identifier for reporting and allocation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the budget line becomes effective for accounting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the budget line ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Period within the fiscal year (e.g., 2024‑01 for January 2024).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year to which the budget line is assigned.']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the amount represents a planned budget rather than a recorded actual.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the line should be included in corporate‑level consolidated reporting.']
  is_forecast boolean [note: 'True if the line reflects a forecasted adjustment rather than an approved budget.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business segment (e.g., Medical, Dental, Vision) to which the budget line applies.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the line within the budget header.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional commentary or justification for the budget line.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day of the budgeting period covered by the line.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the budgeting period covered by the line.']
  prior_year_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual spend recorded in the same period of the previous fiscal year.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (e.g., headcount, service units) associated with the budget line.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Category of the resource (e.g., expense, revenue, capital) that the line supports.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component that generated the record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Financials).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary variance (budgeted minus prior year actual).']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage representation of the variance amount.']

  Note: 'Individual budget line item within a financial budget, capturing the granular period-level allocation by account and cost center. Captures budget line sequence, fiscal period, account code combination, budgeted amount, prior year actual, variance to prior year, and notes. Enables monthly budget-to-actual variance tracking, reforecast adjustments, and drill-down financial analysis by department and LOB.'
}

Table finance.premium_revenue {
  premium_revenue_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the premium revenue record.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group or association for group policies.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan under which the premium is booked.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Premium revenue calculations depend on each member\'s risk score; linking enables traceability for pricing and regulatory audits.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the insured member associated with the premium.']
  underwriter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Underwriting profit and loss analysis depends on linking premium revenue records to the underwriter employee responsible for the policy.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal accounting period identifier (e.g., 2023Q1).']
  capitation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed amount paid under a capitation arrangement for the period.']
  coverage_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of the coverage period in months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the premium revenue record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  deficiency_reserve decimal(15,2) [note: 'Reserve for potential shortfalls in premium collection or underwriting loss.']
  earned_premium decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of written premium that has been earned based on coverage elapsed.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the coverage period for which premium is recognized.']
  effective_to date [note: 'End date of the coverage period for which premium is recognized.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) within the fiscal year for the premium.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year associated with the premium.']
  hcc_score varchar [note: 'Aggregated HCC score for the member influencing risk adjustment.']
  is_capitated boolean [note: 'True if the premium is part of a capitation payment arrangement.']
  lob varchar [note: 'Business line classification of the product (e.g., HMO, PPO).']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification for the premium (individual, group, etc.).']
  mlr_denominator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this premium is included in the Medical Loss Ratio denominator.']
  net_earned_premium decimal(15,2) [note: 'Earned premium after deducting reserves, adjustments, and reinsurance.']
  premium_record_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the premium revenue record.']
  premium_revenue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the premium revenue record.']
  premium_tax_base decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base amount used for calculating premium taxes.']
  premium_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the premium amount (e.g., written, earned).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this premium record must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., CMS, NAIC).']
  reinsurance_ceded_premium decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of premium transferred to reinsurance carriers.']
  revenue_date date [note: 'Date on which the premium revenue was recognized according to ASC 606.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Factor used to adjust premium based on member risk scores.']
  risk_corridor_adjustment decimal(15,2) [note: 'Adjustment amount resulting from the risk corridor (3Rs) mechanism.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the premium record.']
  unearned_premium_reserve decimal(15,2) [note: 'Liability reserve for the portion of premium not yet earned.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the premium revenue record.']
  vbc_shared_savings_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of shared savings distributed under VBC contracts.']
  written_premium decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total premium amount written for the coverage period before any earnings or adjustments.']

  Note: 'Premium revenue recognition record capturing earned premium by member, group, plan, LOB, market segment, and accounting period per ASC 606 / GAAP and SAP statutory standards. Derived from billing and enrollment data and posted to the GL as the authoritative revenue record. Captures member ID, group ID, plan ID, coverage period, written premium, earned premium, unearned premium reserve, deficiency reserve, risk corridor adjustment (3Rs), reinsurance ceded premium, and net earned premium. Supports GAAP revenue recognition, SAP statutory premium reporting, MLR denominator calculations, and premium tax base determination.'
}

Table finance.reinsurance_transaction {
  reinsurance_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reinsurance transaction record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the transaction record in the source system.']
  primary_reinsurance_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Primary key of the reinsurer party involved in the transaction.']
  reinsurance_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the reinsurance treaty associated with this transaction.']
  attachment_point_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Retention threshold amount before reinsurance coverage becomes payable.']
  attachment_point_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the attachment point is per claim, per period, or aggregate.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the reinsurance transaction occurred in the source system.']
  ceded_loss_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Loss amount the insurer cedes to the reinsurer.']
  ceded_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Premium amount transferred to the reinsurer under the treaty.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Last date of coverage period for which the transaction applies.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'First date of coverage period for which the transaction applies.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the transaction becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the transaction ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'True if the transaction reflects an adjustment to a prior entry.']
  is_assumed boolean [note: 'True if the transaction represents an assumed reinsurance amount.']
  is_ceded boolean [note: 'True if the transaction represents a ceded amount to a reinsurer.']
  is_excess boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is part of an excess‑of‑loss reinsurance treaty.']
  is_partial_settlement boolean [note: 'True if the settlement covers only a portion of the total amount.']
  is_quota_share boolean [note: 'True if the transaction pertains to a quota‑share arrangement.']
  is_stop_loss boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is related to a stop‑loss reinsurance treaty.']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum liability the reinsurer will assume under the treaty.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Indicates if the limit is per claim, per occurrence, or aggregate.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net monetary impact after premium, loss, and recovery amounts are applied.']
  rbc_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of RBC credit recognized from the reinsurance transaction.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']
  recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount recovered from the reinsurer for previously ceded losses.']
  reinsurance_transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or details of the transaction.']
  reporting_category varchar [note: 'Category used for internal and regulatory financial reporting.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor used to adjust the transaction for risk weighting in regulatory calculations.']
  risk_factor_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific risk factor applied to the transaction.']
  sap_schedule_f_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator required for SAP statutory Schedule F reporting (Y/N).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled with the reinsurer.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement process for the transaction.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the finance system for tracking.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transaction record.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the transaction: ceded premium, ceded loss, recovery, or adjustment.']
  transaction_type_code varchar [note: 'System code representing the transaction type for integration purposes.']

  Note: 'Reinsurance transaction record capturing ceded and assumed reinsurance activity — stop-loss treaties, quota share arrangements, and specific excess reinsurance. Captures treaty ID, reinsurer name, transaction type (ceded premium, ceded loss, recovery), coverage period, attachment point, limit, ceded premium amount, ceded loss amount, recovery amount, and settlement status. Supports SAP statutory Schedule F reporting, RBC credit for reinsurance, and net claims liability calculations.'
}

  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount before any adjustments, in the transaction currency.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments, representing the amount to be settled.']

  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction has been eliminated for consolidation purposes.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Conversion rate applied when the transaction currency differs from the reporting currency.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) of the fiscal year for the transaction.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the transaction.']
  intercompany_elimination_status varchar [note: 'Status of the elimination process for consolidation.']

  intercompany_transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transaction.']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the transaction amount is part of a budgeted allocation.']
  is_foreign_currency boolean [note: 'True if the transaction currency differs from the entity\'s functional currency.']
  is_statistical boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is for statistical reporting only and does not affect financial balances.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., Medical, Dental, Vision) associated with the transaction.']
  posted_flag boolean [note: 'True when the transaction has been posted to the general ledger.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction was posted to the ledger.']
  receiving_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that receives the intercompany transaction.']
  related_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of a supporting document (e.g., invoice, contract) linked to the transaction.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component within the source system that originated the transaction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source application or system that generated the transaction.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the transaction for reporting (e.g., operating, capital).']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the transaction for reference and reporting.']
  transaction_purpose varchar [note: 'Business reason or objective behind the intercompany movement.']
  transaction_subtype varchar [note: 'More granular classification of the transaction type.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the intercompany transaction occurred in the business process.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the intercompany transaction indicating its economic purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Intercompany financial transaction record capturing intra-entity and inter-subsidiary financial activity requiring elimination in consolidated financial statements. Captures originating legal entity, receiving legal entity, transaction type (management fee, cost allocation, intercompany loan, dividend), transaction date, amount, currency, elimination status, and consolidation period. Supports GAAP consolidated financial statement preparation and SAP statutory entity-level reporting.'
}

Adding legal_entity_id FK normalizes this to the legal_entity master. Populated at account setup — ever']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date the account became effective for cash management.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date the account is scheduled to be closed or become inactive.']
  gl_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the associated GL account for the period.']
  is_claims_disbursement_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used to pay claim reimbursements.']
  is_custodial_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used to hold funds on behalf of a third party (e.g., HSA, FSA).']

  is_lockbox boolean [note: 'True if the account is a lockbox used for receiving payments.']

  reconciliation_period_end date [note: 'End date of the current reconciliation reporting period.']
  reconciliation_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the current reconciliation reporting period.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation process.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing number identifying the financial institution.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual(s) authorized to sign on the account.']
  unreconciled_items_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount of items that remain unreconciled for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bank account record.']

  Note: 'Bank account master, cash management, and reconciliation record in Oracle Financials representing all corporate bank accounts used for premium collection, claims disbursement, capitation payments, payroll, and operating expenses. Captures account attributes (bank name, masked account/routing numbers, account type — operating/lockbox/payroll/claims disbursement/FSA-HSA custodial, currency, legal entity, cost center, status, signatory authorities) and periodic reconciliation records (reconciliation period, GL balance, bank statement balance, outstanding checks, deposits in transit, unreconciled items, reconciled balance, reconciliation status, preparer). Serves as the authoritative register for cash management, bank reconciliation, SOC 1 audit compliance, and internal controls over cash. Single source of truth for both bank account master data and reconciliation history.'
}

Table finance.bank_reconciliation {
  bank_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each bank reconciliation record.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key referencing the corporate bank account being reconciled.']

  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reconciliation was approved or rejected.']
  bank_statement_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance reported on the bank statement for the same period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in the record.']
  deposits_in_transit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of deposits recorded in the GL but not yet reflected on the bank statement.']
  gl_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance recorded in the General Ledger for the reconciliation period, in the account\'s currency.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation required manual adjustments after initial processing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations entered by the preparer or reviewer.']
  outstanding_checks_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of checks issued but not yet cleared by the bank.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who prepared the reconciliation.']
  reconciled_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final balance after reconciling GL and bank statement amounts.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier assigned to the reconciliation run, used in reporting and audit trails.']
  reconciliation_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the accounting period covered by the reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_period_start date [note: 'First day of the accounting period covered by the reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation process.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reconciliation was executed.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reconciliation run (e.g., cash‑balance, bank‑statement, manual adjustment).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the reconciliation data (e.g., Oracle Financials).']
  unreconciled_items_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount of items that remain unreconciled after processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reconciliation record.']

  Note: 'Bank reconciliation record in Oracle Financials matching GL cash balances against bank statement balances for each corporate bank account. Captures reconciliation period, bank account, GL balance, bank statement balance, outstanding checks, deposits in transit, unreconciled items, reconciled balance, reconciliation status, and preparer. Supports internal controls, SOC 1 audit requirements, and cash management accuracy for premium and claims disbursement accounts.'
}

Table finance.tax_filing {
  tax_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax filing record.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: tax_filing has legal_entity_code BIGINT representing the legal entity filing taxes. Adding legal_entity_id FK normalizes this to the legal_entity master. legal_entity has legal_entity_code for JOIN re']
  prior_filing_tax_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original filing that this amendment modifies.']

  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Reference number returned by the tax authority confirming receipt.']

  filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the filing was officially submitted.']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the filing purpose or special circumstances.']

  filing_error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the filing error.']

  filing_number varchar [note: 'External filing number assigned by the tax authority or internal system.']
  filing_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that generated the filing record (e.g., Oracle Financials).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tax filing.']
  filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filing was submitted to the tax authority.']
  final_tax_due_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining tax due after accounting for estimated payments.']
  is_amended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment to a prior filing.']
  is_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing has been successfully submitted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the filing.']
  payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount actually paid for the tax liability.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the tax payment must be made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the tax payment.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date the tax payment was received by the authority.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number associated with the tax payment transaction.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Category of tax for reporting and analysis.']
  tax_form_code varchar [note: 'Standard tax form identifier used for the filing.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter code representing the jurisdiction (e.g., state) for the tax.']
  tax_liability_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated tax liability based on taxable base and rate.']
  tax_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tax payment.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax (federal, state, local, or other).']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the tax filing applies.']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount subject to tax before rates are applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tax filing record.']

  Note: 'Tax filing record capturing federal and state tax obligations and submissions for the health plan legal entities — federal income tax (Form 1120), state premium tax filings, health insurer fee (HIF) under ACA, and 1099 vendor reporting. Captures tax type, tax jurisdiction, tax year, taxable base amount, tax rate, tax liability, estimated payments made, final tax due, filing date, and confirmation number. Supports tax compliance, regulatory reporting, and financial statement tax provision calculations.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation {
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the regulatory obligation record.']
  audit_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of audit observations related to the obligation.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the regulatory obligation.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the regulatory obligation record.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the obligation.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to remediate any compliance gaps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the obligation becomes effective.']
  exemption_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exemption from the obligation is possible.']
  exemption_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions under which an exemption may be granted.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the obligation expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required filings.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the obligation must be satisfied.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that issues the obligation (e.g., CMS, OCR, NAIC, State DOI).']
  is_federal boolean [note: 'True if the obligation applies at the federal level.']
  is_state_specific boolean [note: 'True if the obligation is limited to a particular state.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the obligation (state, province, or federal).']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or process that performed the most recent update.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Date the next compliance action is required.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the regulatory obligation.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory requirement.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty for non‑compliance.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the penalty amount.']
  reference_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official regulation or guidance document.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Framework or statute under which the obligation falls (e.g., HIPAA, ACA, Medicare, State Law).']
  reporting_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required reports.']
  risk_impact varchar [note: 'Potential business impact if the obligation is not met.']
  risk_likelihood varchar [note: 'Likelihood of non‑compliance occurring.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (likelihood × impact).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the obligation data.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Final date for filing required reports or documents.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the obligation definition for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for each regulatory obligation the health plan must fulfill — HIPAA Privacy/Security Rules, ACA market conduct requirements, CMS Medicare/Medicaid mandates, state DOI filings, NCQA/URAC accreditation standards, ERISA requirements, SOC reporting obligations, and state Medicaid fair hearing compliance. Captures the governing body, regulatory framework, obligation type, effective date, jurisdiction, frequency, accountable business owner, compliance status, and obligation risk score (likelihood × impact of non-compliance). Includes tracking of state fair hearing obligations for Medicaid adverse benefit determinations under 42 CFR §438.400. Serves as the authoritative registry of all compliance requirements and the anchor entity linking to submissions, attestations, policies, and corrective actions that demonstrate adherence.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_submission {
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each regulatory submission record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Link to the specific regulatory requirement that this submission satisfies.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Required: Certain regulatory submissions (e.g., network participation filings) are made per contract and must be tracked.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance professional responsible for the filing.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Regulatory Submission: submissions are filed on behalf of an employer group, needed for tracking filing status per group.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Regulatory submissions (e.g., CMS filing) are made for a particular health plan; linking enables tracking submission status per plan.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date the regulator formally accepted the filing.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the submission.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Identifier returned by the regulator confirming receipt of the filing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the submission record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the regulatory filing must be completed.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the filing fee is expressed.']
  filing_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross fee charged by the regulator for processing the submission.']
  filing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the reporting period covered by the submission.']
  filing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period covered by the submission.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing is deemed critical for regulatory compliance (true) or routine (false).']
  last_reminder_sent_date date [note: 'Date the most recent reminder was sent to the responsible party.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between creation of the submission record and its due date.']
  net_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net fee after any adjustments, discounts, or credits.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Government or accrediting entity to which the submission is made.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory filing.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a filing was rejected by the regulator.']
  reminder_schedule varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the reminder cadence (e.g., "30/15/5 days before due").']
  submission_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date the filing was submitted to the regulator.']
  submission_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing the purpose or special considerations of the filing.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Technical or physical channel used to deliver the filing.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the filing by the organization.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory filing (e.g., annual notice, rate filing).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the submission record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each regulatory filing or submission — from scheduled deadline through actual filing. Captures submission type, regulatory body, due date, lead time days, reminder schedule, submission date, filing period, submission method (EDI, portal, paper), status (scheduled, draft, submitted, accepted, rejected), and confirmation identifiers. Covers CMS annual notices, state DOI rate filings, NAIC financial statements, NCQA accreditation submissions, OCR breach notifications, MLR rebate filings, ACA marketplace submissions, and ERISA filings (Form 5500, Summary Annual Report, Summary Plan Description, COBRA notices, QMCSO responses). Supports the annual regulatory calendar view used by compliance officers to manage deadlines across CMS, state DOIs, NCQA, OCR, ERISA, and DOL obligations. Links to the regulatory obligation it satisfies.'
}

Table compliance.audit_engagement {
  audit_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit engagement record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Audit engagements are performed to satisfy regulatory obligations; linking creates required hierarchy.']
  contract_audit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subsequent audit engagement if follow-up is required.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Audit Management: each audit engagement is performed for a specific employer group, required for audit scheduling and reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Audits verify compliance of a specific health plan; auditors need to reference the plan being audited for ACA reporting.']
  lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Audit engagements require a designated lead auditor employee; enables audit scheduling and accountability.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: External audit firms are vendors; linking audit engagements to the responsible vendor allows tracking audit scope, costs, and compliance obligations per vendor.']
  audit_body varchar [note: 'Organization or agency conducting the audit.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the audit focus area.']
  audit_cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual monetary cost incurred for the audit.']
  audit_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost associated with conducting the audit.']
  audit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for cost fields.']
  audit_document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or path to the primary audit report document.']
  audit_engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit engagement.']
  audit_findings_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed findings dataset.']
  audit_followup_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up audit is scheduled.']
  audit_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or business unit where the audit is primarily conducted.']
  audit_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology or standards used to conduct the audit (e.g., COSO, ISO27001).']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the audit by the auditing body.']
  audit_period_end date [note: 'End date of the audit coverage period.']
  audit_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the audit coverage period.']
  audit_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the audit engagement.']
  audit_report_release_date date [note: 'Date when the final audit report was released to stakeholders.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the functional and geographic scope of the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Category of audit based on purpose and scope.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) applicable to the audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit engagement record was first created in the system.']
  critical_findings varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical severity.']
  engagement_end_date date [note: 'Date when the audit engagement concluded or was terminated.']
  engagement_start_date date [note: 'Date when the audit engagement officially began.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit engagement was last reviewed for status or changes.']
  minor_findings varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as minor severity.']
  overall_outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the audit after all findings are addressed.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or statute cited for each finding.']
  remediation_plan_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which all remediation actions must be completed.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation activities for audit findings.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating derived from audit findings.']
  significant_findings varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as significant severity.']
  total_findings varchar [note: 'Total number of findings identified during the audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit engagement record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each formal audit or examination conducted by or against the health plan — CMS program audits (RADV, Part C/D), state DOI market conduct examinations, OCR HIPAA investigations, NCQA accreditation surveys, SOC 2 audits, internal compliance audits, and external financial audits. Captures audit type, auditing body, audit scope, audit period, lead auditor, engagement start/end dates, status, and overall audit outcome. Includes detailed finding tracking: finding type (condition, cause, effect), severity level (critical, significant, minor), regulatory citation, finding description, affected business area, root cause analysis, recommended remediation, and finding status through the lifecycle from identification through remediation and closure. Serves as the authoritative registry of all audit engagements and their findings.'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the audit finding record.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Audit findings belong to an audit engagement; linking provides parent relationship and enables traceability.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim linked to the finding, when relevant.']
  primary_audit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the auditor who recorded the finding.']
  affected_business_area varchar [note: 'Business function or area affected by the finding.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance domain to which the finding relates.']
  audit_finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature of the finding.']
  audit_finding_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the finding: internal audit, external audit, or regulatory review.']
  audit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit finding.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finding was formally closed after remediation.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Specific compliance domain impacted by the finding.']
  corrective_action_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was completed.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation work.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit finding record was created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which remediation actions must be completed.']
  effectiveness_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of remediation effectiveness.']
  effectiveness_score varchar [note: 'Score (0‑100) measuring how effective the remediation was.']
  evidence_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., URL or file ID) to supporting evidence documents.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact associated with the finding.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the finding for tracking and reference.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the finding as a condition, cause, or effect.']
  identified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finding was first identified during the audit.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the finding is deemed critical based on severity and impact.']
  is_repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has been observed previously.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the finding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by auditors or reviewers.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the finding for remediation scheduling.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulation, statute, or standard cited for the finding.']
  related_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number associated with the finding, if applicable.']
  related_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the issue was detected.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Recommended corrective action to address the finding.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the remediation must be completed.']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Final resolution outcome for the finding.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who performed the latest review.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) representing the finding\'s risk level.']
  risk_score_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the risk score calculation.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Analysis of the underlying cause that led to the finding.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating indicating the potential impact of the finding.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated tags for categorization and search.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit finding record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for the finding record to support change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each finding, deficiency, or observation identified during an audit engagement. Captures finding type (condition, cause, effect), severity level (critical, significant, minor), regulatory citation, finding description, affected business area, root cause analysis, and recommended remediation. Links to the parent audit engagement and drives the corrective action plan (CAP) process. Tracks finding status through the lifecycle from identification through remediation and closure.'
}

Table compliance.corrective_action_plan {
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the corrective action plan.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: A Corrective Action Plan is created in direct response to an audit finding. The existing STRING field \'finding_reference\' is a denormalized text reference that should be replaced by a proper FK. The a']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or team responsible for executing the CAP.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the CAP was actually completed.']
  actual_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred for the CAP.']
  audit_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments from auditors or reviewers regarding the CAP.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAP was formally closed.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High-level compliance domain of the issue.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which remediation must be completed.']
  corrective_action_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAP record was created in the system.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected financial cost to implement the corrective actions.']
  evidence_document_path varchar [note: 'File system or URL location of supporting evidence artifacts.']
  is_external_audit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding originated from an external audit.']
  is_fwa_monitoring boolean [note: 'Indicates if the CAP is related to FWA monitoring.']
  last_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Status of the most recent milestone.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of milestones defined for the CAP.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the CAP.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Role of the owner within the organization.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the corrective action plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the CAP.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the CAP based on source of finding.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the CAP.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority associated with the finding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CMS|OCR|NAIC|State_DOI|NCQA|URAC|Joint_Commission — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  remediation_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned approach to address the root cause.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned to the CAP based on severity and impact.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative of the root cause analysis for the finding.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity of the underlying finding.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the CAP should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the CAP record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Corrective Action Plan (CAP) developed in response to audit findings, regulatory violations, or accreditation deficiencies. Captures the triggering finding or violation, CAP type, remediation strategy, responsible owner, target completion date, and implementation status. Includes granular milestone tracking with individual action items, assigned owners, planned/actual completion dates, milestone status, evidence artifacts, and reviewer sign-off for each milestone. Tracks the full CAP lifecycle from submission to regulatory body through milestone completion, validation, and closure. Critical for CMS audit readiness and NCQA accreditation maintenance.'
}

Table compliance.cap_milestone {
  cap_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the corrective action plan milestone record.']
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent corrective action plan to which this milestone belongs.']
  primary_cap_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or team responsible for executing the milestone.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who reviews and signs off on milestone completion.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date the milestone was actually completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date the milestone actually started.']
  cap_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric progress indicator (0.00 – 100.00) representing how much of the milestone work is finished.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance domain that the milestone addresses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hipaa|aca|mlr|ncqa|urac|state_doi|fwa — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  escalated_flag boolean [note: 'True if the milestone has been escalated due to delays or issues.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the artifact or document that provides evidence of milestone completion.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is deemed critical for compliance or business impact.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the milestone for external reference.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work or outcome required for the milestone.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone within the corrective action plan.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category that classifies the nature of the milestone activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the milestone.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date by which the milestone should be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the milestone to begin.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level indicating the importance or urgency of the milestone.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific regulatory requirement, audit finding, or standard tied to the milestone.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the milestone\'s impact on compliance or operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking individual milestones and action items within a Corrective Action Plan (CAP). Captures milestone description, assigned owner, planned completion date, actual completion date, milestone status, evidence artifacts, and reviewer sign-off. Enables granular tracking of CAP progress and supports regulatory reporting on remediation timelines. Links to the parent CAP and provides the audit trail required by CMS, state DOIs, and NCQA for CAP validation.'
}

Table compliance.breach_incident {
  breach_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for breach_incident']
