  baa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.baa. Business justification: When a PHI breach involves a business associate, the specific BAA governing that relationship is critical for determining breach notification requirements, liability, and remediation obligations. brea']
  breach_report_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the breach report filed with external regulator.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Required: PHI breach incidents are reported against the specific provider contract responsible for the data.']
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action_plan. Business justification: A breach incident triggers a Corrective Action Plan to remediate the root cause and prevent recurrence. breach_incident currently has denormalized STRING fields \'corrective_action_plan\' and \'correctiv']
  incident_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PHI breach response is managed by a specific employee; needed for incident response reporting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Business Associate involved, if any.']
  affected_phi_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of PHI categories impacted (e.g., demographic, clinical, claims).']
  audit_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of audit findings related to the breach.']
  breach_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the cause of the breach.']
  breach_discovery_date date [note: 'Date the breach was discovered by the organization.']
  breach_incident_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the breach incident.']
  breach_occurrence_date date [note: 'Date the breach actually occurred (if known).']
  breach_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the external breach report document.']
  breach_resolution_date date [note: 'Date the breach was fully resolved and closed.']
  breach_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the breach (internal staff, external actor, partner, unknown).']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach investigation.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Category of the breach event.']
  business_associate_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Business Associate was involved in the breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach incident record was created in the system.']
  hhs_notification_date date [note: 'Date HHS was notified of the breach.']
  hhs_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the U.S. Department of Health & Human Services was notified.']
  notification_content_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the notification content used.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date the notification was sent.']
  notification_delivery_confirmation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether delivery confirmation was received.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Delivery channel used for the notification.']
  notification_obligation varchar [note: 'Regulatory notification obligations triggered by the breach.']
  notification_recipient_count varchar [note: 'Number of recipients who received the notification.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Type of notification sent.']
  number_of_individuals_affected varchar [note: 'Count of distinct individuals whose PHI was compromised.']
  number_of_records_affected varchar [note: 'Total number of PHI records impacted.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'Date the breach was filed with the regulator.']
  regulatory_filing_status varchar [note: 'Status of required regulatory filings related to the breach.']
  risk_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for risk assessment.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score resulting from breach risk assessment.']
  state_notification_date date [note: 'Date the state authority was notified.']
  state_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the relevant state authority was notified.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach incident record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each PHI (Protected Health Information) breach incident identified under HIPAA Breach Notification Rule (45 CFR §164.400–414). Captures breach discovery date, breach type (unauthorized access, theft, loss, improper disposal), affected PHI categories, number of individuals affected, breach cause, business associate involvement, risk assessment outcome, and notification obligations triggered. Includes full notification tracking: notification type (individual, HHS/OCR, media, state AG), notification method (mail, email, substitute notice), notification date, recipient count, content version, and delivery confirmation. Drives the 60-day OCR notification workflow and annual breach report to HHS. Critical for HIPAA compliance and OCR audit readiness.'
}

Table compliance.breach_notification {
  breach_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for breach notification record.']
  breach_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated PHI breach incident.']
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corrective action plan linked to this notification.']
  notification_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory breach notifications are issued by a designated employee; links notification to responsible staff.']
  affected_member_count varchar [note: 'Number of members whose PHI was affected by the breach.']
  affected_provider_count varchar [note: 'Number of providers affected by the breach.']
  breach_category varchar [note: 'Category of the breach incident.']
  breach_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date the breach was disclosed internally.']
  breach_discovery_date date [note: 'Date the breach was discovered.']
  breach_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the notification record.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the notification process.']
  content_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the notification content used.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach notification record was first created in the system.']
  deadline_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline date by which notification must be sent (typically 60 days from breach discovery).']
  deadline_met boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the notification was sent on or before the deadline.']
  delivery_confirmation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether delivery confirmation was received.']
  media_outlet_name varchar [note: 'Name of media outlet notified, if applicable.']
  notification_content_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the notification content for audit purposes.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date the notification was sent to the recipient.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the breach notification.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the notification was sent.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Category of notification recipient.']
  number_of_recipients varchar [note: 'Count of individuals or entities who received the notification.']
  regulatory_body_notified varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority or entity that was notified.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the breach (0-100).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach notification record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each breach notification sent to affected individuals, HHS/OCR, state regulators, or media outlets as required under HIPAA Breach Notification Rule. Captures notification type (individual, HHS, media), notification method (mail, email, substitute notice), notification date, number of recipients, notification content version, and delivery confirmation. Tracks compliance with the 60-day notification deadline and supports OCR investigation response. Links to the parent PHI breach incident.'
}

Table compliance.hipaa_privacy_request {
  hipaa_privacy_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the HIPAA privacy request record.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who submitted the privacy request.']
  processing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each HIPAA privacy request is processed by a compliance employee; required for audit trails.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal process, if applicable.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the privacy request record was first created in the system.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the privacy request record.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when a request is denied or partially granted.']
  disclosure_authorization_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis authorizing the PHI disclosure.']
  disclosure_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the PHI disclosure, if one occurred.']
  disclosure_logged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PHI disclosure related to this request has been logged.']
  disclosure_phicategories varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of PHI categories disclosed (e.g., diagnosis, treatment, payment).']
  disclosure_purpose varchar [note: 'Business or legal purpose for which the PHI was disclosed.']
  disclosure_recipient_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity receiving the disclosed PHI.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Outcome of the request (e.g., granted, denied, partially granted).']
  is_appealed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has appealed the request decision.']
  is_confidential_communication boolean [note: 'True when the member requests that communications be handled confidentially.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Medium of the request (digital, paper, or phone).']
  request_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description provided by the member detailing the request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the privacy request.']
  request_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the privacy request was initially received.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the request was received (e.g., member portal, email, phone).']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the privacy request.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of the privacy request as defined by HIPAA (e.g., access, amendment, accounting of disclosures, restriction, confidential communication).']
  response_date date [note: 'Date on which the organization completed its response to the request.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which a response must be provided (typically 30 days from receipt).']

  Note: 'Transactional record for HIPAA privacy operations — member privacy rights requests and PHI disclosure tracking. Covers access requests (right to access PHI), amendment requests, accounting of disclosures, restriction requests, and confidential communication requests under 45 CFR §164.500. Captures request type, member identifier, request receipt date, response due date, response date, request disposition (granted, denied, partially granted), denial reason, and appeal status. Includes PHI disclosure logging: disclosure date, recipient organization, recipient type (public health authority, law enforcement, research, legal), purpose of disclosure, PHI categories disclosed, and disclosure authorization basis. Excludes treatment, payment, and healthcare operations disclosures per HIPAA exemptions. Tracks compliance with HIPAA response timelines and supports OCR audit documentation.'
}

Table compliance.phi_disclosure_log {
  phi_disclosure_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each PHI disclosure event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Disclosure logs must record which employee performed the PHI disclosure for compliance reporting.']
  hipaa_privacy_request_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the internal request or workflow that generated the disclosure.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose protected health information was disclosed.']
  policy_document_id varchar [note: 'External document identifier that substantiates the disclosure (e.g., subpoena number).']
  authorization_basis varchar [note: 'Legal justification permitting the disclosure, per HIPAA regulations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the compliance team\'s assessment of the disclosure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the disclosure log entry was initially inserted into the system.']
  disclosure_method varchar [note: 'Means by which the protected health information was delivered to the recipient.']
  disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the disclosure event for audit tracking.']
  disclosure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PHI disclosure occurred, recorded in UTC.']
  is_authorized boolean [note: 'True if the disclosure complied with the documented authorization basis; otherwise false.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional contextual information captured by compliance staff.']
  phi_category varchar [note: 'Standard classification of the type(s) of protected health information disclosed.']
  purpose_of_disclosure varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for disclosing the PHI (e.g., public health reporting, law enforcement request).']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity or person that received the protected health information.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'Category describing why the recipient is allowed to receive PHI.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the disclosure log entry.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each disclosure of PHI to external parties as required by HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures (45 CFR §164.528). Captures disclosure date, recipient organization, recipient type (public health authority, law enforcement, research, legal), purpose of disclosure, PHI categories disclosed, disclosure authorization basis, and member identifier. Supports member requests for accounting of disclosures and OCR audit readiness. Excludes treatment, payment, and healthcare operations disclosures per HIPAA exemptions.'
}

Table compliance.baa {
  baa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each Business Associate Agreement record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each BAA supports compliance with a regulatory obligation; linking enables obligation‑centric reporting.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Business Associate Agreements are overseen by a compliance officer; linking provides accountability.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Required for HIPAA BAA management: each Business Associate Agreement must reference the vendor (business associate) it covers, enabling reporting of vendor‑specific compliance status.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the BAA by the health plan.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the BAA.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments executed against the original BAA.']
  audit_rights varchar [note: 'Rights granted to the health plan to audit the associate\'s compliance with the BAA.']
  breach_notification_requirements varchar [note: 'Procedures and timelines the associate must follow to notify the health plan of a PHI breach.']
  business_associate_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the vendor, TPA, PBM, clearinghouse, or other entity that receives PHI under the agreement.']
  business_associate_type varchar [note: 'Category of the business associate indicating the nature of the relationship.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BAA record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  data_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method the associate must use to dispose of retained PHI after the retention period.']
  data_retention_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the associate must retain PHI before disposal.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the BAA expires or terminates; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the BAA becomes legally binding.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction governing the agreement (e.g., state law, federal HIPAA).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction (state or territory) applicable to the BAA.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to the BAA.']
  permitted_phis varchar [note: 'List or description of specific PHI categories the associate may handle (e.g., claims, enrollment, clinical data).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation(s) that the BAA satisfies (e.g., HIPAA, HITECH).']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that the health plan must provide notice to renew.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the BAA renews automatically, requires manual action, or does not renew.']
  scope_of_phi_use varchar [note: 'Narrative description of permitted uses and disclosures of Protected Health Information under the agreement.']
  security_obligations varchar [note: 'Detailed security controls and safeguards the associate must implement to protect PHI.']
  subcontractor_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the business associate may engage subcontractors to perform PHI‑related services.']
  subcontractor_details varchar [note: 'Description of any approved subcontractors, their roles, and required safeguards.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing conditions under which the BAA may be terminated by either party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BAA record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Business Associate Agreement (BAA) executed with vendors, TPAs, PBMs, clearinghouses, and other entities that handle PHI on behalf of the health plan under HIPAA (45 CFR §164.308). Captures business associate name, agreement effective date, expiration date, PHI use permissions, security obligations, breach notification requirements, subcontractor provisions, termination clauses, and agreement status. Serves as the authoritative registry of all BAAs and supports HIPAA compliance audits and vendor risk management.'
}

Table compliance.fwa_case {
  fwa_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the FWA case.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the investigator assigned to the case.']
  allegation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the alleged fraud, waste, or abuse.']
  audit_log_url varchar [note: 'Link to the immutable audit log for this case.']
  case_disposition varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the case after investigation.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the case for external reference and reporting.']
  case_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was officially opened.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the case as fraud, waste, or abuse.']
  compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier used in external compliance filings (e.g., CMS FWA program ID).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the case disposition was recorded.']
  estimated_exposure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected monetary loss associated with the alleged activity.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to supporting evidence stored in the evidence repository.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the case is considered high risk based on internal scoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by investigators or reviewers.']
  recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount recovered or expected to be recovered from the subject.']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date the referral was received and the case was created.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the case referral, such as claim edit, data mining, tip line, law enforcement, or employee report.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case has been reported to a regulatory body (e.g., CMS, state fraud bureau).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned during triage (0‑100 scale).']
  subject_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the subject (member, provider, or employer group) under investigation.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Entity type that is the subject of the investigation.']
  triage_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the initial triage review, indicating next steps.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the case record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Fraud, Waste, and Abuse (FWA) investigation — from initial referral intake through case disposition. Captures referral source (claim edits, data mining, tip line, law enforcement, employee reports), referral date, allegation description, supporting evidence references, triage outcome, case type (fraud, waste, abuse), subject type (member, provider, employer group), case open date, investigation status, assigned investigator, estimated financial exposure, recovery amount, and case disposition (substantiated, unsubstantiated, referred to law enforcement, civil monetary penalty). Supports CMS FWA program requirements, state insurance fraud bureau reporting, and annual FWA reporting to CMS.'
}

Table compliance.fwa_referral {
  fwa_referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FWA referral record.']
  fwa_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FWA case opened as a result of this referral, if any.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member who is the subject of the referral, when applicable.']
  primary_fwa_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who is the subject of the referral, when applicable.']
  allegation_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the alleged fraud, waste, or abuse.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the referral record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated loss amount.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the referral should be resolved or escalated.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the referral has been escalated to higher‑level review.']
  estimated_loss_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of potential loss associated with the alleged activity.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Link (URL or document ID) to supporting evidence attached to the referral.']
  fwa_referral_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral within the investigation workflow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations captured by the investigator.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the referral for handling urgency.']
  provider_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the provider who is the subject of the referral, when applicable.']
  referral_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the referral was received or logged in the SIU system.']
  referral_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the referral for tracking and reporting.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the referral indicating how it was reported.']
  referral_type varchar [note: 'Category of the alleged wrongdoing.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity that is the subject of the referral.']
  triage_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the initial triage assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the referral record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each FWA referral received by the Special Investigations Unit (SIU) from internal detection systems, employees, members, providers, or external agencies. Captures referral source, referral date, subject identifier, referral type, allegation description, supporting evidence references, triage outcome, and linkage to the resulting FWA case if opened. Tracks the intake and triage workflow for all FWA allegations and supports CMS annual FWA reporting requirements.'
}

Table compliance.mlr_calculation {
  mlr_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mlr_calculation']
  calculated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: MLR calculations are performed by a specific analyst; needed for audit of financial compliance.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: MLR calculations are performed to demonstrate compliance with regulatory obligations; linking ties calculations to obligations.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the health plan for which the MLR is being calculated.']
  product_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the insurance product (e.g., specific benefit design) tied to the calculation.']
  audit_finding_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to any audit findings or regulatory review notes related to this calculation.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'Date on which the MLR calculation was performed.']
  calculation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the MLR calculation for reporting and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MLR calculation record was first created in the system.']
  earned_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total earned premium dollars for the reporting period, forming the MLR denominator.']
  incurred_claims_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of claims incurred for the reporting period.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., HMO, PPO, Medicaid) associated with the MLR calculation.']
  market_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the market segment (e.g., individual, group, Medicare) to which the calculation applies.']
  mlr_calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the MLR calculation.']
  mlr_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated MLR ratio expressed as a percentage (incurred claims ÷ earned premium).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or explanations entered by analysts.']
  quality_improvement_expenses_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount spent on quality improvement activities that count toward the MLR denominator.']
  rebate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the rebate owed to members or the market segment.']
  rebate_disbursement_date date [note: 'Date on which the rebate payment was or will be disbursed.']
  rebate_disbursement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the rebate payment process.']
  rebate_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'True if the calculated MLR exceeds the regulatory threshold and a rebate is required.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the MLR is being calculated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the source data for this calculation (e.g., HealthEdge, Milliman).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MLR calculation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the calculation record for audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) calculation and rebate processing record per ACA Section 2718 requirements. Captures reporting year, market segment, LOB, incurred claims, quality improvement expenses, earned premium, MLR percentage, rebate determination, rebate amount, and disbursement tracking.'
}

Table compliance.accreditation_program {
  accreditation_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each accreditation program record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Accreditation programs are driven by specific regulatory obligations; linking clarifies compliance scope.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the accountable business owner.']
  accountable_owner varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or team accountable for the accreditation.']
  accreditation_program_level varchar [note: 'Level or tier assigned by the accrediting body (e.g., Level 1, Level 2).']
  accreditation_program_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the accreditation (e.g., accredited, provisional, denied).']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accreditation program (e.g., health plan, provider, pharmacy).']
  accrediting_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that issues the accreditation (e.g., NCQA, URAC, CMS).']
  applicable_standards varchar [note: 'List of standards, guidelines, or criteria the program must meet.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Chronological notes capturing audit actions and decisions.']
  benchmark_thresholds varchar [note: 'Target values for performance measures used in the accreditation.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Progress of the accreditation process expressed as a percent.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the accreditation\'s compliance focus.']
  conditions varchar [note: 'Any remedial actions, monitoring, or conditions required for continued accreditation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accreditation record was first created.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Final decision made by the accrediting body.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the accreditation starts to be binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the accreditation ends; null if open‑ended.']
  escalated_flag boolean [note: 'True when issues have been escalated to senior management.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to uploaded evidence files used in the accreditation review.']
  final_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., 0‑100) resulting from the accreditation evaluation.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the accreditation is essential to business operations.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that performed the most recent update.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the accreditation was last reviewed for compliance.']
  measure_thresholds varchar [note: 'Target thresholds for each evaluated measure.']
  measures varchar [note: 'Key performance or quality measures assessed during the accreditation.']
  next_survey_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next accreditation survey cycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations about the accreditation.']
  preliminary_findings varchar [note: 'Initial observations and issues identified during the survey.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned by the accrediting organization.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the accreditation program (e.g., NCQA Health Plan Accreditation).']
  rating varchar [note: 'Star rating or equivalent tier based on the final score.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Suggested improvements or corrective actions from the accrediting body.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulation, statute, or standard governing the accreditation.']
  renewal_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewal surveys.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating reflecting potential impact of non‑compliance.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Description of the functional and geographic scope covered by the accreditation.']
  survey_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the accreditation survey window.']
  survey_start_date date [note: 'First day of the accreditation survey window.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Type of survey associated with the accreditation (initial, renewal, focused).']
  surveyor_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary surveyor or accrediting liaison.']
  surveyor_team varchar [note: 'Name(s) of the surveyor(s) or consulting firm performing the review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the accreditation record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each accreditation program the health plan participates in — NCQA Health Plan Accreditation, URAC Health Plan Accreditation, NCQA HEDIS, CAHPS survey programs, and CMS Star Ratings. Captures accrediting body, program name, accreditation type, current status (accredited, provisional, denied), accreditation level, effective/expiration dates, accountable business owner, and applicable standards/measures with benchmark thresholds. Includes full survey/review cycle tracking: survey type (initial, renewal, focused), survey date range, surveyor team, scope, preliminary findings, final score/rating, accreditation decision, and conditions or recommendations. Serves as the authoritative registry of all accreditation relationships and their evaluation history.'
}

Table compliance.accreditation_survey {
  accreditation_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accreditation survey record.']
  accreditation_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the accreditation program (e.g., NCQA, URAC, CMS) under which the survey is conducted.']
  survey_conductor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Accreditation surveys are conducted by a designated employee; linking enables tracking of survey execution.']
  accreditation_decision varchar [note: 'Final determination of accreditation status.']
  accreditation_survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey record was first created in the system.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accreditation survey was concluded.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the repository containing supporting evidence and documents submitted for the survey.']
  final_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score assigned by the accrediting body after final evaluation (e.g., 0.00 – 100.00).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the surveyor team.']
  preliminary_findings varchar [note: 'Initial observations and findings recorded during the survey before final evaluation.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating tier derived from the final score (1‑5 stars).']
  recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommendations or conditions issued by the surveyor for future compliance.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with identified deficiencies.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accreditation survey officially began.']
  survey_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the survey was performed.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the survey (e.g., onsite visit, remote review).']
  survey_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the accreditation body for this survey cycle.']
  survey_region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the region of the survey.']
  survey_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the functional and geographic scope covered by the survey.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey purpose (e.g., initial accreditation, renewal, focused review).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the survey record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each accreditation survey or review cycle conducted by NCQA, URAC, or CMS. Captures survey type (initial, renewal, focused), survey date range, surveyor team, survey scope, preliminary findings, final score or rating, accreditation decision, and conditions or recommendations. Links to the parent accreditation program and drives corrective action planning for deficiencies identified during the survey. Tracks the full survey lifecycle from scheduling through final determination.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_attestation {
  compliance_attestation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compliance attestation record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Attestations are issued for specific regulatory obligations; linking provides direct traceability.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Required: Attestations certify that a given contract complies with applicable regulations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the officer who signed the attestation.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Compliance Attestation: attestations are signed by representatives of a specific employer group, required for audit trails.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the attestation.']
  attestation_date date [note: 'Date on which the attestation was signed or formally executed.']
  attestation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the attestation by the organization or regulatory body.']
  attestation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the attestation.']
  attestation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the compliance attestation (e.g., HIPAA Security, ACA, CMS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hipaa_security|hipaa_privacy|aca|cms|erisa|state_doi|ncqa|urac — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  attesting_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the officer responsible for the attestation.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the attestation content.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the attestation record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'End date of the compliance period covered by the attestation (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the compliance period covered by the attestation.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documentation or evidence files stored in the repository.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the attestation contains confidential or restricted information.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by compliance staff.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or receipt identifier returned by the regulatory body.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory or accrediting organization to which the attestation is submitted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CMS|OCR|NAIC|DOI|NCQA|URAC|Joint Commission|State Department — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Code or citation of the specific regulation or standard addressed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk associated with the attestation\'s compliance area.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'How the attestation was submitted to the regulatory body.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the attestation was transmitted.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Brief narrative describing the scope and key points of the attestation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the attestation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each formal compliance attestation submitted by the health plan to a regulatory body or accrediting organization — CMS attestations, state DOI certifications, HIPAA security rule attestations, ACA compliance certifications, and ERISA plan administrator certifications. Captures attestation type, attesting officer, attestation date, regulatory body, attestation period, attestation content summary, submission method, and confirmation receipt. Provides the audit trail for all formal compliance certifications.'
}

Table compliance.policy_document {
  policy_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the policy document record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who granted final approval.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Required: Contracts reference specific policy documents governing terms and compliance obligations.']
  primary_policy_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party (e.g., compliance officer, department) responsible for the policy.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who conducted the review.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the policy was formally approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the formal approval workflow.']
  archive_date date [note: 'Date the policy was archived.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Specific compliance domain the policy addresses (e.g., privacy, fraud waste abuse, risk adjustment).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Sensitivity classification governing handling and distribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Audience for the policy (internal employees, external partners, regulators).']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored policy document.']
  document_size_bytes varchar [note: 'File size of the electronic document in bytes.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic version of the policy (e.g., S3, SharePoint).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes operationally binding.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the policy ceases to be in effect; null for indefinite policies.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is currently in effect.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'True if the policy has been moved to archival storage.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'True if the policy is marked confidential; aligns with confidentiality_level.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the subsequent review.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business code or alphanumeric identifier used to reference the policy across systems.']
  policy_document_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping such as HIPAA Privacy, FWA, ACA Compliance, Credentialing, Claims Adjudication, etc.']
  policy_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the compliance policy, procedure, or SOP.']
  policy_owner_department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department that owns the policy.']
  regulatory_citations varchar [note: 'List of statutes, regulations, or standards referenced (e.g., HIPAA, ACA, CMS).']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the most recent review was performed.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the review process.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the review (scheduled annual, triggered by event, or regulatory change).']
  revision_summary varchar [note: 'Brief description of changes made in the latest version.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the policy record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier (e.g., 1.0, 2.1) tracking revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who initially created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each internal compliance policy, procedure, or SOP — including version history and review tracking. Covers HIPAA privacy/security policies, FWA program policies, ACA compliance procedures, credentialing policies, and claims adjudication standards. Captures policy name, category, owner, effective date, version number, approval status, regulatory citations, and distribution scope. Tracks periodic reviews with review date, reviewer, review type (annual, triggered, regulatory change), outcome (approved, revised, retired), revision summary, next review date, and approver sign-off. Serves as the authoritative policy registry supporting NCQA, URAC, and CMS audit readiness.'
}

Table compliance.policy_review {
  policy_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each policy review transaction.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the senior officer who approved the review outcome.']
  policy_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance policy document that is being reviewed.']
  primary_policy_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the employee who performed the review.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the approver.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the review record.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the policy under review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting evidence files stored in the enterprise DMS.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review addresses a critical compliance risk.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the subsequent review of the policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations captured by the reviewer.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the review after evaluation.']
  outcome_date date [note: 'Date the outcome was formally recorded.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the policy at the time of review (e.g., v3.2).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Code or identifier of the specific regulation prompting the review (e.g., 45CFR164.308).']
  review_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the review was conducted.']
  review_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between review start and completion.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the review (e.g., PR‑2023‑0001).']
  review_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (0‑100) assigned by the reviewer based on compliance completeness.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of why the review was performed.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the reviewer.']
  revision_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any changes made to the policy.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk severity associated with the policy after review.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the approver signed off the review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each scheduled or ad-hoc review of a compliance policy document. Captures review date, reviewer, review type (annual, triggered, regulatory change), review outcome (approved as-is, revised, retired), revision summary, next review date, and approver sign-off. Ensures compliance policies remain current with evolving regulatory requirements from CMS, OCR, NAIC, and state DOIs. Links to the parent policy document and maintains the version history required for NCQA and URAC accreditation.'
}

Table compliance.training_program {
  training_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training_program']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Compliance training programs are mandated by specific regulatory obligations (e.g., HIPAA Privacy training mandated by 45 CFR 164.530(b), FWA training mandated by CMS). training_program has \'regulator']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the training program.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the external training vendor.']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Description of accessibility accommodations (e.g., captions, screen‑reader support).']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assessment used to evaluate participants.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents or files attached to the program record.']
  attempt_interval_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days required between assessment attempts.']
  certificate_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the certificate issued upon successful completion.']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'True if successful completion awards a certification or attestation.']
  completion_deadline date [note: 'Date by which participants must complete the training.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Broad compliance domain the training addresses.']
  content_version varchar [note: 'Version of the training content or materials associated with the program.']
  cost_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost to the organization for delivering the program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training program record was first created.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to deliver the training (online, instructor‑led, blended, self‑paced).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the training program becomes effective and available for enrollment.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the training program is retired or no longer offered (nullable for open‑ended).']
  external_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party vendor providing the training.']
  format varchar [note: 'Format of the training material (video, document, quiz, simulation, webinar).']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program has been archived and is no longer active.']
  is_external_provider boolean [note: 'True if the training is delivered by an external vendor.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language of the training content.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the training content was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the most recent update.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training is mandatory for the target audience.']
  max_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a participant may attempt the assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the program.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Role of the owner within the organization.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the assessment, expressed as a percentage.']
  passing_score_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the passing score (percent).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or catalog code for the training program.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the training program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program by regulatory or business mandate.']
  regulatory_mandate varchar [note: 'Regulatory requirement that drives the need for the training.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval in months for periodic content review.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with non‑completion of the training.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Intended audience for the training program.']
  training_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated total duration of the training program in hours.']
  training_program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the content and objectives of the program.']
  training_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training program.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training program record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the overall program (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Master record for each compliance training program required of health plan employees and delegates — annual HIPAA training, FWA training (CMS-mandated for Medicare plans), ACA compliance training, cultural competency training, and code of conduct training. Captures training program name, training type, regulatory mandate, target audience, training format (online, instructor-led, attestation), completion deadline, passing score threshold, and training content version. Includes individual completion tracking: employee/delegate identifier, completion date, assessment score, pass/fail status, certificate identifier, delivery method, and attestation of understanding. Supports CMS FWA training compliance reporting, NCQA workforce training audits, and HIPAA workforce training documentation requirements.'
}

Table compliance.training_completion {
  training_completion_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the training completion record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the record.']
  primary_training_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or delegate who completed the training.']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the training program or course.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified the record.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score achieved on the training assessment, expressed as a percentage.']
  attestation_text varchar [note: 'Employee\'s signed statement confirming understanding of the training material.']
  attestation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the attestation was recorded.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issued training certificate, if any.']
  certificate_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic copy of the training certificate.']
  completion_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the training completion event.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employee completed the training.']
  compliance_requirements_met_flag boolean [note: 'True when the training satisfies all applicable compliance requirements.']
  cost_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for the training session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training completion record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the training (e.g., online, classroom).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the training certificate expires, if applicable.']
  hours_completed decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of training hours the employee completed.']
  is_external_training boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training was delivered by an external vendor.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the training took place.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the training completion.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating whether the employee passed or failed.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or guidance (e.g., CMS‑FWA‑2023) that mandates the training.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training must be renewed before the expiration date.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the training was scheduled to occur.']
  total_hours_required decimal(4,2) [note: 'Total number of hours required for the training program.']
  training_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance category the training satisfies.']
  training_completion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the training (e.g., mandatory, optional, refresher).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training completion record.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor providing the training, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking individual employee or delegate completion of mandatory compliance training programs. Captures employee or delegate identifier, training program, completion date, assessment score, pass/fail status, certificate identifier, training delivery method, and attestation of understanding. Supports CMS FWA training compliance reporting, NCQA workforce training audits, and HIPAA workforce training documentation requirements. Enables compliance officers to identify and remediate training gaps.'
}

Table compliance.employee_screening {
  employee_screening_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each screening transaction.']
  compliance_attestation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_attestation. Business justification: Employee screenings are often triggered by a compliance attestation; linking records the originating attestation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the screening record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  employee_screening_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the screening (e.g., pending, completed, reviewed, rejected).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the screened subject is high‑risk (e.g., senior executive, high‑volume provider).']
  match_detail varchar [note: 'Narrative or code describing the nature of the match, including reference IDs from the source list.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or rationale related to the screening.']
  performed_by varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the staff member who executed the screening.']
  performed_by_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the staff member who performed the screening.']
  re_screening_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory screening of the same subject.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Business decision taken after a match is identified (e.g., investigate further, escalate to compliance, clear the record, terminate employment).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'When the chosen resolution action was finalized.']
  screening_reference varchar [note: 'Business identifier used to track the screening transaction across systems.']
  screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the screening: clear (no match), match (definite match), or potential_match (possible match requiring review).']
  screening_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the exclusion/debarment list (e.g., OIG LEIE, SAM, state Medicaid exclusion list).']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the screening activity occurred.']
  source_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the external exclusion or debarment list for the matched record.']
  subject_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the person or entity that was screened.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the screened party is an employee, provider, vendor, or delegate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the screening record was most recently modified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each OIG (Office of Inspector General) exclusion list and SAM (System for Award Management) debarment screening performed on employees, providers, vendors, and delegates. Captures screening subject type, subject identifier, screening date, screening source (OIG LEIE, SAM, state Medicaid exclusion lists), screening result (clear, match, potential match), match details, resolution action, and re-screening due date. CMS requires monthly screening of all employees and providers participating in Medicare and Medicaid programs.'
}

Table compliance.erisa_filing {
  erisa_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the ERISA filing record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: ERISA filings are required for specific regulatory obligations; linking provides obligation context.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer responsible for the filing.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group (self-funded client) associated with the filing.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date the regulator accepted the filing as compliant.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the filing.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed audit trail for this filing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filing record was created in the system.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the filing fee.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the filing was actually submitted to the regulator.']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the filing purpose or special circumstances.']
  filing_due_date date [note: 'The statutory deadline by which the filing must be submitted.']
  filing_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged by the regulator for the filing.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the filing.']
  filing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the period covered by the filing.']
  filing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by the filing.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the filing with the regulator.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Type of ERISA filing required by the Department of Labor.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the filing is considered critical for compliance.']
  is_fwa_monitoring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing is part of fraud, waste, and abuse monitoring.']
  last_reminder_sent_date date [note: 'Date the most recent reminder about the filing due date was sent.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes related to the filing.']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'The plan year to which the filing pertains.']
  regulator_acknowledged_date date [note: 'Date the regulator confirmed receipt of the filing.']
  regulator_acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulator has acknowledged the filing.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Government agency responsible for the filing.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the specific ERISA regulation or statute governing the filing.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the reason for filing rejection, if applicable.']
  reminder_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency at which reminder notifications are generated.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the filing based on compliance impact.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system where the filing record originated.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the regulator to the filing submission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the filing record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the filing record for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each ERISA-related filing and reporting obligation for self-funded employer group health plans administered by the health plan as TPA or ASO. Captures filing type (Form 5500, Summary Annual Report, Summary Plan Description, COBRA notices, QMCSO responses), plan year, employer group identifier, filing due date, filing date, filing status, and regulatory confirmation. Supports ERISA compliance for ASO/TPA clients and tracks DOL reporting obligations.'
}

Table compliance.state_fair_hearing {
  state_fair_hearing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for state_fair_hearing']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: State fair hearings are filed in response to regulatory obligations; linking enables obligation‑level tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hearing officer assigned to the case.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Medicaid member filing the hearing request.']
  adverse_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of adverse benefit determination prompting the hearing request.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date the appeal was filed after the hearing.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the member filed an appeal following the hearing outcome.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documents attached to the hearing request.']
  compliance_deadline_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the implementation deadline was met.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  hearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the fair hearing took place.']
  hearing_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the hearing in minutes.']
  hearing_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the hearing was conducted.']
  hearing_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the hearing officer.']
  hearing_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the hearing regarding the adverse action.']
  hearing_request_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the fair hearing request.']
  hearing_type varchar [note: 'Mode of the hearing delivery.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the outcome must be implemented per regulatory timelines.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates if the hearing request is considered critical for compliance.']
  member_dob date [note: 'Date of birth of the member filing the hearing.']
  member_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the member filing the hearing request.']
  member_state varchar [note: 'State of residence of the member.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes related to the hearing request.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the regulatory rule governing the hearing (e.g., 42 CFR §438.400).']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fair hearing request was received.']
  state_agency_code varchar [note: 'Code of the state agency handling the fair hearing.']
  state_fair_hearing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the hearing request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each state fair hearing request filed by a Medicaid member challenging an adverse benefit determination — denial of services, reduction of benefits, termination of coverage, or failure to act on a request. Captures member identifier, hearing request date, adverse action type, state agency, hearing officer, hearing date, hearing outcome, and implementation deadline. Tracks compliance with state Medicaid fair hearing regulations and CMS managed care final rule timelines (42 CFR §438.400). Distinct from the appeal domain which manages internal and external appeals.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_change {
  regulatory_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the regulatory change record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_obligation. Business justification: A regulatory change (new legislation, rule update) directly impacts specific regulatory obligations the health plan must fulfill. This FK links each change to its primary affected obligation, enabling']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party responsible for implementing the change.']
  prior_regulation_regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous version of this regulation, if any.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential amendment identifier if the regulation has been amended.']
  change_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the regulatory change (e.g., policy, rate, coverage, eligibility, process, technology, reporting). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: policy|rate|coverage|eligibility|process|technology|reporting — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Primary compliance domain affected by the change.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory deadline for achieving compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory change record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the regulation becomes legally effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the regulation expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that issued the change (e.g., CMS, HHS, NAIC, OCR, NCQA, URAC, Joint Commission, AMA, WHO).']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Narrative assessment of operational and financial impact.']
  impacted_business_areas varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of business domains impacted (e.g., enrollment, claims, billing, provider network).']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current progress of the implementation effort.']
  implementation_target_date date [note: 'Planned date by which implementation should be completed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change is deemed critical for business operations.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction affected (e.g., USA, CA, TX).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the change.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date the regulation was officially published.']
  regulation_code varchar [note: 'Official code or identifier assigned by the governing body (e.g., CMS‑2023‑01).']
  regulation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the regulation or rule change.']
  regulation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulation by its scope.']
  regulatory_change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulation.']
  regulatory_reference_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official regulatory document or notice.']
  reporting_frequency_months varchar [note: 'How often ongoing reporting is required for this regulation.']
  required_response_actions varchar [note: 'Summary of actions the organization must take to comply (e.g., system update, policy revision).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating (0‑100) associated with non‑compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the regulatory change (e.g., compliance portal, external feed).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the regulatory change record.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking regulatory changes, new legislation, and rule updates that impact the health plan\'s compliance obligations — CMS final rules, ACA regulatory updates, state DOI bulletins, HIPAA amendments, NAIC model law adoptions, and IRS guidance on HSA/FSA/HRA. Captures regulatory body, regulation name, publication date, effective date, impacted business areas, required response actions, implementation owner, and implementation status. Enables proactive compliance management and regulatory impact assessment across the enterprise.'
}

Table compliance.breach_report {
  breach_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for breach_report']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the audit finding related to the breach.']
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the corrective action plan associated with the breach.']
  parent_breach_report_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on breach_report (parent_breach_report_id)']
  affected_entity_description varchar [note: 'Description of the affected entity.']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity affected by the breach.']
  audit_finding_date date [note: 'Date when the audit finding was recorded.']
  audit_finding_description varchar [note: 'Description of the audit finding.']
  audit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the audit finding.']
  breach_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the breach impact.']
  breach_category varchar [note: 'Category of data affected by the breach.']
  breach_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause of the breach.']
  breach_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for breach_amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|CAD|AUD|NZD|CNY|HKD — promote to reference product]']
  breach_date date [note: 'Date when the breach event occurred.']
  breach_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the breach event.']
  breach_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the breach event.']
  breach_initiated_by_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contact who reported or initiated the breach.']
  breach_initiated_by_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the contact who reported or initiated the breach.']
  breach_initiated_by_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the contact who reported or initiated the breach.']
  breach_initiated_by_contact_type varchar [note: 'Role of the contact who reported or initiated the breach.']
  breach_location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical location where the breach occurred.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the breach.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the breach event.']
  cap_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken in the corrective action plan.']
  cap_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action plan was completed.']
  cap_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach report record was first created.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date when the breach notification was sent.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to send the breach notification.']
  notification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the notification.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the breach notification.']
  number_of_affected_individuals varchar [note: 'Count of individuals impacted by the breach.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory body overseeing the breach reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HIPAA|ACA|CMS|NCQA|URAC|SOC|FWA|ERISA — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the breach was filed with the regulatory body.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory filing.']
  regulatory_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory filing.']
  report_date date [note: 'Date when the report was generated.']
  report_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the breach report.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known number or code for the breach report.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the report.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the report type, e.g., incident, audit, investigation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach report record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for breach_report. Referenced by breach_report_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the employee.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Required for enrollment processing: links each employee to their employer group for premium allocation, ACA reporting, and group‑level analytics.']
  job_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_role. Business justification: Employees belong to a job role; linking employee to job_role eliminates redundant job_title column and enables role‑centric reporting.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the direct manager.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: An employee fills a specific authorized position (headcount slot). This is a fundamental HR relationship - the position defines the role classification, pay grade, FTE allocation, and compliance requi']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access granted to protected health information systems.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the employee\'s mailing address.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Employee\'s birth date for age and EEO reporting.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for performance bonuses.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the employee\'s mailing address.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code used for expense allocation.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the employee\'s residence.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department to which the employee belongs.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for employee communications.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact in case of emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment state of the employee.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Category of employment relationship.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'Self‑identified ethnicity for equal employment opportunity reporting.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s given name.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender for reporting and analytics.']
  health_plan_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for employer-sponsored health coverage.']
  hipaa_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee completed mandatory HIPAA workforce training.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee commenced employment.']
  i9_verification_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s I‑9 employment eligibility was verified.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s family name.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s middle name or initial, if applicable.']
  organization_level varchar [note: 'Level within the corporate hierarchy (e.g., staff, manager, director, executive).']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the employee is paid.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the employee.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the employee\'s mailing address.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary amount before bonuses or incentives.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the salary amount.']
  security_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee completed required security awareness training.']
  ssn varchar [note: 'Government‑issued identifier for tax and payroll purposes.']
