  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the employee\'s mailing address.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all internal workforce members — full-time, part-time, contract, and temporary employees. Captures employee demographics (name, date of birth, gender, ethnicity for EEO reporting), contact information, employment status (active, inactive, terminated, on leave), hire date, termination date, employment type, FLSA classification, EEO category, I-9 verification status, PHI/PII access authorization level, and current position assignment. This is the single source of truth for internal workforce identity, distinct from the provider domain which governs external clinical staff and from the member domain which governs insured individuals. Sourced from the HRIS and integrated with RBAC systems for PHI access governance.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the position record.']
  job_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_role. Business justification: Positions are defined by a job role; linking position to job_role removes duplicated role attributes stored in position.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the position is eligible for performance bonuses.']
  competency_level varchar [note: 'Standard competency tier required for the position (e.g., Level 1, Level 2).']
  compliance_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory compliance training (e.g., HIPAA, OSHA) has been completed for the role.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center to which the position\'s labor costs are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the department that houses the position.']
  eeo1_category varchar [note: 'Equal Employment Opportunity category for reporting (e.g., Management, Professional, Technical). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: management|professional|technical|sales|administrative|executive|other — promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is retired or closed (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes active for staffing.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship for the position.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Fair Labor Standards Act classification indicating exempt or non‑exempt status.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of a full‑time workload assigned to the position (e.g., 1.00 for full‑time, 0.50 for half‑time).']
  hipaa_privacy_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position must complete HIPAA privacy training.']
  incentive_plan varchar [note: 'Name of any incentive or commission plan linked to the position.']
  is_managerial boolean [note: 'True if the position includes managerial responsibilities.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to identify the job classification for the position.']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of languages required or preferred for the position.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance review for the position.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next performance review.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for overtime compensation.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation band identifier for the position.']
  pay_grade_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum salary amount for the pay grade.']
  pay_grade_mid decimal(10,2) [note: 'Midpoint salary amount for the pay grade.']
  pay_grade_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum salary amount for the pay grade.']
  phi_access_tier varchar [note: 'Level of protected health information access granted to the role.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of duties, responsibilities, and scope of the position.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current staffing status of the position.']
  remote_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position can be performed remotely.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications required for the position (e.g., CPC, PMP).']
  salary_band varchar [note: 'Broad salary band classification used for budgeting.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Typical work shift pattern for the position.']
  supervisory_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating depth of supervisory responsibility (e.g., 0 = individual contributor).']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the position (e.g., Senior Claims Analyst).']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'True if the role requires regular travel.']
  union_member boolean [note: 'True if the position is covered by a labor union.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the position record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all authorized positions (headcount slots) and their role classifications within the organization. Captures position title, job code, role code, role title, job family, job function, FLSA classification, EEO-1 category, pay grade (with minimum/midpoint/maximum range), FTE allocation, cost center assignment, department, required certifications, PHI access tier, and whether the position requires HIPAA privacy training. Serves as the single source of truth for both headcount management and role classification — the position entity is the authoritative owner of all role classification attributes, eliminating the need for a separate job role reference table. Supports workforce planning by tracking filled vs. vacant positions and approved headcount budgets. Serves as the classification backbone for compensation banding, RBAC provisioning, and regulatory EEO reporting. A position can be vacant or filled by multiple employees over time.'
}

Table workforce.job_role {
  job_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the job role record.']
  compensation_band varchar [note: 'Band identifier (e.g., A, B, C) used for salary planning and budgeting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which labor costs for this role are allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the role record was first created in the system.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'Equal Employment Opportunity classification used for federal reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive|managerial|professional|technical|clerical|service|labor|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the role definition is retired or superseded (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the role definition becomes effective for new hires.']
  external_system_source varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originally supplied the role record.']
  flsa_exempt_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the role is exempt from overtime under the Fair Labor Standards Act.']
  hipaa_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role must complete HIPAA privacy and security training.']
  is_clinical boolean [note: 'True if the role involves direct clinical activity or patient care.']
  is_managerial boolean [note: 'True if the role includes supervisory responsibilities.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of related roles (e.g., Clinical, Administrative, Operations).']
  job_function varchar [note: 'Specific functional area within the job family (e.g., Claims Processing, Provider Relations).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the role definition was last reviewed for accuracy or policy changes.']
  legacy_role_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the role in legacy HR systems, if applicable.']
  organization_unit varchar [note: 'Top‑level business unit or division where the role resides (e.g., Claims, Provider Relations).']
  pay_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the pay grades are expressed.']
  pay_grade_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Highest annual salary or hourly rate applicable to the role.']
  pay_grade_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lowest annual salary or hourly rate applicable to the role.']
  phi_access_tier varchar [note: 'Level of access the role has to Protected Health Information, aligned with HIPAA role‑based access controls.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of professional certifications required for the role (e.g., CPC, CPHQ).']
  role_access_level varchar [note: 'General system access level granted to the role.']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the job role across systems.']
  role_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of responsibilities, scope, and expectations for the role.']
  role_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating the role\'s position in the organizational hierarchy.']
  role_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or special considerations.']
  role_owner_department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for maintaining the role definition.']
  role_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on PHI access and business impact.']
  role_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the role definition.']
  role_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the job role as displayed to employees and managers.']
  role_version varchar [note: 'Version number incremented each time the role definition is changed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the role record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining all job roles and role families used across the health plan. Captures role code, role title, job family, job function, FLSA exemption status, EEO-1 category, minimum and maximum pay grade, required certifications, PHI access tier, and whether the role requires HIPAA privacy training. Serves as the classification backbone for compensation banding, RBAC provisioning, and regulatory EEO reporting. Distinct from position (which is a headcount slot) and employee (which is a person).'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the organizational unit.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the senior executive accountable for the unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent unit for hierarchy traversal.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the unit\'s mailing address.']
  annual_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the fiscal year, in US dollars.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent audit.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the unit\'s address.']
  compliance_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff in the unit must complete HIPAA workforce training.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center code associated with the unit for budgeting and reporting.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the unit is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the org unit record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level for data owned by the unit (restricted, confidential, internal, public).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the unit was retired or merged; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the organizational unit became active.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for unit communications.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., ERP) to reference the unit.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account number used to post payroll and expense allocations for the unit.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Number of full‑time equivalent employees assigned to the unit.']
  is_cost_center boolean [note: 'True if the unit is designated as a cost center for financial reporting.']
  is_profit_center boolean [note: 'True if the unit generates revenue and is tracked as a profit center.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal audit of the unit.']
  location varchar [note: 'Primary office or facility name where the unit operates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks about the unit.']
  org_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the unit in the hierarchy (e.g., 1 = enterprise, 2 = division).']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑wide unique code used to reference the org unit in external systems.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the unit\'s purpose and scope.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the department, division, business unit, or team.']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the unit within the corporate hierarchy.']
  payroll_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate payroll costs to the unit.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone for the unit.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the unit\'s address.']
  primary_function varchar [note: 'Core functional area of the unit (e.g., Claims Processing, Member Services).']
  rbac_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of role‑based access control applied to the unit.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the record (e.g., Facets, Workday).']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the unit\'s address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the org unit record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the record for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Master record for all organizational units — departments, divisions, business units, cost centers, and teams — within the health plan enterprise. Captures org unit name, org unit type (department, division, BU, team), parent org unit for hierarchy traversal, cost center code, GL cost center mapping, responsible executive, and effective dates. Supports payroll cost allocation, RBAC scoping, workforce planning, and financial reporting by cost center. Enables full organizational hierarchy traversal from enterprise level to team level.'
}

Table workforce.employment_record {
  employment_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the employment record.']
  primary_employment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom this record pertains.']
  primary_employment_prior_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organizational unit the employee belonged to before the action.']
  primary_employment_prior_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee\'s position before the action.']
  quaternary_employment_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who initially created the employment record.']
  tertiary_employment_hr_business_partner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR business partner assigned to manage the employee\'s case.']
  action_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the employment action becomes effective.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the employment action by the HR system.']
  action_subtype varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the action type (e.g., voluntary termination, involuntary termination, written warning).']
  action_type varchar [note: 'High-level category of the employment action (e.g., hire, promotion, disciplinary). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hire|rehire|transfer|promotion|demotion|leave_of_absence|termination|disciplinary — promote to reference product]']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal filed by the employee against the action.']
  employee_acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has acknowledged receipt of the action notice.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the incident that triggered a disciplinary action.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the employment action record.']
  new_pay_grade varchar [note: 'Pay grade or salary band of the employee after the action.']
  policy_violated varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific HR policy or code of conduct that was breached.']
  prior_pay_grade varchar [note: 'Pay grade or salary band of the employee before the action.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the action (e.g., performance, restructuring, policy violation).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employment record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employment record.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the employment record within the system.']
  suspension_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the employee is suspended, if applicable.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee\'s termination becomes effective, if the action is a termination.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the full employment lifecycle and all formal disciplinary actions for each employee. Each record represents a distinct employment action — hire, rehire, transfer, promotion, demotion, leave of absence, termination, or disciplinary action (verbal/written warnings, PIPs, suspensions, terminations for cause). Captures effective date, action type, action subtype, prior and new position, prior and new org unit, prior and new pay grade, reason code, approving manager, incident description (for disciplinary actions), policy violated, employee acknowledgment status, appeal status, HR business partner assigned, and duration (for suspensions). Provides a complete audit trail of employment history required for ERISA compliance, COBRA eligibility determination, progressive discipline tracking, wrongful termination defense, EEOC charge response, and workforce analytics. Disciplinary records are maintained with restricted access due to sensitive employment law implications. Sourced from the HRIS action history.'
}

Table workforce.compensation {
  compensation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compensation record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the compensation change.']
  compensation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom this compensation applies.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broader compensation plan to which this record belongs.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation change was approved.']
  base_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary amount for salaried employees.']
  bonus_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any bonus component.']
  bonus_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the bonus is paid.']
  bonus_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bonus.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the compensation change (e.g., promotion, market adjustment).']
  compensation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the compensation package.']
  compensation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compensation package (e.g., "2024 Merit Increase").']
  compensation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compensation record.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Category of compensation component.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center associated with the employee\'s compensation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation becomes effective.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation record expires or is superseded.']
  equity_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of equity compensation.']
  equity_type varchar [note: 'Form of equity granted.']
  equity_vesting_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of the vesting timeline for equity awards.']
  hourly_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Hourly compensation rate for non‑salaried employees.']
  incentive_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of base salary for variable incentive plans.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is exempt from overtime regulations.']
  is_flexible boolean [note: 'True if the employee participates in a flexible compensation program.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Geographic location or worksite code for the employee.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or justification.']
  overtime_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for overtime pay.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the employee is paid.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Internal grade or band used for compensation planning.']
  salary_range_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum salary for the employee\'s grade or market band.']
  salary_range_mid decimal(12,2) [note: 'Midpoint of the salary range for the employee\'s grade.']
  salary_range_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum salary for the employee\'s grade or market band.']
  shift_differential decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional percentage added for non‑standard shift work.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compensation record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each employee\'s current and historical compensation package. Captures base salary or hourly rate, pay frequency, pay grade, salary range minimum/midpoint/maximum, incentive target percentage, shift differential, overtime eligibility, effective date, and compensation change reason. Supports payroll processing, compensation equity analysis, merit cycle administration, and regulatory pay equity reporting. Maintains point-in-time compensation history to support retroactive payroll adjustments and audit requirements.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_run {
  payroll_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payroll run record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost‑center to which payroll expenses are allocated.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who executed or approved the payroll run.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health‑insurance entity that is the employer of the workforce.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional information or audit notes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  gl_posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier used when posting the payroll run to the general ledger.']
  is_manual_run boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payroll run was executed manually (true) or automatically (false).']
  pay_date date [note: 'Date on which employees are paid for this run.']
  payroll_batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch grouping multiple payroll runs for bulk processing.']
  payroll_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the recurring payroll cycle (e.g., bi‑weekly, monthly).']
  payroll_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll run.']
  payroll_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run (e.g., regular, off‑cycle, bonus).']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the payroll period covered by this run.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the payroll period covered by this run.']
  run_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the payroll processing engine executed the run.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the payroll run by the payroll system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the payroll run record.']
  total_employee_deductions decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate employee‑withheld amounts (e.g., benefits, retirement, taxes).']
  total_employer_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate employer‑paid tax amounts (e.g., FICA, FUTA) for the run.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all gross earnings before deductions for the run.']
  total_net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of net earnings after all deductions and taxes for the run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payroll run record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each payroll processing cycle executed for the health plan workforce. Captures payroll period start and end dates, pay date, payroll type (regular, off-cycle, bonus), total gross pay, total net pay, total employer tax liability, total employee deductions, payroll status (pending, processed, approved, posted), and GL posting reference. Each payroll run is the authoritative record for a pay cycle and links to individual payroll disbursements. Sourced from the payroll processing system.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_disbursement {
  payroll_disbursement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payroll disbursement line record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the employee receiving the payment.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll run (header) to which this disbursement belongs.']
  time_and_attendance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.time_and_attendance. Business justification: A payroll disbursement is calculated from a specific time_and_attendance record for an employee\'s pay period. This FK creates traceability from the payment back to the timesheet that drove it. Hours o']
  allocated_benefit_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated amount for employee benefit contributions (e.g., medical, dental).']
  allocated_employer_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer‑paid tax portion allocated to the cost center.']
  allocated_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of gross pay amount allocated to the cost center.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the payroll cost allocated to the specified cost center/LOB.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Employee\'s bank account number for direct deposit.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: '9‑digit routing number of the employee\'s bank for direct deposit.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the first clock‑in event for the employee in the period.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last clock‑out event for the employee in the period.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cost center to which payroll cost is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll disbursement record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  employee_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the employee within the organization.']
  federal_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of federal income tax withheld from gross pay.']
  fica_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Social Security (FICA) tax amount deducted.']
  gl_account_number varchar [note: 'GL account number used for posting payroll expense.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions or taxes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the payroll run.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Business line to which the payroll cost is attributed.']
  manager_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the manager approved the timesheet.']
  medicare_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Medicare tax amount deducted.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the employee after all deductions.']
  other_deductions_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of voluntary and involuntary deductions (e.g., 401k, health premiums).']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours (typically >40 per week) recorded for the employee.']
  pay_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the net pay was disbursed.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by this disbursement.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by this disbursement.']
  pay_stub_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference printed on the employee\'s pay stub.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver net pay to the employee.']
  payroll_disbursement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the disbursement (e.g., regular salary, bonus).']
  payroll_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payroll disbursement.']
  pto_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Paid time‑off hours used during the pay period.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of regular (non‑overtime) hours recorded for the employee in the pay period.']
  shift_differential_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked that qualify for shift‑differential pay (e.g., night shift).']
  sick_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sick‑leave hours taken in the pay period.']
  state_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of state income tax withheld from gross pay.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is exempt from certain taxes.']
  timesheet_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the timesheet was approved by the employee.']
  timesheet_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the employee\'s timesheet.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Individual employee-level payroll detail record within a payroll run, serving as the single reconciliation surface from time entry through pay calculation to cost distribution. Captures time/attendance inputs (regular and overtime hours worked, PTO/sick time used, shift differentials, absence codes, clock-in/clock-out timestamps, timesheet approval status, manager approval date), pay calculation (employee gross pay, federal and state tax withholdings, FICA, voluntary deductions for medical/dental/vision/401k/FSA/HSA, net pay, payment method, bank routing and account reference, pay stub reference number), and cost allocation distribution (cost center, GL account, LOB allocation with allocation percentages and allocated amounts for gross pay, employer tax, and benefit cost). Provides the line-level detail supporting payroll reconciliation, W-2 preparation, employee pay stub generation, FLSA overtime compliance, PMPM cost analysis, departmental budget variance reporting, and financial cost center attribution. Links to payroll_run for cycle-level aggregation.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_cost_allocation {
  payroll_cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each payroll cost allocation line.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department that owns the cost center.']
  payroll_disbursement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.payroll_disbursement. Business justification: Payroll cost allocations split a specific employee\'s pay across cost centers. While payroll_run_id + primary_payroll_employee_id implicitly identify the disbursement, a direct FK to payroll_disburseme']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll run to which this allocation belongs.']
  primary_payroll_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee whose payroll cost is being allocated.']
  tertiary_payroll_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user responsible for the most recent change.']
  allocated_benefit_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of employee benefits allocated to the cost center.']
  allocated_employer_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employer-paid tax (e.g., Social Security, Medicare) allocated to the cost center.']
  allocated_gross_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Portion of the employee\'s gross wages allocated to the cost center.']
  allocation_amount_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of gross pay, employer tax, and benefit cost allocated to the cost center.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the allocation entry was created.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of the employee\'s total payroll cost assigned to this cost center (expressed as a percent).']
  allocation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason driving the allocation (e.g., regular payroll, bonus, correction).']
  allocation_sequence varchar [note: 'Line order of the allocation within the payroll run for deterministic processing.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the allocation record.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the cost center to which the payroll cost is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the allocation line was inserted into the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation ceases to be effective; null indicates open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation becomes effective for reporting purposes.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Chart of accounts code that receives the allocated payroll expense.']
  is_manual_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the allocation was a manual correction rather than an automated calculation.']
  lob_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business line (e.g., Medical, Dental, Vision) for cost attribution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional textual remarks providing context for the allocation.']
  payroll_period varchar [note: 'Year‑month string representing the payroll period for this allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system (e.g., HealthEdge, Custom Billing).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the last modification to the allocation line.']

  Note: 'Transactional record distributing payroll costs across cost centers, GL accounts, and lines of business (LOB) for each payroll run. Captures employee, payroll run, cost center, GL account code, LOB allocation, department, allocated gross pay amount, allocated employer tax amount, allocated benefit cost, and allocation percentage. Critical for financial reporting, PMPM cost analysis, and departmental budget variance reporting. Enables the finance domain to accurately attribute workforce costs to operational cost centers.'
}

Table workforce.employee_benefit_enrollment {
  employee_benefit_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each employee benefit enrollment record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who is enrolling in the benefit.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the benefit plan being elected.']
  benefit_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which the enrollment belongs.']
  cobra_end_date date [note: 'Date when COBRA coverage would terminate, if applicable.']
  cobra_start_date date [note: 'Date when COBRA coverage would begin, if applicable.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'How often contributions are deducted from payroll.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Level of coverage selected for the employee.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for contribution amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the elected coverage becomes active.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount the employee contributes toward the benefit.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount the employer contributes toward the benefit.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'External enrollment reference number used in communications and reporting.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the enrollment action.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the enrollment.']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the enrollment transaction was recorded.']
  is_aca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment satisfies ACA employer‑mandate reporting requirements.']
  is_cobra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the enrollment record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional information or comments about the enrollment.']
  payroll_deduction_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used by payroll to apply the contribution.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the benefit (e.g., Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, Disability, Retirement, FSA, HSA, HRA, EAP).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the coverage ends, if applicable.']
  total_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of employee and employer contributions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each employee\'s enrollment in employer-sponsored benefit plans — medical, dental, vision, life insurance, disability (STD/LTD), 401k, FSA, HSA, HRA, and EAP. Captures plan type, coverage tier (employee only, employee + spouse, family), effective date, termination date, employee contribution amount, employer contribution amount, and enrollment source (open enrollment, new hire, qualifying life event). Distinct from member enrollment (which governs insured members) — this tracks the employee as a benefits consumer. Supports COBRA administration and ACA employer mandate reporting.'
}

Table workforce.leave_request {
  leave_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the leave request record.']
  primary_leave_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee submitting the leave request.']
  accommodation_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any ADA or other accommodations requested.']
  ada_accommodation_required boolean [note: 'True if the leave request includes a disability accommodation under the ADA.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was approved or denied.']
  approved_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of leave days approved.']
  approved_end_date date [note: 'Last day of leave as approved.']
  approved_start_date date [note: 'First day of leave as approved by HR/manager.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the leave cost (if any) is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the leave request record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when a leave request is denied.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day the employee wishes to be on leave.']
  fmla_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee meets eligibility criteria for FMLA leave.']
  intermittent boolean [note: 'True if the leave is to be taken on an intermittent schedule.']
  leave_balance_after decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected leave balance after the approved leave is deducted.']
  leave_balance_before decimal(10,2) [note: 'Accrued leave balance (in days) available to the employee before this request.']
  leave_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the specific leave policy governing this request.']
  leave_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leave request.']
  leave_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave being requested (e.g., FMLA, personal, military, parental, bereavement, disability).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or remarks entered by the employee or reviewer.']
  payroll_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount (in USD) that will be deducted or added to payroll due to the leave.']
  payroll_impact boolean [note: 'True if the approved leave affects payroll calculations (e.g., paid vs unpaid).']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the leave request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the leave request was initially submitted.']
  requested_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of leave days requested (including fractions).']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Date the employee is scheduled to resume regular duties after leave.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day the employee wishes to be on leave.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system name that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the leave request record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system name that created the leave request record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for all employee leave requests and approved leave events — FMLA, ADA accommodations, personal leave, military leave (USERRA), parental leave, bereavement, and short-term disability. Captures leave type, requested start and end dates, approved start and end dates, intermittent leave indicator, FMLA eligibility determination, ADA accommodation details, leave status (requested, approved, denied, in-progress, closed), and return-to-work date. Supports FMLA compliance tracking, STD/LTD coordination, and workforce availability planning.'
}

Table workforce.performance_review {

  merit_increase_recommendation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Proposed monetary merit increase based on the review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra comments or observations.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Aggregated rating of employee performance for the cycle.']

  review_cycle_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the review cycle for the employee (e.g., 1 for first annual).']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the review must be completed.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the review cycle (e.g., PR‑2024‑001).']
  review_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the performance review period.']
  review_period_start date [note: 'First day of the performance review period.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the performance review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review cycle (e.g., annual, mid‑year, probationary).']
  salary_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment to base salary resulting from the review.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each formal employee performance evaluation cycle. Captures review period, review type (annual, mid-year, probationary, PIP), overall performance rating, individual goal ratings, competency ratings, manager narrative, employee self-assessment reference, calibration status, merit increase recommendation, and review completion date. Supports merit cycle administration, succession planning, and performance improvement plan (PIP) tracking. Links to compensation for merit-based pay adjustments.'
}

Table workforce.training_record {
  training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the training record.']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the training course.']
  primary_training_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who completed the training.']

  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved on the training assessment (0-100).']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the employee completed the training.']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the training course (e.g., HIPAA-PRIV).']
  course_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the training course.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the training.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the training certification expires and must be renewed.']
  is_expired boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the training certification has expired.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments about the training record.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employee passed the training assessment.']
  recertification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee must recertify the training after expiration.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training record.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the training record (e.g., LMS, HRIS).']
  trainer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the trainer or instructor.']
  training_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours of instruction completed for the training.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the training was delivered.']
  training_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the training record for external reference.']
  training_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Category of training required for compliance or development. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HIPAA Privacy|HIPAA Security|Fraud Waste and Abuse|Compliance|Clinical|Leadership|Technical — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking completion of mandatory and elective training courses for each employee. Captures course name, course code, training type (HIPAA Privacy, HIPAA Security, Fraud Waste and Abuse, Compliance, Clinical, Leadership, Technical), delivery method (eLearning, instructor-led, webinar), completion date, expiration date, pass/fail status, assessment score, and training hours. HIPAA workforce training compliance requires annual completion tracking for all employees with PHI access. Supports NCQA accreditation, CMS audit readiness, and state DOI compliance.'
}

Table workforce.workforce_certification {
  workforce_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each certification record held by an employee.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who holds the certification.']
  certification_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level classification of the credential (e.g., license, certificate).']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Standard code or abbreviation assigned by the issuing body (e.g., RN, CPC).']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the certification or license (e.g., Registered Nurse, Certified Health Coach).']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique number assigned by the issuing organization to this certification instance.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the certification indicating its functional domain.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred to obtain or maintain the certification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the certification cost (e.g., USD).']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Path or identifier to the stored digital copy of the certification document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the certification becomes effective for role eligibility (may differ from issue date).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires and must be renewed or re‑validated.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates if the certification is required for the employee\'s current role.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was originally awarded to the employee.']
  issuing_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the authority or body that granted the certification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the certification.']
  renewal_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewal events.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date the most recent renewal was completed.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification must be renewed periodically.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current renewal state of the certification.']
  required_for_role varchar [note: 'Job role or function that mandates this certification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date the certification was last verified for accuracy and authenticity.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent verification process.']
  workforce_certification_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the certification record.']

  Note: 'Master record for professional certifications and licenses held by health plan employees — clinical certifications (RN, LCSW, CCM, CPC), compliance certifications (CHC, CHPC), IT certifications, and state insurance licenses. Captures certification type, issuing body, certification number, issue date, expiration date, renewal status, and verification date. Distinct from provider credentialing (which governs external clinical staff) — this tracks internal workforce credentials required for role eligibility and regulatory compliance. Supports NCQA care management staffing standards.'
}

Table workforce.rbac_assignment {
  rbac_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the RBAC assignment record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the assignment record.']
  primary_rbac_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom access is granted.']
  role_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the security role assigned to the employee.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the latest update.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access granted (e.g., read‑only, write, administrative).']
  access_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the business reason for the access (e.g., CLAIM_PROCESSING, MEMBER_SERVICE).']
  access_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the data or functional scope covered by the assignment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the access request was approved.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why the employee requires this access, supporting HIPAA minimum‑necessary compliance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the assignment, such as audit observations.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the access (e.g., HIPAA, SOC 2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RBAC assignment record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the access becomes active.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the access is scheduled to expire (null if indefinite).']
  is_temporary boolean [note: 'True if the access is granted for a limited, temporary period.']
  rbac_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date when the access was actually revoked, if earlier than expiration.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the access based on sensitivity of data.']
  system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the system or application for which the role provides access.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']

  Note: 'Association record governing role-based access control (RBAC) assignments for employees accessing PHI/PII systems. Captures employee, assigned role, system or application name, access level, business justification, approving manager, effective date, expiration date, and access revocation date. Critical for HIPAA minimum necessary standard compliance — ensures employees only access PHI required for their job function. Supports OCR audit readiness, SOC 2 compliance, and access recertification campaigns. Owned by the workforce domain as the authoritative source for workforce access rights.'
}

Table workforce.headcount_plan {
  headcount_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the workforce headcount plan record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the plan.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Headcount plans are created per organizational unit. The plan has org_unit_code (STRING) but no proper FK to org_unit. Adding org_unit_id enables joining to get full org unit details (budget, location']
  primary_headcount_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the hire.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified the plan.']
  actual_headcount varchar [note: 'Headcount actually employed at the end of the period.']
  application_deadline date [note: 'Last date candidates may submit applications.']
  approved_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of full‑time equivalent positions approved in the plan.']
  budget_variance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual headcount cost (currency).']
  budget_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between budgeted and actual headcount cost.']
  budgeted_headcount varchar [note: 'Total headcount budgeted for the planning period.']
  candidate_start_date date [note: 'First day of employment for the hired candidate.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal compliance review for the headcount plan.']
  contract_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of contingent or contract workers included in the plan.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which headcount costs are allocated.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the department within the org unit that the plan targets.']
  diversity_hiring_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the hire satisfies diversity hiring goals.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the headcount plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the headcount plan expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  filled_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of FTEs that have been filled to date.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which the plan applies, expressed as a four‑digit year.']
  headcount_variance varchar [note: 'Numeric difference between approved FTE and actual filled FTE.']
  interview_stages_completed varchar [note: 'Number of interview rounds completed for the requisition.']
  is_contractor boolean [note: 'True if the position is filled by a contractor or contingent worker.']
  job_posting_date date [note: 'Date the position was posted to the job market.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the plan.']
  number_of_applicants varchar [note: 'Total count of candidates who applied for the position.']
  offer_accepted_date date [note: 'Date the candidate accepted the job offer.']
  offer_extended_date date [note: 'Date an offer was extended to the selected candidate.']
  plan_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the headcount plan record was initially created.']
  plan_identifier varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier or code for the headcount plan.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the headcount plan.']
  plan_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the plan record.']
  planning_period varchar [note: 'Frequency of the planning horizon for the headcount plan.']
  position_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the position being recruited.']
  recruitment_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the recruitment cycle (e.g., Q1_2024).']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the recruitment requisition linked to the plan.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recruitment requisition.']
  source_of_hire varchar [note: 'Channel through which the candidate was sourced.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from posting to start date.']
  vacant_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of FTE positions still vacant.']

  Note: 'Master record for approved workforce headcount plans and the full recruitment lifecycle by org unit, department, and fiscal year. Captures workforce planning attributes (planning period, org unit, approved FTE count, filled FTE count, vacant FTE count, budgeted headcount, actual headcount, contract/contingent headcount, plan status) and recruitment execution attributes (requisition number, position being filled, hiring manager, recruiter assigned, job posting date, application deadline, number of applicants, interview stages completed, offer extended date, offer accepted date, candidate start date, time-to-fill metric, source of hire, requisition status, and diversity hiring indicators). Supports annual workforce planning cycles, budget variance analysis, executive headcount reporting, recruiter productivity tracking, diversity hiring reporting, and planned-vs-actual staffing comparison across cost centers and lines of business.'
}

Table workforce.workforce_recruitment {
  workforce_recruitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the recruitment record.']
  headcount_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.headcount_plan. Business justification: A recruitment event fulfills an approved headcount plan slot. One headcount plan can spawn multiple recruitment events (e.g., plan approves 5 FTEs, each gets its own requisition). This FK links the ta']
  job_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_role. Business justification: A recruitment requisition is for a specific job role. Linking to job_role enables deriving pay grade ranges, FLSA status, required certifications, and PHI access tier from the authoritative job_role m']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: A recruitment requisition belongs to a specific organizational unit. While department_code and cost_center_code exist as strings, they represent different coding schemes than org_unit\'s primary key. A']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: A recruitment requisition is opened to fill a specific authorized position (headcount slot). Linking workforce_recruitment to position enables tracking which position each recruitment event is filling']
  primary_workforce_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the hire.']
  quaternary_workforce_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who performed the latest update.']
  tertiary_workforce_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the record.']
  application_deadline date [note: 'Last date candidates may submit applications.']
  candidate_start_date date [note: 'First day the hired candidate is scheduled to begin work.']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compensation model for the role.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the position\'s salary will be charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the recruitment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for salary amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the department owning the requisition.']
  interview_stage_completed varchar [note: 'Most advanced interview stage completed for the requisition.']
  is_remote boolean [note: 'True if the position is designated as remote or hybrid.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level of the position (e.g., IC3, Manager, Director).']
  job_posting_date date [note: 'Date the position was posted to internal/external channels.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the primary work location (city/office).']
  net_salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Take‑home salary after standard deductions, as communicated to the candidate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or observations about the requisition.']
  number_of_applicants varchar [note: 'Count of distinct candidates who applied to the requisition.']
  offer_accepted_date date [note: 'Date the candidate accepted the employment offer.']
  offer_extended_date date [note: 'Date an employment offer was formally extended to the selected candidate.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the open requisition.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition.']
  salary_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional monetary adjustments (e.g., sign‑on bonus) attached to the offer.']
  salary_offer_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross annual salary amount offered to the candidate.']
  source_of_hire varchar [note: 'Channel through which the candidate was sourced.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from posting to candidate start date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the recruitment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each open requisition and recruitment event in the talent acquisition lifecycle. Captures requisition number, position being filled, hiring manager, recruiter assigned, job posting date, application deadline, number of applicants, interview stages completed, offer extended date, offer accepted date, candidate start date, time-to-fill metric, source of hire, and requisition status (open, on-hold, filled, cancelled). Supports workforce planning, recruiter productivity tracking, and diversity hiring reporting.'
}

Table workforce.disciplinary_action {
  disciplinary_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the disciplinary action record.']

  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee acknowledged the action.']
  action_date date [note: 'Date the disciplinary action was formally taken.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the disciplinary action.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Category of disciplinary action taken.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Last date the employee may file an appeal.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal process.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal filed against the disciplinary action.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disciplinary action record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount.']
  disciplinary_action_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the disciplinary action.']
  disciplinary_action_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the disciplinary action record.']
  documentation_attached boolean [note: 'True if supporting documentation is attached to the record.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the disciplinary action lasts (applicable to suspensions).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the disciplinary action becomes effective (e.g., start of suspension).']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the incident that triggered the disciplinary action.']
  is_repeat_offense boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a repeat disciplinary offense for the employee.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments or observations related to the disciplinary action.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty associated with the disciplinary action, if any.']
  policy_violated varchar [note: 'Name or code of the company policy that was breached.']
  prior_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of prior disciplinary actions recorded for the employee.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the record for data management purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the disciplinary action originated.']
  suspension_end_date date [note: 'Date a suspension period ends; null if not a suspension.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for formal employee disciplinary actions — verbal warnings, written warnings, performance improvement plans (PIPs), suspensions, and terminations for cause. Captures action type, incident description, policy violated, action date, effective date, duration (for suspensions), HR business partner assigned, manager of record, employee acknowledgment status, and appeal status. Supports progressive discipline tracking, wrongful termination defense, and EEOC charge response. Maintained with restricted access due to sensitive employment law implications.'
}

Table workforce.compliance_event {
  compliance_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each workforce compliance event record.']
  background_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.background_check. Business justification: Compliance events that are triggered by background screening results should reference the specific background_check record. The compliance_event duplicates screening_result, adverse_action_reason, and']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee subject to the compliance event.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Regulatory compliance events must record the screening vendor that performed the check for audit and reporting.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date the compliance activity was completed.']
  compliance_event_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the compliance event.']
  compliance_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the compliance activity after evaluation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance event record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the compliance activity must be completed.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the compliance event (e.g., "HC-2024-00123").']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the compliance event was recorded or became effective.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the compliance event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the compliance event.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date the screening request was placed with the vendor.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Corrective steps taken when the event is non‑compliant.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Individual or role accountable for ensuring the compliance event is addressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the compliance event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all workforce-related regulatory compliance events — HIPAA workforce training deadlines, background screening (criminal history, OIG exclusion, SAM debarment, credit checks, education/employment verification), I-9 re-verification, annual compliance attestations, conflict of interest disclosures, and mandatory reporting obligations. Captures event type, event subtype, due date, completion date, compliance status (compliant, non-compliant, pending, waived), screening vendor, order date, screening result (clear, flagged, adverse action), adverse action reason, adjudication decision, responsible party, and remediation action if non-compliant. OIG exclusion screening is mandatory for all employees with access to federal healthcare programs — non-compliance creates False Claims Act exposure. Supports CMS audit readiness, NCQA accreditation, state DOI market conduct exams, state DOI licensing requirements, and internal compliance program management.'
}

Table workforce.background_check {
  background_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the background check record.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee subject to the background check.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last updated the record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the screening vendor.']
  adjudication_decision varchar [note: 'Final HR decision based on the screening result.']
  adverse_action_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any adverse action taken as a result of the screening.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the background screening process.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the screening request.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the background check was completed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the screening with CMS and DOI requirements.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost charged for the background screening.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the screening cost (e.g., USD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the background check record was first created in the system.']
  is_federal_program_access boolean [note: 'True if the employee has access to Medicare/Medicaid programs, triggering mandatory screening.']
  is_oig_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Result of the OIG exclusion check (true if the employee is on the exclusion list).']
  is_oig_exclusion_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OIG exclusion screening is mandatory for the employee.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the screening.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the background check was ordered.']
  result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the background check.']
  screening_type varchar [note: 'Category of background screening performed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system that originated the background check record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the background check record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for pre-employment and periodic background screening events for health plan employees. Captures screening type (criminal, OIG exclusion, SAM debarment, credit, education verification, employment verification), screening vendor, order date, completion date, result (clear, flagged, adverse action), adverse action reason, and adjudication decision. OIG exclusion screening is mandatory for all employees with access to federal healthcare programs (Medicare, Medicaid) — non-compliance creates False Claims Act exposure. Supports CMS compliance and state DOI licensing requirements.'
}

Table workforce.time_and_attendance {
  time_and_attendance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each time and attendance record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom the time record belongs.']
  absence_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the type of absence for the record.']
  absence_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context for the absence.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time the employee clocked in for a shift.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time the employee clocked out for a shift.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cost center to which labor costs are allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the time and attendance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department identifier for the employee.']
  flsa_compliance boolean [note: 'True if the recorded hours comply with Fair Labor Standards Act regulations.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total earnings before taxes and deductions for the period.']
  holiday_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours recorded for paid holidays.']
  job_role_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the employee\'s job role or title.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the physical location where the employee performed the work.']
  manager_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval state of the timesheet as indicated by the employee\'s manager.']
  manager_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manager approved or rejected the timesheet.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the employee after taxes and deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks associated with the record.']
  overtime_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for overtime pay under FLSA.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond the standard schedule that are payable at an overtime rate.']
  pay_period_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code representing the specific pay period (e.g., 2023-09).']
  payroll_integration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the timesheet has been successfully sent to payroll processing.']
  payroll_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when payroll system completed processing for this timesheet.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by the record.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by the record.']
  pto_used_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours deducted from the employee\'s accrued PTO balance for the period.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked at the standard rate, excluding overtime and other premiums.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the scheduled shift (e.g., DAY, NIGHT).']
  shift_differential_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked during shifts that attract a differential premium (e.g., night shift).']
  sick_hours_used decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours taken as sick leave during the period.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the employee submitted the timesheet.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax withheld from the gross pay for the period.']
  time_and_attendance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the timesheet.']
  time_entry_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device that recorded the time entry.']
  time_entry_error_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any error or exception captured during time entry.']
  time_entry_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the time entry.']
  time_entry_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the time entry record (e.g., badge swipe, web portal).']
  time_entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the time entry (regular, overtime, PTO, etc.).']
  timesheet_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the timesheet for tracking and reference.']
  total_hours_worked decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate number of hours (including all categories) recorded for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing employee time and attendance data for each pay period — regular hours, overtime hours, PTO used, sick time used, holiday hours, shift differentials, and absence codes. Captures clock-in/clock-out timestamps, approved timesheet status, manager approval date, and payroll integration status. Supports FLSA overtime compliance, PTO accrual balance management, and payroll processing accuracy. Feeds directly into payroll_disbursement calculations for hourly and non-exempt employees.'
}

Table workforce.compensation_plan {
  compensation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for compensation_plan']
  parent_compensation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compensation_plan (parent_compensation_plan_id)']
  base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed base salary or compensation amount specified in the plan.']
  benefit_plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the associated benefit plan.']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Specific bonus amount allocated within the plan.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate expressed as a percentage for sales or incentive compensation.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for monetary amounts in the plan.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan ceases to be effective. Null indicates open-ended.']
  equity_type varchar [note: 'Type of equity instrument used in the plan.']
  equity_units varchar [note: 'Number of equity units (e.g., shares, options) granted under the plan.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Category of the plan within the compensation structure.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the compensation plan within the organization.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the compensation plan, including its purpose and scope.']
  plan_document_checksum varchar [note: 'MD5 checksum of the compensation plan document for integrity verification.']
  plan_document_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation plan document was first created.']
  plan_document_format varchar [note: 'Format of the compensation plan document, e.g., PDF, DOCX.']
  plan_document_language varchar [note: 'Human-readable language of the compensation plan document.']
  plan_document_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter code for the document language.']
  plan_document_size varchar [note: 'Size of the compensation plan document in bytes.']
  plan_document_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the compensation plan document.']
  plan_document_type varchar [note: 'File format of the compensation plan document.']
  plan_document_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compensation plan document.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'URL pointing to the electronic document that defines the compensation plan.']
  plan_document_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the compensation plan document.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the compensation plan.']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the compensation plan.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compensation plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of compensation covered by the plan.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the compensation plan, incremented on each update.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compensation plan record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest update.']
  variable_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Variable component of compensation, such as performance bonus or incentive.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the compensation plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for compensation_plan. Referenced by plan_id.'
}
