Table workforce.rbac_role {
  rbac_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rbac_role']
  parent_rbac_role_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rbac_role (parent_rbac_role_id)']
  role_parent_rbac_role_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent role for hierarchical role structures.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the role record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the role becomes active in the system.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the role ceases to be active; null if open-ended.']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code used to identify the role in business processes.']
  role_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the role\'s purpose and responsibilities.']
  role_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of roles for organizational or functional purposes.']
  role_is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is currently active.']
  role_is_archived boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role has been archived.']
  role_is_current boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is the current version in use.']
  role_is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is the default for a given context.']
  role_is_deleted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role has been deleted.']
  role_is_external boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is intended for external users.']
  role_is_internal boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is intended for internal use.']
  role_is_legacy boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is legacy and may be phased out.']
  role_is_persistent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is persistent.']
  role_is_retired boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role has been retired.']
  role_is_revoked boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role has been revoked.']
  role_is_system boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is system-defined.']
  role_is_temporary boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is temporary.']
  role_is_transient boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the role is transient.']
  role_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating role hierarchy depth.']
  role_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the role.']
  role_revocation_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the revocation.']
  role_revocation_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revocation record was created.']
  role_revocation_created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the revocation record.']
  role_revocation_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the role was revoked.']
  role_revocation_effective_from date [note: 'Date when the revocation becomes effective.']
  role_revocation_effective_until date [note: 'Date when the revocation ends; null if open-ended.']
  role_revocation_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes regarding the revocation.']
  role_revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for revoking the role.']
  role_revocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the revocation process.']
  role_revocation_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revocation record.']
  role_revocation_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the revocation record.']
  role_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the role (e.g., global, department, team).']
  role_scope_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the role\'s scope.']
  role_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the role.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the role (e.g., system, business, admin, user, guest).']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the role record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the role.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the role.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for rbac_role. Referenced by role_id.'
}

Table workforce.training_course {
  training_course_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training_course']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the training course record.']
  parent_training_course_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_course (parent_training_course_id)']
  training_course_certification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the certification awarded.']
  training_course_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the training course record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the training course provider.']
  training_course_certification_awarded varchar [note: 'Indicates if a certification is awarded upon completion.']
  training_course_certification_validity_end date [note: 'End date of certification validity period.']
  training_course_certification_validity_start date [note: 'Start date of certification validity period.']
  training_course_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the training course.']
  training_course_compliance_due_date date [note: 'Due date for compliance completion of the training course.']
  training_course_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the training course relative to required standards.']
  training_course_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of delivering the training course.']
  training_course_cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the training course.']
  training_course_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the training course cost.']
  training_course_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training course record was created.']
  training_course_delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Delivery mode of the training course.']
  training_course_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the training course content.']
  training_course_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the training course in hours.']
  training_course_end_date date [note: 'Date when the training course is no longer available.']
  training_course_format varchar [note: 'Format of the training course delivery.']
  training_course_language varchar [note: 'Primary language of the training course content.']
  training_course_learning_objectives varchar [note: 'Learning objectives and outcomes of the training course.']
  training_course_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the training course.']
  training_course_prerequisites varchar [note: 'Prerequisite courses or conditions required before enrolling.']
  training_course_provider_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the training course provider.']
  training_course_provider_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the training course provider.']
  training_course_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the training course provider.']
  training_course_provider_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the training course provider.']
  training_course_required_by varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal requirement that mandates the training.']
  training_course_start_date date [note: 'Date when the training course becomes available.']
  training_course_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training course.']
  training_course_type varchar [note: 'Category of the training course (e.g., mandatory, optional).']
  training_course_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training course record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for training_course. Referenced by course_id.'
}

Table vendor.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor master record.']
  parent_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor (parent_vendor_id)']
  business_category varchar [note: 'More specific business line or market segment of the vendor.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance standing of the vendor with internal policies and regulations.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date the contract became legally effective.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the contract expires or is scheduled for renewal.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the master contract with the vendor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was first created in the system.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned by the finance team. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — promote to reference product]']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA under which the vendor operates.']
  default_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for invoicing the vendor.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Unique DUNS identifier for the vendor used in credit and risk assessments.']
  incorporation_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state (or jurisdiction) where the vendor is legally incorporated.']
  insurance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of insurance certificates held by the vendor (e.g., liability, workers\' comp).']
  last_risk_assessment_date date [note: 'Date the most recent risk assessment was performed.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the vendor as registered with government authorities.']
  minority_owned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as minority‑owned.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor onboarding process.']
  ownership_structure varchar [note: 'Legal ownership model of the vendor organization.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the vendor.']
  primary_naics_code varchar [note: 'Six‑digit North American Industry Classification System code representing the vendor\'s primary industry.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor relationship ended or is scheduled to end; null if ongoing.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor relationship officially began.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) derived from assessments.']
  secondary_naics_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of additional NAICS codes applicable to the vendor.']
  small_business_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor qualifies as a small business under SBA criteria.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system for the vendor master data (e.g., Facets, Cactus).']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Federal employer identification number used for tax reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vendor record.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the vendor relationship.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic importance tier used for sourcing and governance.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the nature of goods or services supplied.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address for the vendor.']
  women_owned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as women‑owned.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all non-provider third-party vendors and suppliers engaged by the health plan — IT service providers, office supply vendors, facilities management companies, consulting firms, print/mail houses, staffing agencies, and other business partners. Captures vendor legal name, DBA name, TIN/EIN, DUNS number, vendor type classification, business category, incorporation state, ownership structure (minority/women-owned, small business), primary NAICS code, vendor tier (strategic, preferred, approved, conditional), onboarding status, active/inactive flag, and relationship inception date. This is the SSOT for vendor identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_contact {
  vendor_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor contact record.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: Contact records for credentialing service vendors must be linked to the delegated entity they serve.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Vendor management assigns an internal employee as primary liaison; needed for responsibility and escalation reports.']
  parent_vendor_contact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_contact (parent_vendor_contact_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor organization to which this contact belongs.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the contact.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the contact\'s address.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance certification state of the contact with respect to regulatory and contractual requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the contact\'s address. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor contact record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department within the vendor company where the contact works.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for communication with the vendor contact.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the contact relationship ended or is scheduled to end; null if still active.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the vendor contact person.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the vendor.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name of the vendor contact person.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vendor contact record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the contact, such as special instructions or historical observations.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the vendor contact, including country code.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the contact\'s address.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel the contact prefers for ongoing communications.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for initial outreach to the contact.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0‑100) representing the risk level associated with this contact based on vendor risk assessments.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Business function the contact fulfills for the vendor relationship.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the contact relationship with the vendor began.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the contact\'s address.']
  title varchar [note: 'Job title or professional designation of the vendor contact.']
  vendor_contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all contacts associated with a vendor organization — account managers, sales representatives, technical leads, billing contacts, legal contacts, and executive sponsors. Captures contact name, title, department, phone, email, preferred communication channel, contact role type, primary/secondary flag, and active status. Supports multi-contact vendor relationships and tracks the appropriate point of contact for each business function (contract negotiations, invoice disputes, incident escalation).'
}

Table vendor.vendor_address {
  vendor_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each vendor address record.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: Physical address of a credentialing vendor is tied to the delegated entity for logistics and compliance.']
  parent_vendor_address_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_address (parent_vendor_address_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor to which this address belongs.']
  address_description varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form description or notes about the address.']
  address_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the address (e.g., "Main Campus", "Billing Office").']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose for the vendor.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the address was added, changed, or deactivated.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the address.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the address location.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the address location.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the address.']
  county varchar [note: 'County or district of the address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the address becomes effective for business use.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the address ceases to be effective (null if still active).']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number associated with the address location.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Confidence level of the geocoding result.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this address is the current active address for the vendor.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates if this address is the primary address for the vendor.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the address was most recently verified.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the address location.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the address location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the address record (e.g., Facets, Cactus).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the address.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., "America/New_York").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  validation_source varchar [note: 'Source system or service used to validate the address.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of address validation checks.']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from the verification process.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing addresses associated with a vendor, including headquarters, billing address, remittance address, and service delivery locations. Captures address type, street lines, city, state, ZIP+4, country, county, address validation status, effective date, and end date. Supports multi-location vendors and tracks address history for remittance accuracy and regulatory correspondence.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_contract {
  vendor_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the vendor contract record.']
  business_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract ownership is vested in a specific employee; linking enables ownership audit and approval workflow.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Contracts must reference the specific regulatory obligations they satisfy, supporting compliance reporting and audit of contractual obligations.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: Contract management process links each credentialing service contract to the specific delegated credentialing entity.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Contract Management report requires linking each vendor contract to the employer group that signed it for compliance and renewal tracking.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Tracks which health plan each vendor contract applies to for regulatory filing and financial reporting.']
  parent_vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_contract (parent_vendor_contract_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Provider Contract Management report requires linking each vendor contract to the specific provider it serves.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Vendor Contract Management per Provider Network links contracts to the specific network they serve, required for network‑level compliance reporting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor party associated with this contract.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments made to the contract.']
  annual_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected yearly spend under the contract.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiration.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of certifications (e.g., HIPAA, SOC 2) the vendor holds to satisfy regulatory requirements.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the contract information.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the contract scope and purpose.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the health plan or vendor.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement defining its legal and operational nature.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the contract record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  data_security_requirements varchar [note: 'Security controls the vendor must adhere to (e.g., encryption, access controls).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the contract becomes legally binding.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date the contract was put into effect (may differ from signed date).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the contract ends unless renewed or terminated earlier.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'State or jurisdiction whose law governs the contract.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Minimum insurance coverage the vendor must maintain (e.g., liability, workers\' comp).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the vendor is the sole provider for the contracted service.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is required for core business operations.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contract amendment.']
  notice_sent_date date [note: 'Date the renewal notice was transmitted.']
  notice_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a renewal notice has been sent to the vendor.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment schedule and conditions.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Terms describing penalties for non‑performance or early termination.']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Key performance indicator used to evaluate vendor performance under the contract.']
  regulatory_compliance varchar [note: 'Regulatory frameworks (HIPAA, PCI, etc.) the contract must satisfy.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration when a renewal notice must be issued.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Method by which the contract may be renewed.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating assigned to the vendor contract, where 1 is low risk and 5 is high risk.']
  service_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of services covered by the contract.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the contract was signed by all parties.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., pending legal review).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the contract was terminated before its expiration, if applicable.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the contract for easy identification.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Overall monetary value of the contract over its full term.']
  url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic copy of the signed contract.']
  vendor_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label for the contract document (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Master record for all contracts and agreements executed between the health plan and third-party vendors — MSAs (Master Service Agreements), SOWs (Statements of Work), SaaS subscription agreements, maintenance agreements, and professional services contracts. Captures contract number, contract type, effective date, expiration date, auto-renewal flag, renewal notice period, total contract value (TCV), annual contract value (ACV), payment terms, governing law, contract status (draft, executed, expired, terminated), and the business owner responsible for the contract. Distinct from provider contracts (owned by the contract domain) — this entity covers non-clinical vendor relationships only.'
}

Table vendor.contract_term {
  contract_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor contract term record.']
  parent_contract_term_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract_term (parent_contract_term_id)']
  responsible_party_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each contract term has a responsible internal employee; required for term‑ownership tracking and compliance reporting.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent vendor contract to which this term belongs.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the amendment that introduced or modified this term.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'True if the term is set to auto‑renew at the end of its period.']
  clause_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier of the clause within the contract (e.g., 1.2, 3.4).']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory regime governing the term, if any.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'True if the term is subject to regulatory compliance (e.g., HIPAA BAA).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the responsible party for this term.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the responsible party for this term.']
  contract_term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the term.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the term record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the penalty amount.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the term expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the term becomes binding.']
  escalation_procedure varchar [note: 'Description of the process to follow when the term is breached.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the term is mandatory (vs. optional).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the legal jurisdiction governing the term. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|... — promote to reference product]']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the term was last reviewed for compliance or performance.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Methodology or tool used to capture the performance metric (e.g., monitoring system, manual report).']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied if the term is breached.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative detail of the penalty calculation and enforcement.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the penalty is a fixed sum, a percentage of invoice, or none.']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Name of the metric used to measure compliance with the term (e.g., uptime, response time).']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that renewal notice must be given.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the term renews automatically, manually, or not at all.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews of the term.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the term based on compliance and financial impact.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Desired performance level that the vendor aims to achieve (e.g., 99.9).']
  term_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the contractual obligation or condition.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Category of the contract term, such as Service Level Agreement (SLA) or Payment term.']
  termination_fee decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee payable upon early termination of the contract.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to give notice before terminating the contract.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target value that must be met or not exceeded for the term (e.g., 99.5).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit for the metric or threshold (e.g., percent, ms, transactions_per_hour).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the term record.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific terms, obligations, SLAs, and performance conditions within a vendor contract. Captures term type (SLA, payment term, liability cap, indemnification, data security obligation, HIPAA BAA requirement, audit right, termination for convenience), term description, numeric threshold or target value, unit of measure, penalty or remedy for breach, and effective date range. Enables granular tracking of contractual obligations and SLA compliance monitoring.'
}

Table vendor.contract_amendment {
  contract_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contract amendment record.']
  approving_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Amendment approvals are performed by employees; linking provides audit trail for regulatory and risk reviews.']
  parent_contract_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract_amendment (parent_contract_amendment_id)']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original vendor contract to which this amendment applies.']
  amendment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the amendment (e.g., price increase or discount).']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'External sequential number or code assigned to the amendment by the vendor management process.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale or trigger for creating the amendment.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the amendment (e.g., scope change, price adjustment).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment was formally approved.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the key modifications made in the amendment.']
  contract_amendment_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the changes introduced by the amendment.']
  contract_amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the amendment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the amendment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the amendment amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment becomes legally binding.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the amendment record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the amendment record.']

  Note: 'Tracks all formal amendments, addenda, and modifications to an existing vendor contract. Captures amendment number, amendment type (scope change, price adjustment, term extension, SLA modification), amendment effective date, description of changes, amendment status (pending, executed, rejected), and the approving authority. Maintains a complete audit trail of contract evolution over the vendor relationship lifecycle.'
}

Table vendor.spend {
  spend_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each vendor spend transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor spend tracking per cost center requires linking spend records to cost_center for budgeting, variance analysis, and regulatory reporting.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: Financial reporting ties spend records to the specific delegated credentialing entity receiving the service.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who approved the spend.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Spend analysis per employer group needs to attribute each vendor transaction to the responsible employer group for budgeting and cost allocation.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Enables plan‑level spend analysis and allocation of vendor expenditures for budgeting and compliance.']
  parent_spend_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on spend (parent_spend_id)']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network Spend Analysis aggregates vendor spend by provider network for financial reporting and budgeting.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order linked to the spend transaction.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract under which the spend was incurred.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor providing the goods or services.']
  amount_discount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any discounts granted on the spend.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before any discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after applying discounts and taxes.']
  amount_original_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Spend amount expressed in the currency in which the vendor invoiced.']
  amount_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on the spend transaction.']
  amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Spend amount after conversion to US dollars for consolidated reporting.']
  approval_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time the spend received approval.']
  budget_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal budgeting window to which the spend is allocated.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the spend meets internal compliance policies and external regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor spend record was first loaded into the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code of the currency in which the spend is recorded.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be made to avoid penalties.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Conversion factor from the transaction currency to USD at the time of posting.']
  expense_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the expense for budgeting and reporting.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter (1‑4) of the fiscal year for the spend.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year in which the spend is recorded for financial reporting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Accounting code used to post the spend in the finance system.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Identifier used by finance to track internal orders linked to the spend.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date printed on the vendor invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Number assigned by the vendor to the invoice for this spend.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spend has been formally approved.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was executed.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or channel used to settle the invoice.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier returned by the payment processor for the transaction.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Code of the project or program that the spend supports.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the physical or electronic receipt was issued.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) reflecting vendor financial and operational risk.']
  spend_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the nature of the spend.']
  spend_description varchar [note: 'Narrative detail describing the goods or services purchased.']
  spend_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the spend transaction in its processing workflow.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the tax regime applied to the spend.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate used to calculate the tax amount.']
  transaction_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing when the spend event occurred in the real world.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the vendor spend record.']
  vendor_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of vendor performance used for category management.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all spend activity against vendor contracts and purchase orders — invoice payments, purchase card transactions, and non-PO payments. Captures spend transaction date, invoice reference, payment amount, currency, cost center, GL account code, spend category (IT services, professional services, facilities, print/mail, office supplies), budget period, contract reference, PO reference, and payment method. Serves as the SSOT for vendor spend analytics, budget tracking, and category management reporting.'
}

Table vendor.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order record.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Link to the budget line item funding this purchase order.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor contract governing this purchase order.']
  parent_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on purchase_order (parent_purchase_order_id)']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Purchase Orders for Network Services tie each PO to the provider network receiving the service, needed for cost allocation and audit.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor to whom the purchase order is issued.']
  approval_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order received final approval.']
  approval_workflow_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the workflow definition governing approval routing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the purchase order.']
  bill_to_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity responsible for payment of the purchase order.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the purchase order expense is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was first captured.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of the purchase order.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of discounts applied to the purchase order.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any discount program applied to the purchase order.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate applied when the purchase order currency differs from the company\'s base currency.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on the vendor invoice associated with the purchase order.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor invoice linked to the purchase order.']
  is_three_way_match_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether three‑way matching (PO, receipt, invoice) is required for this order.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount after tax, discounts, and adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order was created in the system.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount actually paid to the vendor.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was executed.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be made to the vendor.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment for the purchase order.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms defining when payment is due (e.g., Net30, Net45).']
  po_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the purchase order.']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order based on procurement strategy.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the spend type for the purchase order.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Project identifier associated with the purchase order, if applicable.']
  purchase_order_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or scope of the purchase order.']
  purchase_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the purchase order.']
  receipt_received_date date [note: 'Date when the goods or services were received against the purchase order.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that initiated the purchase order.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the goods or services must be delivered.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk rating for the purchase order based on vendor and spend criteria.']
  ship_to_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location where the ordered items will be shipped.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the purchase order.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction or rule applied to the purchase order.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the purchase order before taxes, discounts, and adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the purchase order record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initially created the purchase order record.']

  Note: 'Master record for purchase orders (POs) issued to vendors for goods and services. Captures PO number, PO type (standard, blanket, emergency), issue date, required delivery date, PO status (draft, approved, issued, partially received, closed, cancelled), total PO amount, currency, ship-to location, bill-to entity, payment terms, and the requesting department. Links to vendor contract and budget line. Supports three-way match (PO, receipt, invoice) for accounts payable processing.'
}

Table vendor.po_line {
  po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase order line item.']
  parent_po_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on po_line (parent_po_line_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent purchase order header to which this line belongs.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third‑party vendor supplying the item or service.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the goods or service were actually received.']
  catalog_item_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the catalog item or service being procured on this line.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line complies with internal or regulatory requirements.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract governing this purchase.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center identifier for budgeting and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values on this line. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|CAD|JPY|CHF|AUD|... — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date the vendor is expected to deliver the goods or complete the service.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this line.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the line item is no longer valid (e.g., perishable goods).']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Financial account code to which this line\'s cost is charged.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'True when the line has been approved for purchase.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the line is exempt from tax.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the item (e.g., medical device, software, consulting).']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the goods or services on the line.']
  line_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total amount for the line before discounts and taxes (quantity × unit_price).']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the purchase order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line item.']
  net_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Final amount after discounts and taxes (line_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the line.']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to acquire the item (e.g., purchase, lease).']
  quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Amount of the item ordered, expressed in the specified unit of measure.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Status of receipt matching for the line.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk assessment for the vendor or item on this line.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax calculated for this line based on applicable tax codes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applied.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used to quantify the quantity (e.g., each, box, hour).']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price per unit of the item before discounts and taxes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the line record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the line.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a purchase order, representing individual goods or services being procured. Captures line number, item description, item category, quantity ordered, unit of measure, unit price, line amount, delivery date, line status (open, partially received, fully received, cancelled), and GL account code. Enables granular spend tracking, receipt matching, and budget allocation at the line level.'
}

Table vendor.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the goods receipt record.']
  parent_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on goods_receipt (parent_goods_receipt_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the purchase order linked to this receipt.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor who supplied the goods or services.']
  accepted_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of items accepted after inspection.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes about the receipt.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the receipt record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from the vendor\'s delivery documentation.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the gross amount.']
  freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Transportation cost associated with delivering the goods.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the goods before any discounts or adjustments.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the received goods were inspected.']
  is_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the receipt requires formal inspection.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the three-way match between PO, receipt, and invoice.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after discounts and adjustments.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the receipt, often used in three-way match processes.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the receipt.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the goods were physically received.']
  received_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of items received from the vendor.']
  receiver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or person who recorded the receipt.']
  receiving_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the goods were received.']
  rejected_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of items rejected during receipt inspection.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why items were rejected.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the gross amount of the receipt.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the receipt record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record confirming receipt of goods or services delivered by a vendor against a purchase order. Captures receipt date, received quantity, accepted quantity, rejected quantity, rejection reason, receiving location, receiver name, receipt status (pending inspection, accepted, partially accepted, rejected), and delivery note reference. Supports three-way match for invoice approval and tracks vendor delivery performance.'
}

Table vendor.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor invoice record.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: Invoices for credentialing services must reference the delegated entity to reconcile payments with contracts.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Invoice reconciliation and financial reporting require linking invoices to the employer group that receives the service.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Associates invoices with the specific health plan for accurate financial reconciliation and audit trails.']
  invoice_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the invoice.']
  invoice_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  parent_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on invoice (parent_invoice_id)']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Invoice-to-enrollment mapping supports audit of vendor invoices to the specific enrollment transaction that generated the service.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract governing the goods or services billed.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor that submitted the invoice.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the invoice approval process.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the invoice was approved.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the invoice with respect to required compliance checks.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the invoice expense is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the invoice.']
  currency_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if the invoice currency differs from the company\'s functional currency.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the invoice, if any.']
  discount_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for the discount applied.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is currently under dispute.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Textual reason for the invoice dispute, if any.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be made according to payment terms.']
  early_payment_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the early‑payment discount, if applicable.']
  expense_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the expense type for budgeting and reporting.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the vendor issued the invoice (business event timestamp).']
  invoice_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the goods or services provided.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number assigned by the vendor.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice within the AP workflow.']
  is_early_payment_discount boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an early‑payment discount was applied.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual line items included on the invoice.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after tax, discounts, and retainage are applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments related to the invoice.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which payment was made to the vendor.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to remit payment to the vendor.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment state of the invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment term code defining the number of days until payment is due.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order reference that the invoice is linked to, if applicable.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was received into the accounts payable system.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice complies with applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., HIPAA, ACA).']
  retention_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount held back as retainage pending final acceptance of goods/services.']
  retention_release_date date [note: 'Date when retained funds are scheduled to be released to the vendor.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated on the invoice based on applicable tax rules.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is exempt from tax.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) used to calculate tax_amount.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross amount billed by the vendor before taxes, discounts, or retainage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable invoice record submitted by a vendor for goods delivered or services rendered. Captures vendor invoice number, invoice date, invoice receipt date, invoice amount, tax amount, total amount due, currency, payment due date, payment terms, invoice status (received, under review, approved, disputed, paid, voided), PO reference, contract reference, and cost center allocation. Distinct from finance.ap_invoice which is the GL sub-ledger posting — this is the operational vendor invoice management record tracking the full invoice-to-approval workflow.'
}

Table vendor.performance {
  performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each vendor performance evaluation.']
  delegated_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.delegated_entity. Business justification: Performance evaluations are conducted per credentialing vendor; need FK to the delegated entity for score aggregation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee who performed the evaluation.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Vendor performance scores are evaluated per employer group contract to inform renewal decisions and SLA compliance.']
  parent_performance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance (parent_performance_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor being evaluated.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required certifications (e.g., ISO, SOC) for the vendor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the evaluation record was first created in the system.']
  customer_satisfaction_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average rating from internal customers regarding vendor performance.']
  evaluation_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the evaluation period.']
  evaluation_period varchar [note: 'Frequency of the evaluation (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual).']
  evaluation_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the evaluation period.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the evaluation record.']
  evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the evaluation was finalized.']
  evaluator_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments and observations recorded by the evaluator.']
  financial_stability_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rating of the vendor\'s financial health based on internal risk assessment.']
  issue_resolution_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of days to resolve reported vendor issues.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries completed on or before the agreed schedule.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score representing overall vendor performance for the period.']
  quality_defect_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of delivered items or services that required rework or were defective.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or code used by business users to locate the evaluation.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score derived from security, compliance, and operational risk factors.']
  sla_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of Service Level Agreements met during the evaluation period.']
  tier_decision varchar [note: 'Resulting tier action based on the evaluation (e.g., retain, upgrade).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the evaluation record.']

  Note: 'Periodic vendor performance evaluation record capturing scored assessments of vendor delivery quality, SLA compliance, responsiveness, and relationship health. Captures evaluation period (monthly, quarterly, annual), overall performance score, SLA compliance rate, on-time delivery rate, quality defect rate, issue resolution time, customer satisfaction rating, financial stability rating, and evaluator notes. Supports vendor tiering decisions, contract renewals, and strategic sourcing reviews.'
}

Table vendor.sla_event {
  sla_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA event.']
  parent_sla_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sla_event (parent_sla_event_id)']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor contract associated with this SLA event.']
  actual_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual measured value for the SLA metric.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the SLA breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA event record was created in the system.']
  cure_period_end date [note: 'End date of the cure period.']
  cure_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the cure period granted to the vendor to remediate the breach.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for penalty amount.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA measurement event occurred.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the SLA measurement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the SLA event.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the penalty applied.']
  penalty_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a penalty was triggered due to breach.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the breach was resolved.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Status of the breach resolution process.']
  sla_metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SLA metric measured (e.g., Response Time, Issue Resolution Time).']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SLA for this event.']
  target_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target SLA value as defined in the contract.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the SLA values.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA event record.']
  variance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between actual and target values (actual - target).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual SLA measurement events and breaches for vendor contracts. Captures SLA metric name, measurement date, target value, actual value, variance, SLA status (met, breached, at-risk), breach severity (minor, major, critical), penalty triggered flag, penalty amount, cure period start/end, and resolution status. Provides the granular event-level data underlying vendor performance scorecards and contract compliance monitoring.'
}

Table vendor.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor risk assessment record.']
  parent_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on risk_assessment (parent_risk_assessment_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor being assessed.']
  assessment_conducted_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team that performed the assessment.']
  assessment_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk assessment was performed.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology or framework used for the risk assessment (e.g., NIST, ISO 27001).']
  assessment_reference_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the assessment for external reference and tracking.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment is an initial onboarding, scheduled periodic, or triggered by an event.']
  business_continuity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) evaluating the vendor\'s business continuity capabilities.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of certifications the vendor holds (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001).']
  concentration_risk_flag boolean [note: 'True if the vendor represents a concentration risk to the organization.']
  control_effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the effectiveness of existing controls mitigating the inherent risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  cybersecurity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) assessing the vendor\'s cybersecurity posture.']
  financial_stability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting the vendor\'s financial health.']
  inherent_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the vendor\'s risk before controls are applied.']
  key_risk_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of the most significant risk issues identified during the assessment. [CONFIDENTIAL – business‑sensitive information].']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assessment record.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next risk assessment of the vendor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the assessor.']
  overall_residual_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated residual risk score after weighting all risk domains.']
  recommended_mitigations varchar [note: 'Actionable mitigation recommendations to address identified risks. [CONFIDENTIAL – business‑sensitive information].']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor meets required regulatory compliance standards.']
  regulatory_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) measuring compliance with applicable regulations.']
  reputational_risk_flag boolean [note: 'True if the vendor poses a reputational risk.']
  residual_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the remaining risk after controls are considered.']
  risk_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment.']
  risk_domain varchar [note: 'Primary risk domain evaluated in this assessment.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing overall risk severity on a 0‑100 scale.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Categorization of the vendor into risk tiers for TPRM program.']

  Note: 'Risk assessment record evaluating the operational, financial, cybersecurity, and compliance risk profile of a vendor. Captures assessment date, assessment type (initial onboarding, periodic, triggered), risk domain (financial stability, cybersecurity, business continuity, regulatory compliance, concentration risk, reputational), inherent risk rating, control effectiveness rating, residual risk rating (low, medium, high, critical), key risk findings, recommended mitigations, and next assessment due date. Supports vendor risk tiering and third-party risk management (TPRM) program requirements.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_certification {
  vendor_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each vendor certification record.']
  parent_vendor_certification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_certification (parent_vendor_certification_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor to which the certification belongs.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the certification (e.g., "SOC 2 Type II – Security").']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the issuing body to the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Standardized type of certification held by the vendor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: minority_owned|women_owned|small_business — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of compliance (e.g., security, privacy, financial).']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Key requirements or controls that the certification attests to.']
  compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or industry scope covered by the certification (e.g., HIPAA Security Rule, PCI DSS v4.0).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Internal reference or file name for the stored certification document.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Secure link to the digital copy of the certification document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the certification became effective for the vendor (may differ from issue date).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires and must be renewed.']
  expiration_notice_date date [note: 'Date on which the expiration notice was sent.']
  expiration_notice_sent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether an expiration reminder has been sent to the vendor.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was originally issued to the vendor.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization or authority that issued the certification (e.g., AICPA, HITRUST, PCI SSC).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the certification (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code).']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the certification\'s validity and relevance.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next periodic review of the certification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or special conditions.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which renewal activities should be initiated to avoid lapse.']
  risk_assessment_level varchar [note: 'Categorical risk level derived from the risk assessment score.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the vendor\'s risk posture based on the certification.']
  status_date date [note: 'Date when the current certification status was last changed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  vendor_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']

  Note: 'Master record for compliance certifications, regulatory attestations, and industry accreditations held by a vendor. Captures certification type (SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, HITRUST CSF, PCI-DSS, HIPAA attestation, SSAE 18, minority/women-owned business certification, small business certification), issuing body, certification number, issue date, expiration date, certification status (active, expired, pending renewal, revoked), and document reference. Tracks vendor compliance posture and supports BAA and HIPAA security rule requirements for business associates.'
}

Table vendor.baa_agreement {
  baa_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the Business Associate Agreement record.']
  parent_baa_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on baa_agreement (parent_baa_agreement_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: BAA compliance tracking requires associating each Business Associate Agreement with the covered provider.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor party associated with this BAA.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the BAA by the health plan.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement indicating its legal form.']
  baa_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the BAA.']
  breach_notification_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is contractually required to notify the health plan of a PHI breach.']
  breach_notification_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days within which the vendor must report a PHI breach to the health plan.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor\'s compliance certifications relevant to the BAA.']
  covered_phi_elements varchar [note: 'List or description of the Protected Health Information data elements the vendor may access under the BAA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BAA record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the BAA becomes legally binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the BAA expires or terminates automatically (nullable for open‑ended agreements).']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date the BAA was signed by both parties.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BAA was last reviewed for compliance or renewal.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information about the BAA.']
  permitted_disclosures varchar [note: 'Authorized disclosures of PHI that the vendor may make under the BAA.']
  permitted_uses varchar [note: 'Allowed purposes for which the vendor may use the PHI covered by the BAA.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory BAA reviews.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the vendor\'s risk level for handling PHI.']
  security_safeguard_requirements varchar [note: 'Technical and administrative safeguards the vendor must implement to protect PHI.']
  signatory_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the health plan signatory.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the health plan representative who signed the BAA.']
  signatory_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the health plan signatory.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the health plan signatory.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Required notice period (in days) the vendor must give before terminating the BAA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the BAA record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the BAA (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Business Associate Agreement (BAA) record executed with vendors who handle Protected Health Information (PHI) on behalf of the health plan, as required under HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules (45 CFR §164.308). Captures BAA execution date, effective date, expiration date, BAA version, covered PHI data elements, permitted uses and disclosures, security safeguard requirements, breach notification obligations, BAA status (active, expired, terminated, pending), and the health plan signatory. Distinct from compliance.baa which is the compliance domain\'s regulatory obligation tracker — this record is the operational vendor-level BAA management entity.'
}

Table vendor.incident {
  incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each vendor incident.']
  parent_incident_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on incident (parent_incident_id)']
  provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Incident Impact Reporting links vendor security incidents to affected providers for risk management.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor associated with this incident.']
  affected_member_count varchar [note: 'Count of individual members impacted when PHI is involved.']
  breach_notification_date date [note: 'Calendar date when members received breach notification.']
  breach_notification_sent boolean [note: 'True if affected members were notified per breach‑notification rules.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance certification state of the vendor (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001).']
  corrective_action_required varchar [note: 'Specific remediation steps mandated for the vendor or internal team.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of initial record insertion.']
  impacted_functions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of health‑plan functions (e.g., claims processing, member portal) affected.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the incident was first observed.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the incident event, including observable symptoms.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable incident number assigned by the vendor management process.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the incident within the incident management workflow.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the vendor incident.']
  is_phi_involved boolean [note: 'True if the incident involved protected health information as defined by HIPAA.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date the required regulatory body was notified.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'True when the incident triggers mandatory reporting under HIPAA, CMS, or other regulations.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/system that logged the incident.']
  reporting_channel varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the incident report.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Calendar date when corrective actions were completed and the incident was closed.']
  risk_assessment_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating (e.g., 1‑10) reflecting vendor risk at incident occurrence.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the incident after investigation.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity classification indicating impact and urgency.']
  severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric representation of severity used for reporting and trend analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the latest modification to the incident record.']
  vendor_response_date date [note: 'Date the vendor acknowledged the incident and supplied an initial response.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for operational incidents, service disruptions, data breaches, and compliance violations involving a vendor. Captures incident date, incident type (service outage, data breach, SLA breach, regulatory violation, fraud, billing error, delivery failure), severity level, incident description, impacted health plan functions, affected member count (if PHI involved), vendor response date, resolution date, root cause, corrective action required, and incident status (open, under investigation, resolved, escalated). Supports vendor risk management, HIPAA breach notification workflows, and contract dispute processes.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_document {
  vendor_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the vendor document record.']
  parent_vendor_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_document (parent_vendor_document_id)']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor contract to which this document is attached.']
  business_owner varchar [note: 'Internal business owner responsible for the document and its compliance.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA‑256) used to verify file integrity.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or contractual compliance standards applicable to the document (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR, SOC2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was first created in the system.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier or reference number assigned to the document by the vendor or the organization.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the vendor document (e.g., contract, SOW, BAA, insurance certificate, W‑9, NDA, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: contract|sow|baa|insurance_certificate|w9|certification|audit_report|rfp_response|nda|correspondence — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the document becomes effective or binding for the vendor relationship.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the document expires or is no longer valid.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the stored document file in bytes.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document is a mandatory requirement for the vendor relationship.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the document must be retained to satisfy legal or policy requirements.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the risk associated with the document or its underlying agreement.']
  storage_location_uri varchar [note: 'Uniform Resource Identifier pointing to the physical or cloud storage location of the document.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the document, used to identify its purpose (e.g., "Master Services Agreement").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  uploaded_by varchar [note: 'Name of the internal user who uploaded the document.']
  vendor_document_description varchar [note: 'Brief free‑text description providing context or summary of the document content.']
