  vendor_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document within the vendor management process.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the document, typically following a numeric or alphanumeric scheme (e.g., "1.0", "2.1a").']

  Note: 'Repository and single source of truth for all documents associated with a vendor relationship — executed contracts, SOWs, BAAs, insurance certificates, W-9 forms, certifications, audit reports, RFP responses, NDAs, and correspondence. Captures document type, document title, document version, upload date, effective date, expiration date, document status (current, superseded, expired), storage location reference, and the business owner responsible for the document. Supports contract management, compliance audits, and vendor onboarding workflows.'
}

Table vendor.onboarding {
  onboarding_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each vendor onboarding record.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract associated with the vendor onboarding.']
  parent_onboarding_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on onboarding (parent_onboarding_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor being onboarded, linking to the vendor master record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the onboarding was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who authorized the vendor onboarding.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the business need for engaging the vendor.']
  checklist_baa_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Business Associate Agreement has been executed with the vendor.']
  checklist_diversity_cert_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s diversity certification has been verified.']
  checklist_financial_due_diligence boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial due diligence on the vendor has been completed.']
  checklist_insurance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s insurance certificate has been verified.']
  checklist_security_questionnaire boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s security questionnaire has been completed.']
  checklist_w9_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s W-9 tax form has been received.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the onboarding process was completed and the vendor was approved for activation.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance certification status of the vendor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s active status ends, if applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor becomes active in the system after successful onboarding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations about the onboarding.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the onboarding process.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the onboarding process.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the onboarding request was submitted.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the onboarding request.']
  requestor_department varchar [note: 'Internal department that initiated the vendor onboarding request.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the vendor\'s risk assessment result.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the onboarding workflow.']
  total_onboarding_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregated monetary cost incurred during the onboarding process.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing the end-to-end vendor onboarding lifecycle from initial request through approved vendor status. Captures onboarding request date, requestor department, business justification, vendor type, onboarding checklist status (W-9 received, insurance certificate verified, BAA executed, security questionnaire completed, financial due diligence completed, diversity certification verified), onboarding stage, approvals obtained, onboarding completion date, and onboarding outcome (approved, conditionally approved, rejected). Ensures all compliance and risk gates are cleared before a vendor is activated in the vendor master.'
}

Table vendor.rfp {
  rfp_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the RFP record.']
  awarded_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor that was awarded the contract, if any.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user who approved the solicitation.']
  parent_rfp_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rfp (parent_rfp_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the solicitation received final approval to be issued.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the solicitation before issuance.']
  award_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount of the awarded contract.']
  award_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the award amount is expressed.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date the contract was formally awarded to the selected vendor.']
  award_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the award decision.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or internal compliance obligations tied to the solicitation.']
  contract_end_estimate date [note: 'Projected end date of the contract based on the term.']
  contract_start_estimate date [note: 'Projected start date of the contract once awarded.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the contract in months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFP record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of the estimated value.']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected monetary value of the contract before award.']
  evaluation_criteria varchar [note: 'Set of criteria used to assess vendor responses (e.g., cost, compliance, capability).']
  invited_vendor_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct vendors that were invited to participate in the solicitation.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the solicitation was formally issued to vendors.']
  received_response_count varchar [note: 'Count of vendor responses received by the response due date.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which vendors must submit their responses.']
  rfp_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the goods or services being sourced.']
  rfp_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the solicitation.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating assigned to the solicitation (0‑100 scale).']
  solicitation_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the solicitation for tracking and reference.']
  solicitation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the solicitation is a Request for Proposal, Request for Information, or Request for Quote.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the RFP record.']
  spend_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the spend (e.g., IT services, office supplies, facilities).']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the solicitation that summarizes its purpose.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User name or identifier of the person who last updated the RFP record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the RFP record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User name or identifier of the person who created the RFP record.']

  Note: 'Master record for Request for Proposal (RFP), Request for Information (RFI), and Request for Quote (RFQ) events issued to prospective vendors during the sourcing and procurement process. Captures solicitation type (RFP, RFI, RFQ), solicitation title, category of spend, issue date, response due date, evaluation criteria, number of vendors invited, number of responses received, solicitation status (draft, issued, evaluation, awarded, cancelled), awarded vendor reference, and estimated contract value. Supports strategic sourcing, competitive bidding, and procurement governance.'
}

Table vendor.rfp_response {
  rfp_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the RFP response record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or analyst who performed the evaluation.']
  parent_rfp_response_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rfp_response (parent_rfp_response_id)']
  rfp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.rfp. Business justification: Capture which RFP a response belongs to; a response can only belong to one RFP, while an RFP can have many responses.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the responding vendor (master party).']
  commercial_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting the commercial/price competitiveness of the proposal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RFP response record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary discount offered by the vendor, if any.']
  evaluation_comments varchar [note: 'Detailed comments from the evaluator explaining the scores and decision rationale.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the response within the evaluation workflow.']
  evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the evaluation of the response was completed.']
  net_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount after discounts and taxes (total_price - discount_amount + tax_amount).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks captured during intake.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score combining technical and commercial evaluations, typically weighted per business policy.']
  proposal_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the vendor\'s proposal for tracking and audit.']
  response_type varchar [note: 'Type of solicitation the response addresses: Request for Proposal (RFP), Request for Information (RFI), or Request for Quote (RFQ).']
  short_list_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response was placed on the short‑list for further consideration.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vendor submitted the response.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated tax applicable to the total price based on jurisdiction.']
  technical_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting the technical merit of the vendor\'s solution.']
  total_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount proposed by the vendor before discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the RFP response record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each vendor\'s response to an RFP, RFI, or RFQ solicitation. Captures responding vendor, submission date, proposal reference number, proposed price/total cost of ownership, technical score, commercial score, overall evaluation score, evaluator notes, shortlist flag, and response status (received, under evaluation, shortlisted, selected, rejected). Enables competitive bid evaluation, vendor selection documentation, and sourcing audit trails.'
}

Table vendor.insurance {
  insurance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor insurance record.']
  parent_insurance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on insurance (parent_insurance_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor to which this insurance certificate applies.']
  additional_insured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health plan is listed as an additional insured on the policy.']
  additional_insured_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the entity (e.g., health plan) listed as additional insured.']
  certificate_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored digital copy of the insurance certificate.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date the certificate of insurance was issued to the vendor.']
  certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the insurance certificate.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s insurance meets contractual compliance requirements.']
  coverage_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the insurance coverage becomes effective.']
  coverage_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the insurance coverage expires.']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount the policy will pay for a covered loss.']
  coverage_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the coverage limit amount.']
  deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount the vendor must pay out-of-pocket before insurance coverage applies.']
  deductible_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the deductible amount.']
  insurance_type varchar [note: 'Category of insurance coverage required from the vendor.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier providing the coverage.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insurance record is currently active (true) or inactive (false).']
  minimum_coverage_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contractually required minimum coverage amount for the vendor.']
  minimum_coverage_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the minimum coverage threshold.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations about the insurance certificate.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Unique policy number assigned by the insurer.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the insurance record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the insurance record.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assessing the vendor\'s insurance risk based on coverage and limits.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking insurance certificates and coverage requirements for vendors. Captures insurance type (general liability, professional liability/E&O, cyber liability, workers compensation, umbrella/excess, auto liability), insurer name, policy number, coverage limit, deductible amount, certificate effective date, certificate expiration date, additional insured status (health plan named as additional insured), certificate status (current, expiring, expired, waived), and required minimum coverage threshold. Ensures vendors maintain contractually required insurance coverage throughout the relationship.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_dispute {
  vendor_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor dispute record.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: REQUIRED: Vendor disputes often stem from appeal decisions; linking provides audit trail and regulatory reporting of dispute origins.']
  parent_vendor_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_dispute (parent_vendor_dispute_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order associated with the disputed invoice or service.']
  related_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a claim linked to the dispute, when the dispute originates from a claim payment.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor contract related to the dispute, if applicable.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor against which the dispute is raised.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the dispute involves regulatory or contractual compliance considerations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of the disputed amount.']
  dispute_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the dispute for reporting and analytics.']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the issue, cause, and context of the dispute.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the dispute, used in communications and tracking.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the dispute (e.g., invoice, service delivery, SLA, contract interpretation).']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that is being contested by the vendor.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute was escalated to higher management or external arbitration.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for escalating the dispute.']
  internal_recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommendation from internal stakeholders (e.g., finance, legal) on how to resolve the dispute.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information captured by users.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute was formally opened in the system.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority indicating the urgency of resolution.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of any regulatory filing, audit, or statutory requirement linked to the dispute.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute was formally resolved or closed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating associated with the dispute, used for prioritization and reporting.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount agreed upon to settle the dispute.']
  supporting_evidence_refs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of URLs or document identifiers that provide evidence for the dispute.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dispute record.']
  vendor_dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dispute.']
  vendor_position varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s stated stance or explanation regarding the disputed amount.']
  vendor_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor submitted their response or position on the dispute.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the dispute record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for formal disputes raised against vendors — invoice disputes, service delivery disputes, SLA penalty disputes, and contract interpretation disagreements. Captures dispute type, dispute open date, disputed amount, dispute description, supporting evidence references, vendor response date, vendor position, internal resolution recommendation, dispute status (open, under negotiation, escalated, resolved, arbitration), resolution date, and final settlement amount. Supports accounts payable dispute management and contract enforcement workflows.'
}

Table vendor.spend_category {
  spend_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each spend category record.']
  parent_category_spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent category in the hierarchical taxonomy; null for top‑level categories.']
  parent_spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on spend_category (parent_spend_category_id)']
  applicable_vendor_type varchar [note: 'Vendor type(s) for which this spend category is relevant.']
  budget_allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual budget amount allocated to this spend category (in corporate currency).']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to uniquely identify the spend category within the organization.']
  category_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the depth of the category in the hierarchy: L1 (major), L2 (sub‑category), L3 (detail).']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the spend category.']
  category_owner varchar [note: 'Name or role of the internal stakeholder responsible for the category.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual list of regulatory or policy requirements applicable to the category.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier linked to the category for financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the category record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the category definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the category definition expires or is superseded; null if indefinite.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., ERP, procurement) to reference this spend category.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account code used for posting spend transactions classified under this category.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited string representing the full path from the top‑level category to this node (e.g., "IT Services/Software/SaaS").']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the category was last reviewed for relevance or compliance.']
  preferred_sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic sourcing approach recommended for this category.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews of the category.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative assessment of risk associated with the category (0‑100 scale).']
  spend_category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope and purpose of the category.']
  spend_category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the category.']
  unspsc_code varchar [note: 'Universal Standard Classification code that aligns the spend category to the global UNSPSC taxonomy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the category record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the hierarchical spend category taxonomy used to classify all vendor spend — enabling category management, budget allocation, and strategic sourcing analysis. Captures category code, category name, parent category (for hierarchy), category level (L1: major category, L2: sub-category, L3: detail category), UNSPSC code alignment, applicable vendor types, preferred sourcing strategy (single-source, dual-source, competitive bid), and category owner. Examples: IT Services > Software > SaaS Applications; Professional Services > Consulting > Management Consulting; Facilities > Maintenance > HVAC Services.'
}

Table vendor.relationship {
  relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor relationship record.']
  parent_relationship_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on relationship (parent_relationship_id)']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary contract governing this vendor relationship.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor organization involved in the relationship.']
  annual_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent or upcoming annual performance and compliance review.']
  compliance_certification_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of required certifications (e.g., SOC, ISO) for the vendor.']
  concentration_risk_flag boolean [note: 'True when the vendor represents a high concentration of spend or risk across the portfolio.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level applied to the relationship information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor relationship record was first created in the system.']
  data_security_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific security controls required of the vendor (e.g., encryption, access controls).']
  dependency_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of how critical the vendor\'s services are to core business operations.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the vendor relationship became active and binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the vendor relationship is scheduled to terminate or expire (null for open‑ended).']
  executive_sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the senior executive championing the vendor partnership.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last completed annual or ad‑hoc relationship review.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal employee responsible for day‑to‑day management of the vendor relationship.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context, observations, or action items.']
  regulatory_compliance varchar [note: 'Regulatory frameworks the vendor must adhere to (e.g., HIPAA, PCI, SOC 2).']
  relationship_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code used to reference the vendor relationship in contracts and reports.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor relationship.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score derived from financial, compliance, and operational assessments.']
  single_source_flag boolean [note: 'True if the organization relies on this vendor as the sole source for a given service or product.']
  strategic_importance_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑5) indicating the strategic value of the vendor to the organization.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor relationship based on strategic importance and spend volume.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vendor relationship record.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing the strategic relationship classification and account management structure for each vendor. Captures relationship tier (strategic partner, preferred vendor, approved vendor, spot-buy vendor), relationship manager (internal owner), executive sponsor, relationship inception date, annual review date, strategic importance rating, dependency level (critical, high, medium, low), single-source flag, concentration risk flag, and relationship notes. Supports vendor portfolio management, strategic sourcing decisions, and business continuity planning.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_audit {
  vendor_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vendor audit record.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Vendor audits are a type of audit engagement; linking them enables consolidating audit findings, risk scores, and compliance metrics across internal and vendor audits.']
  parent_vendor_audit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_audit (parent_vendor_audit_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor being audited.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the audit, used for tracking and reference in reports.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the functional, geographic, and regulatory scope covered by the audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the audit performed (e.g., HIPAA security, SOC 2, contract compliance, financial, operational).']
  auditor_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the auditor.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or organization conducting the audit.']
  auditor_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the auditor.']
  auditor_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the auditor is an internal employee or an external third‑party.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the audit was officially completed.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry framework(s) the audit assesses (e.g., HIPAA, SOC 2, ISO 27001).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which all required corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any corrective actions were mandated as a result of the audit.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost incurred to perform the audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the audit cost.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored audit report or supporting documentation.']
  findings_high_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as high severity.']
  findings_low_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as low severity.']
  findings_medium_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as medium severity.']
  initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the audit was formally started.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the auditor during the audit.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating assigned to the audit based on findings and compliance.']
  period_end date [note: 'Last day of the period examined by the audit.']
  period_start date [note: 'First day of the period examined by the audit.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing body or standard organization relevant to the audit.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score derived from audit findings, on a 0‑100 scale.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current audit status, e.g., reason for delay or remediation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for formal audits and compliance reviews conducted on vendors — HIPAA security audits, SOC 2 review assessments, contract compliance audits, financial audits, and operational performance audits. Captures audit type, audit scope, audit period, audit initiation date, audit completion date, auditor (internal or external), audit findings count by severity, overall audit rating, corrective action required flag, corrective action due date, and audit status (planned, in progress, completed, findings remediated). Supports third-party risk management and regulatory compliance obligations.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_lifecycle_event {
  vendor_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each immutable vendor lifecycle event record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that initiated the event.']
  parent_vendor_lifecycle_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_lifecycle_event (parent_vendor_lifecycle_event_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor to which this lifecycle event applies.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or committee that approved the event.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the event required a compliance check or regulatory review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  event_origin_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the event (e.g., Facets, ProviderSource, PBM).']
  event_sequence varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing number to preserve chronological order within a vendor\'s lifecycle.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lifecycle event occurred in the vendor\'s relationship.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical code describing the nature of the lifecycle event.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is considered critical for compliance or risk management.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Vendor status after the event was applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the event.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Vendor status before the event was applied.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of why the lifecycle event was triggered.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory requirement or guideline that triggered the event (e.g., HIPAA, CMS).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the vendor at the time of the event.']
  supporting_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to any external document that substantiates the event (e.g., contract amendment, approval memo).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this event record.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of all significant lifecycle status transitions and events in the vendor master record — onboarding approval, tier reclassification, contract execution, suspension, reinstatement, termination, and offboarding. Captures event type, event timestamp, previous status, new status, triggering reason, event initiator, approving authority, and supporting notes. Provides a complete chronological history of the vendor relationship for compliance, audit, and governance purposes.'
}
